Title: Data-analist
Description:
Ben jij klaar voor een uitdaging als Data Analist bij de snelst groeiende huisdierenverzekeraar van Europa? Lees dan snel verder!




Wat ga je doen?

Als Data Analist ben jij de onmisbare schakel in vele projecten. Jouw focus ligt op data analyse, jij bent de (technische) schakel tussen data en de operationele organisatie. Je bent in staat grote databestanden te koppelen en te doorgronden en vanuit hier naar een presentabele analyse te komen. Je bent nieuwsgierig en verdiept je in de business achtergronden om zo een juiste vertaalslag van data te kunnen maken. Je werkt nauw samen met je collega’s in het Data, Product en Pricing (DPP) team op het kantoor in Amsterdam en met de andere collega’s van de analytics community in heel Europa.

Jouw belangrijkste taken op een rijtje:

opstellen van Power BI dashboards en deze presenteren aan en fine-tunen met bijvoorbeeld het management
interpreteren van (data) verzoeken vanuit de organisatie en selecteren van de relevante databronnen waaruit de onderliggende data moeten worden verzameld en aan elkaar gekoppeld
proactief signaleren van bijzonderheden of opvallende trends of issues in de data
data gedrevenheid van de organisatie verhogen door interne stakeholders te helpen vanuit zichzelf met data te werken en en zo besluitvorming te verbeteren
gevraagd en ongevraagd advies uitbrengen over de mogelijkheden om de digitale vastlegging van onze interne data te verbeteren
je beheert ons Snowflake data warehouse




Wie ben jij?

Figo Pet is een snelgroeiende speler op het gebied van huisdierverzekeringen, gericht op het bieden van hoogwaardige, toegankelijke zorg voor huisdieren. Ons doel is simpel: het leven van huisdieren en hun eigenaren makkelijker en zorgelozer maken om zolang mogelijk van elkaar te kunnen genieten. Dit door hen o.a. te beschermen tegen onvoorziene medische kosten.




Wat wij vragen

Om in deze toffe, centrale functie succesvol te zijn neem je – naast een grote passie voor huisdieren – het volgende mee:

een afgeronde HBO- of WO-opleiding in de richting van bijvoorbeeld Business Information Management, Bedrijfseconomie of Bedrijfskunde
tenminste 2 jaar werkervaring in een soortgelijke rol
kennis van presentatie- en rapportagetechnieken, ervaring met Power BI
kennis van cloudgebaseerde dataplatformen zoals Snowflake of Databricks is een pré
je bent sterk analytisch met een oog voor detail en in staat betrouwbare en herhaalbare analyses uit te voeren
kennis van programmeertaal SQL en/of Python
je bent in staat alle rapportages, modellen, grafieken in de verschillende applicaties, zoals Excel en Power BI, toe te passen en grote databestanden te verwerken
uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden, zowel in het Nederlands als het Engels
je bent woonachtig in Nederland, al onze functies zijn deels op kantoor
tot slot ben je niet allergisch voor honden, in verband met onze kantoorhonden 🐶




Wat je van ons kunt verwachten

Bij Figo vind je een werkomgeving waar een jong team vol enthousiasme bouwt aan ons merk en de organisatie. De sfeer past bij die van een scale-up; vol energie om te groeien. Verder bieden wij jou:

een bruto maandsalaris tussen de €4250 en €4750 per maand op basis van 40 uur per week
25 vakantiedagen bij een 40-urige werkweek
8% vakantiegeld
een goede pensioenregeling
een arbeidsovereenkomst voor 1 jaar, zijn we na dit jaar beide blij met elkaar? Dan verlengen we deze graag
een NS-businesskaart, ons kantoor is centraal gelegen vlakbij station Amsterdam Sloterdijk. Wij bieden geen parkeerplek voor een eigen auto.
laptop en de mogelijkheid om hybride te werken, we werken 50/50
thuiswerkvergoeding van €150 in het verbeteren van jouw thuiswerkplek
jaarlijks opleidingsbudget om jezelf verder te ontwikkelen in jouw functie
€100 budget voor jouw huisdier per jaar en 50% premiekorting op jouw huisdierenverzekering
jaarlijks de mogelijkheid om twee weken fully remote te werken. Waar houd jij jouw ‘workation’?
een uitdagende functie binnen een scale-up met internationale groeiambities en doorgroeimogelijkheden
gezamenlijke lunch en vers fruit op kantoor
gezellige maandelijkse borrel op kantoor of elders en leuke personeelsuitjes
een gezellig en ambitieus team van dierenliefhebbers en de aanwezige vrolijke honden op kantoor




Solliciteer

Zie jij jezelf in deze uitdagende rol? Dan zien wij jouw sollicitatie graag tegemoet via de website: Data Analist - Figo

Title: Junior Data Analyst
HTML:
About the Role:

For a client in Amsterdam, we are looking for a interim Data Analyst. The ideal candidate will use their passion for big data and analytics to provide insights to the business covering a range of topics. They will be responsible for conducting both recurring and ad hoc analysis for business users.


Responsibilities

  • Understand the day-to-day issues that our business faces, which can be better understood with data;
  • Compile and analyse data related to business' issues;
  • Develop clear visualisations to convey complicated data in a straightforward manner


Requirements:

  • 1-3 years of experience;
  • Hands-on experience with SQL;
  • Working experience with Python and/or PySpark;
  • Working experience with Tableau or PowerBI;
  • Stakeholder management and communication skills;
  • Must be based in the Netherlands (sponsorship not offered)


What We Offer:

  • An interim position
  • Flexible Working: Hybrid model, with 2–3 days in the office in Amsterdam (non-negotiable)

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
As a Data Analyst within the Asset Finance EPT (Empowered Product Team) you will play a key role in enabling a data-driven Originate-to-Distribute (OtD) business. Based in The Hague, you will support our Asset Finance professionals by ensuring that data is accurate, reliable, and translated into meaningful insights for both internal stakeholders and external investors. In this role, you will work at the intersection of business, data, and technology, contributing directly to the quality of our reporting, the robustness of our data chain, and the continuous improvement of our Asset Finance processes. Work is hybrid and the official location for this job is The Hague.







Responsibilities

You will conduct data analysis and delivery and support the functional support team’s day-to-day operations, including frequent meetings with users. Other responsibilities include:
Managing the applications.
Working towards team goals and supporting everyday processes and testing for colleagues in your Asset Finance team EPT.
Driving continuous improvements and process automation.
Executing Security Operations tasks to ensure that processes and applications are securely managed and in control.







Qualifications




Experience with data analysis (SQL, Excel, Power BI or similar tools).
Strong logical thinking and problem-solving skills, with the ability to break down complex issues into manageable components.
Affinity with financial data (e.g. loans, portfolios, cashflows).
A structured and methodical approach to analyzing data and identifying root causes.
Ability to communicate insights clearly to both technical and business stakeholders.
A proactive mindset with a focus on continuous improvement.
Fluent in English, Dutch is a preference.




Please note that for this role you need to be already situated in the Netherlands.




For more information, please contact Ritsa (ritsa.paraskevopoulou@experis.nl).

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst - Data Quality, Governance & Access

Are you a data analyst with a strong product mindset and an affinity for data quality? For a major Dutch bank, we are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Customer Data domain within Digital Business Banking. This team provides the capabilities to retrieve, store and validate customer data for both retail and business clients. This data sits at the core of the bank's operations and is used in critical processes such as onboarding, servicing, and regulatory reporting.




About the role

As a Data Analyst, you play a key role in improving and safeguarding the quality of customer data. You combine analytical skills with a product mindset and work closely with stakeholders across the bank.




You will:

Monitor and improve data quality by defining and applying data quality rules and controls
Analyse data issues (e.g. duplicates, missing or incorrect data) and identify root causes across processes and systems
Drive structural improvements by collaborating with upstream and downstream teams
Support consumers of customer data and help them use data effectively
Translate business needs into clear data requirements and user stories
Increase data awareness and promote data quality practices across the organisation

You will take ownership of data quality improvement topics and contribute to prioritisation together with stakeholders across multiple domains.




About you

Strong analytical skills, with decisions based on data and insights
Proficient in SQL and experienced working with large datasets
Hands-on experience with Power BI for building dashboards and visualising insights
Familiar with analytical tools such as Python (pandas) and/or Excel for deeper data analysis
Effective communication with both technical and business stakeholders
Able to translate data into clear, actionable insights that drive impact
Proactive ownership within your domain, driving improvements
Thrives in multidisciplinary, agile teams
Fluent in Dutch and English, both verbal and written
Experience in financial institutions or the banking sector




About Levy Professionals

Since 2000 we provide professional solutions to organizations ranging from tech start-ups to global players. From our offices in Amsterdam and London we have built an international and local network of skilled employed professionals and contractors fuelled by our passion for connecting skills with projects. Over the years we have fulfilled over 2500 requirements and nowadays we consistently have 250+ professionals recruited and relocated from 14 countries allocated to various projects. Our strength is the way that we see and treat people. This will always be a key factor in our strategy for many years to come.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst | Amsterdam




Our client is a fast-growing Dutch SaaS company that lets consumers find and buy insurance through a smart app. With roughly 50 people and ambitions to expand across Europe, they're building out the data team that powers every business decision.




What will you be doing?




Build and own the data model that sits underneath every dashboard, report, and analysis in the company.
Set up and maintain data marts so the business always has clean, ready-to-use data at their fingertips.
Own the numbers - from financial close reports to operational updates - and make sure the right people have the right information at the right time.
Dig into the data for Finance and Insurance: spot patterns before anyone asks, agree on what the key metrics mean, and answer the questions that move decisions forward.




Tech stack

SQL · Python · Databricks · QuickSight · AWS




What do you bring?




3–6 years of experience as a data analyst or in a similar role
Strong SQL & Python skills
Python for data transformation and ad-hoc analysis
Hands-on experience with Databricks
Experience building dashboards (QuickSight, Power BI, Tableau or similar)
Comfortable working independently and owning your output




What's in it for you?

€80,000 - €85,000 gross per year, based on experience
Real ownership - small team, visible impact
Working in an environment where things actually get built




What to expect

The role is office-based in Amsterdam (3 days on-site). They operate in a high-performance environment where you'll be expected to work autonomously, get into the detail when needed, and keep up with a team that moves quickly and changes direction when necessary.




Interested and would you like to hear more? Please send me a direct message on either LinkedIn or email me with your most recent CV to: jan@doghouse-recruitment.nl

Title: Data Analist
Description:
Als Data Analist bij SynTouch ben jij degene die overzicht brengt in datastromen. Je spreekt de taal van de business én van data, en zorgt ervoor dat data slim, duidelijk en bruikbaar wordt ingezet.

Je helpt klanten hun data effectiever te gebruiken – door de juiste vragen te stellen, businessconcepten helder te maken en data op een begrijpelijke manier beschikbaar te stellen. Daarbij kijk je niet alleen naar wat er is, maar vooral naar wat er nodig is. Zo maak je echt het verschil.

Wat breng jij mee?

Je Bent Analytisch, Communicatief En Denkt In Oplossingen. Jij


Hebt ervaring als Data Analist
Kent datamodellering (UML), data profiling en BI-concepten
Hebt interesse in AI, Machine Learning en NLP (pré)
Begrijpt hoe belangrijk datakwaliteit is
Werkt graag samen met verschillende stakeholders – van IT tot business
Are fluent in Dutch


Klinkt dit als jouw rol? Dan horen we graag van je.

Title: Digital Analyst
Description:
ECOMMERCE PERFORMANCE ANALYST

Turn B2C data into sharper journeys, smarter trading and stronger growth.

Lijnden | B2C Analytics | 40 hours | Full-time

At Just Brands, we build menswear brands with character. PME Legend, Cast Iron and Vanguard each have their own voice, their own audience and their own place in the market. In B2C, growth happens across many moving parts: ecommerce, retail, marketplaces, CRM, digital marketing, site merchandising, UX and customer service. The challenge is not getting more data. The challenge is knowing which signals matter, what they mean, and what teams should do next.

But what really sets us apart is how we work together.

We’re team first. No ego, no unnecessary layers, no endless talking, no corporate theatre. We back each other, speak up, take ownership and keep pushing for better. We work hard, stay sharp and make sure there’s room to enjoy the ride too.

That’s where you land.

Why this role matters

This is where analytics becomes commercial action. As our Ecommerce Performance Analyst, you help the B2C team understand customer behavior, channel performance and the full digital journey across our web shops, apps, retail touchpoints and marketplaces. You turn data from ecommerce, CRM, paid media, site experience and sales into insights that improve conversion, retention, customer lifetime value, campaign performance and trading decisions. You are not here to build dashboards that quietly gather dust.

You are here to turn data into decisions that teams trust, use and act on.

What you’ll do


Analyze ecommerce performance across funnel, conversion, traffic, revenue, average order value, product/category performance and customer journeys;
Build and improve dashboards and reports in tools such as GA4, SQL, Power BI, Excel or similar BI platforms;
Translate data into clear recommendations for Ecommerce, CRM, Digital Marketing, Site Merchandising, Retail, Marketplaces, UX and IT;
Monitor digital marketing performance across paid, email, CRM and campaign traffic, connecting channel results to commercial outcomes;
Analyze customer behavior, segmentation, retention, repeat purchase and customer lifetime value to support CRM and lifecycle decisions;
Support ecommerce trading rhythms with weekly/monthly insights on what is selling, what is slowing down and where opportunities sit;
Design, support and evaluate A/B tests and experiments for landing pages, campaigns, content, UX improvements and conversion optimization;
Investigate drop-offs, friction points and underperforming journeys, and turn findings into practical actions for the right teams;
Improve data quality, tracking consistency, definitions and governance across B2C reporting;
Support strategic B2C projects with analysis, business cases, forecasting and performance measurement;
Help teams understand what the numbers mean by presenting insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.


What success looks like in your first year


B2C teams use your dashboards and insights in weekly and monthly decision rhythms
Ecommerce, CRM, marketing and site experience teams make better decisions based on trusted data
You identify conversion, journey or trading opportunities that lead to measurable business improvements
A/B tests and experiments create learnings that change how we build campaigns, pages and customer journeys
Reporting becomes cleaner, faster and less fragmented across channels and teams
Stakeholders see you as a strong sparring partner, not just a reporting resource.


What makes this role exciting


You work across the full B2C ecosystem: ecommerce, retail, CRM, digital marketing, marketplaces, site experience and UX
You sit close to revenue, conversion, customer behaviour and daily trading decisions
You influence both the commercial performance and the customer experience of three strong menswear brands
You get room to test, learn and build better ways of working with data
You help shape how a growing international business becomes more data-driven.


You’ll probably love this role if you…


Enjoy turning messy data into clear direction and practical next steps
Like investigating why performance changes, not just reporting that it changed
Understand that good analytics is part numbers, part business sense and part storytelling
Get energy from working with ecommerce, marketing, CRM and UX teams
Prefer impact over perfection and want your work to change decisions.


What you bring


Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Business Analytics, Statistics, Economics, Marketing, Ecommerce or a related field;
5+ years of experience in digital analytics, business intelligence, ecommerce analytics or B2C data analysis;
Strong knowledge of ecommerce KPIs: conversion rate, traffic quality, AOV, revenue, margin, funnel drop-off retention and CLV;
Strong experience with GA4, SQL, Excel and Power BI or Tableau;
Experience with digital marketing analytics, CRM analytics, campaign performance and customer journey analysis;
Experience with A/B testing, experimentation, CRO or UX analytics is a strong plus;
Comfortable working with cross-functional teams in a fast-moving retail or ecommerce environment;
Strong communication skills: you can explain insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders;
You are eligible to work and live in the Netherlands;
Fluent English in speaking, listening and writing; Dutch is a plus.


What Just Brands is really like

We’re team first. In B2C, that means we challenge assumptions with data, keep conversations sharp and turn insight into action. No ego. Just practical collaboration, honest decisions and a team that likes improving what actually matters.

You’ll Join a Company Where


People help each other out
Ideas and people matter more than job titles
Hard work matters, and so does enjoying the ride
If something can be better, we improve it.


What you get


Salary depending on experience and background
End-of-year bonus equal to one gross monthly salary
Healthy and varied lunch every day
Exclusive access to our own Gym, including classes (boxing, yoga and padel)
Parties and drinks that will be talked about for years to come
Staff discount on our clothing.

HTML:
ECOMMERCE PERFORMANCE ANALYST

Turn B2C data into sharper journeys, smarter trading and stronger growth.

Lijnden | B2C Analytics | 40 hours | Full-time

At Just Brands, we build menswear brands with character. PME Legend, Cast Iron and Vanguard each have their own voice, their own audience and their own place in the market. In B2C, growth happens across many moving parts: ecommerce, retail, marketplaces, CRM, digital marketing, site merchandising, UX and customer service. The challenge is not getting more data. The challenge is knowing which signals matter, what they mean, and what teams should do next.

But what really sets us apart is how we work together.

We’re team first. No ego, no unnecessary layers, no endless talking, no corporate theatre. We back each other, speak up, take ownership and keep pushing for better. We work hard, stay sharp and make sure there’s room to enjoy the ride too.

That’s where you land.

Why this role matters

This is where analytics becomes commercial action. As our Ecommerce Performance Analyst, you help the B2C team understand customer behavior, channel performance and the full digital journey across our web shops, apps, retail touchpoints and marketplaces. You turn data from ecommerce, CRM, paid media, site experience and sales into insights that improve conversion, retention, customer lifetime value, campaign performance and trading decisions. You are not here to build dashboards that quietly gather dust.

You are here to turn data into decisions that teams trust, use and act on.

What you’ll do

  • Analyze ecommerce performance across funnel, conversion, traffic, revenue, average order value, product/category performance and customer journeys;
  • Build and improve dashboards and reports in tools such as GA4, SQL, Power BI, Excel or similar BI platforms;
  • Translate data into clear recommendations for Ecommerce, CRM, Digital Marketing, Site Merchandising, Retail, Marketplaces, UX and IT;
  • Monitor digital marketing performance across paid, email, CRM and campaign traffic, connecting channel results to commercial outcomes;
  • Analyze customer behavior, segmentation, retention, repeat purchase and customer lifetime value to support CRM and lifecycle decisions;
  • Support ecommerce trading rhythms with weekly/monthly insights on what is selling, what is slowing down and where opportunities sit;
  • Design, support and evaluate A/B tests and experiments for landing pages, campaigns, content, UX improvements and conversion optimization;
  • Investigate drop-offs, friction points and underperforming journeys, and turn findings into practical actions for the right teams;
  • Improve data quality, tracking consistency, definitions and governance across B2C reporting;
  • Support strategic B2C projects with analysis, business cases, forecasting and performance measurement;
  • Help teams understand what the numbers mean by presenting insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

What success looks like in your first year

  • B2C teams use your dashboards and insights in weekly and monthly decision rhythms
  • Ecommerce, CRM, marketing and site experience teams make better decisions based on trusted data
  • You identify conversion, journey or trading opportunities that lead to measurable business improvements
  • A/B tests and experiments create learnings that change how we build campaigns, pages and customer journeys
  • Reporting becomes cleaner, faster and less fragmented across channels and teams
  • Stakeholders see you as a strong sparring partner, not just a reporting resource.

What makes this role exciting

  • You work across the full B2C ecosystem: ecommerce, retail, CRM, digital marketing, marketplaces, site experience and UX
  • You sit close to revenue, conversion, customer behaviour and daily trading decisions
  • You influence both the commercial performance and the customer experience of three strong menswear brands
  • You get room to test, learn and build better ways of working with data
  • You help shape how a growing international business becomes more data-driven.

You’ll probably love this role if you…

  • Enjoy turning messy data into clear direction and practical next steps
  • Like investigating why performance changes, not just reporting that it changed
  • Understand that good analytics is part numbers, part business sense and part storytelling
  • Get energy from working with ecommerce, marketing, CRM and UX teams
  • Prefer impact over perfection and want your work to change decisions.

What you bring

  • Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Business Analytics, Statistics, Economics, Marketing, Ecommerce or a related field;
  • 5+ years of experience in digital analytics, business intelligence, ecommerce analytics or B2C data analysis;
  • Strong knowledge of ecommerce KPIs: conversion rate, traffic quality, AOV, revenue, margin, funnel drop-off retention and CLV;
  • Strong experience with GA4, SQL, Excel and Power BI or Tableau;
  • Experience with digital marketing analytics, CRM analytics, campaign performance and customer journey analysis;
  • Experience with A/B testing, experimentation, CRO or UX analytics is a strong plus;
  • Comfortable working with cross-functional teams in a fast-moving retail or ecommerce environment;
  • Strong communication skills: you can explain insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders;
  • You are eligible to work and live in the Netherlands;
  • Fluent English in speaking, listening and writing; Dutch is a plus.

What Just Brands is really like

We’re team first. In B2C, that means we challenge assumptions with data, keep conversations sharp and turn insight into action. No ego. Just practical collaboration, honest decisions and a team that likes improving what actually matters.

You’ll Join a Company Where

  • People help each other out
  • Ideas and people matter more than job titles
  • Hard work matters, and so does enjoying the ride
  • If something can be better, we improve it.

What you get

  • Salary depending on experience and background
  • End-of-year bonus equal to one gross monthly salary
  • Healthy and varied lunch every day
  • Exclusive access to our own Gym, including classes (boxing, yoga and padel)
  • Parties and drinks that will be talked about for years to come
  • Staff discount on our clothing.

Ready to turn B2C data into decisions that actually change results?

Still reading? Good sign.

If you want to work close to ecommerce, CRM, marketing, customer behaviour and real commercial decisions, this could be your next step.

Ready to apply?

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Job Description

Data Analyst Intern

Start date: August/September 2026

Duration: 6 months

Allowance: 625 euro per month (40hrs, 5 days) & full reimbursement of travel expenses  

Platform/Project: R&D Digital Transformation Team

Location: Amsterdam/Nijmegen (Primary location: Amsterdam)

Teams: R&D Digital Transformation

R&D Digital Transformation

You will be part of an international small team with the ambition to drive digital technology forward for Kraft Heinz R&D International Zone. Expect to be part of the team from day one. Strong learning experience on data visualization with many practical deliverables which will be used directly by the organization. 

Assignments And Key Activities


Support dashboard creation to support day to day work of R&D functions 
Support digital transformation across the R&D team (e.g. communication about changes- identification of digital tools required by R&D team) 
Investigate new tools and trends in Digital world 
Participate & contribute into meetings related to Digital strategy 
Collaboration with Digital Ambassadors 


Basic Requirements For All

You should have strong communication skills and fluency in English, both written and verbal (Dutch is not a requirement but other languages are a plus) 


EU - Students: Need to be enrolled at a Dutch university for at least 50% of the duration of the internship.
Non - EU Students: Need to be enrolled at a Dutch University for the whole duration of the internship.


NOTE: EU working rights are required (Either you have EU Citizenship, you are studying in the Netherlands or have relevant student work visa) 

Specific Team Requirements


Master’s or Bachelor’s student in Business Analytics, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field.
Proficiency in SQL
Data visualization skills (Power BI is a big plus)
Being comfortable with working with big data
Having a structured personality with ability to learn fast
Previous internship with data visualization or experience with data analytics is a plus


Recruitment Process


CV review – We screen applications for key eligibility criteria (e.g., EU working rights).
Online Pymetrics assessment
Screening interview – An initial interview conducted via Microsoft Teams.
Hiring Manager interview
Offer – Successful candidates will receive an offer to join the program.


Please note: The recruitment process can take 4–6 weeks in total, from application to offer.

What We Offer You

We take interns seriously. You can expect us to give you 1-on-1 mentorship from one of our experienced colleagues. We will work together on your personal learning goals and are keen to hear all the good ideas you have! We will give you the space and support to make mistakes and grow. Become a part of a close intern community as well as a young and ambitious work environment (including online).

As a Kraft Heinz intern you’ll be the owner of your own projects and especially of your own career. At Kraft Heinz, we provide the playing field. You change the game. Are you up for the challenge?   

About Us 

The Kraft Heinz Company is one of the largest food and beverage companies in the world, with eight $1 billion+ brands and global sales of approximately $25 billion. We’re a globally trusted producer of high-quality, great-tasting, and nutritious foods for over 150 years. Our brands are truly global, with products produced and marketed in over 40 countries. These beloved products include condiments and sauces, cheese and dairy, meals, meats, refreshment beverages, coffee, infant and nutrition products, and numerous other grocery products in a portfolio of more than 200 legacy and emerging brands. 

We spark joy around mealtime with our iconic brands, including Heinz, Kraft, Bull's-Eye, HP, Lea and Perrins, Quero, ABC, Master, Banquete, Orlando, Benedicta, Honig, Pudliszki, Wattie’s among others. 

No matter the brand, we’re united under one vision: To sustainably grow by delighting more consumers globally. Bringing this vision to life is our team of 39,000+ food lovers, creative thinkers, and high performers worldwide. Together, we help provide meals to those in need through our global partnership with Rise Against Hunger. We also stand committed to responsible, sustainable practices that extend to every facet of our business, our consumers, and our communities. Every day, we’re transforming the food industry with bold thinking and unprecedented results. If you share our passion – and are ready to create the future, build a legacy, and lead as a global citizen – there’s only one thing to do: join our table and let’s make life delicious! 

Our Culture of Ownership, Meritocracy & Collaboration 

We're not afraid to think differently. Embrace new ideas. Dream big. We empower our people at every level – from entry-level intern to senior leader – to own their work. We share a responsibility to think like Owners – to be mindful of the collective and sustained success of Kraft Heinz – which we apply to every situation, every day. As part of Kraft Heinz, you're supported to grow and achieve. You’re expected to bring your authentic self to work every day, to lead with humility, and drive outstanding performance at every level – and you’ll be rewarded. You’re given opportunities to leave a mark and build a legacy. But you won’t do it alone.  You’re supported by passionate teammates along the way, and our collective, collaborative spirit fuels our incredible progress. 

Location(s)

Amsterdam

Kraft Heinz is an Equal Opportunity Employer – Underrepresented Ethnic Minority Groups/Women/Veterans/Individuals with Disabilities/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity and other protected classes.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Nikon is a world-leading provider of imaging products and services. Its innovative optics technology—from consumer to professional cameras, lenses to system accessories, healthcare optics, and sports optics —is powered by over 100 years of experience. Globally recognised for setting new standards in design and performance, Nikon’s revolutionary series of mirrorless cameras and NIKKOR lenses represent a new dimension of optical performance.

Nikon is committed to leading imaging culture and increasingly embracing the potential of digital solutions and data, which help our customers to master their craft, find the best equipment and inspire and support. Its award-winning equipment empowers photographers and videographers to realise their creative vision at the highest level.

About The Role

We are looking for a talented, insight-driven  Data Analyst  to join our European Digital Marketing team at Nikon Europe B.V.

You will be the go-to expert for delivering high-quality marketing performance insights that drive smarter decisions across channels including email, social media, web, and paid advertising. Your analysis will help us improve customer understanding, optimize campaigns, improve user journeys, and strengthen customer engagement.

This role focuses on customer data,  media and channel performance analysis, clear reporting, and dashboard creation (primarily in Power BI), with practical tagging and measurement governance. You will also play a key role in automation, measurement governance and privacy-compliant data practices.

Main Responsibilities

Insights & Performance Optimization


Deliver and interpret analytical insights that inform and influence key decisionmakers
Translate complex data into clear, compelling stories and actionable recommendations
Analyze campaign and channel performance (email, social, search, paid media, website) to identify trends, opportunities, and risks
Conduct analyses on customer behavior, segmentation opportunities and funnel bottlenecks
Contribute to advanced measurement approaches like attribution and marketing mix modelling


Reporting & Analytics Maturity


Build, maintain, and continuously improve Power BI dashboards to track digital marketing performance and customer insights
Automate routine reporting to increase efficiency and consistency across markets
Drive analytics maturity uplift through improved methodologies and best practices
Produce regular and ad-hoc reports for marketing stakeholders and senior leadership


Measurement & Data Governance


Define measurement plans and reporting frameworks in collaboration with stakeholders
Manage tagging governance and create clear tagging specifications
Ensure privacy- and consent-compliant measurement in line with regulations and internal policies
Support implementation and validation of tracking via tools such as GTM and GA4


Stakeholder & Partner Collaboration


Work closely with marketing teams, Senior Management and the D2C team to translate business needs into analytical solutions
Coordinate with external partners and agencies on reporting and dashboarding initiatives and optimizing Data Studio dashboards
Act as a trusted advisor on data-driven decision-making across the organization


Job Requirements

Required Profile


Bachelor or Master's degree in the field of Business, Marketing, Statistics, Economics, or related field
3-5 years experience as a data analyst or in a related field
Experience finding, cleaning, and transforming data to build reports, dashboards, and/or data assets
Strong experience building and maintaining dashboards in Power BI
Experience defining metrics, measurement plans and reporting frameworks for marketing campaigns
Comfortable working with marketing teams and external agencies to translate business questions into analytic solutions
Experience with Data Studio and managing external dashboarding partners
Expert with digital analytics platforms (e.g., Google Analytics / GA4) and tag management basics (Google Tag Manager)
Experience communicating and advocating with analytical insights to high-level audiences to inform decision-making
Excellent verbal, written and presentation communication skills (fluency in English required, other languages desirable) with people at all levels


Nice to have


SQL skills sufficient to extract and aggregate data for reporting and analysis
Experience with large-scale data environments and advanced analytics methodologies
Familiarity with attribution modelling or marketing mix modelling
Experience in automation of reporting and scalable data solutions
Understanding of privacy, consent management and data governance principles


What We Offer You


A challenging and rewarding role in a globally recognized organization.
A collaborative and inclusive work environment.
The chance to work at Nikon's European headquarters in Amstelveen, a vibrant and inspiring location.
Good secondary employment conditions, including a fully paid pension by the employer .


How To Apply

If you’re ready to take your career to the next level, we would love to hear from you! You can send your CV and short motivation letter to Alex Damen, Recruitment Business Partner. If we think you are a good fit, we will contact you for an introductory interview with our recruitment team.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




We bouwen met Just Good People een nextgen data team met leuke mensen met sterke data, AI, human & biz skills. Geen PPT-consultancy, maar datakansen kraken en echt waarde realiseren bij toffe klanten. En veel opleiding.




Zie jij in bergen info – naast getallen – vooral kansen? Wil je die liever omzetten in actie dan in rapporten die in een la verdwijnen?




Wil je maximaal leren door in korte tijd bij meerdere bedrijven in de keuken te kijken? Terwijl wij je helpen ontwikkelen op het snijvlak van data, business en people?




Dan hebben we de uitdaging voor jou.




🟡 Just Good People

Bij JGP hebben we twee simpele regels: We nemen goede mensen aan. En we zorgen goed voor deze mensen. Dat betekent dat we samen optrekken om jou maximaal in je kracht te krijgen op een manier die bij jou past.




🌱 Unieke data filosofie

“As machines get better at being machines, humans have to get better at being more human.”

Deze nieuwe tijd vraagt om een nieuw soort data professional. Bij Just Good People Data helpen we bedrijven versnellen met top notch data professionals met goed ontwikkelde data, business én human skills. En trainen je naar nog hoger niveau. Met aandacht voor:




Volgende levels in Data | AI
“Waarde denken” voor klanten/ gebruikers
Stakeholder management
Communiceren & inspireren




⚡ Wat je gaat doen

Je werkt aan projecten bij mooie bedrijven zoals Bol.com, Defensie, Action, Rabo, NS, AMC, ASML, Essent.




Denk aan:




Het maken van veel impact met data
Stakeholders meenemen in de kracht van data – op een begrijpelijke manier
Verbinden van mensen, processen en tech
Vertalen van data naar heldere inzichten, dashboards en rapportages die helpen bij besluitvorming
Signaleren van trends, kansen en verbeterpunten en stakeholders adviseren op basis van data
Meedenken over slimme oplossingen voor complexe vraagstukken




👩 Wie je bent




Je bent technisch sterk maar ook nieuwsgierig naar de mens achter de vraag
Je vindt het leuk om complexe vraagstukken simpel te maken
Je krijgt energie van verandering en nieuwe omgevingen
Je wilt blijven groeien in data, business én human skills
Je hebt 4+ jaar ervaring als Data Analist
Je hebt een WO-opleiding, bijvoorbeeld in Data Science of Econometrie
Je bent ondernemend en denkt in zowel risico’s als kansen
Je schakelt gemakkelijk tussen strategie, business en data
Je durft vragen te stellen en bestaande werkwijzen uit te dagen
Je bent een echte teamspeler en communicatief sterk
Je spreekt en schrijft vloeiend Nederlands en Engels




🚀 Dit ga je krijgen

Vast dienstverband met goed salaris, bonusregeling en aantrekkelijke voorwaarden
Veel persoonlijke aandacht voor waardegroei, inzetbaarheid en “in je kracht komen“ als data professional. Maar ook data inhoudelijke mentoring.
Opleiding en kruisbestuiving met andere professionals in state of the art AI skills
De kans om in diverse keukens te kijken en veel te leren
Leaseauto of NS-Businesscard (ook privégebruik)
Gezellige borrels, kennissessies en team-events
Collega’s die naast slimme analyses ook goed zijn in slechte grappen




Wij zoeken goede data-mensen, die goede dingen doen. Van die types die je er gewoon heel graag bij wil hebben – net zo goed met mensen als met code!

  • Meedenken over slimme oplossingen voor complexe vraagstukken


👩 Wie je bent


  • Je bent technisch sterk maar ook nieuwsgierig naar de mens achter de vraag
  • Je vindt het leuk om complexe vraagstukken simpel te maken
  • Je krijgt energie van verandering en nieuwe omgevingen
  • Je wilt blijven groeien in data, business én human skills
  • Je hebt 4+ jaar ervaring als Data Analist
  • Je hebt een WO-opleiding, bijvoorbeeld in Data Science of Econometrie
  • Je bent ondernemend en denkt in zowel risico’s als kansen
  • Je schakelt gemakkelijk tussen strategie, business en data
  • Je durft vragen te stellen en bestaande werkwijzen uit te dagen
  • Je bent een echte teamspeler en communicatief sterk
  • Je spreekt en schrijft vloeiend Nederlands en Engels


🚀 Dit ga je krijgen

  • Vast dienstverband met goed salaris, bonusregeling en aantrekkelijke voorwaarden
  • Veel persoonlijke aandacht voor waardegroei, inzetbaarheid en “in je kracht komen“ als data professional. Maar ook data inhoudelijke mentoring.
  • Opleiding en kruisbestuiving met andere professionals in state of the art AI skills
  • De kans om in diverse keukens te kijken en veel te leren
  • Leaseauto of NS-Businesscard (ook privégebruik)
  • Gezellige borrels, kennissessies en team-events
  • Collega’s die naast slimme analyses ook goed zijn in slechte grappen


Wij zoeken goede data-mensen, die goede dingen doen. Van die types die je er gewoon heel graag bij wil hebben – net zo goed met mensen als met code!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
For ROSEN Europe BV in Oldenzaal, we are looking for two analytical and ambitious Data Analysts with 0–3 years of experience.




Due to the continued growth of our organization and increasing demand for data-driven insights, we currently have two vacancies available within our Data Analytics team.

This is a broad data-driven role within an organization that is rapidly evolving in the areas of data and digitization. You will have the opportunity to help build this further and make a direct impact on how data is utilized within the organization.




Description




In this role, the focus is on analyzing, structuring, and visualizing data, as well as developing dashboards and reports. You will work with data from various systems and translate it into insights used for better decision-making and tangible improvements within the organization. At the same time, you will be involved in improving processes, systems, and ways of working, ensuring that data is effectively applied in practice.

Depending on your experience and profile, the exact role will be tailored within the organization. This could be more corporate-focused (central data, reporting, and strategic analyses) or positioned in one of our Regions responsible for Europe, Central Asia, and Africa.




ROSEN is an international and innovative organization focused on high-end technology and data-driven solutions for industrial applications. Within ROSEN Europe, a culture of quality, innovation, and continuous development is key. The organization is experiencing strong growth and offers ample opportunities for initiative, development, and long-term career progression.




Your tasks




• Conduct data analyses and develop dashboards and reports (Power BI and similar tools)

• Combine, structure, and analyze data from different systems

• Identify trends, patterns, and deviations and translate these into actionable insights and improvements for the organization

• Actively contribute to improving data quality, reporting, and data-driven decision-making

• Support teams and management with clear analyses and practical insights

• Identify and implement improvements in processes and ways of working, including through automation

• Write and optimize policies and documentation, supported by AI tools such as Copilot

• Contribute to various data and business intelligence projects within the organization




What you bring




• Completed Master’s degree in, for example, Mathematics, Business IT, Data Science, or a comparable field

• Strong analytical skills and a high level of abstract thinking

• Experience or strong interest in data analytics and AI tools

• Good knowledge of Excel and Power BI (SQL or similar tools is a plus)

• Affinity with AI tools such as Copilot and modern data practices

• Excellent communication skills and a team-oriented mindset

• Eager to learn, independent, and proactive

• Able to easily switch between analysis, investigation, and practical process improvements

• Living in the Twente region (or willing to commute within 45 minutes) and fluently speaking Dutch




Please note that, due to the composition and balance of the current team, we are specifically looking for candidates who are fluent in Dutch and already have permanent eligibility to work within the European Union. Please note that an orientation year (zoekjaar) permit does not meet this requirement.




If you do not meet these requirements, we kindly ask you not to apply, as your application cannot be considered for this position.




Offer




• A challenging entry-level position with significant responsibility and impact

• Working within an international, innovative, and fast-growing organization

• Plenty of opportunities for personal development and career growth

• Collaboration with experienced professionals in data, engineering, and business

• A competitive salary and attractive secondary benefits

• A professional yet informal working environment with short communication lines

• A role with long-term prospects and international career opportunities




Company profile




ROSEN is an international and innovative organization focused on high-end technology and data-driven solutions for industrial applications. Within ROSEN Europe, a culture of quality, innovation, and continuous development is key. The organization is experiencing strong growth and offers ample opportunities for initiative, development, and long-term career progression.

Title: Data Analist
Description:
Als Data Analist bij SynTouch ben jij degene die overzicht brengt in datastromen. Je spreekt de taal van de business én van data, en zorgt ervoor dat data slim, duidelijk en bruikbaar wordt ingezet.

Je helpt klanten hun data effectiever te gebruiken – door de juiste vragen te stellen, businessconcepten helder te maken en data op een begrijpelijke manier beschikbaar te stellen. Daarbij kijk je niet alleen naar wat er is, maar vooral naar wat er nodig is. Zo maak je echt het verschil.

Wat breng jij mee?

Je Bent Analytisch, Communicatief En Denkt In Oplossingen. Jij


Hebt ervaring als Data Analist
Kent datamodellering (UML), data profiling en BI-concepten
Hebt interesse in AI, Machine Learning en NLP (pré)
Begrijpt hoe belangrijk datakwaliteit is
Werkt graag samen met verschillende stakeholders – van IT tot business
Are fluent in Dutch


Klinkt dit als jouw rol? Dan horen we graag van je.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Rotterdam Hybrid minimum three days in the office

Salary €65,000 to €80,000




This is an opportunity to step into a highly visible Data Analyst role where your work directly shapes business priorities and decision making. Joining during a period of change, you will have real ownership of projects, strong exposure to stakeholders, and the chance to influence how data is used across the organisation.




The Company

They are a growing organisation operating in a dynamic market, with data playing an increasingly central role in strategic decisions. The business values clear communication, ownership, and practical insights over theoretical analysis. With a lean data setup, they offer a collaborative environment where your impact will be felt quickly.




The Role

Deliver actionable insights through business intelligence and data analysis
Own and manage analytical projects from requirement gathering through to delivery
Work closely with stakeholders to understand needs, set priorities, and challenge assumptions
Build and maintain SQL based data models feeding Power BI dashboards
Balance larger strategic initiatives with shorter, ad hoc analytical requests




Your Skills and Experience

Strong commercial experience using SQL for analysis and data modelling
Proven capability building insights and dashboards in Power BI
Confident stakeholder management and the ability to translate data into clear recommendations
Comfortable working independently and taking ownership of priorities
Experience operating in a lean or fast moving environment
Exposure to Python or advanced analytics is beneficial but not essential




What They Offer

Salary between €65,000 and €80,000 depending on experience
Hybrid working with flexibility and a strong on site culture in Rotterdam
A role with genuine ownership and visibility across the business
Career development as the data function continues to evolve




How to Apply

Apply now to explore this Data Analyst opportunity and discuss how your skills could make an impact.

Title: Strategy & Analytics Associate
Description:
About The Role

The Global Strategy & Planning team at Uber Eats shapes the long-term direction of our marketplace and helps teams around the world execute against a unified strategy. We identify growth opportunities, define where Uber Eats should play, and build the insights that guide decisions across product, commercial, and market teams.

Within this team, the Selection Strategy & Intelligence pod helps Uber Eats understand its restaurant and merchant selection opportunity by benchmarking our marketplace, assessing competitive positioning, and quantifying where we can expand, densify, or strengthen our offering.

As a Strategy & Analytics Associate, you will turn complex data, market signals, and ambiguous business questions into clear strategic recommendations. You will lead analyses, build decision-making frameworks, develop competitive intelligence metrics, and craft executive-ready narratives that help teams prioritize opportunities and take action. This role requires strong analytical judgment, SQL fluency, clear communication, and the ability to partner effectively with cross-functional stakeholders.

What You'll Do


Lead Strategic Analysis: Quantify market potential, merchant value, and competitive positioning to guide business decisions.
Own Competitive Intelligence: Develop metrics to benchmark merchant selection, identifying gaps and expansion opportunities.
Solve Ambiguous Problems: Use exploratory analysis, market sizing, and forecasting to turn open-ended questions into structured insights.
Craft Executive Narratives: Synthesize findings into high-impact dashboards, visualizations, and presentations for leadership.
Drive Cross-Functional Impact: Partner with Product, Engineering, and Operations teams to shape roadmaps and translate data into action.
Innovate & Scale: Leverage new data sources, advanced analytical methods, and AI-enabled tools to accelerate intelligence work.


Your Profile


Experience: 2+ years in a quantitative, insights-driven role (e.g., strategy, analytics, consulting, or marketplace operations).
Technical Skills: Strong SQL proficiency is required; familiarity with Python or working with unstructured/web-scraped data is a strong plus.
Analytical Power: Proven ability to build data models, size markets, forecast opportunities, and work with large, complex datasets.
Communication: Exceptional data storytelling skills with the ability to influence cross-functional partners and present clearly to non-technical audiences.
Execution: Comfort with ambiguity, sharp problem-solving skills, and a rigorous approach to ensuring data accuracy.

Title: Market Data Analyst
Description:
This Is What We Offer You


Salary: Gross monthly salary between EUR 3,516 and EUR 5,021 (scale 07) for a 36-hour work week.
Extras: a thirteenth month, 8% holiday allowance, and a 10% Employee Benefit Budget.
Development budget: EUR 1,400 development budget per year for your growth and development.
Hybrid working: a balance between home and office work (possible for most roles).
Pension: decide for yourself the amount of your personal contribution.


Or view all our benefits.

Market Data Analyst

Imagine yourself spending your time working with people and markets around the world. As a Market Data Analyst you can make a difference by a continuous delivery of high quality market data to our internal customers. You will be involved in the development and implementation of complex processes to ensure a timely delivery of accurate data required to value our financial instruments on a daily basis.

You and your job

You will be working with other departments in the bank such as Front Office and Risk to discuss their need for changes in your data to react quickly to the ever-changing financial markets. You drive change in the team by focusing on implementing new technology that can help make our processes more efficient. You like to work together and get the best out of yourself and the team.

Top Responsibilities


Collecting, validating and delivering accurate and timely market data throughout the bank to value trading positions such as Interest Rate or FX derivatives, commodity and credit products;
Discussing with traders in hubs such as Hong Kong, London, New York or Sao Paulo what the best setup for their valuation instrument is;
Looking for continuous improvements of processes by automation or simplification.


Together we achieve more than alone

Collaboration is at the heart of everything we do. Our squad Market Data EOD Valuation brings talented people together to ensure delivery of high quality market data and create a safe environment where you can thrive.

‘[You will be part of the squad Market Data EOD Valuation within the Area Valuation & Market Data. This is a young and diverse team with a range of different nationalities, eager to learn and having fun while doing so. ].’

Chris America, Area Lead – Valuation & Market Data.

Our department

The Area Valuation & Market Data falls within the Tribe Finance & Product Control, which is part of the Risk and Finance domain and responsible for providing reliable financial and management information for global supported businesses to the Wholesale and Rural, Treasury and Local Bank businesses in a timely, efficient and professional manner. Reliable financial information is supported by an independent daily valuation of financial instruments in the bank’s trading and banking portfolios and analysis of evidence of performed controls.

Talking of Rabobank

We are a Dutch bank that operates in 38 countries for over 9,500,000 customers. Together with these customers, our members and partners we stand side by side to create a world in which everyone has access to enough healthy food. In the Netherlands we work to create a country in which people are happy with how they live, work and do business.

You and your talent

Customer focus, problem solving and analytical skills as well as a result driven and a pro-active attitude are essential for the role of Market Data Analyst.


You hold a master’s degree with a focus on Finance, Economics or Econometrics or a degree in another discipline but with a keen interest in the financial sector;
You can demonstrate your knowledge on financial products that are valued at fair value;
You have a tangible interest in IT related systems and processes;
You have a tangible interest in the management of data
You are able to clearly express your ideas in English both orally and in written form;
CFA, FRM, PRM or equivalent is considered an asset or willingness to work towards one;


Interested?

Do you want to become the ideal version of yourself? We would love to help you achieve this by focusing firmly on your growth, development, and investing in an environment where you keep learning every day. We give you the space to innovate and initiate. In this way, we offer you numerous opportunities to grow and help you exceed your expectations, to do the right thing exceptionally well, and to therefore grow as a professional. In addition, with us:


A gross monthly salary in function group 8 or 9 depending on your experience;
a thirteenth month and holiday pay;
an Employee Benefit Budget. You decide how to spend this budget. This may include purchasing extra leave days, making extra pension contributions or even receiving a monthly cash pay-out;
a personal budget that you can spend on activities related to your personal development and career;
flexible working times and location-independent working;
100% reimbursement of commuting costs if you travel by public transport! Do you still prefer to travel by car or motorbike? Then choose a commuting allowance;


Let's meet

Are you the person we're looking for? Are you ready to join Rabobank as a Market Data Analyst and to make a difference to yourself, our customers and to society? We look forward to receiving your application for this vacancy in Utrecht.

You and the job application process


Any questions about the job content? Contact: Lu Fan (Product Owner Market Data) at lu.fan@rabobank.com
Any questions about working at Rabobank and the process? Contact Jelle Nelemans (Corporate Recruiter) at jelle.nelemans@rabobank.nl
You can find answers to the most frequently asked questions on Frequently asked questions - Careers at Rabobank
A security check is part of the process.
We respect your privacy.

Title: Junior Data Analyst Retail
Description:
Note: This job posting is only open to native level or near-native level Dutch speakers. If you are not proficient in Dutch, please have a look at our other job postings instead!)

Your role in our team

As a Junior Data Analyst Retail, you support client teams with analysis, dashboards, KPI insights and data quality checks. You work with data from retail processes such as sales, customer behavior, inventory, promotions, e-commerce or supply chain and help make insights understandable for business stakeholders. At CGI, you grow by working in projects with experienced data consultants, project managers and technical specialists.

How You Strengthen The Team


Analyze datasets and translate findings into clear insights and visualizations.
Build and maintain dashboards and reports in Power BI, Tableau or comparable tooling.
Perform basic data quality checks and document definitions, assumptions and issues.
Support requirements, user stories, KPI definitions and stakeholder questions.
Work with senior consultants, data engineers, business analysts and client teams.
Contribute to presentations, workshops and analyses for retail and consumer-services clients.


What You Bring


1-2 years of experience, internship or traineeship in data analysis, BI, business analysis or analytics.
Solid foundation in SQL, Excel and at least one BI tool such as Power BI, Tableau or Looker.
Affinity with retail, e-commerce, supply chain, customer analytics or commercial analysis.
Able to explain data outcomes clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Accurate, eager to learn, communicative and comfortable with client contact.
Professional Dutch and English, or demonstrably quick to develop to that level.


We would also value


Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300) or demonstrable Power BI experience.
Microsoft Azure Data Fundamentals (DP-900), Azure AI Fundamentals (AI-900) or comparable fundamentals.
Basic Python or R for data cleaning, analysis or automation.
Experience with KPIs such as revenue, margin, conversion, inventory, customer segments or promotion effectiveness.
Experience with Jira, Azure DevOps, Confluence or working in an agile team.


Where will you work?

You will join CGI’s retail team and work on assignments where data, business questions and technology come together. You will have room to learn, while contributing from the start to analyses, dashboards and insights that help clients make better decisions.

What We Offer


A learning environment where you can grow quickly towards data consulting in retail.
Guidance from experienced consultants and access to CGI’s broader knowledge network.
Varied assignments where data directly influences commercial and operational decisions.
Room to obtain certifications and further develop your technical and consulting skills.
A team culture centered on collaboration, knowledge sharing and ownership.


Why CGI?

CGI is one of the world's largest IT consultancy firms. Our people keep the Netherlands running. Government agencies, banks, (aero-) space, infrastructure organizations and energy and manufacturing companies are clients of CGI. We work on interesting projects that touch the daily lives of millions of people. We are present regionally, augmented with global domain knowledge, our ecosystem, and colleagues spread all over the world. We are proud of the impactful projects that we, together with our colleagues, make a success.

At CGI, we place great value on diversity and inclusion, as this not only promotes collaboration but often leads to better results. We look forward to your contribution to our team!


Work in small self-managing teams consisting of experts in your field
Take control of your own career with the space to continue developing yourself
You work with the latest technologies
An excellent salary and attractive secondary employment conditions
Immediate permanent contract
Part-time possibilities
And the opportunity to become a CGI shareholder


Join our team of experts and apply now!

We will contact you as soon as possible. Questions about the position? Ask them to Floris Diemel, Director Consulting Services via floris.diemel@cgi.com

For more information about the application procedure, please contact recruiter Jana Kreckova via jana.kreckova@cgi.com

We werken niet samen met externe recruitment bureau’s. Acquisitie wordt daarom niet op prijs gesteld.

Your future duties and responsibilities

Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.

Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.

Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

Title: Functional / Data Analyst
Description:
Functional / Data Analyst




As a preferred supplier to one of our biggest Financial Clients, I am seeking for a Functional / Data Analyst for a position in Amsterdam, Netherlands to support a redesign project within the data landscape. The assignment focuses on delivering clearly defined outcomes related to the redesign, simplification, and future-proofing of the current data model and reporting landscape. You will work independently on agreed deliverables and bring expertise in functional analysis, data modelling, and SQL/query performance optimization.




Project scope:

This project focuses on redesigning and standardising the DM-CAN data landscape and the Banking data model, with the goal of making it scalable, easier to use, and ready for onboarding additional countries and use cases.
The professional will play an active role in translating functional requirements into concrete data solutions, and will contribute to both the design and implementation of the future-state landscape.




Scope of the assignment:

Design and implementation of new data tables and views for business usage
Translation of business requirements into scalable data models and structures
Build and optimisation of SQL queries and data objects
Analysis and improvement of performance of tables, views, and queries
Definition of data mappings and support in data model implementation
Supporting adoption of the redesigned data landscape
Contributing to onboarding and knowledge materials for users and analysts
Supporting the redesign of One Business Banking-related tables




Expected deliverables:

The assignment is output-focused and includes:

Functional design and specifications for new or redesigned tables/views
Concrete proposals to simplify and improve the DM-CAN landscape
Implemented data structures (tables/views) aligned with the target data model
Recommendations on end-state solution design and implementation approach
Performance improvement recommendations and optimisations
Knowledge transfer and onboarding/support materials
Contribution to the design of One Business Banking tables




Required knowledge and experience

Strong experience in functional analysis within data or reporting environments, translating business requirements into clear data logic and practical solutions
Strong SQL skills, with experience building and optimising queries, tables, and views in governed data environments such as Oracle, BigQuery, or SAS
Solid knowledge of data modelling, data mappings, and analytical datasets for reporting, analytics, or modelling use cases
Experience improving the performance of tables, views, and queries, and translating this into robust, future-proof data solutions
Experience contributing to end-state data design and supporting implementation, adoption, or knowledge transfer for new data structures and ways of working




Nice to have

Experience in data roles within banking
Knowledge of Business Banking products or propositions




Contract: 6 months+

Rates: Excellent

Location: Amsterdam

Interview: 2 stages, 1 technical + 1 assessment




Nick Chinweerapunt

Account Manager

Gazelle Global Consulting

Title: Data Analist
Description:
Als Data Analist bij SynTouch ben jij degene die overzicht brengt in datastromen. Je spreekt de taal van de business én van data, en zorgt ervoor dat data slim, duidelijk en bruikbaar wordt ingezet.

Je helpt klanten hun data effectiever te gebruiken – door de juiste vragen te stellen, businessconcepten helder te maken en data op een begrijpelijke manier beschikbaar te stellen. Daarbij kijk je niet alleen naar wat er is, maar vooral naar wat er nodig is. Zo maak je echt het verschil.

Wat breng jij mee?

Je Bent Analytisch, Communicatief En Denkt In Oplossingen. Jij


Hebt ervaring als Data Analist
Kent datamodellering (UML), data profiling en BI-concepten
Hebt interesse in AI, Machine Learning en NLP (pré)
Begrijpt hoe belangrijk datakwaliteit is
Werkt graag samen met verschillende stakeholders – van IT tot business
Are fluent in Dutch


Klinkt dit als jouw rol? Dan horen we graag van je.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
HTML:
Ben jij analytisch en heb je affiniteit met vastgoed? Ben jij communicatief sterk en vind je het leuk om de schakel te zijn tussen de business en ICT? Wil je met verschillende stakeholders en teams tot een mooi eindresultaat komen? Dan is de functie van Junior Data Analyst iets voor jou!


Wat ga je doen?

Je gaat werken in het Digital and Tech Application Enablement team en ondersteunt de introductie en beheer van applicaties die de CBRE-collega’s in staat stellen uitmuntende diensten aan klanten te leveren. Je vertaalt ruwe data naar bruikbare informatie en presenteert deze in heldere rapportages voor collega’s en andere belanghebbenden. Op basis van betrouwbare data en jouw analyses kunnen vervolgens strategische beslissingen worden genomen. Bovendien word je verantwoordelijk voor de verbetering van de data zodat deze steeds beter aansluit op werkprocessen en de implementatie van AI. Dit doe je samen met de data governance specialist, de data-analisten en de businessanalisten.


De belangrijkste taken van Junior Data Analyst binnen CBRE zijn:

  • Het ontwikkelen van data pipelines;
  • Vertalen van de informatiebehoeften naar gerichte analyses om daarmee extra waarde toe te voegen aan de diensten van CBRE;
  • Creëren van heldere rapportages;
  • Controleren en verbeteren van de datakwaliteit door onder andere de implementatie van het datamodel te ondersteunen.


Wat heb je ons te bieden?

  • Je bent in het bezit van een afgeronde opleiding op HBO- of WO-niveau bij voorkeur in business analytics, bedrijfskunde, data science of computer science;
  • Je hebt affiniteit met het structureren en analyseren van grote databestanden;
  • Je gaat gestructureerd en accuraat te werk;
  • Je bent enthousiast, en gaat met gedrevenheid en klantgericht te werk;
  • Je kunt je flexibel opstellen;
  • Je beschikt over uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden (woord en schrift) in zowel Nederlands als Engels;
  • Je hebt ervaring met coderen in SQL, en andere codeertalen;
  • Je hebt ervaring met of interesse in geavanceerde analysetools zoals Tableau, Power BI of Alteryx (pré)


Wat hebben we jou te bieden?

Bij CBRE Advisory werk je samen met allerlei verschillende specialisten aan een gedegen totaaladvies. Professionals met een commerciële, financiële, bouwkundige of zelfs creatieve achtergrond, en allemaal met dezelfde drive voor vastgoed.


Onze organisatie is de perfecte plek om je professioneel en persoonlijk te ontwikkelen. De CBRE Academy geeft je de kans om je door middel van opleidingen, trainingen en workshops verder te ontwikkelen. Als Young Professionals maak je onderdeel uit van het Young Professionals programma van CBRE, waar business en pleasure worden gecombineerd. Binnen CBRE Advisory wordt hard gewerkt maar tijd voor ontspanning vinden wij net zo belangrijk. Er worden geregeld activiteiten georganiseerd zoals borrels, bedrijfs-en afdelingsuitjes. Wij bieden je een functie in een enthousiast, jong en gedreven team en een fijne werkomgeving met veel uitdagingen en mogelijkheden om jezelf te ontplooien.


Wat hebben wij jou nog meer te bieden?

  • Een marktconform salaris;
  • Een aantrekkelijke discretionaire bonusregeling;
  • Een bijna premievrij pensioen;
  • Je ontvangt reiskostenvergoeding als je reist met het OV, de auto én de fiets;
  • Een laptop en Iphone;
  • Je krijgt 25 vakantiedagen per jaar o.b.v. 40 uur + 3 extra vakantiedagen aangewezen door ons bestuur en de mogelijkheid om 5 vakantiedagen per jaar bij te kopen;
  • De opbouw van vakantiegeld van 8% van je bruto jaarsalaris;
  • De optie tot 2 weken remote werken;
  • De optie tot omruilen van religieuze feestdagen (je mag een feestdag ruilen voor een andere religieuze feestdag die belangrijker is voor jou);
  • Een sportregeling, waarbij je je kunt aansluiten bij meerdere sportscholen/activiteiten door het hele land;
  • En een voordelige fietsenregeling, koop een nieuwe fiets en trek dit af van je brutosalaris.


Wat wordt je team?

Het Tech & Data team bestaat uit 3 teams: het Analytics team, het Engineering team en het Product Enablement team. De vacature voor Junior Data Analyst is in het Product Enablement team, wat momenteel bestaat uit 5 personen. Het team is verantwoordelijk voor de applicaties die binnen CBRE Nederland worden gebruikt. Er wordt veel geïnvesteerd in het verbeteren van deze applicaties door onder andere de implementatie van AI. Hierdoor wordt er heel nauw samengewerkt met alle afdelingen binnen CBRE Nederland en Europese teams. Binnen het team wordt er hard en enthousiast gewerkt, met daarnaast veel aandacht en ruimte voor plezier, flexibiliteit en persoonlijke ontwikkeling.


Wie is jouw nieuwe werkgever?

CBRE is een organisatie waarbij het niet uitmaakt of je een ervaren professional of een jong talent bent, en op welke positie je speelt. We bieden iedereen de vrijheid om kansen te zien en te pakken. We werken hard voor onze successen en vieren die met elkaar, bijvoorbeeld na het sluiten van een deal of tijdens onze wekelijkse borrel.


Met ruim 115.000 medewerkers verspreid over 500 kantoren in 100+ landen is CBRE wereldwijd marktleider in real estate consultancy. In Nederland werken onze 750 professionals vanuit Amsterdam, Den Haag, Eindhoven, Rotterdam, Utrecht en Zwolle elke dag aan de mooiste vastgoedprojecten.


Sinds begin 2019 heeft CBRE een hoofdkantoor in de Schinkelbuurt in Amsterdam, genaamd The Core. Een nieuw, innovatief kantoor onder het vijftig jaar oude dak van een voormalige autogarage. Met dit kantoor slaan wij een nieuwe weg in, geven we ondernemerschap de ruimte en werken we intensiever met opdrachtgevers en elkaar samen.


Solliciteren?

Wij kijken uit naar jouw sollicitatie! Zorg dat je bij je sollicitatie naast een CV ook een motivatiebrief toevoegt waarin je aangeeft waarom je voor de functie van Junior Data Analyst kiest.

Ben jij een analytische Data Analyst/Researcher die met data en technologie impact wil maken? Bij Robidus werk je aan vraagstukken rondom gezondheid, preventie en duurzame inzetbaarheid.

Title: Intern - Data Analyst
Description:
We are partnering with a Dutch organization to evaluate governance structures and dissect historical data. To support this mandate, JHC Consulting is hiring a full-time Intern - Data Analyst. In this role, you will help our principals retrieve complex organizational figures, verify historical performance metrics, and isolate the core assumptions driving our partner's internal cases. This is a paid, full-time hybrid position that offers competitive pay for an entry-level professional eager to learn a rigorous approach to strategic counsel.

The role
At JHC Consulting, we do not produce visual presentations or coordinate typical corporate ceremonies. Our final output is strictly a written memorandum. As an intern, your primary responsibility is to find the truth within the numbers. You will spend your days cataloging financial disclosures, identifying contradictions in existing reports, and representing raw data in clean, logical tables.
Specifically, you will reconstruct historical data paths, verify math in corporate filings, and draft preliminary sections of our advisory papers. Every piece of work we produce is subject to successive revision and intense reading. You will work closely with a principal to refine your drafts, ensuring every statement is backed by verifiable analysis.

What you bring
We are looking for an individual with an analytical mind and a strong affinity for the discipline of writing. This role is designed as an entry-level internship for a current student or recent graduate who wants to develop foundational skills in strategic analysis.
You should have a basic understanding of corporate finance and quantitative research methods. Comfort with spreadsheets is essential, as you will regularly audit financial statements and model governance structures. More importantly, you must be a precise writer. We look for candidates who can explain a statistical variance in a single, clear sentence without relying on professional jargon. An academic background in economics, finance, mathematics, or policy is helpful, but intellectual curiosity and attention to detail are what matter most.

How we work
Our practice operates under a single-author model. We believe that true clarity is achieved when an individual takes full ownership of an argument. While you will receive guidance from our principals, you will be expected to stand behind your research and defend your findings under internal scrutiny.
We do not rely on consensus to find answers. We believe in the analytical rigor of a dissenting opinion. When we analyze a client's strategy, we look for what they have omitted. Your contributions will directly inform the signed memoranda we deliver to corporate leaders. Because an author's name sits at the foot of every page, the work is conducted with a high degree of individual accountability and discretion.

Working at JHC Consulting
We offer a hybrid working structure that balances hours of quiet concentration with scheduled days of in-person debate. You will work from your own space for the majority of the week, allowing for the uninterrupted reading and analysis crucial to our work. On designated hybrid days, you will join our principals in a shared professional space to review drafts and polish our final written pieces. This structure supports both the independent discipline needed for rigorous analysis and the direct mentorship that helps entry-level analysts progress.
Every mandate we accept represents a serious commitment to intellectual honesty. By joining us for this internship, you will learn to build written arguments that survive deep scrutiny and help leaders address their most pressing governance questions. If you appreciate the quiet discipline of written analysis and are prepared to take responsibility for the integrity of your numbers, this role offers an exceptional entry into the advisory profession.
