Specifically, you will reconstruct historical data paths, verify math in corporate filings, and draft preliminary sections of our advisory papers. Every piece of work we produce is subject to successive revision and intense reading. You will work closely with a principal to refine your drafts, ensuring every statement is backed by verifiable analysis.

What you bring
 We are looking for an individual with an analytical mind and a strong affinity for the discipline of writing. This role is designed as an entry-level internship for a current student or recent graduate who wants to develop foundational skills in strategic analysis.
You should have a basic understanding of corporate finance and quantitative research methods. Comfort with spreadsheets is essential, as you will regularly audit financial statements and model governance structures. More importantly, you must be a precise writer. We look for candidates who can explain a statistical variance in a single, clear sentence without relying on professional jargon. An academic background in economics, finance, mathematics, or policy is helpful, but intellectual curiosity and attention to detail are what matter most.

How we work
 Our practice operates under a single-author model. We believe that true clarity is achieved when an individual takes full ownership of an argument. While you will receive guidance from our principals, you will be expected to stand behind your research and defend your findings under internal scrutiny.
We do not rely on consensus to find answers. We believe in the analytical rigor of a dissenting opinion. When we analyze a client's strategy, we look for what they have omitted. Your contributions will directly inform the signed memoranda we deliver to corporate leaders. Because an author's name sits at the foot of every page, the work is conducted with a high degree of individual accountability and discretion.

Working at JHC Consulting
 We offer a hybrid working structure that balances hours of quiet concentration with scheduled days of in-person debate. You will work from your own space for the majority of the week, allowing for the uninterrupted reading and analysis crucial to our work. On designated hybrid days, you will join our principals in a shared professional space to review drafts and polish our final written pieces. This structure supports both the independent discipline needed for rigorous analysis and the direct mentorship that helps entry-level analysts progress.
Every mandate we accept represents a serious commitment to intellectual honesty. By joining us for this internship, you will learn to build written arguments that survive deep scrutiny and help leaders address their most pressing governance questions. If you appreciate the quiet discipline of written analysis and are prepared to take responsibility for the integrity of your numbers, this role offers an exceptional entry into the advisory profession.

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
About Lucid

At Lucid, we are creating exceptional mobility experiences through innovation to drive the world forward. Built on Lucid’s proprietary technology and software-defined vehicle architecture, our award-winning vehicles bring our “Compromise Nothing™” approach to the global automotive market. That means refusing to choose between performance and sustainability, design and engineering, ambition and integrity. In Lucid Air and Lucid Gravity, we have designed and built vehicles that have redefined their segments, combining exceptional range, performance, design, and expansive space in a single experience.

We achieve this through deep vertical integration, with design, engineering, and production happening in-house across our global offices and manufacturing facilities. Our teams come from industries around the world, united by a shared commitment to excellence. By refusing to settle, you can help redefine what’s possible and shape the future of mobility.

At Lucid, we are reimagining the future of mobility. As a Data Analyst Intern within our Customer Experience team, you will help turn customer and operational data into insights that improve the end-to-end Lucid journeyn from first interaction to aftersales support.

This internship is a great opportunity for a student who enjoys working with data, is curious about customer behavior, and wants to gain hands-on experience in an international, fast-moving environment.

What you’ll do


Support the maintenance and improvement of dashboards related to Customer Care, Voice of the Customer (VoC), and Customer Journey performance
Help ensure data accuracy and consistency across reporting tools such as Power BI and Tableau
Assist in monitoring key performance indicators such as NPS, CSAT, response times, and resolution rates
Support the analysis of customer care performance, including ticket volumes, SLAs, and resolution trends
Help identify patterns, inefficiencies, and opportunities for improvement through data analysis
Prepare recurring reports and dashboards for team reviews and leadership updates
Assist in combining survey results, customer feedback, and operational data into clear insights
Support the analysis of customer feedback to identify common themes, pain points, and trends
Contribute to Customer Journey analysis across Sales, Delivery, and Aftersales
Work with different teams such as CX, Service, Retail, and Digital to gather data inputs and business context
Support small improvements in reporting processes, including data clean-up and basic automation
Present findings in a clear, structured, and easy-to-understand way


What we’re looking for


Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s program in a field relevant to this internship, such as Data Analytics, Business Analytics, Economics, Statistics, Business Administration, or a similar quantitative or analytical study
Looking for an internship where you can apply your academic knowledge in a practical business environment
Strong interest in data analysis, customer experience, and using insights to improve processes or services
Good working knowledge of Excel or Google Sheets, including formulas and pivot tables
Comfortable working with data, spotting patterns, and approaching problems in a structured way
Detail-oriented and organized, with the ability to manage multiple tasks at once
Eager to learn, open to feedback, and proactive in asking questions
Able to communicate clearly in English, both written and verbal
Enjoy working collaboratively with different stakeholders and teams


Nice to have


Familiarity with data visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau
Basic exposure to SQL, Python, or R
Interest in customer experience, digital journeys, service operations, or the automotive/EV industry
Previous academic, internship, or project experience involving data analysis, dashboards, reporting, or customer insights


What you’ll gain


Hands-on experience working with real business and customer data in an international company
Insight into how data supports decision-making across different parts of the customer journey
The opportunity to develop your analytical, reporting, and communication skills in a practical setting
Exposure to cross-functional collaboration with teams such as Customer Experience, Service, Retail, and Digital
A chance to learn how customer feedback and operational metrics can be translated into meaningful business improvements
Mentorship and guidance from experienced professionals in a dynamic and innovative environment


Lucid Motors is committed to maintaining a safe and trustworthy environment. As such, a background check may be required during the hiring process. This will be performed in line with European data protection laws, and candidates will be informed and asked to provide consent before the check is conducted.

At Lucid, we don’t just welcome diversity - we celebrate it! Lucid Motors is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, national or ethnic origin, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity and expression, marital status, and any other characteristic protected under applicable local laws and regulations.

Compensation & Benefits: Lucid offers a comprehensive and competitive benefits package including medical, dental, and vision insurance; life and disability coverage; paid time off; paid holidays; and a 401(k) retirement plan. Eligible employees may also participate in Lucid’s equity program and/or a discretionary annual cash incentive program. Incentive and equity awards, if applicable, are determined based on individual performance, role scope, market considerations, and overall company results, in accordance with the terms of the applicable plans.

Equal Opportunity: At Lucid, we believe diversity strengthens everything we build. Lucid Motors is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and is committed to providing an inclusive workplace for all. We consider all qualified applicants without regard to race, color, national or ethnic origin, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, marital status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable state or federal laws and regulations.

Accessibility: Lucid Motors is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you need any accommodation to participate in the application process, please contact us at TA-Operations@lucidmotors.com.

Candidate Data Privacy: By submitting your application, you understand and agree that your personal data will be processed in accordance with our Candidate Privacy Notice.

To all recruitment agencies: Lucid Motors does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward resumes to Lucid Motors. Lucid Motors is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Title: Data Analist
Description:
Als Data Analist verbind je business, processen en data om organisaties te voorzien van inzichten, stuurinformatie en verbeterkansen die leiden tot betere besluitvorming en digitale transformatie.




Je analyseert processen, systemen en databronnen.
Je vertaalt businessbehoeften naar heldere analyses en concrete oplossingen.
Je signaleert kansen voor optimalisatie en efficiënter werken, zoals automatisering en maakt gebruik van AI.
Je ontwikkelt dashboards en rapportages met duidelijke stuurinformatie.
Je zet data om in inzichten waarmee organisaties slimmer kunnen sturen.
Je schakelt tussen business en IT en bewaakt de voortgang van projecten.
Je ondersteunt organisaties bij procesverbetering en digitale transformatie.




Jij kijkt verder dan alleen de vraag van vandaag en helpt organisaties toekomstbestendig werken.







Dit ben jij

Onze Factbuilders zijn nieuwsgierig, creatief en niet bang om anders te denken.

Je beschikt over WO werk- en denkniveau.
Je brengt senioriteit mee in je houding, aanpak en manier van werken. Wij kijken niet naar het aantal jaren.
Je hebt een positieve en proactieve instelling in combinatie met een groot relativeringsvermogen. Het is uiteindelijk maar werk ;)
Je brengt passie, plezier en af en toe wat gekkigheid. Je krijgt het immers ook terug.
Jij kan goed samenwerken en communiceren.
Je beheerst de Nederlandse taal goed, het draait niet alleen maar om eentjes en nulletjes.







Dit krijg jij ervoor terug

Onbeperkt verlof.
Hybride werken.
Flexibiliteit. Wil je bijvoorbeeld 32 uur of 36 uur werken, dat kan allemaal!
Goed salaris, in overleg en afhankelijk van jouw ervaring en senioriteit in de gesprekken.
Ruime mobiliteitsregeling: mogelijkheden voor een leaseauto, NS Business Card, etc.
Een sportieve omgeving. Wil jij een marathon lopen of een toernooi voor het hele team organiseren? Leuk, wij vergoeden het voor je!
Ruimte om je zowel persoonlijk als inhoudelijk verder te ontwikkelen met diverse trainingen. Allemaal in lijn met jouw eigen ambities.
Passie en plezier. Naast hard werken vieren wij succes met borrels, uitjes en sportiviteit, zoals ons jaarlijkse skiweekend in Oostenrijk :).
We zijn een scale-up organisatie: je staat met ons aan de basis van de ontwikkeling van de organisatie. Jouw ideeën doen ertoe.







Dit zijn wij

Wij zijn Forfacts, een sterk groeiende consultancy organisatie.

Wij geloven in een eenvoudige wereld zonder bureaucratie en helpen organisaties met verandering, procesverbetering en datagedreven werken.




Ons uiteindelijke doel? Eenvoud.

Hoe doen we dat? We creëren inzicht, vanuit vrijheid en met realisme.







Ben jij onze nieuwe Data Analist? Neem dan rechtstreeks contact op met Rik Govers via rik@forfacts.nl. Kijk voor meer informatie op www.forfacts.nl

Title: Global BI & Analytics Internship
Description:
GLOBAL BI & ANALYTICS INTERNSHIP

AMSTERDAM, COTY HQ

About Coty

At Coty, we are seeking motivated Global BI & Analytics Interns to join our team as part of Coty’s Data Talent Factory, a structured 6-month rotation program designed for students eager to build hands-on skills in data, analytics, and generative AI. You’ll spend your first 2 months training with the Global BI team at our HQ in Amsterdam-Zuid, then rotate through 2 business analytics teams (2 months each), applying your skills to real projects across the organization.

We’re Coty, one of the world’s largest beauty companies with an iconic portfolio of brands across fragrance, color cosmetics, and skin and body care. Coty is the global leader in fragrance and number three in color cosmetics. Coty’s products are sold in over 150 countries around the world. Coty and its brands are committed to a range of social causes as well as seeking to minimize its impact on the environment.

About The Business Facing BI Team In Amsterdam

The Business Facing BI team plays a pivotal role in implementing a variety of business intelligence and data analytics solutions to enable data-driven decision-making globally. We actively set and refine Power BI guidelines to ensure scalable, secure, and innovative BI practices across Coty. Although our team is small, we are diverse with members based in Amsterdam and New York City. We embrace continuous learning and rapid adoption of cutting-edge technologies. We are actively exploring how generative AI is reshaping the way we build and deliver analytics. As an intern, you’ll be part of that journey.

What We Offer

At Coty, we offer an exceptional opportunity for our Global BI & Analytics interns to gain hands-on experience in a vibrant and dynamic environment.


Months 1-2: Training in Power BI and Databricks fundamentals, with a focus on Coty’s standards and best practices. Includes exploration of generative AI capabilities within these platforms.
Months 3-4: First project rotation with a business analytics team.
Months 5-6: Second project rotation with a different business analytics team.
By the end of the program, you’ll have a portfolio of real deliverables, cross-functional exposure, and a strong foundation for a career in data & analytics. You will receive direct mentorship from the Business Facing Power BI Manager, fostering both personal and professional growth. Additionally, you’ll benefit from an inclusive, supportive work environment that encourages collaboration and innovation.


Read a testimonial from a former intern about their time with the Global BI & Analytics team:

“My internship with the Global BI & Analytics team was truly unique. It went far beyond dashboards and data, offering a deep dive into strategy, stakeholder management, and the complexities behind scalable BI products. I was exposed to projects across finance, HR, and transformation teams, which helped me understand the company better while building a strong foundation that supports my current role as a Supply Finance Analyst.” – Esmat Fayaz, Supply Finance Analyst

ARE YOU A COTY FIT


Student (Bachelor/Master), it is important that you remain enrolled with your university.
Demonstrated passion for data and an interest in specializing in Power BI and Databricks.
Strong analytical skills and proficiency with numbers.
Familiarity with data analysis tools such as Excel, Python, SQL, and R is highly advantageous.
Strong interest in generative AI and its practical applications in data & analytics.
Adaptability and willingness to work across different teams and business domains.
Keen eye for detail to ensure high-quality, aesthetically pleasing outputs.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English.


Our Benefits


Great opportunities to work across multiple analytics teams and manage end-to-end projects.
Internship allowance, NS business card (if you were not eligible for a student public transport card), staff discount of Coty products, access to Coty Academy.
Convenient office location (near Amsterdam Zuid station) with lots of fun in-house events.
First-hand knowledge about the beauty industry.


Period

This internship is full-time (40 hours per week), for a duration of 5-6 months and starts from Summer 2026. Remote working is possible up to 50% of your time, in alignment with your line manager. You will receive an internship allowance and NS Business Card if you do not have a student public transport card. The internship can be combined with a thesis but be aware that this will largely have to be done in your own time.

INTERESTED?

At Coty, we believe in equality and inclusion for all, and stand together with those fighting for freedom, justice, and equal rights regardless of who you are or who you love.

Our people make us who we are. They are the brightest minds bringing the boldest ideas to life every day. If you’re the kind of person who refuses to settle, if you want to think bigger, learn faster and go further than you ever thought possible – we’d love to hear from you.

Please send us your motivation letter together with your CV, stating your availability and for how long.

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst, Marketing

Amsterdam, hybrid working,

up to 5,000 per month plus benefits




This role offers the chance to sit close to commercial decision making and see your analysis directly influence marketing strategy. You will work across the customer journey, helping teams move towards a more customer centric and data driven way of working while operating in a supportive, modern data environment.




The Company

They are a well established organisation with a strong consumer focus and a growing ambition around data and analytics. The business combines long term stability with increasing investment in modern data capabilities. Marketing and commercial teams are actively engaged in using data to improve performance, customer experience, and decision making.




The Role

Analyse marketing and customer data to uncover actionable insights.
Build and maintain dashboards and reports to support marketing performance tracking.
Partner with marketing stakeholders to support evidence based commercial decisions.
Translate complex data into clear, compelling stories for non technical audiences.
Contribute to improving data literacy and analytical maturity across the business.




Your Skills and Experience

Hands on experience in data analysis within a commercial or marketing context.
Strong SQL and Python capability for data extraction, analysis, and exploration.
Experience building dashboards using tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
Confident communicator with strong stakeholder engagement and storytelling skills.
Comfortable working with imperfect or evolving data and still delivering value.




What They Offer

One year fixed term contract with exposure across the full customer journey.
Hybrid working model based in Amsterdam.
Clear line of sight between your work and real marketing impact.
The opportunity to grow within a stable organisation with modern data ambitions.




How to Apply

If you are looking for a marketing focused data analyst role with visible business impact, apply now to find out more.

Data Analyst, Marketing

Amsterdam, hybrid working,

up to 5,000 per month plus benefits


This role offers the chance to sit close to commercial decision making and see your analysis directly influence marketing strategy. You will work across the customer journey, helping teams move towards a more customer centric and data driven way of working while operating in a supportive, modern data environment.


The Company

They are a well established organisation with a strong consumer focus and a growing ambition around data and analytics. The business combines long term stability with increasing investment in modern data capabilities. Marketing and commercial teams are actively engaged in using data to improve performance, customer experience, and decision making.


The Role

  • Analyse marketing and customer data to uncover actionable insights.
  • Build and maintain dashboards and reports to support marketing performance tracking.
  • Partner with marketing stakeholders to support evidence based commercial decisions.
  • Translate complex data into clear, compelling stories for non technical audiences.
  • Contribute to improving data literacy and analytical maturity across the business.


Your Skills and Experience

  • Hands on experience in data analysis within a commercial or marketing context.
  • Strong SQL and Python capability for data extraction, analysis, and exploration.
  • Experience building dashboards using tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
  • Confident communicator with strong stakeholder engagement and storytelling skills.
  • Comfortable working with imperfect or evolving data and still delivering value.


What They Offer

  • One year fixed term contract with exposure across the full customer journey.
  • Hybrid working model based in Amsterdam.
  • Clear line of sight between your work and real marketing impact.
  • The opportunity to grow within a stable organisation with modern data ambitions.


How to Apply

If you are looking for a marketing focused data analyst role with visible business impact, apply now to find out more.

Title: Data Controller
Description:
Location: Hengelo, Netherlands

Are you looking for a role where accuracy, cooperation and helpfulness are central? We are looking for an enthusiastic Data Controller for our team. Someone who takes charge of our data with a smile.

About The Team

As part of the supportability engineering group (ca. 65 domain experts) within the Services Segment, you will be member of a great and inspiring team of Logistic Engineering and Obsolescence Management consisting of approximately 22 colleagues.

You enjoy working in a team and are a facilitator in the broadest sense.

About The Job

The main part of the work consists of control and maintaining our database which are essential for delivering our services to our customers. Consistency and accuracy are essential here.

The work requires knowledge of several standards which are used in the field of Logistic Engineering within a military environment. You should find it inspiring and challenging to acquire, maintain, and expand this knowledge and experience to become a professional in this field. To gain this knowledge you will be supported by your team members and additional trainings.

You're the main contact with the agencies responsible for disseminating and maintaining the required standards and regulations.

A technical education is not a requirement, but affinity within a technical environment is beneficial.

Personality and attitude (fitting in the team) is of more importance than the specific knowledge of handling our databases, you need to have the capability and eagerness to adapt this knowledge during your period of training on the job.

About You


We're looking for a candidate with a degree on MBO/HBO level.
Knowledge and experience with controlling and maintaining databases. (i.e. Windchill, Oracle, Teamcenter)
Strong communicative and social skills with a high degree of initiative, creativity, pragmatism and persuasiveness;
Proactive and facilitating attitude.
Accurate and well-organized.
Interested in maintenance and service logistics (e.g. corrective, preventive and condition-based maintenance, Integrated Logistics Support)
You like working in a new and challenging environment and contributing to the further development of our team.
Proficiency in English, both spoken and written.



What's In It For You

A role with a lot of freedom and responsibility.

An inspiring work environment with enthusiastic colleagues.

Room for initiative and personal growth.


A challenging job in a great team within our high-tech, innovative work environment. Includes the freedom for initiative and good ideas.
Room for personal and professional growth within our organization, for example in regards to technical, commercial or international skills.
Valuable international collaboration with coworkers from 68 different countries around the globe.
A comfortable work space in a modern environment, with a focus on flexibility and the right work life balance.
You can usually work where and whenever you want. Within Thales, we aim for a 60/40 balance, where you work from home at least two days a week, when your role allows this.
A good salary, fitting travel allowance, and the ability to personalize your employment conditions: for instance, you can trade PTO for internet costs, our bicycle plan, company fitness and relocation costs.
40 days off per year (PTO).



Interested?

Apply now! Click on the button below to upload your profile and show your interest. For questions you may reach out to our Talent Acquisition Partner, Laurence Janssen at laurence.janssen@external.thalesgroup.com.

To ensure that we are a good match, we can ask you to participate in an assessment.

Diversity Statement

We actively support a working pattern that suits your lifestyle and helps you reach your ambitions. That means that equal opportunities, inclusion and an informal culture are integral to our success. It also means that your well-being and happiness matter to us! That’s why we offer you the flexibility to do what’s important to you; whether that’s part time hours, job sharing, remote working, or the ability to flex your start and finish times.

Security Requirements

In line with Thales' Baseline Security requirements, candidates will be asked to provide evidence of identity, eligibility to work in The Netherlands and employment and/or education history. Some vacancies may require a security clearance which can require further evidence to be provided. For further details of the evidence required to apply for security clearance please refer to the General Intelligence and Security Service.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Amsterdam | 40 hours




Urban Gym Group is an industry pioneer within the European wellness and fitness community, with over 60 clubs and studios in major cities across the Netherlands and the United Kingdom. You might know us from brands such as Trainmore, ClubSportive, THE CLB, TRIB3, PILAT3S and Gymbox. Our ambitious domestic and international growth plans require an expansion of our Business Intelligence team.




To support this growth, we are currently looking to hire two BI Analysts with different levels of experience:

BI Analyst with 2 - 4 years of experience
BI Analyst with 5+ years of experience.




Depending on your experience, your role will vary in scope, with additional responsibilities around coaching and strategic impact for more experienced candidates.




We are looking for driven BI Analysts to join our team. In this role, you will drive data-driven decision-making across the organisation and, depending on experience, contribute to coaching and developing other analysts, as well as shaping our data strategy and ensuring high-quality insights that directly impact business performance.




Working closely with Finance, Marketing, Operations, and senior leadership, you will extract actionable insights from our data, helping optimise operations, enhance member experiences, and support our ambitious growth plans.

Key responsibilities:




Data & Analytics

Conduct market research and analyses to identify new club locations
Develop business cases for expansion, including new builds, mergers, and acquisitions
Design, build, and maintain robust ETL processes to structure data from various sources
Create data models, views, tables, and reports supporting operational and strategic goals
Develop dashboards and visualisations to enable informed, data-driven decisions
Analyse growth, customer behaviour, and performance to identify opportunities and risks
Query databases to uncover insights related to revenue, member retention, and engagement
Translate data into actionable recommendations to support commercial and strategic decisions




Leadership & Team Management (depending on experience)

Coach and develop Data Analysts
Establish best practices in analytics, reporting, and data governance
Act as a key stakeholder for senior leadership, translating business needs into analytical solutions
Drive continuous improvement within the BI team, including tools, processes, and capabilities




You:

We encourage candidates with different levels of experience to apply. You bring:

2–4 years or 5+ years of experience in a data-focused role (e.g., Data Analyst, BI Analyst, or Analytics Consultant)
Proven experience in data modelling, pipeline creation, and analytics
Advanced proficiency in SQL, with solid experience in Azure SQL environments
Hands-on experience building ETL pipelines, ideally using Python or Azure Synapse Analytics
Experience with Power BI, with a track record of building impactful dashboards
Strong business acumen, ideally within fitness, wellness, retail, or a consumer-focused industry
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
Strong problem-solving mindset and attention to detail
Experience in fast-paced, scaling organisations is a plus
Experience coaching or mentoring others is a plus
Located in the Netherlands




To foster collaboration, coaching, and team energy, we expect our colleagues to work on-site (head office or clubs) at least 4 days per week (and up to 5 days depending on the role).

We offer:

A competitive salary plus 8% holiday pay
26 holidays per year (based on full-time employment) and the option to exchange public holidays
Pension plan to support your future
The chance to join our Work in Club program and contribute to charity work once a year
Unlimited access to all our high-end fitness locations for you and a +1
Unlimited access to well-being services for you and your family via OpenUp
An inspiring head office, a short 10-minute walk from Amsterdam Central Station
A diverse and energetic team of 500 colleagues pushing boundaries and supporting one another

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Senior Data Analyst

Amsterdam, Netherlands

Competitive salary and benefits




This is an opportunity to join a high-impact data team at the heart of a large-scale digital platform. You will work on complex, real-world problems that directly influence product direction, commercial strategy, and customer experience. This role offers strong exposure to senior stakeholders and the chance to shape how data drives decision-making across the business.




The Company

They are a leading digital platform operating at scale across a high-traffic consumer marketplace. With millions of monthly users, they enable both individuals and businesses to connect, transact, and grow. Their product spans mobile and web, and they operate in a fast-paced, data-rich environment. Data plays a central role in how they optimise user experience, monetisation, and long-term growth.




The Role

Partner with product, commercial, and marketing stakeholders to define analytical priorities and deliver insights that drive decision-making
Translate ambiguous business questions into structured analysis and actionable outcomes
Build dashboards and reporting that focus on meaningful KPIs across revenue, growth, and customer behaviour
Analyse large-scale datasets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities across the platform
Influence stakeholders at all levels through clear and compelling communication of insights
Support experimentation and measurement strategies to improve product and commercial performance
Collaborate with data and engineering teams to improve tracking, data quality, and metric definitions




Your Skills & Experience




Strong commercial experience in data analysis within a product-led digital environment such as e-commerce or marketplaces
Advanced SQL skills, including working with complex datasets and optimised queries
Hands-on experience with Python for analysis, modelling, or automation
Familiarity with modern cloud data platforms such as BigQuery, Databricks, or AWS
Experience using BI tools such as Tableau to create impactful dashboards
Strong understanding of key business concepts including customer lifecycle, monetisation, and growth metrics
Confident communicator with the ability to influence stakeholders and translate data into business value
Comfortable working in ambiguous environments and proactively identifying opportunities




What They Offer




Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package
A collaborative, cross-functional working environment
Opportunity to influence business and product strategy at scale
Strong career progression and exposure to senior leadership
Access to modern data tools and technology




How to Apply

If you are a commercially minded Senior Data Analyst looking to make a tangible impact, apply now to find out more.

Title: Business Intelligence Intern
Description:
Who We Are




Metyis is a global and forward-thinking firm operating across a wide range of industries, developing and delivering AI & Data, Digital Commerce, Marketing & Design solutions and Advisory services. At Metyis, our long-term partnership model brings long-lasting impact and growth to our business partners and clients through extensive execution capabilities.

With our team, you can experience a collaborative environment with highly skilled multidisciplinary experts, where everyone has room to build bigger and bolder ideas. Being part of Metyis means you can speak your mind and be creative with your knowledge. Imagine the things you can achieve with a team that encourages you to be the best version of yourself.




We are Metyis. Partners for Impact.




What We Offer




2 months of intensive training from our super skilled BI team, on topics like data warehousing, data modelling and Power BI reporting (Data transformation, DAX, report building).
Followed by 4 months of relevant working experience on a live business case.
Interact with clients regularly to drive their business towards impactful change.
Play a key role in the team by creating new business solutions via data-driven insights.
Receive guidance by a dedicated mentor to make sure you make the most out of your time at Metyis.
Experience being part of a fast-growing international and diverse team.




What You Will Do




Assist in the design, development and maintenance of BI reporting systems.
Understand with data warehousing and data modelling activities.
Participate in the creation of data-driven solutions to support business needs.
Collaborate with data scientists, business analysts and software engineers to understand and implement reporting requirements.
Participate in report reviews and providing constructive feedback.
Stay up to date with the latest developments in all the BI tech stacks and best practices.




What You’ll Bring




Currently pursuing a Master's degree in Business Analytics, Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to derive insights from complex data sets.
Basic understanding of SQL or BI is helpful.
Ability to work in a team environment.
A passion for data analysis and a desire to learn and grow, in the field of business intelligence.




At Metyis, we are driven by curiosity and collaboration. We value diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging (DEIB) in all its forms as it makes us stronger as an organisation and promotes creativity and innovation. We welcome all talents and are committed to creating a workplace where every employee can make a meaningful impact and grow.

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
Business Analyst | AI Readiness & Data Governance | Rotterdam

Grote organisatie binnen de telecomsector |




OVER DE FUNCTIE Binnen het AI & Automation team van een grote speler in de telecomsector wordt gewerkt aan de automatisering van interne processen via innovatieve AI-tools. De ambitie is helder: een stap zetten naar geavanceerde Workflow Automation en Agentic AI, te beginnen met use cases binnen het HR-domein. Denk aan AI-gedreven onboarding en slimme interne chatbots voor HR-beleidsvragen.

Een AI-agent is zo sterk als de data waarmee deze gevoed wordt. In deze rol focus jij je op Fase 1: Discovery & Data Readiness. Als analytische bruggenbouwer onderzoek je welke data nodig is, waar deze staat, hoe schoon deze is, en hoe we deze structureel klaarstomen voor AI-integratie.

WAT GA JE DOEN

Use Case Scoping & Prioritization

Je analyseert de voorgestelde HR-use cases en weegt businesswaarde af tegen technische haalbaarheid en databeschikbaarheid.

Data & System Blueprinting

Je brengt het datalandschap in kaart: welke bronsystemen zijn essentieel en kunnen deze ontsloten worden door de AI-architectuur?

Data Quality Assessment & Gap Analysis

Je toetst de huidige staat van de data: schoon, gestructureerd en actueel genoeg?
Je definieert de remediatiestappen om data 'AI-ready' te maken.

Kennismanagement Architectuur

Je onderzoekt hoe ongestructureerde documentatie (zoals HR-policies) momenteel wordt beheerd.
Je adviseert over versiebeheer en structurering ten behoeve van AI-integratie.

Data Governance & Stewardship

Je ontwerpt een werkbaar model voor data-eigenaarschap met heldere rollen en verantwoordelijkheden, zodat broninformatie ook in de toekomst up-to-date blijft.

WAT WIJ ZOEKEN We zoeken geen developer, maar een procesdenker met sterke affiniteit voor data en AI.

Aantoonbare ervaring als Business Analyst, Data Analyst of Data Steward, bij voorkeur rondom datakwaliteit, compliance of digitalisering.
Ervaring met Data Readiness assessments en/of Discovery trajecten binnen corporate omgevingen.
Conceptueel begrip van hoe AI-modellen (LLM's, RAG-systemen) werken en welke eisen dit stelt aan datakwaliteit en documentbeheer.
Sterk in het analyseren en optimaliseren van (kennis)processen.
Ervaring met het opzetten van version control en data stewardship frameworks.
Uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden als brug tussen business (proceseigenaren) en IT-architecten.
Gestructureerd, vasthoudend en in staat complexe bevindingen te vertalen naar een helder adviesrapport of blueprint.

WAT WIJ BIEDEN

Een vaste functie bij een vooraanstaande organisatie in de telecomsector.
Salaris tussen €5.500 en €7.500 per maand, afhankelijk van ervaring.
Werken aan het snijvlak van data, AI en bedrijfsprocessen — een domein vol groei en innovatie.
Intensieve samenwerking met een gedreven AI & Automation team.
Een dynamische werkomgeving waar jouw analytische input direct impact heeft.

INTERESSE? Neem contact op via info@truelegends.nl of 020 – 26 16 791

  • Ervaring met Data Readiness assessments en/of Discovery trajecten binnen corporate omgevingen.
  • Conceptueel begrip van hoe AI-modellen (LLM's, RAG-systemen) werken en welke eisen dit stelt aan datakwaliteit en documentbeheer.
  • Sterk in het analyseren en optimaliseren van (kennis)processen.
  • Ervaring met het opzetten van version control en data stewardship frameworks.
  • Uitstekende communicatieve vaardigheden als brug tussen business (proceseigenaren) en IT-architecten.
  • Gestructureerd, vasthoudend en in staat complexe bevindingen te vertalen naar een helder adviesrapport of blueprint.

WAT WIJ BIEDEN

  • Een vaste functie bij een vooraanstaande organisatie in de telecomsector.
  • Salaris tussen €5.500 en €7.500 per maand, afhankelijk van ervaring.
  • Werken aan het snijvlak van data, AI en bedrijfsprocessen — een domein vol groei en innovatie.
  • Intensieve samenwerking met een gedreven AI & Automation team.
  • Een dynamische werkomgeving waar jouw analytische input direct impact heeft.

INTERESSE? Neem contact op via info@truelegends.nl of 020 – 26 16 791

Title: BI Specialist
HTML:
Standplaats: Apeldoorn

Attero is een landelijk en internationaal opererend milieubedrijf dat al ruim 90 jaar werkt aan een schonere, circulaire toekomst. Wij winnen energie en grondstoffen terug uit afval en dragen zo bij aan een duurzame leefomgeving. Data speelt een steeds belangrijkere rol binnen onze organisatie. Daarom zoeken wij een BI Specialist die helpt onze data-infrastructuur verder te professionaliseren en toekomstbestendig te maken.

Wat ga je doen?Als BI Specialist ben je een belangrijke schakel in het ontsluiten, structureren en benutten van data binnen Attero. Je werkt aan betrouwbare, schaalbare en veilige data-oplossingen die collega’s ondersteunen bij rapportages, analyses en besluitvorming.

Je Houdt Je Onder Andere Bezig Met

  • Het ontwerpen, bouwen en onderhouden van databases, datasets en datamodellen;
  • Het optimaliseren van datastromen en het waarborgen van datakwaliteit;
  • Het ontwikkelen en beheren van ETL/ELT-processen in Azure Data Factory, met behulp van bestaande tooling;
  • Het mede vormgeven en doorontwikkelen van de datawarehouse-architectuur;
  • Het combineren van data uit verschillende bronnen en het beschikbaar maken daarvan voor analyses en visualisaties, bijvoorbeeld in Power BI;
  • Het toepassen van best practices op het gebied van data security en privacy;
  • Het meewerken aan data-innovatie, migraties en nieuwe data-projecten;
  • Het zorgen dat data-oplossingen na oplevering goed landen in de lijnorganisatie en daadwerkelijk gebruikt worden.

Je werkt nauw samen met collega’s uit IT, data en de business en neemt actief deel aan projecten binnen een agile/scrum werkwijze.

Wat neem je mee?Je bent analytisch sterk, werkt gestructureerd en krijgt energie van complexe data-vraagstukken. Je denkt in oplossingen en weet technische keuzes goed te onderbouwen.

Daarnaast Neem Je Mee

  • Minimaal 3 tot 5 jaar werkervaring in een vergelijkbare functie;
  • Een afgeronde HBO-opleiding in IT, bij voorkeur met een specialisatie in data of bedrijfskundige ICT;
  • Ervaring met SQL en het schrijven van schone, onderhoudbare en goed gestructureerde code;
  • Kennis van dimensioneel modelleren en datamodellering;
  • Ervaring met Azure-omgevingen, bij voorkeur Azure Data Factory;
  • Affiniteit met data-analyse en datavisualisatie, bijvoorbeeld Power BI en IBCS-concepten;
  • Ervaring met werken volgens agile en scrum;
  • De motivatie om samen te werken, kennis te delen en continu te verbeteren.

Wie ben jij?Je bent een teamplayer die verantwoordelijkheid neemt en graag impact maakt. Je werkt zorgvuldig, denkt mee over verbeteringen en zoekt actief de samenwerking op. Je herkent jezelf in de Attero-principes: samen werken, blijven verbeteren en bijdragen aan een duurzame toekomst.

Wat krijg je?Bij Attero zorgen we goed voor je, in elke fase van je loopbaan. Je kunt rekenen op:

  • Een marktconform salaris, afgestemd op jouw opleiding en ervaring;
  • Een goede verlofregeling, met de mogelijkheid om extra verlofuren bij te kopen;
  • Een persoonlijk arbeidsvoorwaardenbudget van 13,5%, dat je naar eigen inzicht kunt laten uitbetalen of inzetten;
  • Een flexbudget dat je naar eigen invulling kunt besteden;
  • Ruimte voor ontwikkeling en doorgroei. Je krijgt vrijheid om jezelf verder te brengen en toegang tot onze Attero Academy met e-learnings die aansluiten op jouw functie;
  • Het Attero Fit & Vitaal programma, met initiatieven die bijdragen aan jouw duurzame inzetbaarheid;
  • Voordeel op je zorgverzekering en particuliere verzekeringen, zoals auto, brand, opstal en aansprakelijkheid;
  • Een goede pensioenregeling, zodat het ook na je loopbaan goed geregeld is.

Klaar om impact te maken?Mail gerust met Ruben Kieft, Recruiter. Hij is bereikbaar via ruben.kieft@attero.nl.

Wil je solliciteren? Druk dan op de knop ‘Direct solliciteren’. Wie weet zien we je snel op locatie!

Disclaimer externe recruitmentbureausExterne recruitmentbureaus dienen een overeenkomst met Attero B.V. te hebben getekend, uitgegeven door een Corporate Recruiter, om CV's te mogen indienen. Daarbij mag alleen een CV worden ingediend wanneer het bureau is uitgenodigd door een Corporate Recruiter om mee te zoeken naar geschikte kandidaten. Alle ongevraagde CV's die buiten deze voorwaarden worden aangeboden, zullen als eigendom van Attero B.V. worden beschouwd. Attero B.V. is hierbij geen plaatsingskosten verschuldigd.

Title: Data Analyst Marketing
Description:
Are you analytical, commercially minded, and passionate about improving marketing performance through real data insights? Priva is looking for a Data Analyst Marketing to help strengthen our data-driven marketing organization and drive smarter decision-making across our global marketing teams.

About Priva   |  Technology for sustainable growth

Priva is a global leader in climate control, energy efficiency, and water management solutions for horticulture and buildings. Our technologies help customers reduce their environmental footprint and contribute to multiple Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

With over 600 colleagues, 16 international offices, and customers in more than 100 countries, we combine hardware, software, and data to drive innovation in sustainable urban environments.

You enjoy working in an environment where you can build, improve, and take ownership, and where your work directly contributes to business success and sustainability.

Why this role matters

In this role, you will analyze marketing data from our own systems, campaigns, and customer interactions, not relying on external research agencies but working directly with the data we generate ourselves every day. You collect, interpret, and translate complex marketing data into actionable insights and strategic recommendations that help improve campaign performance, lead generation, conversion, and customer journeys.

You will play an active role in building a culture of testing, learning, and continuous optimization. Together with the marketing team, you will set up and guide A/B tests, motivate teams to experiment more, and help them make decisions based on data instead of assumptions. Based on test results and performance insights, you will provide concrete recommendations to optimize campaigns, funnels, content, targeting, and conversion rates.

In addition, you will contribute to the development of AI agents and smart automations that help make marketing insights more accessible, improve reporting, and support teams with faster and smarter decision-making.

You will create dashboards and performance reports that provide clear visibility into marketing results for stakeholders across the organization. Besides being strong analytically, you are also able to communicate insights clearly and translate complex data into practical business recommendations.

You will report to the marketing director, work closely with campaign management, channel marketeers and other marketing teams within Priva, acting as a sparring partner on data, performance, and optimization.

What will you do?


Collect, analyze, and interpret our own marketing and campaign data
Build dashboards and marketing performance reports
Translate data into actionable insights and optimization recommendations
Set up, guide, and analyze A/B tests together with our marketing hubs
Encourage and support teams in becoming more data-driven
Help improve funnel performance, campaign effectiveness, and conversion rates
Develop AI agents and smart automations for marketing insights



What do you bring?


Experience as a Data Analyst, Marketing Analyst, or Digital Analyst
Strong affinity with marketing analytics, dashboards, and performance optimization
Experience with A/B testing, experimentation, and conversion optimization
Interest in AI, automation, and data-driven marketing
Strong communication skills
Experience within an international or B2B environment is a plus
You are fluent in English and Dutch (German or Spanish is a plus)



What You Get

In numbers


A gross monthly salary between €3,000 and €4,000 based on 40 hours, depending on experience. For this job, 32 hours a week is also an option.
A healthy worklife balance with flexible working hours, 30 vacation days, and the option to purchase additional days.
8% holiday allowance.
Travel allowance of €0.23 per km, with a daily maximum of 150 km.



For your own sustainability


Participation in our collective health insurance and pension plan.
A participation plan to feel engaged with Priva’s success.
Personal development opportunities with an education budget.
A hybrid working culture: 2 days (Tuesday & Thursday) onsite in De Lier, 2 days working from home.
Access to a healthy cafeteria and onsite fitness facilities, including CrossFit, Pilates, spinning, table tennis, and darts.



 

Ready to make an impact?

Apply now and help us become a data-driven company!

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Senior Data (Marketing) Analyst

Amsterdam, Netherlands, hybrid,

Up to 100k annually




This is an opportunity to step into a high impact analytics role within a large scale digital marketplace, where data sits at the heart of product, commercial, and marketing decisions. You will work closely with cross functional teams to shape strategy, improve user experience, and drive measurable growth across a platform used by millions.




The Company

They are a leading online platform operating across the Benelux region, supporting both consumers and businesses to buy and sell goods and services at scale. The organisation runs a multi platform environment across web and mobile, with data playing a central role in decision making. Sustainability and the circular economy are key themes, alongside long term customer value and trust.




The Role

You will act as a strategic analytics partner across product, business, and marketing teams, using data to influence decisions and optimise performance.

Translate complex business questions into clear analytical approaches
Analyse large scale behavioural and commercial datasets to uncover insights
Design and evaluate experiments including A B tests and causal analysis
Build and maintain dashboards and reports for senior stakeholders
Work closely with product managers, marketers, and engineers to drive impact
Communicate findings clearly and turn insights into actionable recommendations




Your Skills and Experience

Strong commercial experience in data analytics within a digital or marketing focused environment
Advanced SQL skills working with complex, large scale datasets
Experience using Python for analysis and modelling
Hands on experience with experimentation and statistical analysis
Proven use of data visualisation tools, ideally Tableau
Practical experience with GA4, including event modelling and reporting
Confident communicator able to influence non technical stakeholders
Strong curiosity around user behaviour, products, and growth




What They Offer

Competitive base salary
Annual bonus scheme
Employee support and wellbeing programme
Collaborative, high trust culture with clear opportunities for progression
The chance to work on products used by millions, with visible impact




How to Apply

If you are looking for a senior analytics role where your work directly shapes products and strategy, apply now to learn more.

Title: Product Data Analyst
Description:
The AI orchestration of your wildest imagination.

n8n is the open workflow orchestration platform built for the new era of AI. We give technical teams the freedom of code with the speed of no-code, so they can automate faster, smarter, and without limits. Backed by a fiercely inventive community and 500+ builder-approved integrations, we’re changing the way people bring systems together and scale ideas for impact.

Since our founding in 2019, we’ve grown into a diverse team of over 260 - working across Europe and the US, connected by a shared builder spirit and with our centre of gravity in Berlin. Along the way, we’ve:


Cultivated a community of more than 650,000 active developers and builders
Earned 190K+ GitHub stars, making us one of the world’s Top 40 most popular projects
Backed by top investors, from Sequoia’s first German seed to our SAP's recent strategic investment - bringing us to a $5.2bn valuation


That’s the company we’ve built. Now we’d love to see what you can build. If you’re applying, try n8n out - whether you’re technical or not - and share a screenshot of your first workflow with us. The easiest place to start is here: app.n8n.cloud/register.

We’re in a defining moment of an incredible journey. Come and build with us.

Join n8n as the dedicated data partner to a product team that's already hands-on with data.

Our PMs are technical and data-literate. They write SQL, run their own analyses, and know their corner of the product deeply. What they're missing is a dedicated analyst counterpart - someone who takes the data work further than they have time for, brings the outside perspective they can't get from inside their squad, and pairs with them on designing and improving the product.

That's the role. You'll be embedded with a product squad as the PMs' dedicated data partner, working on the deeper analyses, the cross-cutting views, and the questions that shape what gets built - with activation and adoption of n8n as your headline focus.

🎯 Your mission: be the dedicated data partner that helps a hands-on product team go deeper, see further, and design better products.

🤝 Be the PMs' data partner


Our PMs already do their own analyses. You're the counterpart who takes the work deeper and broader than they can in a sprint.
Take on the analyses they don't have time for, accelerate the ones they're running, and pair on the questions that matter most.
Free PMs to focus on building product by being the dedicated owner of the data work that supports it.


🔭 Bring perspective they can't get themselves


Cross-product cuts, segment views, comparative analyses across the user base — the outside lens that's hard to see from inside a single squad.
Challenge assumptions, including the ones PMs are most attached to.
Surface the analyses nobody asked for but everyone needed.


🚀 Help design and improve the product


Co-create hypotheses with PMs and pair on how to test them properly.
Activation and adoption are the headline: how users get to value, what drives lasting use, where we're losing them, and what to build to fix it.
Go deep on retention drivers, cohort dynamics, and the leading indicators of expansion. Connect feature-level analyses back to the bigger picture.


🗣 Influence the roadmap with a real POV


Sit in product planning as a peer. Bring strong, data-backed opinions about what we should build next — and what we shouldn't.
Push back constructively. Translate complex analysis into crisp recommendations that move decisions.


🧪 Raise the bar on rigor


Bring statistical depth where it matters and keep it out of the way when it doesn't.
Make experiments and analyses trustworthy — well-designed, properly powered, honestly read.
Partner with PMs and Engineering on event instrumentation so the data the team relies on is solid.


Requirements

Must-haves


📊 Senior product analytics chops: 5+ years analyzing product usage in B2B SaaS, ideally in a PLG environment.
🤝 Partner mindset: You thrive working alongside technical, hands-on PMs as a counterpart — making them better and being made better by them.
🚀 Activation & adoption expertise: You've owned the analysis behind onboarding funnels, time-to-value, feature adoption, and the behavioral signals that separate stuck users from successful ones.
🔎 Depth, not just breadth: You're known for going deeper than the obvious — behavioral segmentation, retention drivers, feature impact done properly.
🤓 Real applied statistics: Not "I took a stats class" — you can spot a biased metric, an underpowered test, or a confounder, and explain it clearly to a non-technical stakeholder.
🧭 Opinionated and high-agency: You don't wait for tickets. You see what's worth analyzing, you do it, and you bring a clear recommendation — even when the team didn't ask.
🧪 Solid experimentation skills: You can design and read A/B tests properly. It's one tool in your kit, not the only one.
🔧 Instrumentation fluency: You've defined event taxonomies and partnered with engineering on tracking specs.
💻 Technical toolkit: Expert SQL; comfort with modern stack (BigQuery, dbt, Metabase or equivalent).
🗣️ Communication that lands: You write memos PMs read end-to-end and present findings that change decisions.


Nice-to-haves


👾 Devtool / OSS / technical product experience: You get how technical audiences adopt and use products.
🐍 Python for analysis: pandas, statsmodels, scipy when SQL isn't enough.
🔗 Causal inference: For the questions A/B testing can't answer.
🧮 Experimentation platforms: GrowthBook, Statsig, Eppo, or similar.


Why join us?

At n8n, your work won't disappear into a slide deck. You'll pair with a technical, data-literate product team that already does its own analysis and genuinely values having a real analyst counterpart. You'll be embedded, trusted, and in the room where decisions get made — owning the questions that matter most for a fast-growing PLG product: how users activate, what drives adoption, and what keeps them coming back. You'll join a small, sharp data team with strong foundations already in place, inside one of Europe's fastest-growing open-source companies, at a moment where what we ship matters a lot.

Sound like a challenge you're ready to take on?

Apply now - and help us build the future of automation.

n8n is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

We can sponsor visas to Germany; for any other country, you need to have existing right to work.

Our company language is English.

You care about diversity and inclusion? We do too! Check out our Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging initiatives at n8n (https://www.notion.so/n8n/Diversity-inclusion-and-belonging-n8n-c1bec2fff536422d868b1a438d990e35).

Location disclaimer: If you see multiple job postings for the same role, it is most likely because we're hiring remotely for this role and posting in different locations to make sure every potential candidate can see the role. Please apply to the location you're the most likely to work from in the future.

Benefits


Competitive compensation 💸 – We offer fair and attractive pay.
Ownership 💪 – Our core value is to “empower others,” and we mean it—you’ll get a slice of n8n with equity.
Work/life balance 🏖️ – We work hard but ensure you have time to recharge:
Europe: 30 days of vacation, plus public holidays wherever you are.
US: 20 vacation days, 8 sick days, plus public holidays wherever you are.
Health & wellness 🩺 –
Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*
US: Multiple low-premium, low-deductible medical plans with coverage for individuals and families—plus a no-cost premium HDHP option with a pre-seeded HSA—along with dental and vision coverage.
Future planning 💰 –
Europe: We provide pension contributions according to local country norms.*
US: 401(k) retirement plan with a 4% employer match.
Financial security 🛡️ –
Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*
US: Company-paid short-term and long-term disability insurance, plus life insurance to support you and your loved ones.
Career growth 📈 – We hire rising stars who grow with us! You’ll get €1K (or equivalent) per year to spend on courses, books, events, or coaching to level up your skills.
A passionate team 🤩 – We love our product, and we prove it with regular hackathons where we see who can build the coolest thing with it!
Remote-first 🌏 – Our team works remotely across Europe, with regular off-sites for team bonding. Some roles, like sales in the US, are hybrid—please check the job description.
Giving back 🤝 – We're big fans of open source, and you'll get $100 per month to support projects you care about.
AI enablement 🤖 – We believe in working smarter—everyone gets an unlimited AI budget to explore and use the best tools to boost productivity and creativity.
Transparency 🙏 – We all know what everyone’s working on, how the company is doing—the whole shebang.
Country-specific details are provided in your contract.

  • 🤝 Partner mindset: You thrive working alongside technical, hands-on PMs as a counterpart — making them better and being made better by them.
  • 🚀 Activation & adoption expertise: You've owned the analysis behind onboarding funnels, time-to-value, feature adoption, and the behavioral signals that separate stuck users from successful ones.
  • 🔎 Depth, not just breadth: You're known for going deeper than the obvious — behavioral segmentation, retention drivers, feature impact done properly.
  • 🤓 Real applied statistics: Not "I took a stats class" — you can spot a biased metric, an underpowered test, or a confounder, and explain it clearly to a non-technical stakeholder.
  • 🧭 Opinionated and high-agency: You don't wait for tickets. You see what's worth analyzing, you do it, and you bring a clear recommendation — even when the team didn't ask.
  • 🧪 Solid experimentation skills: You can design and read A/B tests properly. It's one tool in your kit, not the only one.
  • 🔧 Instrumentation fluency: You've defined event taxonomies and partnered with engineering on tracking specs.
  • 💻 Technical toolkit: Expert SQL; comfort with modern stack (BigQuery, dbt, Metabase or equivalent).
  • 🗣️ Communication that lands: You write memos PMs read end-to-end and present findings that change decisions.

Nice-to-haves

  • 👾 Devtool / OSS / technical product experience: You get how technical audiences adopt and use products.
  • 🐍 Python for analysis: pandas, statsmodels, scipy when SQL isn't enough.
  • 🔗 Causal inference: For the questions A/B testing can't answer.
  • 🧮 Experimentation platforms: GrowthBook, Statsig, Eppo, or similar.

Why join us?

At n8n, your work won't disappear into a slide deck. You'll pair with a technical, data-literate product team that already does its own analysis and genuinely values having a real analyst counterpart. You'll be embedded, trusted, and in the room where decisions get made — owning the questions that matter most for a fast-growing PLG product: how users activate, what drives adoption, and what keeps them coming back. You'll join a small, sharp data team with strong foundations already in place, inside one of Europe's fastest-growing open-source companies, at a moment where what we ship matters a lot.

Sound like a challenge you're ready to take on?

Apply now - and help us build the future of automation.

n8n is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

We can sponsor visas to Germany; for any other country, you need to have existing right to work.

Our company language is English.

You care about diversity and inclusion? We do too! Check out our Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging initiatives at n8n (https://www.notion.so/n8n/Diversity-inclusion-and-belonging-n8n-c1bec2fff536422d868b1a438d990e35).

Location disclaimer: If you see multiple job postings for the same role, it is most likely because we're hiring remotely for this role and posting in different locations to make sure every potential candidate can see the role. Please apply to the location you're the most likely to work from in the future.

Benefits

  • Competitive compensation 💸 – We offer fair and attractive pay.
  • Ownership 💪 – Our core value is to “empower others,” and we mean it—you’ll get a slice of n8n with equity.
  • Work/life balance 🏖️ – We work hard but ensure you have time to recharge:
    • Europe: 30 days of vacation, plus public holidays wherever you are.
    • US: 20 vacation days, 8 sick days, plus public holidays wherever you are.
  • Health & wellness 🩺 –
    • Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*
    • US: Multiple low-premium, low-deductible medical plans with coverage for individuals and families—plus a no-cost premium HDHP option with a pre-seeded HSA—along with dental and vision coverage.
  • Future planning 💰 –
    • Europe: We provide pension contributions according to local country norms.*
    • US: 401(k) retirement plan with a 4% employer match.
  • Financial security 🛡️ –
    • Europe: We provide benefits according to local country norms.*
    • US: Company-paid short-term and long-term disability insurance, plus life insurance to support you and your loved ones.
  • Career growth 📈 – We hire rising stars who grow with us! You’ll get €1K (or equivalent) per year to spend on courses, books, events, or coaching to level up your skills.
  • A passionate team 🤩 – We love our product, and we prove it with regular hackathons where we see who can build the coolest thing with it!
  • Remote-first 🌏 – Our team works remotely across Europe, with regular off-sites for team bonding. Some roles, like sales in the US, are hybrid—please check the job description.
  • Giving back 🤝 – We're big fans of open source, and you'll get $100 per month to support projects you care about.
  • AI enablement 🤖 – We believe in working smarter—everyone gets an unlimited AI budget to explore and use the best tools to boost productivity and creativity.
  • Transparency 🙏 – We all know what everyone’s working on, how the company is doing—the whole shebang.
  • Country-specific details are provided in your contract.

Title: Data Intelligence Specialist
Description:
For one of our clients in Den Haag, we are looking for a Data Intelligent Specialist, you will work in a team with colleagues who are responsible for analyzing, preparing, and implementing changes within the Risk and Data domain. Additionally, you will collaborate with subject matter experts in the field of Asset Finance.







Responsibilities

Ensure Asset Finance reports comply with NN Bank’s data governance principles by gathering, analyzing, and documenting report requirements.
Translate requirements into clear, actionable specifications for (DevOps) teams in terms of “people, process, technology and/or data”.
Hands-on support in the construction of investor reporting for Asset Finance.
Evaluate existing reporting processes to identify gaps and recommend improvements.
Develop and maintain detailed documentation, including functional specifications.
Work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure successful project delivery and implementation.




Qualifications

You know how to work in an Agile environment where collaboration, and continuous improvements are the foundations. Additionally, you possess:Master’s degree in Business Administration, Computer Science, or a related field.
A minimum of 5 years of proven experience in business analysis, requirements gathering or a related role.
A minimum of 5 years of proven experience in data analyses and data driven risk management within the Financial Services industry, preferably in the field of Asset Finance.
Strong quantitative, analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex data effectively. Being able to analyze and substantiate problems (step by step).
Being critical, understanding the question behind the question (asking questions!).
Result-oriented mindset and the ability to perform under pressure to meet deadlines.
Excellent communication, interpersonal, and stakeholder management skills; be able to represent complex matters in an understandable way, be able to switch between - and being a sparring partner for both - operations and management.
Proficiency in tools such as PowerBI, SQL and Python; hands-on experience with DataBricks is preferred.
Experience with Logical Data Modelling and knowledge of data mart design.
Dutch and English proficient level in both oral and written communication.
Hands-on mentality and pro-active.







Please note that for this role you need to be already situated in the Netherlands.




For more information, please contact Ritsa (ritsa.paraskevopoulou@experis.nl)

Title: E-commerce Business Intelligence & Magento Specialist
Description:
About the Role

Are you an experienced data-driven e-commerce professional who enjoys turning insights into action? At ASUS, we’re looking for someone who can seamlessly connect data, tools, and execution to drive commercial performance across our Western Europe D2C business.

In this role, you’ll sit at the intersection of business intelligence, pricing strategy, and e-commerce operations. You’ll take ownership of how pricing and promotions are executed in our Magento platform, while continuously analyzing performance and market dynamics to optimize results.

You won’t just report on data—you’ll actively shape commercial decisions, ensuring our pricing and promotions stay competitive and impactful across markets.




About the Contract

This agreement is offered on a temporary basis to cover a maternity leave absence.




What You’ll Do

Own Pricing & Promotions Execution (Magento)

Configure and manage pricing, discount rules, and promotional campaigns in Magento
Ensure accuracy, consistency, and timely execution across markets
Validate setups and troubleshoot issues in a fast-paced environment

Turn Data into Commercial Impact

Analyze sales performance during campaigns and identify optimization opportunities
Monitor competitor pricing and market trends in real time
Translate insights into clear actions that improve conversion and revenue

Act as a Regional Commercial Advisor

Proactively identify pricing gaps or missed opportunities
Provide actionable recommendations to local country teams
Support markets in reaching revenue targets through smarter pricing strategies

Build Reliable Data Foundations

Maintain and improve data pipelines across tools (e.g., GA4, Looker Studio, BI dashboards)
Ensure data accuracy and real-time availability for decision-making
Connect multiple data sources (internal + external) into a coherent insight layer




What You Bring

Strong Analytics & BI Expertise

Hands-on experience with tools like Power BI, Tableau, Excel, GA4, or Looker Studio
Ability to structure, analyze, and interpret complex datasets

Understanding of Data Systems & Connectivity

Familiarity with APIs, data flows, and how systems integrate
Comfortable working with multiple tools and data sources

E-commerce Experience (2+ years)

Experience in D2C or B2C e-commerce environments
Understanding of pricing, promotions, and conversion drivers

Execution-Focused & Detail-Oriented

Able to move between strategy and hands-on execution quickly
High attention to detail when working in technical platforms

Strong Communicator

Fluent in English
Able to translate data into clear, actionable recommendations




Nice to Have

Experience with Magento (Adobe Commerce), especially pricing rules and promotions
Experience working in international or regional e-commerce teams




Why Join ASUS?

Work with a globally recognized tech brand at the forefront of innovation
Be part of a high-impact, data-driven D2C team
Take ownership of work that directly influences revenue and market competitiveness
Enjoy a hybrid working model based in Amsterdam

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Department

WDTech - R&D

We are Walker & Dunlop. We are one of the largest providers of capital to the commercial real estate industry, enabling real estate owners and operators to bring their visions of communities — where people live, work, shop, and play — to life. We are committed to creating meaningful social, environmental, and economic change in our communities.

Department Overview

WDTech is W&D’s in-house technology team – a group of collaborative and highly skilled technology professionals, all of whom are leading experts in real estate data, data science, and technology.

WDTech Engineering builds solutions that impact not only our products but also the people and processes across our organization. A commitment to innovation and a passion for disrupting the old-fashioned real estate industry are our highest priorities.

The Impact You Will Have

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will play a key role in delivering trusted data insights and analytics that support business and product decision-making across the organization. This role is responsible for analyzing complex datasets, developing reporting solutions and dashboards, and translating data into actionable insights that improve operational effectiveness and support strategic initiatives.

Working closely with Data Analysts, BI Analysts, Data Engineers, Data QA Engineers, Data Scientists, and Product Managers, you will help ensure data products are accurate, scalable, and aligned with evolving business needs as Walker & Dunlop continues to grow as a data-driven organization.

Primary Responsibilities


Analyze and curate data sources to support business and product stakeholders in making informed decisions.
Lead key components of the analytical lifecycle, including data exploration, analysis, reporting, and insight delivery.
Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for technical and non-technical audiences.
Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards, reports, and analytical solutions.
Perform data wrangling, transformation, and analysis using SQL and enterprise analytical databases.
Define and document datasets, business logic, and reporting definitions to support consistent data usage.
Partner with data and product teams to support data governance, data quality, and data management processes.
Analyze user engagement, operational, and product data to identify trends and opportunities for improvement.
Mentor junior analysts and promote best practices in analytics, visualization, and stakeholder collaboration.
Perform other duties as assigned


Education And Experience


Bachelor’s degree preferred, preferably in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Information Systems, Mathematics, or a related field.
6+ years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or analytics roles with demonstrated ownership of end-to-end analytical projects.
Strong experience working with enterprise analytical databases such as Snowflake, Redshift, BigQuery, or similar technologies.
Experience developing dashboards and reporting solutions using Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization tools.
Experience with SQL, data transformation, and analytical reporting workflows.
Experience with Python and geospatial analysis preferred.
Real estate, financial services, or related industry experience preferred.


Knowledge, Skills And Abilities


Deep expertise in SQL and data analysis techniques.
Strong proficiency with data visualization and reporting tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
Understanding of data modeling, ETL processes, and analytical best practices.
Ability to communicate technical concepts and analytical findings clearly to business and technical audiences.
Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
Ability to work collaboratively across technical and business teams in a fast-paced environment.
Familiarity with AI-enabled tools that support reporting, documentation, and analysis workflows preferred.
High level of accountability, attention to detail, and commitment to continuous improvement.
Ability to show ownership of your work, take on challenges and acknowledge growth opportunities, and demonstrate patience when learning new processes
Courtesy, respect, and thoughtfulness in teaming with colleagues and other stakeholders


What We Offer 


You will have the opportunity to accelerate in our rapidly growing organization.
Agile working environment with flexible working hours and location, career advancement, and competitive compensation package.
We are a family friendly company.
We arrange social activities to help our employees and families become familiar with each other and our culture.
In addition to other benefits, we offer a pension scheme and 100% complimentary and confidential mental health services.


EEO Statement

We are committed to equity in all steps of the recruitment and employment experience. We believe in equal access to opportunities in our workplace. We do not tolerate discrimination, including harassment, based on any characteristic protected by applicable law, such as race, color, national origin, religion, gender identity, sexual orientation, sex, age, disability, veteran or military status, and genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We strive to be a safe place to ask questions, build professional relationships, and develop careers.

SPAM

Please be wary of recruitment scams. An indication of a scam might be a request for sensitive or bank information at the time of application or emails coming from a non walkerdunlop.com email address. Please call us at 301.215.5500, if you have any concerns about information requested during or after the application process.
