Title: BI-Specialist
HTML:
Wij zijn Liberein. De ouderenzorgorganisatie waarbij je het niet alleen hoeft te doen. Maar ook de ouderenzorgorganisatie die uitgaat van je eigen kracht. Want wij geloven dat het in je zit. Met kernwaarden als samen, vitaal en met lef moedigen wij jou aan juist datgene eruit te halen.


Bij Liberein bieden we intensieve, complexe en specialistische (verpleeghuis)zorg aan zo’n 1.500 mensen in Enschede en Glanerbrug, die zorg of ondersteuning nodig hebben. We organiseren de zorg thuis als het kan. En als dat niet meer lukt, doen we dat vanuit een van onze verschillende locaties.


De zorgsector staat voor grote uitdagingen: stijgende kosten, een toenemende zorgvraag en de behoefte aan betrouwbare stuurinformatie. Data speelt hierin een steeds belangrijkere rol. Daarom zoeken wij een BI-Specialist (12 – 16 uur per week) die met slimme dashboards, betrouwbare data en scherpe analyses helpt om Liberein toekomstbestendig te maken. Je standplaats is locatie De Eschpoort in Enschede.


Wat ga je doen?

Als BI-Specialist speel jij een belangrijke rol in het verbeteren en professionaliseren van onze informatievoorziening. Jij vertaalt data naar heldere inzichten waarmee de organisatie beter kan sturen en onderbouwde keuzes kan maken.


Je houdt je onder andere bezig met:

  • Het vertalen van informatiebehoeften van interne stakeholders naar rapportages en dashboards;
  • Het bouwen en onderhouden van dashboards;
  • Het ondersteunen van de overgang van QlikSense naar Power BI, waarin jij een prominente rol speelt;
  • Het waarborgen van datakwaliteit en betrouwbaarheid;
  • Het verbeteren en standaardiseren van informatievoorziening;
  • Het vertalen van complexe data naar begrijpelijke managementinformatie;
  • Het koppelen van verschillende systemen voor efficiënte informatiestructuren;
  • Samenwerken met de Functioneel Beheerder rondom het technisch beheer van het dataplatform;
  • Samenwerken met én begeleiding krijgen van een ervaren Business Controller.


Wat breng je mee?

Jij bent analytisch sterk, werkt gestructureerd en weet complexe informatie begrijpelijk te maken voor verschillende doelgroepen. Daarnaast ben je communicatief vaardig en werk je graag samen.


Verder neem je mee:

  • HBO werk- en denkniveau, bijvoorbeeld richting ICT, Bedrijfskunde of Bedrijfseconomie;
  • Ervaring met BI-tools zoals QlikSense en/of Power BI is een pré;
  • Goede kennis van Excel, financiële applicaties en business intelligence tooling;
  • Basiskennis van datamodellen en architectuur;
  • Een proactieve houding, analytisch vermogen en oog voor kwaliteit;
  • Een gezonde dosis humor en een positieve werkhouding.


Wat bieden we

Bij Liberein kom je terecht in een informele en betrokken organisatie waar collega’s elkaar kennen en helpen. We vinden het belangrijk dat jij jezelf kunt ontwikkelen en krijgt de ruimte om jouw talenten verder te benutten.


Daarnaast bieden wij jou:

  • Een arbeidsovereenkomst voor de duur van een jaar met mogelijkheden voor verlenging;
  • Een functie voor 12 tot 16 uur per week;
  • Inschaling conform CAO VVT, afhankelijk van opleiding en ervaring;
  • De mogelijkheid om gedeeltelijk thuis te werken;
  • Een laptop en telefoon van de zaak;
  • Een eindejaarsuitkering en vakantiegeld;
  • Goede secundaire arbeidsvoorwaarden, zoals een fietsplan, korting op zorgverzekering en fitnessabonnement;
  • Een uitstekende pensioenregeling bij PFZW;
  • Volop mogelijkheden voor ontwikkeling, scholing en groei.


Voor deze functie vragen wij een Verklaring Omtrent Gedrag (VOG). Uiteraard vergoeden wij de kosten hiervan.


Nieuwsgierig?

Wil je meer weten, neem dan contact op met Patrick Wijlens, Concern Controller, via 0623141668. We vertellen je graag wat deze functie en het werken bij Liberein zo leuk maakt!


Geïnteresseerd?

Reageer dan direct! Je kunt solliciteren via de button ‘solliciteren’.


De sluitingsdatum van de vacature is 26 juni. De sollicitatiegesprekken vinden plaats op vrijdagochtend 3 juli.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
Business Analyst, AI and Prompt Engineering | Utrecht | 36hrs | Payroll




We are working with a leading financial services organisation in the Netherlands on an exciting assignment for a hands-on Business Analyst with a strong interest in AI and prompt engineering.

The use cases are already validated. Your job is to take them from exploration into build, test, and production readiness.




What you will be doing

You will sit at the intersection of business, technology, and risk/compliance, working across teams to ensure that AI-driven solutions are mature, documented, and ready for adoption.

Day to day this means developing and refining prompts, translating validated concepts into detailed requirements and acceptance criteria, facilitating stakeholder workshops, supporting UAT, and producing governance documentation covering model governance, Responsible AI, compliance, and data privacy.




What we are looking for

5+ years as a Business Analyst in IT or data-driven environments
Hands-on experience with GenAI, LLMs, or prompt-based solutions
Strong requirements engineering and process design skills
Experience working with risk, compliance, and architecture stakeholders
Familiarity with Responsible AI principles and model governance frameworks
Comfortable working in English in an international environment (Dutch is a strong plus)
Based in the Netherlands




The details

Location: Utrecht, Monday and Wednesday in office
Start: 1 July 2026
ZZP not possible for this role




Apply today and we will contact you to discuss the opportunity further.




We will endeavor to respond to your application as soon as possible.

However, due to high volumes of applications, this may not always be feasible.

If unsuccessful on this occasion, we will retain your details for future suitable opportunities.




Amoria Bond operates as an employment agency and employment business. We are committed to equal opportunities and welcome applications from all suitably qualified candidates.

Title: BI Specialist | Veldhoven
Description:
Werken aan Power BI-rapportages binnen een volledig nieuw data warehouse, terwijl je direct invloed hebt op de kwaliteit van data en informatievoorziening? Als BI Specialist werk je aan inzichten die afdelingen echt verder helpen, in een organisatie waar samenwerking en plezier hand in hand gaan. Welkom bij Van den Udenhout!

Een dag als BI Specialist


De werkdag begint met een overleg met het BI-team, waarin jullie samen de planning, prioriteiten en lopende projecten doornemen.
Gedurende de dag werk je aan nieuwe Power BI-rapportages op basis van een volledig nieuw data warehouse, waarmee afdelingen beter kunnen sturen op resultaten en klantinzichten.
Samen met de Manager BI vertaal je informatiebehoeften naar datamodellen en selecteer je de juiste velden uit het DWH voor heldere en bruikbare dashboards.
Tussendoor onderhoud en verbeter je bestaande rapportages, zodat de informatievoorziening actueel en betrouwbaar blijft.
In samenwerking met de Process Specialist beoordeel je de datakwaliteit en zet je acties uit om processen, software-inrichting en controles verder te verbeteren.
Ook bewaak je zorgvuldig de AVG-richtlijnen, onder andere rondom verwerkingsovereenkomsten, data-anonimisering en interne communicatie.
In deze rol lever je een zichtbare bijdrage aan een organisatiebreed 360 graden klantbeeld en ondersteun je de bedrijfsvoering met slimme inzichten.



Profiel


Minimaal een hbo-opleiding, bij voorkeur in de richting van Business IT & Management, Data Science of vergelijkbaar
Minimaal 1 jaar ervaring met het bouwen van rapportages in Power BI
Een teamspeler die ook goed zelfstandig werkt
Bij voorkeur ervaring met DevOps, Microsoft Azure, DAX en SQL
Goede beheersing van de Nederlandse taal
In het bezit van rijbewijs B



Wij bieden


Een gezellig en compact team met een fijne werksfeer
Leuke personeelsuitjes en een actieve personeelsvereniging
De mogelijkheid om deel te nemen aan sportieve activiteiten van VDU Fit
Een reiskostenvergoeding en pensioenregeling
Een collectieve ziektekostenverzekering bij ONVZ/Zilveren Kruis
192 verlofuren op basis van een fulltime dienstverband
Gratis bedrijfsfysio
Volop doorgroeimogelijkheden binnen de VAG-merken
Een persoonlijk opleidingsplan via Pon Academy, passend bij jouw interesses
Korting op de aanschaf van occasions, onderhoud en automaterialen



Van den Udenhout als organisatie

Sinds onze oprichting in 1957 is Van den Udenhout uitgegroeid tot een van de grootste en meest vooruitstrevende mobiliteitsorganisaties van Zuid-Nederland. Vanuit onze vestigingen in Noord-Brabant bieden we verkoop en onderhoud van auto’s, aangevuld met mobiliteitsoplossingen zoals verhuur, lease, schadeherstel, bedrijfswageninbouw en (e-)bikes.

Wij zijn De Nieuwe Dealer voor Volkswagen, Audi, SEAT, Škoda, CUPRA en Volkswagen Bedrijfswagens met vestigingen in Best, Boxtel, Breda, Den Bosch, Eindhoven, Halsteren, Oss, Oud Gastel, Roosendaal en Veldhoven. Daarnaast hebben we een belang in diverse Porsche- en Bentley-dealerbedrijven in Brabant en Limburg, waaronder Porsche Centrum Brabant, Porsche Centrum Maastricht en Bentley Maastricht.

Werken bij Van den Udenhout betekent werken in een warme, ambitieuze Brabantse organisatie waar persoonlijke aandacht, groei en passie voor mobiliteit centraal staan. Samen met zo’n 1.000 collega’s bouwen we iedere dag aan de mobiliteit van morgen.

Contactinformatie

Meer weten? App met recruitment via 06-23364172 of mail naar werkenbij@udenhout.nl.

  • Tussendoor onderhoud en verbeter je bestaande rapportages, zodat de informatievoorziening actueel en betrouwbaar blijft.
  • In samenwerking met de Process Specialist beoordeel je de datakwaliteit en zet je acties uit om processen, software-inrichting en controles verder te verbeteren.
  • Ook bewaak je zorgvuldig de AVG-richtlijnen, onder andere rondom verwerkingsovereenkomsten, data-anonimisering en interne communicatie.
  • In deze rol lever je een zichtbare bijdrage aan een organisatiebreed 360 graden klantbeeld en ondersteun je de bedrijfsvoering met slimme inzichten.


Profiel

  • Minimaal een hbo-opleiding, bij voorkeur in de richting van Business IT & Management, Data Science of vergelijkbaar
  • Minimaal 1 jaar ervaring met het bouwen van rapportages in Power BI
  • Een teamspeler die ook goed zelfstandig werkt
  • Bij voorkeur ervaring met DevOps, Microsoft Azure, DAX en SQL
  • Goede beheersing van de Nederlandse taal
  • In het bezit van rijbewijs B


Wij bieden

  • Een gezellig en compact team met een fijne werksfeer
  • Leuke personeelsuitjes en een actieve personeelsvereniging
  • De mogelijkheid om deel te nemen aan sportieve activiteiten van VDU Fit
  • Een reiskostenvergoeding en pensioenregeling
  • Een collectieve ziektekostenverzekering bij ONVZ/Zilveren Kruis
  • 192 verlofuren op basis van een fulltime dienstverband
  • Gratis bedrijfsfysio
  • Volop doorgroeimogelijkheden binnen de VAG-merken
  • Een persoonlijk opleidingsplan via Pon Academy, passend bij jouw interesses
  • Korting op de aanschaf van occasions, onderhoud en automaterialen


Van den Udenhout als organisatie

Sinds onze oprichting in 1957 is Van den Udenhout uitgegroeid tot een van de grootste en meest vooruitstrevende mobiliteitsorganisaties van Zuid-Nederland. Vanuit onze vestigingen in Noord-Brabant bieden we verkoop en onderhoud van auto’s, aangevuld met mobiliteitsoplossingen zoals verhuur, lease, schadeherstel, bedrijfswageninbouw en (e-)bikes.

Wij zijn De Nieuwe Dealer voor Volkswagen, Audi, SEAT, Škoda, CUPRA en Volkswagen Bedrijfswagens met vestigingen in Best, Boxtel, Breda, Den Bosch, Eindhoven, Halsteren, Oss, Oud Gastel, Roosendaal en Veldhoven. Daarnaast hebben we een belang in diverse Porsche- en Bentley-dealerbedrijven in Brabant en Limburg, waaronder Porsche Centrum Brabant, Porsche Centrum Maastricht en Bentley Maastricht.

Werken bij Van den Udenhout betekent werken in een warme, ambitieuze Brabantse organisatie waar persoonlijke aandacht, groei en passie voor mobiliteit centraal staan. Samen met zo’n 1.000 collega’s bouwen we iedere dag aan de mobiliteit van morgen.

Contactinformatie

Meer weten? App met recruitment via 06-23364172 of mail naar werkenbij@udenhout.nl.

Title: Data Specialist 32 uur - Start People
Description:
Wat je gaat doen

Ben jij analytisch sterk en werk je graag met datasets, slimme automatiseringen en e-commerceplatformen? Als data specialist in Harderwijk speel je een belangrijke rol in het optimaliseren van productdata en commerciële processen. Je werkt met tools zoals KNIME, AI-oplossingen en Business Central, en draagt direct bij aan de kwaliteit van onze webshop en interne systemen. In deze functie krijg je de kans om jouw technische vaardigheden verder te ontwikkelen in een omgeving waar initiatief en datakwaliteit centraal staan.

Wat ga je doen:



Artikeldata importeren, structureren en onderhouden in Navision / Business Central.
Productinformatie verrijken met gegevens uit leveranciersbestanden, 2BA en AI-tools.
Prijsstructuren en klantafspraken controleren en afwijkingen signaleren.
Datakwaliteit bewaken en inconsistenties oplossen voordat ze impact hebben op processen of webshopinformatie.
Actief meedenken over efficiëntere datastructuren, workflows en automatiseringen.



Wat we je bieden



Een bruto maandsalaris tussen €2.800 en €2.900 op basis van een fulltime dienstverband.
Kans op een vast contract bij goed functioneren.
Een werkweek van 32 uur, met flexibele start- en eindtijden na de inwerkperiode.
Mogelijkheid om deels vanuit huis te werken.
Toegang tot interne trainingen en praktijkgerichte opleidingen om jouw vaardigheden verder te ontwikkelen.
Een kleinschalige werkomgeving met korte communicatielijnen en directe impact op e-commerce data.



Bij Start People belonen wij volgens het principe van gelijkwaardige beloning; je arbeidsvoorwaarden sluiten altijd aan bij die van collega’s die rechtstreeks bij de opdrachtgever in dienst zijn. Dit betekent dat naast het uurloon bijvoorbeeld ook het aantal vrije dagen, toeslagen, ADV-vergoedingen en overige looncomponenten onderdeel zijn van je arbeidsvoorwaarden.

Functie-eisen

Je bent analytisch sterk en hebt affiniteit met data en automatisering.



Afgeronde hbo-opleiding in Data Analytics, Informatica of vergelijkbaar.
Ervaring met Excel en data verwerking.
Kennis van ERP-systemen zoals Navision of Business Central is een pré.
Uitstekende beheersing van de Nederlandse taal, zowel mondeling als schriftelijk.
Nauwkeurig, zelfstandig en analytisch ingesteld.



Over het bedrijf

Deze technische groothandel in Harderwijk werkt dagelijks aan het verbeteren van productdata en e-commerceprocessen. Je komt terecht in een compact team waar snel geschakeld wordt en waar ruimte is voor jouw ideeën en initiatief. Daarbij werk je met moderne tools zoals AI en KNIME binnen een praktische dataomgeving.

Klaar om jouw analytische skills in te zetten binnen een functie met directe impact? Solliciteer vandaag nog!

Heb je vragen? Bel ons gerust op 055-5261112. Evalin, Marianne of Tamara helpen je graag verder.

Title: Associate Partner
HTML:
Geassocieerd Partner (associatie nader te bepalen)


Toegepaste Vertraging — Bureau voor Beheerste Traagheid — zoekt, op termijn en zonder al te veel aandrang, een Geassocieerd Partner.


Wij groeien. Langzaam, dat wel, en in een richting die wij nog niet hebben vastgesteld, maar onmiskenbaar. Om die groei waardig te kunnen negeren, breiden wij ons partnerschap uit met iemand die de kunst van het achterwege laten op het hoogste niveau

beheerst.


Over de rol

Als Geassocieerd Partner bent u verbonden aan ons bureau, zonder dat de precieze aard van die verbondenheid ooit wordt uitgesproken. Het woord "geassocieerd" duidt op nabijheid; het woord "partner" op gewicht. Samen vormen zij een functie die indruk maakt en tot niets verplicht — precies zoals het hoort.

U draagt medeverantwoordelijkheid en vertegenwoordigt het bureau bij gelegenheden waar wij niet verschijnen, en denkt op strategisch niveau mee over vraagstukken die wij bewust onbeantwoord laten.


Wat u gaat doen

  • u associeert zich, op gepaste afstand;
  • u opent gesprekken met prospects en laat ze rustig openstaan;
  • u neemt deel aan de Raad van Advies, die nooit bijeenkomt;
  • u draagt de titel met overtuiging, en de inhoud met mate;
  • u doet, in nauw overleg, het nodige niet.


Wat wij zoeken

  • aantoonbare ervaring met het nastreven van bijzonder weinig -tot geen- resultaat;
  • het vermogen partner te zijn zonder ooit volledig partner te worden;
  • een gezonde weerstand tegen daadkracht;
  • discretie omtrent uw exacte takenpakket, dat ook ons niet geheel duidelijk is;
  • u solliciteert bij voorkeur niet; gretigheid geldt bij ons als rode vlag.


Wat wij bieden

  • een titel die op verjaardagen en LinkedIn tot gepaste trots strekt;
  • een werkplek die u nimmer hoeft te bezoeken;
  • doorgroeimogelijkheden naar "Partner" (zonder voorvoegsel), te zijner tijd, mogelijk nooit;
  • een marktconforme vergoeding, uit te keren per niet-gewerkt uur;
  • een directe collega; de Interim-CEO als fractioneel leider op moleculair niveau, die u nooit zult tegenkomen.


Solliciteren

Wordt geassocieerd. Verder verwachten wij niets, en u van ons evenmin.

U kunt zich wenden tot: noreply@toegepastevertraging.com


#Toegepastevertraging #GeassocieerdPartner #StilstandIsContinuïteit #WijKomenErNogOpTerug #Vertraging #Toegepast

Title: Chief of Staff
Description:
Chief of Staff at Blue Health Intelligence




Blue Health Intelligence is building a new category in preventive healthcare and is now looking for a chief of staff that helps us scale from start-up phase to scale-up.




About Blue; most people only get a detailed view of their health when something goes wrong. We believe that should change. Customers complete an extensive, doctor-led health check that measures hundreds of health data points in around one hour. We translate those results into clear insights and practical recommendations, helping people understand their health today and make better decisions for the future.




Our ambition is to create Europe’s leading preventive health platform, combining physical health centres, medical expertise, software and longitudinal health data in one seamless customer experience. We are looking for a Chief of Staff who will work directly with the CEO and leadership team to turn that ambition into execution.




This is a highly hands-on role for someone who can bring structure, pace and financial discipline to a fast-growing company. You will help build the operating system of Blue, manage key financial and company processes and lead the preparation for new locations.




What you will own




Finance, budgeting, cash planning and investor reporting
Company cadence: weekly priorities, KPI tracking, leadership meetings and follow-up
Expansion planning for new centres, starting with the Netherlands and later abroad
Location opening playbooks, timelines, budgets and cross-functional coordination
Structuring our HR ways of working
Strategic projects that do not clearly sit with one function yet




Who we are looking for




3 to 8 years of experience in consulting, finance, scale-ups, venture building, operations or a founder-like role
Very strong analytical and financial skills
Highly structured, but pragmatic enough for an early-stage company
Comfortable switching between strategy and execution
Strong written communication and investor-facing skills
High ownership, low ego, high pace




Why apply?




This role will help build the operating system of Blue. You will help us keep Amsterdam growing and healthy, open the next locations and build the foundation for international scaling. All that, in a fun and hardworking VC backed start-up with strong traction and even higher ambition.

Title: Strategy Analyst at Marktlink
Description:
Strategy Analyst at Marktlink




Requirements*

Over 4 years of experience in top-tier strategy consulting
Proven ability to translate complex analyses into clear, structured narratives
Strong presentation skills, including building executive-level decks
Entrepreneurial selfstarter
Comfortable working directly with C-level stakeholders




For over thirty years, Marktlink has been advising entrepreneurs on M&A and has grown into the market leader in the Dutch SME segment. The next target is even bigger: becoming Europe’s leading M&A advisor by 2030. The Strategic Analyst translates the growth strategy into a narrative forty partners and five hundred employees can follow and act on.




About Marktlink

With over 1,200 completed transactions in three decades, Marktlink has built an unmatched track record in the Dutch SME M&A market. The entrepreneurs it works with are often at significant moments in their professional lives, whether they are selling a business they built, acquiring a new one, or preparing for what comes next. Deals typically range between ten and fifty million euros in value, although deals of all sizes occur.




Marktlink’s growth trajectory has been steep. In just a few years, the scale-up went from a single office outside the Netherlands and one hundred and fifty people to nineteen European offices with a partner group of forty and five hundred employees – numbers that are set to grow substantially as the firm executes its growth ambitions. The targets keep moving: over the next five years, the firm aims to double in revenue, offices, and headcount, and claim the number one position in M&A across Europe.




Alongside its core M&A practice, Marktlink offers Exit Ready trajectories, Transaction Services, Legal, and Debt Advisory. Additionally, in 2020, Marktlink launched Marktlink Capital, a subsidiary that gives entrepreneurs who have sold their businesses the opportunity to invest in private equity and venture capital funds. Earlier this year, Inflexion, one of Europe’s leading mid-market PE firms, acquired a minority stake in Marktlink Capital to back its international expansion.




The growth mindset is also reflected in how Marktlink structures its organization. The firm builds its deal teams almost entirely by hiring graduates and developing them into dealmakers. People are its most important asset, and it treats them accordingly. Those who bring good ideas and make a compelling case for them tend to see those ideas become reality fast. Several of its youngest partners reached that level faster than anyone anticipated, by seizing opportunities and doing excellent work.




Vacancy: Strategic Analyst

Marktlink has grown faster than most companies plan for. Following a scalability assessment, the management team has developed a strategic plan toward 2030 and created the role of Strategic Analyst to support its execution and bring the partner group along in the process.




The ambitious targets can only be met if strategy translates into a narrative that resonates across all levels of the organization. Shaping that narrative is exactly what this position is about.

She/he works closely with CFO Daan Holla as his right hand, managing deliverables end to end, sparring on substance and direction, and presenting the resulting narrative directly to forty partners across twelve countries. The tandem with the CFO is close, but the role itself demands a self-starting attitude and a high level of autonomy and ownership. In addition to the CFO, the Strategic Analyst works closely with the other members of the management team, including the CHRO, CMO, CIO, and the incoming Chief Growth Officer.




The strategic plan is built around three priorities: growing the number of deal teams across Europe, increasing revenue per deal team, and improving the scalability of support functions. Translating those priorities into clear analyses and actionable recommendations is one part of the job. Getting them to stick across a forty-strong partner group spread across Europe is the other. Quarterly partner meetings are the main stage for that, bringing together the full European partner group to align on strategy and performance.Preparing those sessions and making sure the right narrative lands with the right people is where the impact of this role is most visible. That is as much about people as it is about content. Strong stakeholder instincts are essential: knowing who needs to be brought along before something hits the room, and how to navigate effectively across all levels of the organization.




“You’re stepping into a company that is well on its way to becoming the largest M&A player in Europe. As Strategic Analyst, you help build and shape that next chapter and bring the rest of the organization along to make sure everyone is moving in the same direction.” – Daan Holla, CFO




For a consultant ready to trade the consulting world for a business environment, this role offers something most exit opportunities take years to reach: a seat at the table with senior leadership, in a company on the verge of European market leadership. The person who steps in now will help shape what this organization looks like as it scales, and grow with it.




Interested?

Marktlink is working with Top of Minds to fill this vacancy.

Title: Junior pricing analist
Company: BeleuchtungDirekt | WWL World Wide Lighting GmbH
Description:
Functieomschrijving

Job description: Junior Pricing Analyst – World Wide Lighting

We are looking for a motivated Junior Pricing Specialist who is eager to learn, has affinity with data and wants to discover how pricing, strategy, and business growth connect. You will work within an international eCommerce and wholesale environment, where you will support our team in making our pricing structures future-proof.

In this role, you will collaborate closely with marketing, sales and product teams. You will help maintain and optimize pricing structures for 15 countries and 28 webshops, both online and offline. We are not just looking for someone to crunch numbers, but someone who wants to understand pricing from a commercial perspective. You will learn how to gather information from different departments and help translate it into concrete pricing actions.

Where will you be working?

You will be working at World Wide Lighting . World Wide Lighting is one of the largest digital lighting wholesalers in Europe with a fast-paced, no-nonsense mentality. Our goal is to make our customers successful; to get lighting done reliably, save energy and reduce costs, while still creating the perfect lighting solutions. We currently work with approximately 180 enthusiastic colleagues to continuously raise customer satisfaction to a higher level with a personal approach .

Do you also want to make an impact and exert influence on this organization? Then don't wait any longer and get in touch!

What are you going to do?


Develop and Manage Pricing Strategy: Help implement pricing strategies for new products and ensure a logical, consistent price structure across our product families. You will actively contribute to our overall pricing strategy and the continuous improvements of it.
Market & Data Analysis: Gather input from sales and product teams as well as from data analysis. You will combine scraped web data with traditional industry insights to support our pricing decisions.
Maintain Discount & Price Structures: Assist in setting up, maintaining, and updating discount structures within our existing system. You will ensure consistency and help make sure these structures work smoothly across multiple countries.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with a senior pricing analyst focusing on online pricing, as well as the eCommerce team. You will help test pricing implementations and ensure pricing aligns with information gathered from the sales and product teams.
International Coordination: Assist in keeping track of pricing across 15 countries. You will help gather feedback from local international colleagues to understand their market needs and ensure alignment.


Vereisten

Your Profile


Education: HBO/WO working and thinking level, preferably in a business, economics, or commercial field.
Experience: 0–3 years of experience (ecommerce experience is a plus).
Mindset: Commercially minded, curious, and eager to learn how a business operates.
Skills: Good analytical skills and comfortable translating data to insights.
Communication: Strong communication skills; you are a team player who enjoys collaborating with different departments.


What do we offer?


A great entry-level role where you’ll really learn about pricing
Mentorship from experienced colleagues
Plenty of opportunities for personal development through training and coaching
A hybrid working environment (office + home)
A competitive starting salary in line with a junior role
A laptop, pension scheme and training budget


What’s next? Are you the right candidate for this role, or would you like more information about it? Then share your talents with us via the ‘apply’ button below. For further information, please contact Christel van Hoppe on 06-14320007.

 

The application process consists of a telephone interview and two face-to-face interviews.

We currently work with approximately 180 enthusiastic colleagues to continuously raise customer satisfaction to a higher level with a personal approach .

Do you also want to make an impact and exert influence on this organization? Then don't wait any longer and get in touch!

What are you going to do?

  • Develop and Manage Pricing Strategy: Help implement pricing strategies for new products and ensure a logical, consistent price structure across our product families. You will actively contribute to our overall pricing strategy and the continuous improvements of it.
  • Market & Data Analysis: Gather input from sales and product teams as well as from data analysis. You will combine scraped web data with traditional industry insights to support our pricing decisions.
  • Maintain Discount & Price Structures: Assist in setting up, maintaining, and updating discount structures within our existing system. You will ensure consistency and help make sure these structures work smoothly across multiple countries.
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with a senior pricing analyst focusing on online pricing, as well as the eCommerce team. You will help test pricing implementations and ensure pricing aligns with information gathered from the sales and product teams.
  • International Coordination: Assist in keeping track of pricing across 15 countries. You will help gather feedback from local international colleagues to understand their market needs and ensure alignment.

Vereisten

Your Profile

  • Education: HBO/WO working and thinking level, preferably in a business, economics, or commercial field.
  • Experience: 0–3 years of experience (ecommerce experience is a plus).
  • Mindset: Commercially minded, curious, and eager to learn how a business operates.
  • Skills: Good analytical skills and comfortable translating data to insights.
  • Communication: Strong communication skills; you are a team player who enjoys collaborating with different departments.

What do we offer?

  • A great entry-level role where you’ll really learn about pricing
  • Mentorship from experienced colleagues
  • Plenty of opportunities for personal development through training and coaching
  • A hybrid working environment (office + home)
  • A competitive starting salary in line with a junior role
  • A laptop, pension scheme and training budget

What’s next? Are you the right candidate for this role, or would you like more information about it? Then share your talents with us via the ‘apply’ button below. For further information, please contact Christel van Hoppe on 06-14320007.

 

The application process consists of a telephone interview and two face-to-face interviews.

Title: Internship - Agile Team at HEINEKEN's Global Indirect Procurement
Description:
Internship - Agile Team at HEINEKEN’s Global Indirect Procurement

“Exploring how AI & Data analysis can drive efficiency, turn complex problems into intelligent solutions and enhance ways of working, that’s why I enjoy going to my internship!”

The internship

Are you passionate about procurement, data, and digital innovation including AI? Do you get energy from working in cross-functional teams? Join our new to be developed Agile Global Procurement team. HEINEKEN is introducing an Agile procurement team to function as a high-profile inhouse consulting-style and project management team to increase emphasis on strategic thinking, drive procurement excellence and address shifting business priorities delivering at maximum impact and pace.

We are looking for a flexible, hands-on intern who is eager to contribute wherever the team needs support most. Whether it’s building dashboards, diving into data modelling, supporting with increasing efficiency through AI, or assisting with project management and reporting design. You’ll play a key role in setting up the new Agile team and driving procurement excellence. During this internship, you will work on:


Data Insights & Power BI Dashboarding: maintain and enhance Power BI dashboards for performance and category tracking, and develop Excel-based macros and data visualizations for operational insights.
AI Integration & Analytics: identify and prioritize AI opportunities, develop and test AI prompts and tools using Microsoft Copilot, Power Platform, and Azure AI, and support financial data modelling and reporting for Agile managers.
Cross-functional Collaboration: work closely with colleagues across procurement and the business, and support the team to streamline operations tasks based on business needs.


Your new team and workplace

You will be part of the Agile team within Indirects Procurement, based at our Schiphol and Amsterdam offices. During your internship, you will gain experience working in an international environment and be in contact with colleagues across the Globe. This structure focuses on high-impact, dynamic projects and ensures cost-reduction efforts are closely aligned with business needs and prioritised by return on investment. You will receive trust and freedom, so you can work like a real entrepreneur.

During your internship, you’ll have every opportunity to get to know HEINEKEN as an organization, not just within your own team, but across departments. We trust and empower you to work with an entrepreneurial mindset. At HEINEKEN, we love bringing the outside world in and welcome fresh, new ideas with open arms. Of course, we’ll make sure you get the guidance that suits you.

The unique you!

We are looking for someone who is digital savvy, curious about AI, and thrives in a dynamic international environment. You bring the energy, drive and analytical mindset to grow. Do you recognize yourself in the profile below?

We believe everyone has unique talents, which is why we’re looking for you! Energy, drive and the ambition to grow are what make the difference at HEINEKEN. In addition, you bring:


Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s program in Engineering, Data Science, AI or Business.
Digital savvy with expertise in Microsoft Office applications, with particular focus on Excel, Power BI, PowerPoint and Copilot, and curiosity about AI and its practical applications in a business setting.
Programming proficiency in SQL or other programming languages is a plus, as is previous coursework or projects related to machine learning or AI. Proficiency with numbers, modelling and data analytics is essential.


Even if you feel you don’t meet all the criteria, we encourage you to apply. An internship is the perfect opportunity to learn and grow!

Conditions of HEINEKEN


You are enrolled at an educational institution for the entire duration of your internship and can provide proof of enrollment for the current academic year during your onboarding.
In the case of non-EU nationality, you must be registered as an intern with a Dutch educational institution.
In case of EU nationality, you may also be registered as an intern at educational institutions outside the EU.
You reside in the Netherlands for the entire duration of your internship. Please note that HEINEKEN does not provide housing.
You are in possession of a valid citizen service number (BSN).

Our Internship Conditions

During your time as an intern at HEINEKEN, you’ll work in a dynamic environment where growing and learning are at the heart of everything we do. Together, we brew your future step by step. In addition, we offer you:


A full-time practice-oriented internship, starting on August / September 1, 2026
The duration of the internship is 5 or 6 months (to be agreed upon with your internship supervisor)
Your home office will be located in The Base, Schiphol, Amsterdam
At HEINEKEN, we work with a hybrid model, typically spending three days a week at the office and two days working from home
You will receive a monthly internship allowance of €650 gross based on a 40-hour workweek
You will earn 2 vacation days per month (based on a full-time internship)
If you do not have a public transport student subscription, your commuting costs will be fully reimbursed


Interested?

You can easily apply via the green “Apply Now” button. In addition to your CV, please include a motivation letter with your application, as we are very curious to hear your story and why this internship appeals to you. The hiring team will get back to you as soon as possible, but please note that the timeline may vary per internship vacancy.

Bring your true self

We know AI is part of today’s world, and chances are, you might use it while preparing your application. That’s absolutely fine. Just remember, we’re genuinely curious about you.

So feel free to use AI as a sparring partner, but make sure your application reflects your own ideas, personality and motivation. That’s what we value most and what helps you stand out.

If you have any questions about the vacancy, feel free to reach out via [email protected].

Working at HEINEKEN

Every day we work with pride on our great products that you have certainly had in your hand at a party, during a work meeting or at home on the couch. HEINEKEN is much more than just beer. What about brands like Heineken 0.0, Apple Bandit, Amstel Radler and Birra Moretti. We actively work to create a pleasant work environment where everyone feels respected and has equal opportunities. Your personality, knowledge and skills are the success of our organization.

Want to know more about our company? Click here.

Curious what working at HEINEKEN looks like? Have a peek on our Instagram or Facebook!

Title: Business Analyst (Creatio)
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Business Analyst (Creatio) based in Netherlands.

This role is an excellent opportunity for a business analyst with experience in CRM and business process management platforms to contribute to impactful digital transformation initiatives. You will act as a key link between business stakeholders and technical teams, helping translate business needs into scalable solutions using the Creatio platform. Working in a collaborative and agile environment, you will support the full project lifecycle, from requirements gathering and process analysis to testing, deployment, and user adoption. The position offers exposure to low-code and no-code technologies, workflow automation, and process optimization projects. You will play a central role in improving operational efficiency, enhancing user experiences, and driving successful platform implementations while working alongside multidisciplinary teams.

Accountabilities


Collaborate with stakeholders to gather, analyze, and document business, functional, and system requirements.
Evaluate and model business processes, identifying opportunities for optimization and automation through the Creatio platform.
Configure and customize Creatio applications using low-code and no-code tools to meet business objectives.
Support projects throughout the entire lifecycle, including requirements analysis, solution design, testing, deployment, and post-implementation support.
Create detailed user stories, acceptance criteria, process documentation, and test scenarios to ensure successful delivery.
Work closely with developers, quality assurance specialists, and solution architects to align business needs with technical solutions.
Facilitate user acceptance testing (UAT), collect feedback, and support end-user training initiatives.
Maintain accurate system documentation, process guides, and knowledge base materials.
Promote best practices related to no-code development, workflow automation, and effective use of the Creatio ecosystem.


Requirements


Minimum 2 years of experience as a Business Analyst, ideally within CRM, BPM, or enterprise application environments.
At least 1 year of hands-on experience working with the Creatio platform, including CRM or Studio solutions.
Strong understanding of business process management concepts, BPMN methodologies, CRM principles, and workflow automation.
Proven experience gathering requirements, conducting workshops, creating specifications, and managing stakeholder expectations.
Familiarity with Agile and Scrum methodologies and participation in cross-functional project teams.
Basic knowledge of low-code development principles and Creatio configuration capabilities.
Excellent communication, collaboration, facilitation, and stakeholder management skills.
Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving abilities, and attention to detail.
Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Information Systems, Computer Science, or a related discipline.
Additional advantages include Creatio certification, experience with integrations using REST APIs or OData, knowledge of PostgreSQL or MS SQL databases, data modeling expertise, and familiarity with reporting or data visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau.


Benefits


Flexible work arrangement with the option to work remotely or from the office.
Fully remote onboarding process.
Performance-based bonus programs available annually or quarterly, depending on role eligibility.
Access to continuous learning opportunities through internal training resources, company libraries, and partner-led educational programs.
Comprehensive health and life insurance coverage.
Wellbeing initiatives, including access to professional psychological support.
Mobile communication expense reimbursement.
Opportunity to work on innovative CRM, BPM, and low-code transformation projects.
Collaborative and supportive environment that encourages professional growth and knowledge sharing.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst (m/w/d)
Description:
Wer wir sind


Wir machen Wünsche machbar – unsere Mission ist es, das Leben unserer Kund:innen zu bereichern und mit unseren Finanzdienstleistungen Träume zu verwirklichen. Wir verstehen uns nicht als typische Bank, sondern als lebendige Gemeinschaft von Gestalter:innen, die keine Angst davor haben, neue Wege zu gehen. Dabei setzen wir uns alle auch noch für Themen mit Impact wie Nachhaltigkeit und Digitalisierung ein – ob in unserer Zentrale in Stuttgart, an unserem Standort in Offenbach oder deutschlandweit in unseren 21 Filialen.

Bei der Creditplus denken wir größer, denn wir profitieren von Synergien mit unseren (inter)nationalen Schwestereinheiten und Business Units unter dem Dach der Crédit Agricole. Du siehst, trotzdem mögen wir es familiär: Mit Wertschätzung und Teamspirit arbeiten wir Hand in Hand für die Innovation von Finanzdienstleistungen. Und feiern gemeinsam die großen und kleinen Erfolge. Tschüss eintöniger Arbeitsalltag. Hallo machbar.

Für unseren Bereich Produktmanagement in Stuttgart suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt eine/n Data Analyst (m/w/d) in Vollzeit.

Im Produktmanagement der Creditplus gestalten wir Bankingprodukte von Grund auf neu – datengetrieben, kundenzentriert und wirkungsorientiert. In interdisziplinären Teams begleiten wir Produkte über ihren gesamten Lebenszyklus: von der Idee über Priorisierung und Umsetzung bis zur messbaren Wirkung im Markt.

Daten spielen dabei eine Schlüsselrolle: Sie machen Nutzung, Performance und Kundennutzen sichtbar und bilden die Grundlage für fundierte Produkt- und Managemententscheidungen. Hier entsteht Wirkung mit dem Anspruch, Banking jeden Tag ein Stück besser zu machen.



Woran du arbeitest

Konzeption und Aufbau eines skalierbaren Product-Measurement- und Performance-Frameworks für neue Produkte – von KPI-Modell über Event-Tracking bis hin zu Dashboards und Entscheidungslogiken
Erstellung und Weiterentwicklung regelmäßiger Reports zu Nutzung, Performance, Conversion, Retention und Cross-Selling von Produkten
Analyse von Produkt- und Kundendaten zur Identifikation von Performance-Treibern, Auffälligkeiten und Optimierungspotenzialen
Aufbereitung fundierter Analysen zu Pricing, Funnel-Performance, Feature-Nutzung und Kundenverhalten für Produkt Management und Management
Dokumentation und Harmonisierung von Datenquellen, Berechnungslogiken und Reporting-Annahmen
Identifikation von Datenlücken, Inkonsistenzen und Tracking-Themen sowie Abstimmung mit Data-, IT- und Produkt-Stakeholdern
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Product Managern und relevanten Schnittstellen zur Übersetzung fachlicher Fragestellungen in analytische Ansätze und Entscheidungsgrundlagen
Unterstützung bei Management-Auswertungen, Ad hoc Analysen und Deep Dives zur Beantwortung konkreter Produktfragen, sowie bei Mess- und Reportinganforderungen für neue Produkte oder Produktänderungen


Was du mitbringst

Abgeschlossenes Studium in BWL VWL, Finance, Statistik, Data Analytics, Wirtschaftsinformatik oder vergleichbar
3 – 5 Jahre Berufserfahrung in Product Analytics, Commercial Analytics, Business Intelligence oder einer vergleichbaren analytischen Rolle
Fundierte Erfahrung in Produktanalyse, Businessanalyse oder Performance Reporting in einem digitalen Umfeld
Sicherer Umgang mit SQL sowie Erfahrung mit BI- und Dashboarding-Tools (z. B. Qlik Sense, Power BI, Tableau, Looker o. ä.)
Sehr gutes Verständnis relevanter Produkt-KPIs wie Adoption, Conversion, Retention, Pricing Performance und Cross-Sell
Erfahrung in der Strukturierung von Datenquellen, Definition von KPI-Logiken sowie im sorgfältigen Umgang mit Reporting- und Governance-Anforderungen
Ausgeprägtes analytisches Denkvermögen, hohe Detailgenauigkeit, Verlässlichkeit und die Fähigkeit, komplexe Daten in umsetzbare Produkt-Insights zu übersetzen
Verhandlungssichere Deutschkenntnisse sowie sehr gute Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift


Worauf du dich freuen kannst

30 Tage Urlaub und nicht zu vergessen: die zusätzlichen zwei Bankfeiertage am 24. und 31. Dezember
Attraktives Gehaltspaket: 13 Gehälter, Jahresbonus, vermögenswirksame Leistungen
Räumliche Flexibilität: bis zu 60% mobiles Arbeiten sowie Workation
Zuschuss zum Deutschlandticket
Early Bird oder Nachteule? Flexible Arbeitszeiten mit attraktivem Gleitzeitmodell
Sinnstiftend: unser soziales und grünes Engagement im Alltag manifestiert sich in unserer Arbeit an zahlreichen wertvollen ESG- und CSR-Projekten für ein besseres Zusammenleben in Umwelt und Gesellschaft
Work & Life: mit unserem Partner voiio (Employee Assistance Program) unterstützen wir dich in Beruf und Privatleben. Von Alltagsfragen bis Notfallhilfe - hier findest du alles von Coaching, Vermittlung, Live-Events und noch viele weitere tolle Angebote
Ob Soft Skills oder fachliches Know-how: wir investieren mit dir in dein Development mit bedarfsgerechten Trainings, Konferenzen, LinkedIn Learning und vielem mehr
Gesundheit: unsere Mitarbeitenden sind uns wichtig - deshalb unterstützen wir ihre physische und psychische Gesundheit mit verschiedenen Gesundheitsangeboten wie z.B. Vorsorgeuntersuchungen und Gesundheitstagen
Vor der Arbeit, in der Pause oder nach Feierabend: Trainiere kostenlos im Fitnessstudio auf unserem Firmengelände
Für unser Wir: Freue dich auf Sommerfest, Weihnachtsfeier und Teamevents
Attraktive Mitarbeitervorteile: Zuschuss zur gesunden Mitarbeiterverpflegung im Büro, kostenloses Wasser, Kaffee & Tee, Vitaminkick: regelmäßiger Obst- und Gemüsekorb, Fahrradleasing, Mitarbeiter werben Mitarbeiter, Corporate Benefits, Mitarbeiterdarlehen
Betriebliche Altersversorgung und eine Beteiligung an Aktien zu Sonderkursen


Wir freuen uns auf dich


Creditplus Bank AG




People & Culture
Frau Theresa Häcker
Augustenstraße 7
70178 Stuttgart

Unsere Recruiterin Theresa Häcker gibt dir unter extern.THaecker@creditplus.de auch gerne Vorabinformationen.

Du siehst, trotzdem mögen wir es familiär: Mit Wertschätzung und Teamspirit arbeiten wir Hand in Hand für die Innovation von Finanzdienstleistungen. Und feiern gemeinsam die großen und kleinen Erfolge. Tschüss eintöniger Arbeitsalltag. Hallo machbar.

Für unseren Bereich Produktmanagement in Stuttgart suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt eine/n Data Analyst (m/w/d) in Vollzeit.

Im Produktmanagement der Creditplus gestalten wir Bankingprodukte von Grund auf neu – datengetrieben, kundenzentriert und wirkungsorientiert. In interdisziplinären Teams begleiten wir Produkte über ihren gesamten Lebenszyklus: von der Idee über Priorisierung und Umsetzung bis zur messbaren Wirkung im Markt.

Daten spielen dabei eine Schlüsselrolle: Sie machen Nutzung, Performance und Kundennutzen sichtbar und bilden die Grundlage für fundierte Produkt- und Managemententscheidungen. Hier entsteht Wirkung mit dem Anspruch, Banking jeden Tag ein Stück besser zu machen.



Woran du arbeitest
  • Konzeption und Aufbau eines skalierbaren Product-Measurement- und Performance-Frameworks für neue Produkte – von KPI-Modell über Event-Tracking bis hin zu Dashboards und Entscheidungslogiken
  • Erstellung und Weiterentwicklung regelmäßiger Reports zu Nutzung, Performance, Conversion, Retention und Cross-Selling von Produkten
  • Analyse von Produkt- und Kundendaten zur Identifikation von Performance-Treibern, Auffälligkeiten und Optimierungspotenzialen
  • Aufbereitung fundierter Analysen zu Pricing, Funnel-Performance, Feature-Nutzung und Kundenverhalten für Produkt Management und Management
  • Dokumentation und Harmonisierung von Datenquellen, Berechnungslogiken und Reporting-Annahmen
  • Identifikation von Datenlücken, Inkonsistenzen und Tracking-Themen sowie Abstimmung mit Data-, IT- und Produkt-Stakeholdern
  • Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Product Managern und relevanten Schnittstellen zur Übersetzung fachlicher Fragestellungen in analytische Ansätze und Entscheidungsgrundlagen
  • Unterstützung bei Management-Auswertungen, Ad hoc Analysen und Deep Dives zur Beantwortung konkreter Produktfragen, sowie bei Mess- und Reportinganforderungen für neue Produkte oder Produktänderungen


Was du mitbringst
  • Abgeschlossenes Studium in BWL VWL, Finance, Statistik, Data Analytics, Wirtschaftsinformatik oder vergleichbar
  • 3 – 5 Jahre Berufserfahrung in Product Analytics, Commercial Analytics, Business Intelligence oder einer vergleichbaren analytischen Rolle
  • Fundierte Erfahrung in Produktanalyse, Businessanalyse oder Performance Reporting in einem digitalen Umfeld
  • Sicherer Umgang mit SQL sowie Erfahrung mit BI- und Dashboarding-Tools (z. B. Qlik Sense, Power BI, Tableau, Looker o. ä.)
  • Sehr gutes Verständnis relevanter Produkt-KPIs wie Adoption, Conversion, Retention, Pricing Performance und Cross-Sell
  • Erfahrung in der Strukturierung von Datenquellen, Definition von KPI-Logiken sowie im sorgfältigen Umgang mit Reporting- und Governance-Anforderungen
  • Ausgeprägtes analytisches Denkvermögen, hohe Detailgenauigkeit, Verlässlichkeit und die Fähigkeit, komplexe Daten in umsetzbare Produkt-Insights zu übersetzen
  • Verhandlungssichere Deutschkenntnisse sowie sehr gute Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift


Worauf du dich freuen kannst
  • 30 Tage Urlaub und nicht zu vergessen: die zusätzlichen zwei Bankfeiertage am 24. und 31. Dezember
  • Attraktives Gehaltspaket: 13 Gehälter, Jahresbonus, vermögenswirksame Leistungen
  • Räumliche Flexibilität: bis zu 60% mobiles Arbeiten sowie Workation
  • Zuschuss zum Deutschlandticket
  • Early Bird oder Nachteule? Flexible Arbeitszeiten mit attraktivem Gleitzeitmodell
  • Sinnstiftend: unser soziales und grünes Engagement im Alltag manifestiert sich in unserer Arbeit an zahlreichen wertvollen ESG- und CSR-Projekten für ein besseres Zusammenleben in Umwelt und Gesellschaft
  • Work & Life: mit unserem Partner voiio (Employee Assistance Program) unterstützen wir dich in Beruf und Privatleben. Von Alltagsfragen bis Notfallhilfe - hier findest du alles von Coaching, Vermittlung, Live-Events und noch viele weitere tolle Angebote
  • Ob Soft Skills oder fachliches Know-how: wir investieren mit dir in dein Development mit bedarfsgerechten Trainings, Konferenzen, LinkedIn Learning und vielem mehr
  • Gesundheit: unsere Mitarbeitenden sind uns wichtig - deshalb unterstützen wir ihre physische und psychische Gesundheit mit verschiedenen Gesundheitsangeboten wie z.B. Vorsorgeuntersuchungen und Gesundheitstagen
  • Vor der Arbeit, in der Pause oder nach Feierabend: Trainiere kostenlos im Fitnessstudio auf unserem Firmengelände
  • Für unser Wir: Freue dich auf Sommerfest, Weihnachtsfeier und Teamevents
  • Attraktive Mitarbeitervorteile: Zuschuss zur gesunden Mitarbeiterverpflegung im Büro, kostenloses Wasser, Kaffee & Tee, Vitaminkick: regelmäßiger Obst- und Gemüsekorb, Fahrradleasing, Mitarbeiter werben Mitarbeiter, Corporate Benefits, Mitarbeiterdarlehen

Title: Data Analyst (m/w/d) Sales
Description:
Wer wir sind


Wir machen Wünsche machbar – unsere Mission ist es, das Leben unserer Kund:innen zu bereichern und mit unseren Finanzdienstleistungen Träume zu verwirklichen. Wir verstehen uns nicht als typische Bank, sondern als lebendige Gemeinschaft von Gestalter:innen, die keine Angst davor haben, neue Wege zu gehen. Dabei setzen wir uns alle auch noch für Themen mit Impact wie Nachhaltigkeit und Digitalisierung ein – ob in unserer Zentrale in Stuttgart, an unserem Standort in Offenbach oder deutschlandweit in unseren 21 Filialen.




Bei der Creditplus denken wir größer, denn wir profitieren von Synergien mit unseren (inter)nationalen Schwestereinheiten und Business Units unter dem Dach der Crédit Agricole. Und feiern gemeinsam die großen und kleinen Erfolge. Tschüss eintöniger Arbeitsalltag. Hallo machbar. Du bringst Berufserfahrung in der Finanzdienstleistungsbranche oder im Vertrieb mit und jetzt hast du Lust die Führung eines Teams zu übernehmen? Dann haben wir genau die richtige Position für dich!

Für unsere Abteilung Partnermanagement in Stuttgart suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Termin ein Data Analyst (m/w/d) Sales. 

Du bist das strategische Bindeglied zwischen Data Science, Risk und dem Vertrieb. Deine Mission ist es, durch die intelligente Analyse von Pricing-Strukturen und die präzise Steuerung von Vertriebskanälen (Omnichannel) die Profitabilität und das Wachstum unseres Kreditgeschäfts nachhaltig zu steigern.



Woran du arbeitest

Überwachung und Analyse des Risk-Based Pricing für Konsumentenkredite
Analyse und Steuerung des Routings zwischen Online-Anfragen und Filialgeschäft
Entwicklung von Handlungsempfehlungen um das Neu- sowie Bestandsgeschäft zu steuern
Erstellung von Reportings und Performance-Analysen
Mitarbeit in nationalen und internationalen Projekten


Was du mitbringst

Abgeschlossenes Studium der Wirtschaftswissenschaft, Mathematik, Statistik, Informatik oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation mit starkem Fokus auf Analytik
Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung in einer ähnlichen Schnittstellenfunktion - idealerweise im Finanzdienstleistungssektor
Fundiertes Wissen über Bankprodukte Kredite, Zinsrechnung und idealerweise Risk-Based-Pricing Mechanismen
Kommunikationsstärke und Teamgeist
Lösungsorientierte und durchsetzungsfähige Arbeitsweise
Sicherer Umgang mit MS-Office, SQL, SAS und BI-Software
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse

30 Tage Urlaub und nicht zu vergessen: die zusätzlichen zwei Bankfeiertage am 24. und 31. Dezember

Ob Soft Skills oder fachliches Know-how: wir investieren mit dir in dein Development mit bedarfsgerechten Trainings, Konferenzen, LinkedIn Learning und vielem mehr

Title: Financial Data Analyst
Description:
Job Number: R0242500

Financial Data Analyst

The Opportunity:

As a Financial Data Analyst, you’ll bring your technical, analytical, and operational support expertise to work alongside thought leaders in data analytics, digital engineering, and financial management on projects supporting operations across EUCOM and AFRICOM Areas of Responsibility. You’ll use your skills and expertise to integrate, analyze, and improve financial data processes and systems that enable mission execution.

Work with us to identify inefficiencies, improve fiscal data workflows, and deliver actionable insights that enhance decision-making, continuity, and operational effectiveness across complex defense financial systems.

This position supports an essential financial data environment and is structured to encompass two skill sets under one role. These include process and change management and program support, driving process improvement, supporting continuity, developing SOPs, helping stabilize and mature financial workflows and reporting, data engineering, and technical analytics supporting data integration, transformation, and analysis across structured and unstructured financial data sources and enabling scalable analytics and reporting. To be successful in this role, you should be comfortable operating in a collaborative environment.

What You’ll Work On:


Lead or support change management efforts and workflow improvements.
Support continuity of operations by maintaining institutional knowledge, SOPs, and process documentation.
Support data validation, quality checks, and compliance-focused financial analysis.
Collaborate with stakeholders to improve reporting processes and data usability.
Support data transformation, automation, and visualization efforts.
Integrate and analyze financial data from multiple systems to ensure accuracy and completeness.
Compile, review, and format fiscal data extracts and reports for leadership awareness.
Identify trends, recurring issues, and process inefficiencies within financial data workflows.



Join us. The world can’t wait.

You Have:


Experience with data exploration, data cleaning, data analysis, data visualization, or data mining
Experience working with structured and unstructured data sources
Knowledge of financial data workflows, reporting processes, or fiscal data management
Ability to identify trends, inconsistencies, and process inefficiencies within datasets
Ability to communicate analytical findings clearly to technical and non-technical stakeholders
Secret clearance
Bachelor's degree



Nice If You Have:


Experience supporting Department of Defense (DoD) financial systems, data platforms, or analytics environments
Experience with programming or scripting languages such as Python, R, or SQL for data analysis
Knowledge of data engineering concepts such as ETL pipelines, data integration, and automation
Knowledge of process improvement or change management methodologies
Ability to develop dashboards or data visualizations with tools such as Power BI or Tableau



Clearance:

Applicants selected will be subject to a security investigation and may need to meet eligibility requirements for access to classified information; Secret clearance is required.

Compensation

At Booz Allen, we celebrate your contributions, provide you with opportunities and choices, and support your total well-being. Our offerings include health, life, disability, financial, and retirement benefits, as well as paid leave, professional development, tuition assistance, work-life programs, and dependent care. Our recognition awards program acknowledges employees for exceptional performance and superior demonstration of our values. Full-time and part-time employees working at least 20 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in Booz Allen’s benefit programs. Individuals that do not meet the threshold are only eligible for select offerings, not inclusive of health benefits. We encourage you to learn more about our total benefits by visiting the Resource page on our Careers site and reviewing Our Employee Benefits page.

Salary at Booz Allen is determined by various factors, including but not limited to location, the individual’s particular combination of education, knowledge, skills, competencies, and experience, as well as contract-specific affordability and organizational requirements. The projected compensation range for this position is $99,000.00 to $225,000.00 (annualized USD). The estimate displayed represents the typical salary range for this position and is just one component of Booz Allen’s total compensation package for employees. This posting will close within 90 days from the Posting Date.

Identity Statement

As part of the hiring process, we will ask you to complete an identity verification process that leverages advanced biometrics and artificial intelligence to ensure authenticity and protect against identity fraud. You are expected to be on camera during interviews and assessments. We reserve the right to take your picture to verify your identity and prevent fraud.

Candidate AI Usage Policy

AI is a part of our daily work at Booz Allen, and we are committed to the responsible and ethical use of AI tools. However, we want to ensure a fair candidate process based on your own skills and knowledge. As part of this commitment, the use of artificial intelligence (AI) or other tools to assist with responses during interviews (whether in-person or virtual) is prohibited unless permission is explicitly provided.

Work Model

Our people-first culture prioritizes the benefits of collaboration, whether it occurs in person or virtually. To support engagement and effective communication, employees working virtually are generally expected to have their cameras on during meetings.


Remote: If this position is listed as remote, there may still be occasions when you are required to work in person at a Booz Allen or customer facility.
Hybrid: If this position is listed as hybrid, you will be expected to work from a Booz Allen facility frequently, in alignment with leadership expectations and the needs of the role. You may also be required to work from or visit a customer facility.
Onsite: If this position is listed as onsite, work will primarily be performed at a Booz Allen office or customer facility, where employees will collaborate directly with colleagues and customers as required by the role.



Commitment to Non-Discrimination

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to disability, status as a protected veteran or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, local, or international law.

Title: Sustainability Data Analyst (f-m-d)
Description:
Techtronic Industries (TTI) is a world leader in cordless technology spanning power tools, accessories, hand tools, outdoor power equipment, as well as floorcare & cleaning products. Our focus is on end-users that range from professionals in the industrial, construction and infrastructure sectors to DIYers in home improvement, repair, and maintenance. TTI’s powerful brand portfolio includes MILWAUKEE®, RYOBI®, AEG® - recognized worldwide for their deep heritage and innovative product platforms of superior quality.

The company maintains a global manufacturing and product development footprint, with record world-wide sales of approximately US$15.2 billion and around 50,000 employees in 2026. Hiring exceptional people is a top focus at TTI. This drives a high-performance culture across all levels of our organization and helps to achieve our vision of being number one in the industries we serve. Our unique high-speed decision-making process supports our acute focus on developing superior products and meeting high customer expectations. We view our winning culture as a competitive advantage that powers our growth.

Key Responsibilities:


Collaborate with business stakeholders and leadership to gather requirements, develop, and support data modelling and BI solutions for organizational stakeholders
Integrate different data sources into a cohesive data fabric, ensuring data consistency, accuracy and reliability
Conduct business analysis activities to understand business user requirements and translate data and information flows within business processes
Analyze sustainability, quality & regulatory data to identify trends, patterns and areas for improvement
Provide insights and recommendations based on data analysis to support informed decision-making in sustainability initiatives
Perform data profiling, validation and cleaning to ensure accuracy, completeness, and quality of enterprise data
Maintain data quality standards and enforce data governance policies, standards and best practices
Maintain clear documentation for data orchestrations, highlighting their value and contribution to improved data modeling and governance


Requirements:


Bachelor's or similar degree in Data Science, Environmental Science, Sustainability Management, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience
Proven experience with Power BI, Power Automate, VBA, C++ programming, Python, and SAP QuickViewer
Good communication skills in German and English, both written and spoken
Knowledge of Qlik Sense and IBM Envizi is considered an advantage
Relevant experience in data analysis, reporting, and data visualization


#TTI-EMEA

At TTI, we are committed to being an equal opportunity employer. We believe in creating a supportive environment where everyone can thrive and grow. Techtronic Industries (TTI) is a world leader in cordless technology spanning power tools, accessories, hand tools, outdoor power equipment, as well as floorcare & cleaning products. Our focus is on end-users that range from professionals in the industrial, construction and infrastructure sectors to DIYers in home improvement, repair, and maintenance. TTI’s powerful brand portfolio includes MILWAUKEE®, RYOBI®, AEG® - recognized worldwide for their deep heritage and innovative product platforms of superior quality.

The company maintains a global manufacturing and product development footprint, with record world-wide sales of approximately US$15.2 billion and around 50,000 employees in 2026. Hiring exceptional people is a top focus at TTI. This drives a high-performance culture across all levels of our organization and helps to achieve our vision of being number one in the industries we serve. Our unique high-speed decision-making process supports our acute focus on developing superior products and meeting high customer expectations. We view our winning culture as a competitive advantage that powers our growth.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Collaborate with business stakeholders and leadership to gather requirements, develop, and support data modelling and BI solutions for organizational stakeholders
  • Integrate different data sources into a cohesive data fabric, ensuring data consistency, accuracy and reliability
  • Conduct business analysis activities to understand business user requirements and translate data and information flows within business processes
  • Analyze sustainability, quality & regulatory data to identify trends, patterns and areas for improvement
  • Provide insights and recommendations based on data analysis to support informed decision-making in sustainability initiatives
  • Perform data profiling, validation and cleaning to ensure accuracy, completeness, and quality of enterprise data
  • Maintain data quality standards and enforce data governance policies, standards and best practices
  • Maintain clear documentation for data orchestrations, highlighting their value and contribution to improved data modeling and governance

Requirements:

  • Bachelor's or similar degree in Data Science, Environmental Science, Sustainability Management, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience
  • Proven experience with Power BI, Power Automate, VBA, C++ programming, Python, and SAP QuickViewer
  • Good communication skills in German and English, both written and spoken
  • Knowledge of Qlik Sense and IBM Envizi is considered an advantage
  • Relevant experience in data analysis, reporting, and data visualization

Title: Sr Data Analyst/Content Manager
Description:
Job Description


BAE Systems is looking for an experienced Senior Data Analyst/Regional Content Manager excited to provide vital analysis in support of the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency mission. The ideal candidate will perform Regional Content Management (RCM) and support to Combatant Commands and NGA Support Teams with dissemination of Source Foundation in Maps, Charts & Geodesy, commercial GEOINT, and commercial data. The candidate shall provide data and content analysis services as described below.



Required Education, Experience, & Skills


12+ years of experience with performing Regional Content Management (RCM) support to:


Provide customer support with dissemination of Source Foundation GEOINT consisting of Maps, Charts & Geodesy, commercial GEOINT, and commercial data.
Provide on-site and virtual instructions to mission partners on how to use online commercial GEOINT, and commercial data dissemination services.
Advise customers on various methods for data delivery to include the use of digital or electronic transfer i.e., Enterprise File Delivery (EFD) System, and use of media (CDs, DVDs, Blue-Ray discs, hard drives, NAS units, etc.) and online self-service tools. Assess and document assigned content profile and associated workflows and storage and dissemination architecture. Provide recommendations on opportunities to improve the Combatant Command’s profile.
Provide data dissemination support to include hard-drives, EFD, and other dissemination methods to support mission partner capabilities to receive, store and organize GEOINT data on local file servers.
Serve as a Data Transfer Officer transferring data between networked computer systems and approved removable media.
Perform management, data manipulation and analysis to assess integrity, identify patterns, and determine and correct shortfalls
Develop and document data management and dissemination workflows, and transfer data between multiple formats, and provide advanced data visualization.
Develop and apply methods to identify, collect, process, and analyze large volumes of data to build and enhance GEONT processes, and systems.
Work with ESRI Portal technology to develop and maintain map services, source governance documents including but not limited to Strategic Implementation Plan, Concept of Operations (CONOPS), Communications Plan, and customer strategy.




Preferred Education, Experience, & Skills


Bachelor's Degree and 12+ years work experience or equivalent experience


Pay Information

Full-Time Salary Range: $107359 - $182510

Please note: This range is based on our market pay structures. However, individual salaries are determined by a variety of factors including, but not limited to: business considerations, local market conditions, and internal equity, as well as candidate qualifications, such as skills, education, and experience.

Employee Benefits: At BAE Systems, we support our employees in all aspects of their life, including their health and financial well-being. Regular employees scheduled to work 20+ hours per week are offered: health, dental, and vision insurance; health savings accounts; a 401(k) savings plan; disability coverage; and life and accident insurance. We also have an employee assistance program, a legal plan, and other perks including discounts on things like home, auto, and pet insurance. Our leave programs include paid time off, paid holidays, as well as other types of leave, including paid parental, military, bereavement, and any applicable federal and state sick leave. Employees may participate in the company recognition program to receive monetary or non-monetary recognition awards. Other incentives may be available based on position level and/or job specifics.



About BAE Systems Intelligence & Security


BAE Systems, Inc. is the U.S. subsidiary of BAE Systems plc, an international defense, aerospace and security company which delivers a full range of products and services for air, land and naval forces, as well as advanced electronics, security, information technology solutions and customer support services. Improving the future and protecting lives is an ambitious mission, but it’s what we do at BAE Systems. Working here means using your passion and ingenuity where it counts – defending national security with breakthrough technology, superior products, and intelligence solutions. As you develop the latest technology and defend national security, you will continually hone your skills on a team—making a big impact on a global scale. At BAE Systems, you’ll find a rewarding career that truly makes a difference.

Intelligence & Security (I&S), based in McLean, Virginia, designs and delivers advanced defense, intelligence, and security solutions that support the important missions of our customers. Our pride and dedication shows in everything we do—from intelligence analysis, cyber operations and IT expertise to systems development, systems integration, and operations and maintenance services. Knowing that our work enables the U.S. military and government to recognize, manage and defeat threats inspires us to push ourselves and our technologies to new levels.

This position will be posted for at least 5 calendar days. The posting will remain active until the position is filled, or a qualified pool of candidates is identified.
