Title: Data Analyst - Business Intelligence
Description:
Job Description



WHAT YOU’LL DO​



As a Business Intelligence Analyst within Central Business Analytics at H&M Group, you will play a key role in transforming data into accessible, impactful insights for decision-makers across the organization. Working close to the core business, you will help shape and scale H&M’s analytical toolkit by building intuitive, reliable, and business‑relevant BI solutions that empower thousands of users globally.



In this role, you combine strong technical BI skills with a genuine curiosity for the business. You will collaborate closely with stakeholders across functions to understand their analytical needs and translate them into clear, actionable reporting and datasets that support better, faster decisions.



To succeed in this role, you will…​



Build, maintain, and continuously improve reports, data models, and BI solutions using the Microsoft Power BI platform.
Work closely with business stakeholders to understand their questions, challenges, and priorities, ensuring insights delivered have real business impact.
Transform large and complex datasets into clear, user‑friendly visualizations and data stories.
Collaborate with IT and data platform teams to ensure the data environment supports scalable and reliable BI solutions.
Contribute to best practices, knowledge sharing, and capability building within the BI and data community.





WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH​



Business Analytics is a central unit working closely with Group Controlling, Strategy, and Business Development functions. Our mission is to deliver relevant and reliable decision support that accelerates H&M’s strategic plans and improves our decision making. Our function plays a key role in enabling fast, data-driven, and trustworthy insights that support H&M Group’s top priorities.







WHO YOU ARE​



We are looking for people with…​



A degree in a quantitative field such as engineering, business, economics, or statistics.
Experience in data, insights, or BI roles, in-house or as a consultant.
A genuine interest in creating business value through data and analytics.
Experience working with large datasets and complex analytical problems.
Strong skills in Power BI (or similar tools), including data modelling and visualization.
Good knowledge of SQL for querying and transforming data.
A passion for data visualization and clear data storytelling.



And people who are…​



Self‑driven, structured, and pragmatic in their way of working.
Communicative team players who enjoy collaborating with diverse stakeholders.
Curious and eager to learn, actively following developments in BI and analytics.
Motivated by making insights accessible and useful for others.
Aligned with H&M’s values, with a passion for growth, innovation, and creating customer value.​

WHO WE ARE​



H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​ We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​



​



WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE ​

At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​



​All our employees are included in our H&M Incentive Program (HIP)
All our employees receive a staff discount card, usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online. Brands covered by the discount are H&M (Beauty and Move included), COS, Weekday, H&M HOME, & Other Stories, ARKET.
A dynamic, entrepreneurial environment where your insights directly influence strategic decisions.
A collaborative culture that values innovation, continuous learning, and knowledge sharing.





JOIN US​



Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.



The role is based in our office in Stockholm, Sweden. Apply by sending in your CV in English as soon as possible. Due to data policies, we only accept applications through our career page.



Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here. We are eager to meet you!​





*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.​

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Want to influence the bigger picture? At Saab, you can realise amazing innovations that keep people and societies safe. Together, we can truly make an impact – and your part matters.

Your role

Operational Development within Procurement, SAAB Surveillance, is in a strong transformation phase where data, analytics, and digital tools are key enablers to improve efficiency, quality, and performance across procurement operations. We are now looking for a Data Analyst, who will be part of the Operational Development team supporting Procurement in Surveillance.

In this role, you will work closely with procurement operations, strategic buyers, and operational procurement teams, as well as cross-functional stakeholders, to transform data into actionable insights that drive operational improvements, process efficiency, and better decision-making. You will collect, structure, analyze, and interpret data from ERP and procurement-related systems to identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, trends, and improvement opportunities across procurement processes, supplier interactions, and operational workflows. You will also develop and maintain analytical solutions using SQL and BI tools such as QlikSense or similar platforms, enabling transparency and supporting fact-based decision-making. The role combines hands-on analytics with strong collaboration in a highly operational environment. You will be part of a team where collaboration, curiosity, and delivery focus are central. We value openness, continuous learning, and a strong delivery mindset, where everyone contributes to driving real operational change.

This position can be based in Solna or Gothenburg.

Main responsibilities:


Drive data-driven operational improvement initiatives within Procurement Operations, SAAB Surveillance
Analyze operational procurement data to identify inefficiencies, bottlenecks, trends, and improvement opportunities
Work closely with procurement operations teams to understand processes, performance, and daily challenges
Support operational development initiatives through data analysis and insight generation
Build dashboards and reporting solutions using QlikSense or similar BI tools
Extract, transform, and analyze data using SQL, primarily from ERP systems
Develop KPIs and performance metrics to support operational excellence in procurement
Communicate insights, findings, and recommendations to operational and cross-functional stakeholders
Contribute to digitalization, automation, and process improvement initiatives within procurement operations
Continuously identify best practices and translate them into practical operational improvements



Your profile

We're looking for a structured, analytical, and hands-on individual who thrives in an operational environment. You are curious, proactive, and motivated by improving how things actually work in practice, not only in theory. You have a strong "do-it attitude" and enjoy solving real operational problems through data. With solid technical skills and strategic thinking, you're comfortable diving into complex challenges and translating them into clear, actionable insights. You take initiative without waiting for direction, and you're not afraid to roll up your sleeves and get things done. You work naturally close to the business, engaging with stakeholders across functions and turning technical knowledge into practical solutions. You're collaborative, communicative, and able to navigate a dynamic environment where priorities shift quickly. You enjoy change and actively contribute to improving processes, tools, and ways of working, combining your technical expertise with strategic perspective to drive real impact.

Qualifications:


Master of Science (MSc) in Industrial Engineering, Industrial Economics, Mechanical Engineering, Data Science, Information Systems, or similar
Experience from manufacturing, industrial operations, procurement operations, or supply chain environments
Strong proficiency in SQL, Excel, and data analysis
Experience with BI tools such as QlikSense, Power BI, Tableau, or similar
Good understanding of operational procurement processes and supply chain workflows
Experience working with ERP systems (preferably IFS)
Ability to handle, structure, and analyze large and complex datasets
Strong communication skills in both English and Swedish
Several years of relevant experience within manufacturing, procurement, or supply chain is required



Meriting experience:


Experience with operational excellence, Lean, or continuous improvement methodologies
Experience with process automation tools
Experience with AI tools and business adoption in industrial settings
Data modeling or advanced analytics experience in operational contexts



This position requires that you pass a security vetting based on the current regulations around/of security protection. For positions requiring security clearance additional obligations on citizenship may apply.

What you will be part of

Explore a wealth of possibilities. Take on challenges, create smart inventions, and grow beyond. This is a place for curious minds, brave pioneers, and everyone in between. Together, we achieve the extraordinary, each bringing our unique perspectives. Your part matters.

Saab is a leading defense and security company with an enduring purpose, to help nations keep their people and society safe. Empowered by its 28,000 talented people, Saab constantly pushes the boundaries of technology to create a safer and more sustainable world.

Saab designs, manufactures and maintains advanced systems in aeronautics, weapons, command and control, sensors and underwater systems. Saab is headquartered in Sweden. It has major operations all over the world and is part of the domestic defense capability of several nations. Read more about us here

Title: Data Analyst Manager
Description:
Job Description



WHAT YOU’LL DO​

As a Data Analytics Manager you will lead and develop a team of Data Analysts within the In Season Optimization area, while driving both business delivery and transformation initiatives. This role combines strategic and operational responsibility with people leadership, where your focus will be to enable business impact through data.



The In-Season Optimization mission is to secure capabilities, processes, and insights to get the right product to the right place at the right time, supporting product allocation, availability, and pricing decisions across channels.



You will:



Lead, coach, and develop a team of Data Analysts to strengthen their capabilities and support their career growth
Drive the RUN and TRANSFORMATION agenda, ensuring strong delivery aligned with business goals
Identify and lead strategic areas of analysis to uncover opportunities and improve performance
Work hands-on with data to generate insights using modern analytics and business intelligence tools
Enable data-driven decision making through testing, quantitative analysis, and close collaboration with stakeholders
Oversee projects and initiatives, ensuring effective planning, prioritization, and delivery

​



WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH​



You will be part of the In Season Optimization area and collaborate closely with cross-functional stakeholders across product, tech, and business teams. Together with your team, you will play a key role in shaping how data supports business priorities and decision-making.

​



WHO YOU ARE​



We are looking for people with…​



​5–10 years of experience in people management within Data Analytics or similar fields
A university degree in Data Science, Engineering, or a related area, or equivalent experience
Strong experience within Supply Chain and understanding of end-to-end product flow
Solid experience in project and product management, combining strategic thinking with hands-on execution
Strong analytical and technical skills, with the ability to translate data into clear insights and actions​

And people who are…​



Strong leaders who inspire, develop, and support their teams
Collaborative and communicative, with the ability to work effectively across stakeholders
Proactive and opportunity-driven, identifying improvements through data
Structured and analytical, with a strong data-driven mindset



WHO WE ARE​



H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.​



​



We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. ​



​



WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE ​

At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. ​



We offer all our employee’s attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe. All our employees receive a 25% staff discount usable on all our H&M Group brands in stores and online. In addition to our staff discount, all our employees are included in our H&M Incentive Program – HIP. You can read more about our H&M Incentive Program here.
​30 days holiday
We offer a collective agreement which includes pension and life insurance
H&M Incentive Program
Wellness benefit 4000kr/year
Benify Benefits Portal
Community with activities





JOIN US​



Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.​



​



Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.​



​



​

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

Velory empowers companies to automate and secure IT asset management in one seamless platform. From purchase to end-of-life, our software ensures complete control over IT assets with a sustainable, circular approach.

Velory is transforming how companies buy, manage, and reuse IT. Our platform automates the entire IT lifecycle from procurement to end-of-life helping companies operate smarter and save both time and money.

A core part of Velory is our network of IT retailers. They are not just partners they are essential to how we deliver value. Through Velory, customers purchase hardware via IT retailers, making them a critical part of our product and delivery. At the same time, retailers are a powerful growth engine. By reselling Velory as part of their offering, they bring new customers, new revenue, and help us scale across markets.

We’ve already proven that this model works. Now, we’re scaling it across Europe.

About The Role

We are looking for a Junior Data Scientist to join our Data & Analytics team. This is a full-time position based in Stockholm, with hybrid working arrangements.

You will spend the majority of your time working on team deliverables: analytics tasks, statistical analysis, dashboard engineering, web analytics and data quality work.

What You Will Work On

Analytical and technical work across our data infrastructure, including but not limited to:


Statistical analysis and reporting across product, sales, and finance data
Dashboard development and standardization
Web analytics and user behavior analysis
Data querying, pipeline support and backend/frontend app improvements
Meeting preparation and documentation support


You will work closely with the Product, Finance, Sales and Partners, and Customer Success teams.

Required Skills


Completed higher education corresponding to at least three years of full time studies in computer science, data, engineering or a related technical field
Working knowledge of Python, SQL, R, or similar tools
Experience with data visualization or BI tools such as Luzmo, Grafana, or Power BI
Strong analytical thinking and attention to detail
Curious, structured, and able to manage multiple workstreams
Fluency in English, both written and spoken.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills


Nice to Have


Experience in a startup or fast-paced environment
Eagerness to learn and a proactive attitude.


Who You Are

To excel in this role, you should embody Velory's core values and bring a creative, innovative spirit to our team:


Show ownership: Look at the big picture and take the initiative to contribute where needed.
Manage speed: Eliminate obstacles to maintain a high, sustainable pace.
Represent quality: Aim high, sweat the details, and iterate.
Earn trust: Be communicative, transparent, and follow through on commitments.
Deliver wow: Go beyond expectations in a creative and unexpected way.


What We Offer


A unique opportunity to grow and learn in a fast-paced scale-up environment.
A collaborative and inclusive work culture.
Flexible work arrangements.
Daily breakfast buffet at the office.
Workouts with PT at the office gym.
Private healthcare insurance and a generous wellness grant.
Six weeks of paid vacation and ITP1-level pension.
Select your preferred equipment via our own Velory Platform.
Stockholm HQ with remote flexibility


Join Us

At Velory, you’ll be more than just an employee you're a key player in our mission to revolutionize the digital landscape. If you’re ready to bring your creativity, skills and passion to our team, we’d love to hear from you!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Är du en analytiker som drivs av att förstå användarbeteenden och omsätta data till konkreta förbättringar? Vill du arbeta nära produktutveckling, användarupplevelse och affär där dina insikter påverkar hur miljontals användare interagerar med digitala tjänster? Då kan detta vara uppdraget för dig.




Om verksamheten

Vår kund är en av Sveriges mest välkända digitala medieaktörer med ett starkt fokus på att utveckla användarupplevelser i världsklass. Organisationen befinner sig i en spännande digital utvecklingsresa där data, teknik och användarbehov står i centrum.




Du blir en del av ett team som ansvarar för den digitala upplevelsen i app och webb med fokus på användarnas konsumtion av innehåll i realtid. Teamets mål är att skapa engagerande användarresor som får besökare att hitta rätt innehåll, stanna kvar längre och interagera med plattformen.




Uppdraget är placerat i Centrala Stockholm med möjlighet till hybridarbete.




Om rollen

Som analytiker arbetar du nära produktteam, redaktionella funktioner och andra intressenter för att förstå användarbeteenden och identifiera utvecklingsmöjligheter. Du ansvarar för att analysera publikdata och omsätta insikter till rekommendationer som bidrar till förbättrade funktioner, användarresor och användarupplevelser.




Du kommer att ha en central roll i att stötta produktutveckling och verksamhetsbeslut genom datadrivna analyser och uppföljning.




I rollen arbetar du bland annat med:




Analys av användarbeteenden i digitala plattformar, både app och webb

Funnel, cohort och retentionanalyser

Identifiering av förbättringsmöjligheter i digitala användarresor

Framtagning av insikter och rekommendationer till produktteam och verksamhet

Uppföljning och utvärdering av förändringar i digitala produkter

Analys av hur innehåll, produktupplevelse och distribution samverkar för att skapa användarvärde

Arbete med tracking, datainsamling och analysverktyg




Om dig

Vi söker dig som har några års erfarenhet av analysarbete inom digitala produkter och som trivs i en verksamhet där förändring, utveckling och samarbete är en naturlig del av vardagen.




Du är nyfiken, lösningsorienterad och har förmågan att se samband mellan data, användarbeteende och affärsmål. Samtidigt är du pedagogisk och trygg i att kommunicera insikter till olika målgrupper.




Vi ser gärna att du har:

2 till 3 års erfarenhet som analytiker inom produktutveckling för publika digitala plattformar
Erfarenhet från exempelvis digital media, streamingtjänster, e handel eller liknande verksamheter
God erfarenhet av Googles analys och dataplattformar
Erfarenhet av Google Tag Manager för både app och webb
Grundläggande kunskaper i SQL
Erfarenhet av att arbeta i tvärfunktionella produktteam
Relevant akademisk utbildning inom exempelvis datavetenskap eller motsvarande
Mycket god förmåga att uttrycka dig på svenska i tal och skrift




Det är meriterande om du även har:

Erfarenhet av Looker Studio, Data Studio eller Power BI
Kunskap om rekommendationssystem och personaliseringsalgoritmer
Intresse för digitala beteendetrender, nya plattformar och förändringar inom algoritmer och användarbeteenden




Som person är du:

Analytisk och strukturerad
Kommunikativ och pedagogisk
Samarbetsorienterad och prestigelös
Nyfiken och utvecklingsdriven
Självständig samtidigt som du trivs i team
Lösningsorienterad med ett starkt användarfokus




Om uppdraget

Start: September 2026
Omfattning: Heltid
Uppdragsperiod: Till juni 2027
Placering: Centrala Stockholm
Hybridarbete erbjuds med möjlighet att arbeta hemifrån upp till två dagar per vecka beroende på verksamhetens behov.




Intresserad?

Urval och intervjuer sker löpande, så skicka gärna in din ansökan så snart som möjligt.




Välkommen med din ansökan!

  • Uppdragsperiod: Till juni 2027

  • Hybridarbete erbjuds med möjlighet att arbeta hemifrån upp till två dagar per vecka beroende på verksamhetens behov.

Title: Business Intelligence & Analytics Intern
Description:
BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE & ANALYTICS INTERN

Exciting news for all ambitious and talented Master students out there! Stillfront, a leading gaming company, is seeking a Business Intelligence & Analytics intern to join the Data Platform team. This is not just any internship, but an incredible opportunity to gain hands-on experience in a globally distributed startup-like work environment in the gaming industry.

As a part of Stillfront’s data function, you will have the chance to work on various projects, from traditional reporting and other ad hoc analyses for finance and group business management, to evaluating and improving the performance of AI-driven analytics tools (e.g. accuracy of generated insights, user adoption, and business impact) , a field we are heavily investing in. You will have the opportunity to collaborate with and learn from experienced professionals in the field, and have exposure to senior management at a group level.

YOUR BACKSTORY


You are likely, or will be, a Master student in Business, Engineering or Finance planning to graduate in 2026-2028.
Skills in SQL and/or Python/R are required.
Skills in Excel and financial modeling are required.
Strong skills in presenting comprehensive and complex analyses.
Fluent in spoken and written English, Swedish is meriting.
Familiarity with software best practices such as version control.


YOUR MISSION


Work in the BI & Analytics team supporting monthly and quarterly group processes.
Work closely with Finance and Group Business Management to develop and improve Stillfront’s internal data and reporting processes.
Work with the data platform team to improve observability and measurability of AI-driven analytics.


The internship is located at Stillfront’s Stockholm HQ (Sveavägen 21), with a time requirement of a minimum of 60 hours per month. The exact distribution of work can be tailored to suit the candidate's study commitments. The internship is primarily focused on group-level BI & financial analytics (≈70%), with exposure to advanced topics such as AI-driven analytics and data platform development (≈30%).

Don't miss this unique opportunity to kick-start your career and join the innovative and exciting world of Stillfront. Apply now to become part of a leading team in the gaming industry!

Stillfront is a global gaming company. We develop a wide range of digital games that attract millions of players each month. From well-established franchises like Supremacy, BIG, and Jawaker to niche games, we span multiple genres, including strategy, simulation, RPG, action, and casual and mash-up games.

We believe gaming can be a force for good, and we want to create a gaming universe that is digital, affordable, equal, and sustainable. To achieve this, we focus on developing games that are all about having a rewarding hobby, a great social experience, or a strategic challenge.

Stillfront was founded in 2010, and since then, we’ve grown rapidly. Our professionals thrive in an organization that embodies the spirit of entrepreneurship, and we’re proud to connect and empower game teams around the world.

Our HQ is in Stockholm, Sweden, but our game development is done by teams and studios all over the world. Our main markets are the US, Japan, MENA, Germany, and the UK. We’re proud that our company shares are listed on the Nasdaq Stockholm Large Cap.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
THE OPPORTUNITY IN A NUTSHELL

You? A data professional who enjoys building reliable platform tools and supporting others to make data-driven decisions.

Role? Join us as a Data Engineer to develop end-to-end platform tools, automate data governance with AI, and support our data community.

Tech stack? Snowflake, dbt, Terraform, CI/CD pipelines, and data-intensive ETL applications.

Location? Stockholm.

Company? Vend, home of DBA, Bilbasen, FINN, Blocket, Oikotie & Tori, where millions of people across the Nordics connect to find what they need, whether it’s a way to move, a job, a home, a way to move, or a fresh start.

Why us? This is your chance to build the very foundation of how our iconic Nordic brands use data. Down the road, you will have a direct impact on our platform's future by bringing AI into automated governance, all while growing your skills in a highly collaborative, psychologically safe engineering culture that values continuous learning.

Sounds like your cup of tea? Check out the details below!

WHO ARE YOU?


Experienced with cloud data warehousing & dbt: You have a solid foundation in building or supporting data warehouse solutions (such as Snowflake, Google BigQuery, or AWS Redshift) and feel right at home using dbt for data transformations.
Infrastructure as Code & CI/CD: You bring practical experience with Terraform and enjoy designing and maintaining CI/CD pipelines to ensure smooth, automated deployments.
User-focused problem solver: You care about the developer experience. You enjoy building tools that make life easier for the consumers and producers of data products.
Naturally collaborative and open-minded: You thrive when sharing knowledge and tackling fresh, evolving topics, like finding creative ways to use AI for automation.
Clear communicator: Since we support diverse teams across multiple regions, you can explain technical platform concepts in a clear, accessible way to colleagues with varying levels of data expertise.


WHAT’S THE JOB LIKE?

As a Data Engineer within our Data Platform business area, your mission is to empower teams across Vend to access, trust, and utilize data seamlessly. You won’t just be managing data; you’ll be building the product that enables our entire data ecosystem.

Your Main Focus Areas Are


Platform Tooling: Developing and improving end-to-end tools that make it simple for internal teams to build and consume data products.
Community Support: Actively helping and onboarding data consumers and producers across the company, troubleshooting platform hurdles, and advocating for modern data engineering practices.
Stakeholders: You will collaborate closely with other platform engineers, data scientists, analysts, and product teams across our Nordic brands (like Tori, Blocket, and FINN).
AI-Driven Governance: Taking ownership of Snowflake governance, with a specific focus on developing innovative tooling and automations assisted by AI down the road.

Title: Risk Data Modeller to Finance, Risk, and Compliance | SEB, Stockholm
Description:
SEB is more than just a bank with an IT function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fueled by collaboration. From COBOL to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we're innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone.

Finance, Risk, and Compliance are undergoing a major transformation—driven by business and regulatory changes as well as significant shifts in architectural design. We are therefore looking for a Risk Data Modeller with experience in data modelling to develop an integrated data architecture that meets regulatory requirements and business needs. You will be a vital part of our community driving the Finance, Risk, and Compliance agenda in the bank. Are you ready to take on a new challenge? If so, this opportunity is for you!

About the role:

As a Risk Data Modeller in our team, you will take responsibility for the data architecture within the risk area. Your work will involve a high degree of complexity, both from a business and data perspective as well as from a people and process perspective. You’ll work closely with business stakeholders and architects to:


Capture and interpret business and regulatory requirements driven by our major initiatives.
Develop an integrated data architecture for Risk that supports all risk types, ensuring data is consistent, integrated, traceable, and fit for regulatory and business use, while connecting to the domain‑wide data architecture.
Develop business information and logical data models that align with business needs and integrate with business and application architectures, supporting metadata capture, consistency, lineage, and traceability across the data landscape.
Support the definition of business terms, and ensure they are correctly represented, consistently used, and traceable across models, data flows, and solutions.
Ensure compliance with BCBS 239, Basel/CRR, IFRS, and other applicable regulatory requirements, and alignment with the domain architecture.
Collaborate with stakeholders from the risk organisation, data management professionals, and architects across Finance, Risk, and Compliance.


To thrive in this role, we believe you have:


Strong professional background in risk management within a banking or financial services (e.g., credit, market, counterparty, or liquidity risk).
Competence in developing ontologies, business information models, and logical data models (including normalised and dimensional models), as well as data definitions, data mapping specifications, and data flows.
Good understanding of data warehouse architectures and methodologies, and familiarity with the Data Mesh framework and data products.
Knowledge of regulatory and data management requirements in Finance and Risk areas, and how data architecture enables compliance and governance.
Experience with BCBS 239 (Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting, RDARR) would be an advantage.
Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills.
Ability to collaborate effectively with a positive, solution‑oriented mindset.
Excellent communication and documentation skills to convey complex ideas effectively to both business and IT professionals.
Fluency in English.


What we offer:


A key role in one of SEB’s most challenging areas
Innovative working environment with highly motivated colleagues
Agile and modern ways of working
Hybrid work model, meaning remote work is possible up to 2 days/week
Attractive compensation and access to SEB staff banking with exclusive benefits


Do you want to be part of SEB?

It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial to our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.

Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than June 25th.

HTML:
SEB is more than just a bank with an IT function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fueled by collaboration. From COBOL to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we're innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone.

Finance, Risk, and Compliance are undergoing a major transformation—driven by business and regulatory changes as well as significant shifts in architectural design. We are therefore looking for a Risk Data Modeller with experience in data modelling to develop an integrated data architecture that meets regulatory requirements and business needs. You will be a vital part of our community driving the Finance, Risk, and Compliance agenda in the bank. Are you ready to take on a new challenge? If so, this opportunity is for you!

About the role:

As a Risk Data Modeller in our team, you will take responsibility for the data architecture within the risk area. Your work will involve a high degree of complexity, both from a business and data perspective as well as from a people and process perspective. You’ll work closely with business stakeholders and architects to:

  • Capture and interpret business and regulatory requirements driven by our major initiatives.
  • Develop an integrated data architecture for Risk that supports all risk types, ensuring data is consistent, integrated, traceable, and fit for regulatory and business use, while connecting to the domain‑wide data architecture.
  • Develop business information and logical data models that align with business needs and integrate with business and application architectures, supporting metadata capture, consistency, lineage, and traceability across the data landscape.
  • Support the definition of business terms, and ensure they are correctly represented, consistently used, and traceable across models, data flows, and solutions.
  • Ensure compliance with BCBS 239, Basel/CRR, IFRS, and other applicable regulatory requirements, and alignment with the domain architecture.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders from the risk organisation, data management professionals, and architects across Finance, Risk, and Compliance.

To thrive in this role, we believe you have:

  • Strong professional background in risk management within a banking or financial services (e.g., credit, market, counterparty, or liquidity risk).
  • Competence in developing ontologies, business information models, and logical data models (including normalised and dimensional models), as well as data definitions, data mapping specifications, and data flows.
  • Good understanding of data warehouse architectures and methodologies, and familiarity with the Data Mesh framework and data products.
  • Knowledge of regulatory and data management requirements in Finance and Risk areas, and how data architecture enables compliance and governance.
  • Experience with BCBS 239 (Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting, RDARR) would be an advantage.
  • Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills.
  • Ability to collaborate effectively with a positive, solution‑oriented mindset.
  • Excellent communication and documentation skills to convey complex ideas effectively to both business and IT professionals.
  • Fluency in English.

What we offer:

  • A key role in one of SEB’s most challenging areas
  • Innovative working environment with highly motivated colleagues
  • Agile and modern ways of working
  • Hybrid work model, meaning remote work is possible up to 2 days/week
  • Attractive compensation and access to SEB staff banking with exclusive benefits

Do you want to be part of SEB?

It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial to our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.

Title: Insights and Measurement Lead, Amazon Ads
Description:
Description

Amazon Ads is the digital advertising arm of Amazon and one of our fastest growing businesses. We operate at the intersection of commerce, entertainment and advertising, offering unique advertising products, enterprise ad tech and a rich array of unique creative, format and measurement solutions. We offer advertisers premium brand placements on our owned and operated properties, including Prime Video ads, Twitch, and a rich slate of retail media solutions.

As an Insights and Measurement Lead, you will work as a senior individual contributor to accelerate our advertising business growth in the Nordics and Benelux regions. With Prime Video's launch in Sweden and Netherlands, you'll have a pivotal role in shaping the streaming TV and video landscape in the region. You will serve as a strategic advisor, implementing analytics solutions to drive business transformation. You will lead Ad measurement discussions, create actionable insights, and present clear recommendations to executive stakeholders.

Key job responsibilities


Leverage Amazon's proprietary data, third-party research, and ad measurement solutions to generate actionable insights
Own senior-level measurement and insights customer relationships, partnering across strategic learning objectives that influence business decision-making
Collaborate cross-functionally to influence sales and marketing initiatives across multiple business functions and geographies
Advocate for data-driven decision-making and guide advertisers to implement recommended solutions


A day in the life

On a daily basis, the Insights and Measurement Lead performs hands-on data extraction and analysis using SQL and Python, leveraging Amazon measurement solutions to build insights and track success metrics for advertisers' businesses and industries. The role requires developing data-driven recommendations for executive client leadership and communicating those insights in a clear and compelling manner.

About The Team

The Analytics & Insights (A&I) organization leverages Amazon's data to develop actionable insights for advertisers, enhancing marketers' decision-making and measurably improving their success both on and off Amazon.

Basic Qualifications


7+ years of external customer facing analytics role.
Experience in customer segmentation, customer behavior analysis, data science modeling.
Working experience with data analysis tools SQL, Python/R.
Experience in advertising measurement methodologies, including incrementality, marketing mix modeling, and media attribution.


Preferred Qualifications


Excellent Swedish and English communication skills.
Experience with advertising publishers and marketing/creative agencies
Advanced degree (MBA, Master's) in Economics, Marketing, Statistics, Advertising, or Business; or equivalent Digital media planning experience


Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

Title: Junior Data Engineer
Description:
Playground Data


Stockholm
Hybrid


Junior Data Engineer

The Playgorund Pitch

Some data problems are neat and tidy. Ours usually aren’t. They are messy, evolving, and tied to real decisions. We work closely with our clients to understand what actually needs to be solved and build from there.

At Playground Data, we build modern data platforms for analytics, AI and machine learning. We are a collaborative team that values solid engineering and shared growth. Our engineers take ownership and work closely with clients to deliver high-quality solutions.

What You'll Actually Do

You'll join our client engagements as part of a team, working on data platforms that matter to the business. Many of our projects are greenfield builds or major platform transformations, so there's plenty to learn from people who've done it before. This role suits someone early in their data engineering career who wants to build real things and grow fast.

In Practice, You'll


Build and maintain data pipelines that ingest, model, and structure data for analytics, AI and ML workloads.
Work alongside senior engineers and pick up how modern data platforms are designed and operated.
Take growing ownership of your own tasks as you find your feet, with support whenever you need it.
Contribute to how we work internally, share ideas, and help improve our data engineering frameworks and ways of working.


You'll never sit idle, there's always something meaningful to build, learn, or untangle.

Who are you?

You're early in your data engineering journey, maybe fresh out of school or with a first project or two behind you, and you already know that data doesn't magically organize itself. Python and SQL are tools you're comfortable with and keen to get sharper at, and you've had your first taste of a modern data stack (AWS, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake etc.).

You Would Probably Describe Yourself As Someone Who

Wants to understand data engineering in practice: You're eager to learn how data flows, scales, and holds up in production for analytics and AI and ML workloads.

Enjoys building from scratch: Greenfield work doesn't scare you, it motivates you.

Stays curious when things are unclear: You don't panic when requirements are fuzzy and the system diagram needs three whiteboards, you ask questions and dig in.

Wants to grow into a consultant role: You're up for customer dialogue and learning how to guide clients, with senior colleagues alongside you.

As a person, you're humble, genuinely curious about technology, and motivated, while knowing that asking for help is a strength, not a weakness. Friendly, curious, and playful.

You've graduated from KTH, Stockholms Tekniska Institut, Nackademin or a similar program in a relevant field.

Language requirement

You need to be fluent in Swedish and able to use it confidently in a professional environment. Why? Most of our collaborations and client projects are conducted in Swedish, and several of our clients have formal requirements.

Let's Play!

Applying to us is a breeze!


Upload your resumé or use your Linkedin profile
Answer the choice-questions (no "please-rewrite-your-resume" nonsense, promise)
Feel free to include links to your portfolio, GitHub or similar if you have stuff you're proud of that you want to share with us


Note

Playground Group does not provide visa sponsorship or relocation support. For this role applicants must have a swedish or EU-citizenship.

Welcome to Playground!

At Playground, you work on real, complex assignments for well-established and ambitious clients, not bench projects or short-lived experiments. Our teams are trusted with production systems, platforms, and data that actually matter. Here growth comes from ownership, curiosity, and doing the work properly. You’re trusted to think, contribute, and keep getting better, one meaningful step at a time. Playground brings together specialized domains under one umbrella, with a shared culture and way of working, and easy access to broad technical expertise when it helps move the work forward.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
We are looking for AI Engineers / Data Engineers to join our Recommendation Engine team. In this role, you will develop and maintain solutions that leverage large language models (LLMs) and agentic frameworks to deliver customer-facing services. You will also be responsible for maintaining and improving our cloud platform to ensure stability and enable efficient development.

You have a solid background in Data Engineering. We value your experience in data management, data analytics, and machine learning, as well as your programming skills in Python and SQL, and previous experience working with Google Cloud Platform (GCP).

You are communicative, curious, and accountable. You enjoy solving problems, have a structured approach to your work, and are comfortable working in agile teams with a DevOps mindset.

You will work closely with business stakeholders and play a key role in translating business requirements into technical solutions, as well as planning and executing implementations. Experience working with AI-powered conversational systems is considered an advantage.

Profile & Qualifications

Experience processing and transforming large volumes of data using SQL and DBT
Experience working with Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
Strong programming skills in Python
Experience with Generative AI and Agentic Workflows (preferably using ADK on GCP)
Experience working with ontologies, knowledge models, and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) solutions
Software engineering experience is a plus
Proven ability to collaborate with business stakeholders and product owners
Fluent in Swedish, both written and spoken

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Join our global force of 400+ innovators, blending the latest in tech with the greatest in soundtracking — from our Stockholm HQ to offices in London, New York, Los Angeles, Berlin, Paris, Oslo, and Seoul. We're an industry leader with a startup mentality. We take what we do seriously, but we don't take ourselves too seriously. Come create with us, and let the world feel your work.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you'll join an embedded, AI-native analytics team that sits inside the commercial and product domains driving our growth. You'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with the CPO and product teams as we evolve from a music-library product into a broader creative-workflow platform, defining what success looks like for surfaces that don't exist yet and proving it with data.

What you'll do


Partner with the CPO and product teams to define success metrics, surface growth opportunities, and shape product strategy with evidence.
Stand up and advise on measurement for new product experiences end to end — instrumentation, KPI design, and leading-indicator tracking.
Run the full analytics stack — from descriptive analysis through experimentation — to evaluate performance and drive decisions.
Close data gaps and build scalable, self-serve measurement infrastructure so teams can monitor long-term impact without you in the loop.
Make our data trustworthy for agentic analytics by resolving gaps and enriching context and metadata so both AI tools and people can rely on it.


What you'll bring


Strong stakeholder management and business acumen. Track record of influencing senior decision-makers.
High agency. Habit of driving decisions through tailored communication and data storytelling.
Sharp analytical instincts. You scope vague questions with vetted frameworks, state hypotheses up front, and reason to a defensible answer even with imperfect data.
Sound judgement about data. A solid grasp of statistics and the discernment to know when past data predicts the future and when to override it for where the business is heading.
Technical versatility. Proficiency across SQL and Python (notebooks in Hex), dbt (on BigQuery), plus experience with AI coding tools and techniques including context engineering and evals.
Experienced experimentalist. Fluent in A/B testing and causal inference.


Music to our ears


Data modelling know-how and experience with semantic layers.
Experience in PLG and SaaS environments (B2C and/or B2B).
A track record of defining metrics for new 0→1 product surfaces.


What we'll offer


A fast-paced environment with real room for personal growth and tangible impact on the business.
The chance to help write the new playbook for running Analytics in the AI era combining timeless principles with fresh learnings from emerging tooling.
A diverse, collaborative team of Analysts and Engineers building together.


Equal opportunity employer

We believe that bringing people together from different backgrounds, experiences and perspectives makes for a healthy workplace, a more successful business and a better world. We value diversity and encourage everyone to come and soundtrack the world with us.

Application

Ready to make the world feel your work? Please apply, in English.

  • A track record of defining metrics for new 0→1 product surfaces.

What we'll offer

  • A fast-paced environment with real room for personal growth and tangible impact on the business.
  • The chance to help write the new playbook for running Analytics in the AI era combining timeless principles with fresh learnings from emerging tooling.
  • A diverse, collaborative team of Analysts and Engineers building together.

Equal opportunity employer

We believe that bringing people together from different backgrounds, experiences and perspectives makes for a healthy workplace, a more successful business and a better world. We value diversity and encourage everyone to come and soundtrack the world with us.

Application

Ready to make the world feel your work? Please apply, in English.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
At Viaplay Group, we entertain millions of people every day through our streaming services, radio networks, and TV channels. We believe in the power of content not just as a way of telling stories and touching lives, but also expanding worlds.  

We’re looking for the best people to join us on our journey. Right now, we’re searching for a Data Engineer in our Stockholm office – are you ready to hit play on an exciting career change? 

The role 

The Ad Platform Solutions team sits within Viaplay Digital Ad Product, bridging commercial priorities and technical delivery in Viaplay. You will be joining a small, growing team where your work connects directly to how the sales organisation operates every day.

As our new Data Engineer, you will play a central role in developing and improving the data infrastructure that powers ad sales across all Viaplay markets. We’ve built a solid foundation that supports our operations today, and we’re now looking for someone to take ownership of it - improving and strengthening what’s already in place, while also influencing architecture and shaping how the platform evolves.

Role

This is a hands-on engineering role. In a small team, you will work across the full data lifecycle - from pipeline development and orchestration to data modelling, ensuring the right data reaches the right people. Your day-to-day will include:


Building and maintaining data pipelines that power ad sales reporting and operations across Sweden, Norway, and Denmark
Structuring and modelling data so it is clean, consistent, and genuinely useful to the business
Identifying and resolving issues in existing pipelines, reducing manual work and improving reliability
Translating needs from stakeholders into solid technical solutions
Contributing to architectural decisions as the platform evolves


We actively use AI-assisted tools as part of our development workflow to improve efficiency and speed up delivery. We expect engineers to use these tools effectively, while critically evaluating outputs and maintaining full ownership of the systems they build.

What we’re looking for 

We understand you may feel confident ticking certain boxes more than others and that’s why we always keep an open mind in our recruitment process. But, in order to thrive in this role, we do believe you’ll have at least some experience in the following: 


Solid, production-level data engineering experience - you have done this before and know what good looks like
Strong skills in Python and/or C#, with confident SQL and hands-on database experience, relational databases such as SQL Server and PostgreSQL.
Experience working with cloud-based data platforms, particularly in Azure environments.
Experience building and maintaining data pipelines using orchestration tools (such as Airflow or Meltano), along with a solid understanding of data warehouse principles (e.g. dimensional modelling, data quality, and performance optimisation)
Experience using AI-assisted coding tools (e.g. GitHub Copilot, Claude) in a development workflow
Comfortable working autonomously in a small team, with a strong sense of ownership and the ability to drive work forward without much hand-holding


It’s a bonus if you have: 


Experience with ad sales, digital advertising data, or media industry systems
A can-do attitude, sense of humor and the ability to work humbly with others.


What we offer you

We’ve got you covered! 30 days of paid vacation every year, an attractive pension and insurance scheme, and generous parental leave pay lift.

Your wellbeing matters. We provide you with various wellbeing initiatives including wellness allowance.

A safe space to grow and up-skill. Our learning culture puts you in the driver’s seat of your own development.

Entertainment is what we love, and entertainment is what we do. So, unlimited access to Viaplay seems only fair for you to get to know the product – including series & viewing events, new release movie rentals, linear channels and more.

Hit play today 

If this feels like your kind of challenge, make sure you apply by attaching your CV here – you may also want to add your LinkedIn profile. Please don’t send us your application via email because we won’t be able to accept it. We do, however, welcome any questions you may have about this particular position. 

Want to learn more about who we are and what we do? Check out our careers page or follow us on Instagram! We’re only ever a few clicks away.

Title: BI-Specialist
Description:
Professional Galaxy är ett IT- och teknikkonsultbolag som tillhandahåller högspecialiserad kompetens inom IT, mjukvaruutveckling, SAP, inköp, elektronik och mekanisk konstruktion. Vi samarbetar med erfarna seniora experter och levererar strategiskt värdeskapande kompetens till några av Sveriges mest komplexa och analytiskt krävande projekt. Vårt fokus ligger alltid på hög kvalitet, professionalism och tydliga, mätbara resultat.

Vi söker nu en BI-Specialist hos vår kund.

Om uppdraget

Kompetensnivå

Kompetensroll: BI-specialist - Qlik Sense-utvecklare/Nprinting

Kompetensnivå: Nivå 4

Kunskap - Hög generalistkompetens eller mycket hög kompetens inom området. Utbildning motsvarande kandidatexamen eller motsvarande kunskap förvärvad genom arbetslivserfarenhet inom området.

Erfarenhet - Har deltagit i stora uppdrag inom olika områden och genomfört uppdrag med mycket hög kvalitet. Nivån uppnås normalt tidigast efter 9–12 års erfarenhet inom området. Har kompetensmässigt befunnit sig på nivå 3 under minst 2 år.

Ledning - Tar huvudansvar för ledning av större grupp.

Självständighet - Mycket stor

Ska-krav:


Se Bilaga - Krav på tjänsten för Ska-kraven. Fyll i information om kravuppfyllnad i Bilaga 4. Kravmatris och bifoga ansökan.


Mervärdeskrav:


A. Arbete med ekonomidata (ekonomidata i Qlik Sense)


För att anbudsgivaren ska tilldelas mervärdesavdrag ska den offererade konsulten ha dokumenterad erfarenhet av att ha arbetat med ekonomidata.


B. Arbete med ekonomidata (HR-data i Qlik Sense)


För att anbudsgivaren ska tilldelas mervärdesavdrag ska den offererade konsulten ha dokumenterad erfarenhet av att ha arbetat med ekonomidata.

Fyll i information om kravuppfyllnad för mervärdeskraven i Bilaga 6. Mervärdeskrav och bifoga ansökan.

Startdatum: Preliminärt 2026-08-17.

Slutdatum: Preliminärt till och med 2026-12-31.

Plats: Stockholm. Utförandet sker preliminärt på plats på Södersjukhuset.

Är du rätt person för uppdraget, eller vill du rekommendera en stark kandidat? Tveka inte att kontakta oss.

Ansök gärna redan idag, urval och intervjuer sker löpande

Ansök genom att bifoga ditt CV (vi vill inte ha något personligt brev) och besvara urvalsfrågorna. Frågorna utgår från kravprofilen för den här rekryteringen och dina svar ligger till grund för det urval som sker i processen. Att besvara frågorna är en förutsättning för att din ansökan ska anses komplett.

Vänligen notera att krav och eventuella meriter ska vara uppfyllda vid ansökningstillfället.

Title: Finance Data Analyst till Business Control på Coop
Description:
Bli en del av Coop, tillsammans gör vi mat-Sverige hållbart




Vill du arbeta i gränslandet mellan Finance, Business Control, BI och dataanalys? Trivs du i en roll där du får kombinera analys, rapportering, verksamhetsförståelse och teknisk nyfikenhet? Då kan rollen som Finance Data Analyst på Coop vara rätt nästa steg för dig.




Coop befinner sig i en spännande förflyttning där data, analys och beslutsstöd blir allt viktigare för att kunna fatta snabba, träffsäkra och affärsnära beslut. Nu söker vi en Finance Data Analyst som vill bidra till att utveckla och stärka vårt BI-, analys- och rapporteringsarbete inom Business Control.




Om tjänsten




Som Finance Data Analyst blir du en viktig länk mellan Business Control, Controlling, BI-avdelningen och Coops datamiljöer. Du hjälper organisationen att förstå, tillgängliggöra och använda data på ett smartare sätt, både genom snabba ad hoc-analyser och genom mer strukturerad rapportering och utveckling av beslutsstöd.




Rollen passar dig som vill arbeta verksamhetsnära, men samtidigt ha ett tydligt datadrivet och tekniskt inslag i vardagen. Du kommer inte enbart att kravställa eller samordna, utan också själv bygga, analysera och vidareutveckla rapporter, framför allt i Power BI.




Du kommer bland annat att arbeta med att:




• Driva och utveckla BI-, analys- och rapporteringsarbetet inom Business Control
• Fungera som länk mellan Controlling, BI-avdelningen och Coops datamiljöer
• Ta fram snabba analyser, datauttag och svar på ad hoc-frågor
• Bygga, vidareutveckla och paketera rapporter och beslutsstöd, framför allt i Power BI
• Arbeta med data i Excel, SQL och BI-verktyg för att skapa relevanta analysunderlag
• Kravställa och paketera behov gentemot BI- och datateam
• Utbilda och stötta kollegor i hur data, rapporter och beslutsstöd kan användas i vardagen
• Bidra i projekt där det finns behov av analys-, rapporterings- eller datastöd




Det här är inte en klassisk controllerroll med huvudsakligt fokus på bokslut, budget och prognos. Rollen har sin hemvist inom Business Control, men tyngdpunkten ligger på data, BI, analys, rapportering och verksamhetsnära beslutsstöd.



Kvalifikationer



Om dig




Vi söker dig som är analytisk, nyfiken och lösningsorienterad, med en stark vilja att förstå både data och verksamhet. Du trivs med varierande frågor, där du ibland behöver ta fram snabba svar och ibland skapa mer långsiktiga rapporteringslösningar.




Du är kommunikativ, relationsskapande och bekväm med att samarbeta med olika delar av organisationen. Du har lätt för att ta kontakt, ställa frågor och bygga nätverk internt, och du kan förklara data och analys på ett sätt som blir begripligt och användbart för andra.




Vi tror att du kan ha en bakgrund inom exempelvis dataanalys, BI, Business Control, controlling, management consulting eller verksamhetsnära analys. Det viktiga är att du har erfarenhet av att kombinera analys, rapportering och verksamhetsförståelse.




För att lyckas i rollen ser vi gärna att du har:




• Erfarenhet av BI-verktyg, gärna Power BI
• Mycket goda kunskaper i Excel och trygghet i att bearbeta, strukturera och analysera data
• Grundläggande kunskaper i SQL och en vilja att utvecklas vidare inom området
• Förmåga att förstå verksamhetsbehov och omsätta dem till rapporter, analyser eller beslutsunderlag
• God kommunikativ förmåga och trygghet i att samarbeta med både tekniska och icke-tekniska intressenter
• Akademisk utbildning inom relevant område, exempelvis ekonomi, industriell ekonomi, data/analytics, statistik eller motsvarande




Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet från FMCG, retail, dagligvaruhandel eller annan transaktionsintensiv verksamhet. Det är även meriterande om du har erfarenhet av processutveckling, rapportutveckling, SAP-nära miljöer eller intresse för nya analysverktyg och generativ AI.




Vårt erbjudande till dig




Hos Coop får du en bred och synlig roll där du kan vara med och påverka hur vi arbetar med data, analys och beslutsstöd inom Business Control. Du kommer in i en verksamhet med stora datamängder, hög komplexitet och många spännande affärsfrågor, där rätt analys kan göra verklig skillnad.




På Coop blir du en del av en värderingsdriven organisation med ett viktigt uppdrag: att göra mat-Sverige mer hållbart. Vi erbjuder en inkluderande arbetsplats, engagerade kollegor och möjlighet att utvecklas i en miljö där samarbete, ansvar och affärsnära analys är centrala delar av vardagen.




Så ansöker du




Låter detta som din nästa utmaning? Skicka in din ansökan så snart som möjligt då vi arbetar löpande med urval och intervjuer.




För att rättvist matcha kandidater med de krav som rollen ställer kommer vi att använda tester som en del av urvalsarbetet.




Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

På Coop blir du en del av en värderingsdriven organisation med ett viktigt uppdrag: att göra mat-Sverige mer hållbart. Vi erbjuder en inkluderande arbetsplats, engagerade kollegor och möjlighet att utvecklas i en miljö där samarbete, ansvar och affärsnära analys är centrala delar av vardagen.


Så ansöker du


Låter detta som din nästa utmaning? Skicka in din ansökan så snart som möjligt då vi arbetar löpande med urval och intervjuer.


För att rättvist matcha kandidater med de krav som rollen ställer kommer vi att använda tester som en del av urvalsarbetet.


Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

Title: Student Research Assistant (Part-Time)
Description:
YouGov is an international online research data and analytics technology group. Our innovative solutions help the world’s most recognised brands, media owners and agencies to plan, activate and track their marketing activities better. With operations in the UK, the Americas, Europe, the Middle East, India and Asia Pacific, we have one of the world's largest research networks. At the core of our platform is an ever-growing source of connected consumer data that has developed daily over our 20 years of operation. We call it living data. All of our products and services draw upon this detailed understanding of our 20+ million registered panel members to deliver accurate, actionable consumer insights. Job description You will be supporting fast-turnaround research projects on many different topics, which is a part of YouGov’s Client Service offering.




As Student Research Assistant, you will support Research Executives on various tasks. The role relies on excellent communication skills. You’ll be working closely with other YouGov teams as well as directly with clients to design and deliver research which is in line with the brief.




Your primary responsibility will be:

Proof-reading of client-based questionnaires.
Checking and adjustment of questionnaire scripting instructions.
Scripting of surveys in YouGov’s own self-service tool.
Checking of questionnaire test links before launch.
Data output checking and delivery for clients.
Supporting on custom report preparations.




These are the essentials you’ll need to get an interview:

Swedish in writing/speaking and excellent written/spoken English skills (corporate language).
Working proficiency in the basic MS package.
Strong attention to details and the ability to deliver results and meet deadlines.
Problem-solving, analytical and numerical skills.




This is a part-time, paid vacancy of maximum 20 hours per week for student candidates. It is hybrid working arrangement of 40% in office, 60% remote working. The salary is SEK160 per hour, with a pay review after 6 months.

If you have questions to the position, please reach out to Amir Redzepovic, amir.redzepovic@yougov.com or Gina Soana, gina.soana@yougov.com.




Why join YouGov?

Join our global team to help us achieve our social mission: to make millions of people’s opinions heard for the benefit of our local and international communities.

Understanding diversity of opinion requires diversity of background. Although our global panel of millions of people worldwide powers our research, our biggest asset is our people. If our research is to be truly representative of what the world thinks, we need people from all walks of life to be part of the team to bring their perspective to the work we do.

Life at YouGov

We are driven by a set of shared values. We are fast, fearless, and innovative. We work diligently to get it right. We are guided by accuracy, ethics, and proven methodologies. We respect and trust each other, bringing these values into everything that we do.

We strive to provide YouGovers with best-in-class benefits to support their physical, financial, and emotional wellbeing. We want our employees to have a sense of belonging and uniqueness in a supportive workplace, so they can bring their full selves to work.

Data Privacy

To find out how we collect and use your personal data when you apply for a role at YouGov, please read our privacy notice at https://jobs.yougov.com/privacy

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Help design, build and continuously improve the clients online platform.

Research, suggest and implement new technology solutions following best practices/standards.

Take responsibility for the resiliency and availability of different products.

Be a productive member of the team.





Requirements


6+ years of experience in Data Engineering, with strong expertise in cloud-based data warehouse environments.

Proven hands-on experience with AWS Redshift, including deep knowledge of: Redshift internals, Workload Management (WLM), Distribution and sort keys, Query execution plan analysis, Performance tuning and optimization techniques

Strong experience using dbt for data transformation, model development, and analytics engineering workflows.

Advanced SQL skills with a strong focus on query optimization and performance tuning within Redshift environments.

Solid understanding of data modeling methodologies including dimensional modeling (Kimball), normalized schemas, and hybrid data modeling strategies.

Experience with monitoring and performance analysis tools such as AWS CloudWatch and Redshift Console.

Familiarity with data version control, CI/CD pipelines, and collaborative development best practices within dbt ecosystems.





Benefits


A challenging, innovating environment.

Opportunities for learning where needed.

Title: Data Steward/Senior Data & Requirements Analyst (Custody)
Description:
emagine is looking for a Data Steward/Senior Data & Requirements Analyst (Custody) to one of our nordic banking clients.

Start: ASAP

End: 2027-06-22

Location: Stockholm (3 days onsite, 2 days remote)

Description

The area of Custody is running a major transformation, implementing a new IT platform that will support our business and operation in future proofing the services in the area.

Role Overview

This role combines Data Stewardship, Data Mapping, and Reference Data Management, with a strong focus on hands-on execution.

You will ensure that:


Business requirements are translated into clear, traceable data mappings
Reference data is defined, governed, and consistently used
Data assets are aligned, controlled, and usable across integrations and downstream consumption


The role is not focused on data modeling, but on ensuring that:


Data mappings are correct, complete, and implemented
Reference data is structured and managed
Integration teams receive clear and usable schema inputs and mapping logic
You will also work with GitHub-based data mapping workflows/agents to maintain and execute mappings in a structured and version-controlled way.


Requirements:


Several years of experience working as a Business Analyst within financial services, preferably within a highly tech-focused area.
Experience working with regulatory and compliance-driven initiatives
Proven ability to write clear, structured, and implementable business requirements
Strong analytical skills and experience working with large and complex datasets
Experience working in complex stakeholder environments involving business and IT teams
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills

Title: Web Analyst
Description:
About The Role

We’re looking for a Web Analyst who wants to turn data into decisions – not just dashboards, and who’s curious about how new tools (including AI) are changing the way we work with data.

This is a 12-month parental leave cover, with the possibility to extend into a permanent role.

In this role, you’ll work closely with our clients to set up the right measurement from the start. That means translating business goals into tracking, building solid data foundations, and making sure insights actually lead to action.

You’ll be part of a cross-functional team and involved early in client engagements – from workshops and tracking plans to implementation and ongoing optimisation.

What You’ll Do


Lead workshops to define measurement frameworks and KPIs
Set up and maintain tracking using GA4 and GTM
Build dashboards and reports in Looker Studio that drive decisions
Analyse user behaviour, funnels and performance to identify opportunities
Present insights and recommendations in a clear and engaging way
Collaborate with developers, designers and marketers to improve performance
Use modern tools, including AI-supported analysis, to speed up insights and improve decision-making


We believe you have


A few years of experience in web analytics (GA4 & GTM)
Experience building dashboards and working with data visualisation
Solid understanding of tracking implementation and data quality
Some years experience in SQL
Worked in BigQueary
Strong analytical skills and a business mindset
Fluent in English and Swedish


We’ll be extra excited if you also have


Experience with A/B testing and CRO
Worked in multi-market or complex environments


What We Offer


A collaborative, international environment with 60+ colleagues across Sweden and Mumbai
A strong focus on learning – through sessions, projects and your own development budget
Access to an external coach
A hybrid setup – we meet on Wednesdays + one more day per week
A people-first culture where balance and long-term growth matter


About Us

We are a digital growth consultancy that work closely with our clients to develop their business and solve their digital needs. We work with a great mix of clients ranging from Toyota, Isadora, Fabege, Electrolux and many more. With 60+ passionate individuals with different nationalities and cultures we believe that we enrich each other's lives and the solutions we create.

Practical info

Start: 17th of August - 1 year parental leave

Work extent: Full time, flexible office hours

Location: Stockholm or Malmö

We give you the possibility to work from home three days a week! If that suits you, that is - our office is always open.

Apply sooner than later since our process will be closed as soon as a match has been found. If you have questions regarding the position please contact caroline.andersson@bombayworks.se.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Vill du vara med och bygga framtidens datalösningar inom AI, machine learning och datalager?




Nexer Insight, partner till marknadsledande molnplattformar, där innovation inom data och AI står i fokus, söker nu en Data Engineer som vill bli en del av vårt team. Hos oss får du arbeta i en engagerad och kunnig miljö tillsammans med spännande kunder, i varierande och utmanande uppdrag – samtidigt som du erbjuds goda möjligheter till utveckling och förmåner.




Kanske är du vår nästa “Data Ninja”




Hos Nexer Insight blir du en viktig del av ett bolag som präglas av entreprenörskap och nytänkande. Hos Nexer Insight, partner med databricks, arbetar vi med den senaste tekniken från Microsoft. Här får du möjlighet att pusha gränserna inom data och molnteknik, experimentera med nya verktyg och omsätta cutting-edge tech till lösningar som skapar verkligt affärsvärde. Här möts data engineering och data science – och du är med och driver lösningar från data till affärsnytta.




I rollen som Data Engineer kommer du att:

Designa, bygga och vidareutveckla skalbara och högpresterande datalösningar
Arbeta end-to-end med moderna dataplattformar, datamodeller och pipelines
Samarbeta nära kunder och omsätta affärsbehov till smarta tekniska lösningar
Säkerställa robust arkitektur med fokus på datakvalitet, tillförlitlighet och prestanda
Jobba i framkant med den senaste tekniken inom Data och Analytics
Arbeta med datainhämtning, transformation och lagring
Vara med och driva våra kunders resa mot datadrivna arbetssätt genom innovativa molnlösningar




Vi tror att du:

Har stark erfarenhet av att arbeta med molnplattformar som Azure, AWS eller GCP
Har flera års hands-on erfarenhet av ETL och dataintegration, exempelvis med Azure Data Factory, Databricks/Fabric, DBT eller Snowflake
Är van vid att arbeta med moderna dataplattformar som Data Lakes eller Lakehouse-arkitektur
Har mycket goda kunskaper i SQL och trivs med att jobba nära data
Har erfarenhet av informationsmodellering och dimensionell modellering
Har god förståelse för DevOps-principer, versionshantering i Git och CI/CD-flöden
Kommunicerar obehindrat på både svenska och engelska




Det är meriterande om du även har erfarenhet av:

Modernisering av dataplattformar
Tabular-modeller och DAX
Data Vault-modellering
Att sätta upp infrastruktur i Azure, AWS eller GCP

Title: Trainee - Commercial Operations & Insights
Description:
Purpose of the Role

NORNORM is scaling across Europe, and our commercial and delivery teams work from one shared set of data. This is a development role for someone early in their career who wants to grow into real ownership. You'll start by learning and supporting the commercial systems and performance dashboards the teams across four regions rely on, and over time take on more of running them yourself. It's an analytical, hands-on role with a clear path from supported work to genuine ownership. It will suit someone who enjoys making systems run cleanly and turning data into decisions the business acts on.




Job description

You'll run, and over time own, the day-to-day of our core commercial systems and produce the KPIs and dashboards our regional teams and leadership review on an ongoing basis. We work with AI tools as part of how we operate, and we're looking for someone keen to build on that. As you ramp, you'll take on more independent ownership and support the build of new capabilities as the function grows, working closely with commercial, delivery and supply teams across Europe.




Key responsibilities:

Run and maintain our core commercial systems day to day: configuration, data quality, automation and integrations, and the fixes that keep them working for the teams who depend on them.
Build and maintain the KPIs and dashboards in our analytics platform that the regional teams and leadership review.
Produce and progressively automate the performance reporting across all four regions.
Support new builds as the function grows; within sales, retention, delivery, operations, and self–serve analytics.




Success criteria (2026):

You've got up to speed quickly on our systems and ways of working, and you're using new tools, including AI, to do the work better over time.
Our commercial systems run reliably and the teams trust the data in them.
The regional teams can steer on data they trust, available how and when they need it. As much of this as possible runs self-serve and automated, rather than produced by hand each month.
The dashboards and metrics the teams need get delivered and actually adopted.




What we're looking for:

This is a graduate trainee position, we expect a bachelor's degree in business, economics, or a related field.
An open, curious mind and real enthusiasm for working with AI and new tools. You need to be the kind of person who wants to try new systems, learn fast, and find better ways to work.
Strong analytical skills, comfortable working with data and building dashboards or reports people can rely on.
Self-directed, organized and reliable, with a genuine ownership mindset.
Fluent English; able to work from Stockholm.




A plus, but not required:

Experience administering commercial or CRM systems (configuration, automation, integrations).
SQL or hands-on experience with a BI tool.
Experience in a fast-scaling or international company.




What we offer you:

A 6-month traineeship where we mentor and help you grow as a professional, with the aim to hire on a full-time basis based on the performance success criteria.
Starting salary (based on full-time) ~30,000 SEK/month. We review salary after the probationary period (provanställning) and expect to raise it as you take full ownership.




If you are driven, determined and want to grow within an exciting scaleup, this role is for you. Come and join us!

Title: Testledare
Description:
Ska-krav:


Dokumenterad erfarenhet som testledare i större IT-initiativ/program
Erfarenhet av att driva test i miljöer utan dedikerade testteam


vErfarenhet av komplexa integrationsmiljöer (flera system och externa parter)


Erfarenhet av release management / releaseplanering
Förmåga att arbeta självständigt och driva arbete framåt
Teknisk bakgrund (integration, systemarkitektur, utveckling eller motsvarande ingenjörsnivå)
Förmåga att bryta ner krav till testbara scenarion och tydliga instruktioner
Mycket god strukturerings- och dokumentationsförmåga
Minst 10 års arbetslivserfarenhet inom området
Flytande svenska i tal och skrift


Meriterande:


Erfarenhet av arbete med externa underhållsleverantörer
Erfarenhet av EAM-/underhållssystem
Erfarenhet av förändringsledning kopplat till införande
Erfarenhet av miljöer med flera parallella lösningar (interim + målbild)


Var god förklara hur du uppfyller alla kraven.
