Title: Senior Data Analyst (Stockholm)
Description:
Xebia is a global AI-first, digital transformation, and engineering partner. With over 25 years of experience and a team of 5,000 professionals across 16 countries, we help organizations design and build scalable products, platforms, and data-driven solutions.







We specialize in Artificial Intelligence, Data and Cloud, Intelligent Automation, and Digital Products, combining deep technical expertise with a strong focus on engineering excellence and a people-first culture.




In the CEE region, we’re a team of nearly 1,000 experts delivering modern applications, data platforms, and AI solutions for clients such as McLaren, Aviva, Deloitte, Spotify, Disney, ING, UPS, Tesco, Truecaller, AllSaints, Volotea, Schmitz Cargobull, Allegro, InPost, and many, many more. We work with leading technologies including AWS, Azure, GCP, Databricks, and Snowflake, and combine strong engineering culture with a consulting mindset and a continuous focus on growth and knowledge sharing.




You will be:

owning the measurement of recommendation experiments by designing A/B tests, defining success metrics, and delivering readouts that explain not just whether something worked but why,
investigating model and system behavior, including ranking quality, retrieval coverage, exposure and feedback effects, and the gaps between offline metrics and online outcomes,
building the dashboards, data models, and self-service tooling the team relies on to monitor recommendation health, while leaving them in a state the team can maintain after the engagement,
collaborating with engineers and PMs to scope analytical questions clearly by clarifying objectives and sizing opportunities before they turn into long projects,
proactively surfacing insights, such as unusually behaving segments, drifting metrics, and counterfactuals worth testing.




Your profile:

working from the office in Stockholm (mandatory),
5+ years in an analytics or data science role, with a track record of operating independently,
direct experience with recommendation, search, ranking, ads, or another large-scale ML-driven product,
strong SQL and comfort with large datasets (we work in BigQuery),
fluency in Python for analysis,
a real grasp of experimentation - A/B testing, statistical significance, and the common traps (peeking, interference, novelty effects),
an instinct to clarify the objective before reaching for a solution, and the discipline to separate what the data shows from what you'd like it to show,
clear communication: you can take a messy analysis and land a single, defensible takeaway for any audience, from an engineer to a director,
a very good understanding and application of advanced statistics.




Nice to have:

familiarity with recommender systems internals - retrieval, ranking, candidate generation, offline vs. online evaluation,
exposure to bandits, off-policy evaluation, or causal inference,
experience building data models and dashboards others depend on (dbt, Looker, or similar).







Recruitment Process:

CV review – HR call – Technical Interview – Client Interview – Decision

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Join EQT as a Data Engineer in the Client Tech Value Stream, where you will build and scale our modern data platform. You will work across the full data lifecycle, enabling reliable, high-quality data products that support decision-making across the business.

About The Client Tech Value Stream Team

The Client Tech Value Stream focuses on delivering technology and data solutions that support client, investor, and fund-related processes. The team works closely with engineering, product, CRM, and business stakeholders to build scalable platforms that enable insights and operational efficiency across EQT.

About The Role

You will play a key role in designing, building, and operating EQT’s cloud-based data platform. You will work across ingestion, transformation, and data modeling to ensure high-quality, reliable, and accessible data.


Design, build, and operate scalable data pipelines across multiple data sources.
Ingest data from APIs, external platforms, Fivetran connectors, and cloud storage.
Develop and maintain data models using dbt on Snowflake.
Structure data around core business entities such as clients, investors, funds, and transactions.
Enable analytics and reporting in tools such as Power BI, Salesforce CRM Analytics, Tableau and our AI stack.
Monitor and troubleshoot pipelines to ensure reliability and performance.
Collaborate with stakeholders across engineering, analytics, CRM, and business teams.
Contribute to platform improvements and modernization of legacy pipelines.
Support infrastructure and access management using GitHub, Terraform, and Snowflake.


About You

You are an experienced Data Engineer who enjoys working across the full data stack and collaborating with diverse teams. You are comfortable working in a modern cloud environment and translating business needs into scalable data solutions. You are curious about AI and new technology, and can translate both human and machine needs into code.

Essential skills and experience:


Experience building data pipelines and working with cloud data platforms.
Experience collaborating with nontechnical stakeholders for requirements and maintenance.
Strong SQL skills and experience with data modeling for usage by reporting tools and LLM models.
Experience with Snowflake and dbt or similar tools.
Experience integrating data from multiple systems such as APIs and SaaS platforms.
Familiarity with data visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau.
Experience with version control and infrastructure tools such as Git and Terraform.


Nice to have:


Experience with GCP and Fivetran.
Experience working with CRM systems such as Salesforce.
Understanding of financial or private markets data.


What We Offer

You will be part of a collaborative and forward-looking team where data is central to how EQT creates value. We offer opportunities to work with modern technologies, contribute to impactful projects, and grow your skills in a global environment.

Inclusion at EQT

Our vision for EQT employees is to build high performing & engaged teams. Our competitive edge comes from fostering an environment where every individual feels valued, empowered, and motivated to drive business impact. Our commitment to inclusion is not just about fairness; We understand and believe that being a great place to work drives the best performance.At EQT, inclusion is a business imperative and it's embedded into our talent strategy, decision-making, and culture to ensure that every individual and team operates at their full potential. By doing so, we unlock better collaboration, stronger innovation, and superior investment outcomes.

About EQT

EQT is a purpose-driven global investment organization focused on active ownership strategies. With a Nordic heritage and a global mindset, EQT has a track record of over three decades of developing companies across multiple geographies, sectors and strategies. EQT has investment strategies covering all phases of a business’ development, from start-up to maturity. EQT has EUR ‌ 270 billion in total assets under management (EUR ‌ ‌141 billion in fee-generating assets under management), within two business segments – Private Capital and Real Assets.

With its roots in the Wallenberg family’s entrepreneurial mindset and philosophy of long-term ownership, EQT is guided by a set of strong values and a distinct corporate culture. EQT manages and advises funds and vehicles that invest across the world with the mission to future-proof companies, generate attractive returns and make a positive impact with everything EQT does. EQT has offices in more than 25 countries across Europe, Asia and the Americas and has more than 1,900 employees.

More info: www.eqtgroup.com

Follow EQT on LinkedIn, X, YouTube and Instagram

Title: Senior Data Analyst (within financial risk)
Description:
emagine is looking for a Senior Data Analyst with experience from the financial risk area for one of our client within the Banking/Financial industry in Stockholm.

Start: ASAP

Duration: 1 year to start with

Location: Stockholm, 2-3 days/week at the office.

About The Assignment


Analyze consumer credit risk across the full lifecycle — underwriting/decisioning, delinquency, loss trending, and exposure — using SQL, Python/notebooks, and LLM tooling against the companies data platforms
Review and validate data models and visualization tools used to measure consumer performance and built by other teams within the company, identifying risks or limitations in data accuracy and providing your recommendations on how data integrity can be improved.
Independently investigate questions like "why is delinquency moving in this segment?" or "is this decisioning policy performing as intended?" — framing the problem, pulling the data, and forming your own view
Provide 2nd-line challenge to first-line underwriting and credit decisioning: scorecard cutoffs, policy rules, affordability, champion/challenger outcomes
Conduct sample testing of credit decisions and delinquent accounts
Build and maintain risk-monitoring queries, dashboards, and notebooks that make the whole team faster
Support counterparty risk monitoring and data preparation for quarterly risk reporting and risk appetite work
Turn analysis into clear, decision-ready deliverables for management, committees, and regulators


Your profile


Strong data analyst. You're comfortable diving into large, complex, and imperfectly documented datasets — and you can tell when the numbers are wrong. Independent SQL from day one; Python/notebooks (Pandas, visualization) for analysis.
Solid consumer credit risk foundation. You understand the credit lifecycle — and crucially the underwriting/decisioning front end: decision engines, policy rules, scorecards/cutoffs, affordability, as well as delinquency and PD/LGD.
Independent thinker. You can take an ambiguous question, structure it, and arrive at a defensible opinion you'll stand behind — including with stakeholders who disagree.
Clear communicator. You're a detailed thinker who can summarize at the level a business leader or regulator can act on, in both verbal and written format
Self-directed and curious. Small team, broad mandate — you scope your own work, move fast, and close loops without hand-holding.


Nice to have


Experience in a 2nd- or 3rd-line function (risk, audit, controls) or a consulting-style role where you've ramped quickly on new topics, engaged stakeholders, and developed your own point of view
Track record of producing deliverables for regulator or senior-management visibility
Familiarity with automated consumer decisioning systems that make real-time credit risk assessments in high volume.

Title: Business IT Analyst
Description:
About Justera Group

Justera Group is a leading Swedish IT consulting company specializing in building high-performing technology teams and delivering end-to-end IT solutions. We help organizations accelerate their digital transformation through expert consultancy, software development, and talent solutions. Our consultants work on impactful projects across industries, helping clients achieve sustainable business success through innovation and technology.

About The Role

We are currently looking for a Senior Business IT Analyst to join an exciting assignment with one of Sweden's leading automotive companies in Södertälje.

In this role, you will be responsible for identifying and analyzing information used across business processes and applications, translating business needs into clear requirements, and supporting implementation initiatives. You will work closely with stakeholders across multiple functions and global teams to ensure business and technical requirements are aligned and effectively delivered.

The assignment also includes aspects of data privacy and information governance, making it ideal for candidates with experience in regulatory and compliance-related initiatives.

Responsibilities


Analyze business processes, information flows, and application usage
Gather, document, and manage business and system requirements
Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to define solutions
Support implementation and continuous improvement initiatives
Ensure alignment between business needs and IT solutions
Contribute to data privacy and information governance activities
Work closely with global cross-functional teams within the organization


Required Qualifications


Academic degree in a relevant field
Extensive experience as a Business IT Analyst or similar role
Experience working within automotive projects
Previous experience working with our automotive client in Södertälje
Strong analytical and stakeholder management skills
Excellent communication and documentation abilities


Meritorious Qualifications


Experience within information governance, including legal, architecture, or information security perspectives
Experience working with Data Privacy initiatives
Knowledge of vehicle electrical systems and/or embedded systems
C/CE driver's license
Experience working in global and cross-functional environments


What We Offer


A dynamic and innovative workplace
Opportunities to work on impactful projects with leading industry clients
Collaboration with talented and experienced professionals
Competitive compensation and benefits package
Continuous learning and professional development opportunities
Flexible hybrid working model supporting work-life balance
25 days of annual leave
Annual wellness allowance of 3,500 SEK


If you are a driven and experienced Business IT Analyst with a passion for the automotive industry, we would love to hear from you.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Playground Data


Stockholm
Hybrid


Data Engineer

Some data problems are neat and tidy. Ours usually aren’t. They are messy, evolving, and tied to real decisions. We work closely with our clients to understand what actually needs to be solved and build from there.

At Playground Data, we build modern data platforms for analytics, AI and machine learning. We are a collaborative team that values solid engineering and shared growth. Our engineers take ownership and work closely with clients to deliver high-quality solutions.

What you'll actually do

You’ll work closely with our clients on high-impact initiatives where data is critical to the business. Many of our engagements involve greenfield builds or major platform transformations. This role suits engineers who enjoy creating structure from complexity and taking end-to-end ownership.

In Practice, You’ll


Design and build modern data platforms from scratch.
Ingest, model, and structure data so it’s usable for analytics, AI and ML workloads.
Work close to production, with growing responsibility for operating, evolving, and improving platforms over time.
Contribute internally by improving how we work, helping build and manage our data engineering frameworks, shaping our technical approaches, being part of presales and supporting Playground’s continued growth.


You’ll never sit idle, there’s always something meaningful to build, improve, or untangle.

Who are you?

You’ve been building data platforms for a few years and know that data doesn’t magically organize itself. Python and SQL are part of your daily toolkit, and you’ve wrestled with at least one modern data stack (AWS, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake etc.) and lived to tell the tale.

You Would Probably Describe Yourself As Someone Who

Understands data engineering in practice: You know how data needs to flow, scale, and hold up in production for analytics and AI and ML workloads.

Enjoys building from scratch: Greenfield work doesn’t scare you, it motivates you.

Stays calm when things are unclear: You don’t panic when requirements are fuzzy and the system diagram needs three whiteboards.

Is comfortable in a consultant role: You handle customer dialogue well, can contribute in presales, and help guide clients through difficult decisions.

As a person, you’re humble, genuinely curious about technology, and motivated, while knowing that asking for help is a strength, not a weakness. Friendly, curious, and playful.

You must be based in the Stockholm area and have a valid work permit for Sweden (no visa sponsorship or relocation).

Buzzword bingo: AWS, Azure, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake, DBT, Python, SQL, Terraform

Language requirement

You need to be fluent in Swedish and able to use it confidently in a professional environment. Swedish citizenship is required.

Why? Most of our collaborations and client projects are conducted in Swedish, and several of our clients have formal requirements for both Swedish citizenship and full professional fluency in the language.

Let's Play!

Applying to us is a breeze!


Upload your resumé or use your Linkedin profile
Answer the choice-questions (no "please-rewrite-your-resume" nonsense, promise)
Feel free to include links to your portfolio, GitHub or similar if you have stuff you're proud of that you want to share with us


Note

Playground Group does not provide visa sponsorship or relocation support.

For this role applicants must have a swedish citizenship

Welcome to Playground!

At Playground, you work on real, complex assignments for well-established and ambitious clients, not bench projects or short-lived experiments. Our teams are trusted with production systems, platforms, and data that actually matter. Here growth comes from ownership, curiosity, and doing the work properly. You’re trusted to think, contribute, and keep getting better, one meaningful step at a time.

Playground brings together specialized domains under one umbrella, with a shared culture and way of working, and easy access to broad technical expertise when it helps move the work forward.

Is comfortable in a consultant role: You handle customer dialogue well, can contribute in presales, and help guide clients through difficult decisions.

As a person, you’re humble, genuinely curious about technology, and motivated, while knowing that asking for help is a strength, not a weakness. Friendly, curious, and playful.

You must be based in the Stockholm area and have a valid work permit for Sweden (no visa sponsorship or relocation).

Buzzword bingo: AWS, Azure, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake, DBT, Python, SQL, Terraform

Language requirement

You need to be fluent in Swedish and able to use it confidently in a professional environment. Swedish citizenship is required.

Why? Most of our collaborations and client projects are conducted in Swedish, and several of our clients have formal requirements for both Swedish citizenship and full professional fluency in the language.

Let's Play!

Applying to us is a breeze!

  • Upload your resumé or use your Linkedin profile
  • Answer the choice-questions (no "please-rewrite-your-resume" nonsense, promise)
  • Feel free to include links to your portfolio, GitHub or similar if you have stuff you're proud of that you want to share with us

Note

Playground Group does not provide visa sponsorship or relocation support.

For this role applicants must have a swedish citizenship

Welcome to Playground!

At Playground, you work on real, complex assignments for well-established and ambitious clients, not bench projects or short-lived experiments. Our teams are trusted with production systems, platforms, and data that actually matter. Here growth comes from ownership, curiosity, and doing the work properly. You’re trusted to think, contribute, and keep getting better, one meaningful step at a time.

Playground brings together specialized domains under one umbrella, with a shared culture and way of working, and easy access to broad technical expertise when it helps move the work forward.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
⚡️ What We Do

Flower is Flexible Power. We are a next-gen energy company leveraging AI and machine learning to make renewable energy stable and always available – even when the sun isn’t shining and the wind isn’t blowing.

Through smart optimization and trading of energy assets like wind and solar farms, battery systems, and EV chargers, we make renewable energy reliable and predictable, leading the charge towards the energy system of tomorrow.

🌟 Who We Are

Tech company at heart – purpose in our DNA. Flower consists of a diverse group of innovative individuals with a strong desire to improve the state of the world.

At Flower, we believe trust, collaboration and diversity are essential to not only create an inclusive work environment, but also drive career growth. By embracing varying perspectives, we allow creativity and progress to flourish.

To accelerate towards our goal of becoming the pioneering force powering the energy system of tomorrow, we are now looking for a passionate and skilled Senior Software Engineer to join our Asset Optimisation domain.

👩‍💻 About the role

As a Data Engineer, you will play a pivotal role in guiding the future of the Wholesale squad within the Asset Optimization domain. Our mission is to master short-term energy-intensive markets, including Intraday Continuous.

You will join a small, highly talented team at a moment of expansion and help us build robust data pipelines that will push the performance of our strategies to the next level, guarantee observability of our trading activities, and enable the discoverability of new opportunities. Your contribution will help the team scale with the company’s growth, and excel in an ever-changing energy landscape.

What You’ll Do


Contribute to the backbone of our data ecosystem: Design and operate robust, low-latency data pipelines that reliably feed our trading algorithms and analytics systems.
Model and monitor trading intelligence: Develop and maintain efficient data models, analytical dashboards, and monitoring frameworks that bring clarity to the team’s trading performance and system health.
Design for reliability: Automate data quality checks, fallbacks, and alerting systems that prevent outages, protect trading operations, and ensure compliance.
Accelerate backtesting and research: Work alongside extremely ambitious and talented colleagues to design data pipelines and models that enable high-fidelity backtesting and rapid hypothesis testing.
Drive technical excellence: Contribute to code quality, observability, and maintainability; champion good engineering patterns and regulatory compliance in everything you build.


Who You Are


Experienced data engineer: 4+ years of experience designing and operating production-grade data systems in Python and SQL, ideally in time-sensitive or high-availability environments.
Engineering leader: Strong programming skills (especially Python and SQL) and deep expertise in building robust data pipelines in cloud-native solutions. You are fluent in IaC practices and tools (Terraform, CloudFormation) and experienced in designing scalable, fault-tolerant systems.
Full-stack problem solver: Comfortable owning everything in the data lifecycle, from ingestion to insight, from analytics to operational readiness, with the mindset that no problem is “out of scope” if it advances the team’s mission.
Great communicator: Adept at explaining technical concepts to both technical peers and business stakeholders, helping align decisions across the organization.
Bonus points: You have working experience in or around trading desks, especially with limit orderbook data. You have working experience in the energy sector, understand market data structures, or have experience enabling algorithmic or live trading environments.


📍 Location

Our beautiful office is located in the heart of Södermalm, just a short walk from Slussen subway station. We encourage in-office collaboration but support a hybrid work model.

✉️ Apply

Our corporate language is English, as we have about 30 nationalities in the office. We therefore appreciate it if you could submit your CV in English.

Throughout the recruitment process you will meet with the Talent Partner, the Head of Wholesale, the Team and VP of Engineering.

We look forward to hearing from you!

—-

We kindly but firmly decline any engagement in recruitment assistance for our hiring processes. This includes partnership offers or the sale of recruitment tools.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
We’re a fast-growing, product-led e-commerce platform undergoing a major transformation of our data and engineering landscape.




As part of this journey, we’re building a modern data platform that not only supports analytics, but also powers applications, APIs, workflows, and AI-driven systems across the business.




🚀 The Role




We’re looking for a Data Engineer who operates at the intersection of data engineering and software engineering.




This is not a traditional pipeline-only role.




You will:

Build data pipelines and infrastructure
Develop backend services, APIs, and workflows around data
Contribute to production systems that consume and serve data

👉 Think: data as a product, not just data movement




🔧 Key Responsibilities

Build and maintain scalable data pipelines (ingestion → transformation → serving)
Develop APIs and backend services that expose and operationalise data
Design and implement data-driven workflows and automation systems
Work across both data platform and application layers
Contribute to data modelling (dbt + warehouse design)
Ensure system reliability, scalability, and observability
Collaborate with product and engineering teams to support real use cases
Own the full lifecycle: design → build → deploy → monitor




🧱 Tech Stack

Languages: Python, SQL
Data & Modelling: dbt, BigQuery
Cloud: GCP
Infrastructure: Docker, Terraform
Ingestion: Airbyte (or similar)




✅ Requirements




Core Skills

Strong SQL and data modelling fundamentals
Solid Python experience (pipelines, services, or backend development)
Experience building data pipelines AND software/data services
Understanding of cloud-based data platforms




🔥 Critical

✅ Experience building applications, APIs, or workflows around data

✅ Ability to operate beyond the warehouse (e.g. services, integrations, automation)

✅ Understanding of how data flows into real production systems




Engineering & Infrastructure

Experience with Docker (containerisation)
Familiarity with Terraform or other IaC tools
Knowledge of data ingestion patterns




👤 Ideal Candidate




You are:

A hybrid data + software engineer
Comfortable building both pipelines AND backend/data services
Strong in system design and architecture thinking
A problem solver who focuses on fundamentals, not just tools
Able to work in low-structure, fast-moving environments
Product-minded — you understand how data is used, not just how it’s built




🌱 Nice to Have

Experience with event-driven or streaming systems
Exposure to AI/ML or data-enabled applications
Experience building internal tools or automation frameworks




💡 What You’ll Get

Build a modern data platform from the ground up
Work across data engineering, backend engineering, and infrastructure
High ownership and autonomy in a growing team
Direct impact on real product and business systems
Exposure to AI-driven and automated workflows




📈 Career Growth

Clear progression toward:

Data Platform Engineer
Backend / Distributed Systems Engineer
Technical / Architecture leadership roles
Show more

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
What will you do?

As a Data Engineer at Rebtel, you will do more than build pipelines; you'll help shape the future of our data platform and influence how data is used across the company. At Rebtel, data sits at the heart of how we build products, understand customers, optimize growth, and make decisions.




This is a hands-on engineering role with significant ownership. You'll work across our modern data stack, build scalable data products, and help define the architecture that supports analytics, automation, and future AI initiatives.




If you're excited by clean architecture, modern data engineering practices, and solving meaningful business problems at scale; if you care about data quality and believe trust in data is built through engineering discipline; if you enjoy solving complex problems and turning ambiguity into practical solutions; and if you're excited to experiment with new technologies and approaches, we'd love to talk.

Areas Of Ownership

Design, build, and maintain scalable, reliable data pipelines that power our Snowflake data platform
Integrate data from a wide range of business and product systems
Enable data activation across customer-facing and operational platforms, including CRM, customer support, and marketing tools
Develop robust monitoring, alerting, and observability practices to ensure platform reliability
Troubleshoot, optimize, and continuously improve data workflows and platform performance
Design and implement scalable ELT workflows using modern data engineering practices
Build well-structured dimensional models (facts and dimensions) that enable self-service analytics and reporting
Implement automated testing and quality controls throughout the data lifecycle
Ensure data is accurate, accessible, and trusted across the organization
Work closely with analysts, product managers, engineers, marketers, and business stakeholders to understand data needs and deliver impactful solutions
Help translate complex business challenges into scalable data products and datasets
Support decision-making by making data easier to discover, understand, and use
Evaluate emerging technologies, frameworks, and industry best practices
Run proof-of-concepts and contribute to strategic technology decisions
Help define the future architecture of Rebtel's data platform
Continuously improve engineering standards, tooling, and developer experience

Requirements

You are an excellent communicator and collaborator. We work in English, but you will hear many languages in our Stockholm office
3+ years of experience in Data Engineering or a similar role
Strong SQL skills and experience building production-grade data pipelines
Experience with modern ELT methodologies and data warehousing concepts
Experience integrating data from multiple systems and sources
Experience with dbt, Python, AWS, or similar cloud-based data platforms
Strong understanding of data modeling and analytics engineering best practices
Ability to operate independently and take ownership of technical solutions
Experience working in fast-growing technology or digital product companies is a plus
It's a bonus if you've been exposured to customer analytics, marketing analytics, experimentation, or machine learning workflows
Experience with BI tools such as Looker, Tableau, or Power BI is a plus
It's a plus with a degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field




Why Rebtel?

Rebtel has been connecting people across borders for nearly 20 years. Today, we’re profitable, growing, and at a pivotal moment in our journey. As we enter our next phase, we’re building an organisation designed for speed, ownership, and real impact, where every role contributes directly to shaping what comes next.

This is a place with global ambition and a strong foundation, where ideas move quickly and decisions matter. You won’t get lost in layers of process or slow-moving structures. Instead, you’ll find the space to take ownership, collaborate across teams, and make meaningful contributions from day one. Based in Stockholm, we bring together a diverse, international team united by a shared purpose: to simplify the way people connect worldwide.




Benefits

At Rebtel, you are the most important asset and we strive to provide a comprehensive package of benefits and perks that enhance your well-being and work experience. Here are some of the things you can expect from us:

Pension Plan
Health Checkups, Influenza shots and Private Medical Insurance
Dental Insurance
Occupational insurance
Wellness allowance (5,000 SEK)
Discount on gym memberships
Bonus program
Extra parental pay
30 days annual vacation
Monday breakfasts
Relocation Support, if you're joining us from afar, we'll assist you in making a smooth transition.




We are Rebtel. We come from all around the world to create products for anyone who has crossed a border. We believe in equal opportunity and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
We're Looking for a Data Engineer




At VetFamily, we’re reimagining how data engineering drives intelligence and innovation in the veterinary industry. We’re seeking a Data Engineer who combines deep technical skills with strong communication instincts – someone who can design scalable data architectures, guide engineering direction, and empower cross-functional teams to turn data into decisions.

This is a role for a hands-on engineer who is comfortable writing SQL and orchestrating pipelines in DBT and Airflow. You’ll have the independence to make architecture decisions, prioritize strategically, and shape how data fuels our products and platform. You’ll also be encouraged to collaborate widely—gathering input from product managers, analysts, and business stakeholders—to ensure our data systems serve real-world needs.




About the Role

As a Data Engineer at VetFamily, you will play a key role in modernizing and scaling our global data platform - one that processes billions in transactions across EMEA, the US, and Asia. You’ll take ownership of the technical direction, ensuring the platform’s performance, reliability, and scalability support our rapidly growing ecosystem of clinics, suppliers, and partners. You will coordinate between our Commercial team, Data Scientists and Data Engineers and external partners.

Our data stack is modern and still evolving: API/sFPT, Airflow, python, AWS s3 & rds, DBT, Snowflake, Power BI and Metabase. You’ll influence how we evolve this architecture to deliver robust data products, streamline data flow, and enable advanced analytics and AI applications across the company as we growth.




What You’ll Do

Pipeline Ownership – Design, implement, and optimize ETL/ELT pipelines using DBT, Airflow, python and AWS components.
Cross-Functional Collaboration – Work closely with product, data, and engineering teams to gather requirements and translate them into robust, maintainable solutions.
Technical Leadership – Mentor team members, establish best practices, and promote engineering excellence and data reliability.
Stakeholder Partnership – Engage with business and technical stakeholders to understand priorities, clarify goals, and ensure alignment across functions.
Decision-Making & Autonomy – Drive initiatives independently, make informed trade-offs, and balance delivery speed with technical rigor.
AI-Enhanced Productivity – Use modern tools like ChatGPT, Cody, or Cursor to accelerate development, documentation, and troubleshooting.




About You

You have strong experience designing and managing modern data architectures in cloud environments (AWS preferred).
Deep expertise in SQL, Python, DBT, AirFlow, and data warehousing solutions such as Snowflake.
Proven ability to lead data initiatives end-to-end—from discovery and design to deployment and maintenance.
Strong understanding of data modeling, ETL frameworks, and DevOps practices for data.
Excellent communication skills—you can explain technical trade-offs to non-technical stakeholders clearly.
You thrive in autonomy but are humble enough to seek advice and input when needed.
You bring strategic thinking combined with hands-on problem-solving.




Why Join VetFamily?

Make an Impact – Shape a global data platform used across thousands of veterinary clinics.
Lead with Autonomy – Steer architecture and data strategy while enjoying the trust and independence to execute.
Modern Stack, Modern Mindset – Work with cutting-edge tools, cloud-native infrastructure, and AI-augmented workflows.
Growth Opportunity – Build leadership experience within a scaling, international organization.
Fantastic Office & Flexibility – Enjoy hybrid work and a vibrant office in central Stockholm.
Mission-Driven Culture – Contribute to improving animal healthcare through better data and technology.




Apply now and help shape the future of veterinary care through scalable systems, strong data foundations, and thoughtful leadership.

 

Show more

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
In this role, you will work with our customers on various data engineering projects. This role

is perfect for those with a good foundation in data engineering, and a passion for operational

excellence in data management and analytics. You will design, build, and maintain efficient,

scalable data pipelines and work closely with data scientists, analysts, and stakeholders to

deliver customized data solutions that drive impactful business decisions.

Develop and manage scalable data pipelines, ensuring efficient and reliable data flows.

Implement processes and solutions to improve data quality, accuracy, and consistency.

Create scripts and tools to automate repetitive tasks, improving efficiency and reducing manual work.

Build and maintain data integrations, including file, messaging, and API-based systems.

Develop data models tailored to meet business requirements.

Contribute to implementing infrastructure as code solutions.

Collaborate with data scientists, architects, and software developers to support the

creation of data architecture and governance models.

Enhance the performance of existing data pipelines, optimizing queries and indexing for faster data retrieval.

Implement security measures to protect data from unauthorized access.

Ensure compliance with data governance policies and regulatory requirements, maintaining documentation and data lineage.

Monitor cost efficiency and identify areas for optimization.

You are passionate about building data products and enabling data-driven decisions.

You enjoy sharing knowledge and learning new things in the data, analytics & AI area.

You want to develop and maintain robust, scalable data and analytics systems.

You have the ability to work both independently and collaboratively within a team environment.

Fluent in English both written and verbal.

You are eager to learn and have a basic understanding of working with data sets

You are gaining experience with ETL workflows and excited to improve your skills in

streamlining these processes.

You have a foundational knowledge of big data concepts, cloud technologies, and data modeling.

Your skills in SQL and Python are strong, with a focus on writing efficient queries.

You are gaining hands-on experience with cloud platforms like AWS, Azure, or Google

Cloud and are keen to expand your knowledge.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
VVi söker Data Engineers

Vi rekryterar inte till en specifik roll, utan söker ständigt efter nya duktiga kollegor med en positiv inställning och en vilja att lära och dela med sig av sina kunskaper. Med den inställningen vet vi att du kan lyckas som konsult hos ten10. Därför lyssnar vi på dig och ser, utifrån din erfarenhet och kompetens, vilken typ av spännande uppdrag där du trivs och utvecklas bäst.

Hos ten10 blir du en del av en organisation som värdesätter den personliga relationen, där varje individ får vara med och påverka både sin egen och ten10s utveckling.

Vi tror på att bygga ett bolag av olika personer som bidrar på olika sätt till att vi alla trivs och lyckas. Utöver att göra skillnad i de uppdrag vi gemensamt väljer utifrån ditt intresse och din utveckling är du en viktig del av ett framåtlutat bolag där våra medarbetare alltid står i fokus.

Hos oss ser man…

…positivt på utmaningar

…positivt på samarbete men klarar också av att vara självgående när det behövs

…positivt på nya teknologier, data och komplexa problem

…positivt på kompetensutveckling och att ständigt bli bättre

…positivt på att vara med och påverka

Att vara en konsult hos ten10

Vi vill ständigt bli bättre och kunna hjälpa fler av våra kunder i deras utmaningar. Vi därför söker vi nu fler Data Engineers som vill vara med på en spännande resa, tillsammans med oss och våra kunder.

Som Data Engineer-konsult hos ten10 har du en nyckelroll i att demokratisera data ute hos våra kunder. Tillgänglig, tillförlitlig och välstrukturerad data är grunden för både Analytics, Machine Learning och AI-lösningar och där gör du verklig skillnad.

Våra uppdrag varierar, men gemensamt är att du arbetar nära både verksamhet, analytiker, data scientists och utvecklingsteam för att skapa hållbara dataplattformar och datadrivna lösningar.

I din roll som Data Engineer…


flytande i både Svenska och Engelska


…har du vilja och intresse av att lösa kundernas problem och se det som en intressant utmaning.

…har du minst tre års arbetslivserfarenhet inom rollen eller liknande roller


har du gärna erfarenhet eller ett stort intresse av Databricks


…är du skicklig i ett skriptspråk som Python.

…gillar du att bygga pipelines med (med ApacheSpark, Flink eller liknande).

…har du en djup förståelse för systemdesign, datastrukturer och algoritmer och kunskapen att tillämpa dem för att skapa pragmatiska lösningar.

…har du erfarenhet av molnplattformar som AWS, Azure, GCP eller liknande.

…har du erfarenhet av av big data-teknologier som Hadoop, Kafka, SQL, Spark m.m.

…är du noggrann men också lösningsorienterad

…trivs du att jobba i agila team och gärna också i en mer ledande roll.

…har du ett stort intresse för Data och Analytics området i allmänhet

Vad får man ut av att vara en ten10are

Hos ten10 är det viktigt att hitta en sund balans mellan privatliv och arbetsliv. Vi arbetar tillsammans för att hitta ett upplägg som fungerar i din vardag – både när det gäller uppdrag, arbetssätt och ersättning.

Vi erbjuder en av marknadens mest attraktiva lönemodeller, där du får tydlig kompensation för det värde du skapar.

Vi lägger också stor vikt vid gemenskap. Du får möjlighet att dela erfarenheter, idéer och perspektiv när vi ses på AWs, resor, julbord och andra gemensamma aktiviteter.

Vi pratar inte bara om att vi bryr oss om våra medarbetare – hela vår verksamhet bygger på dem. Vår ambition är att skapa rätt förutsättningar för just dig.
Show more

Title: Senior Data Analyst (Relocate to Cyprus)
Description:
We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join our Player Care team at Scorewarrior. The Player Care team is at the forefront of providing customer service to our players, as well as safeguarding the community integrity through enforcing Fair & Safe Play, improving moderation efforts, and ensuring operational excellence while at it.




Scorewarrior is a game developer and publisher based in Limassol, Cyprus. The company focuses on the 4X strategy genre, with an ambitious goal to grow its hit game, Total Battle, to become the #1 strategy game in the world, and this role is key to achieving this. Our team consists of 200+ passionate, talented, self-driven, and continually developing professionals who love making high-quality games.




Our product: We are currently working on Total Battle — an MMO 4X strategy game with a deep political system and lots of player interaction. Players take on the role of a commander and need to develop their city, forge alliances, fight against monsters, and conquer other players. We're constantly developing new game mechanics, and the project is continuing to grow.




At the heart of our Player Care department, our mission is to excite our players with exceptional customer care, ensuring they feel valued and supported throughout their journey with us.




What you’ll do

Build and maintain the single "Source of Truth" semantic data layer for the entire Player Care department, enabling non-technical leads to perform ad-hoc analysis.
Design, build, and maintain reliable metrics dashboards tailored to the operational needs of different Support and Trust & Safety teams.
Ensure all operational metrics (CSAT, SLA, etc.) are clearly defined, reliable, and calculated correctly across all reports.
Assist and mentor the support team with SQL query composition and data literacy.
Conduct A/B tests to measure the effect of Support and Trust & Safety initiatives on relevant departmental and broader business metrics.
Analyze the correlation between support quality, bug-related friction, and subsequent player behavior, with a strong focus on VIP cohorts.
Partner with the safety team to analyze data for fraud detection, including social engineering and account sharing.
Perform time-series forecasting for workload volume based on the LiveOps calendar to optimize shift headcount and SLA coverage.
As a future ambition, build and maintain an early warning predictive model for VIP player churn.




What you have

Strong SQL skills and data modeling experience
Experience with BI tools (e.g. Tableau or equivalents)
Proven experience in product or operational analytics
Ability to design analytical events and metrics aligned with product goals
Strong analytical thinking and ability to translate data into actionable insights
Confident knowledge of mathematical statistics and probability theory
Hands-on experience with causal inference and A/B testing
Patience and willingness to advocate for data literacy and help operational teams learn basic data concepts




Nice to haves

Hands-on experience with ClickHouse and dbt
Experience analyzing LiveOps features, complex game economies, or VIP player behavior, ideally in mid-core or 4x strategy games.
Experience working with Customer Support or Trust & Safety (fraud detection, toxicity)
Experience working with large player bases and complex event-driven systems




Where you’ll work

We value face-to-face communication, and we strongly believe that genuine human interactions move us closer to our ambitious goal. This is part of our culture, and this is why this job offers no remote or hybrid work opportunities.

You will be working in Limassol, Cyprus. If you’re not from around here, fear not! Our dedicated relocation team will provide comprehensive support helping you and your family move to Limassol.




What we offer

The best team in the world!
An excellent remuneration package, including a 13th salary.
Fully-covered relocation to Limassol, Cyprus for you and your family.
A modern and sunny office space close to the sea.
Private health insurance for you and your family members.
A monthly school allowance for your children at pre-school and school-age.
Lunch compensation.
Personal development opportunities paid for by the company: professional and language courses, books, etc.
Bonuses for outstanding results, years worked, getting married, and the birth of a child.
Monthly office parties and epic, annual offsite company birthday and Christmas parties.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Vill du bygga den datadrivna motorn som formar framtidens bankupplevelse?

På Avanza drivs vi av att göra sparande billigare, bättre och enklare. För att lyckas behöver vi data som inte bara är tillgänglig, utan också korrekt, smart och handlingskraftig. Vi söker nu tre Data Engineers till olika delar av vår verksamhet. Oavsett om du brinner för finansiell precision, avancerad experimentering eller att jobba produktnära med en fot i backend, har vi en plats för dig!




Data Engineering på Avanza

Som data engineer på Avanza får du förvandla rådata till verksamhetsnytta genom robusta pipelines och hållbara datamodeller. Vi arbetar efter data mesh-principer, vilket innebär att du både tillhandahåller och konsumerar data i ett decentraliserat landskap. Vår techstack vilar på BigQuery, med Kafka för realtidsströmmar och dbt för transformationslogik.




Du blir del av ett autonomt team med stort mandat att forma både arkitektur och arbetssätt. Vi värdesätter data engineering-hantverket och tillämpar mjukvaruprinciper som spårbarhet och skalbarhet, ofta genom parprogrammering och tätt samarbete över rollgränser.




Våra team

Vi söker förstärkning till följande områden:




Produktnära Data Engineer (Backend-fokus)

Det här är rollen för dig som trivs i skärningspunkten mellan data och traditionell mjukvaruutveckling.

Ditt uppdrag: Du ser till att data flyter sömlöst från produktion till analys genom att röra dig obehindrat i teamets mikrotjänster. Du fungerar som en brygga mellan ren data engineering och backend-logik.




Din Specifika Profil:

Erfarenhet av mjukvaruutveckling (Java) och API-design i distribuerade miljöer.
Du har din tyngdpunkt i data men kan koda applikationslogik när det behövs.
Intresse för systemstöd för monitorering och analys.




Experimentering, analys och personalisering

Här jobbar du i hjärtat av vår satsning på A/B-tester och personalisering.

Ditt Uppdrag: Du blir en viktig pusselbit i teamet för experimentering och rekommendationer, där din specifika expertis är inom experimentdesign, analys och metodutveckling. Du hjälper till att stötta produktteam i deras experimentresor och bygger tekniska lösningar som skalar vårt användande av experimentplattformen Confidence. Du är bryggan som översätter komplex statistik till begripliga insikter, samtidigt som du naturligt bidrar till teamets hela mission.




Din Specifika Profil:

Djup förståelse för A/B-testning, p-värden och proxy-metrics.
Meriterande med studier inom matematik eller statistik.
Intresse för personalisering, rekommendationer och machine learning är ett plus.




Finansiell Business Intelligence

Rollen för dig som vill skapa ordning och reda i komplexa finansiella strömmar.

Ditt uppdrag: Du bygger robusta pipelines och semantiska modeller som förvandlar rådata till en "single source of truth" för legala och operativa siffror. Du säkrar precisionen i de flöden som ligger till grund för företagets ekonomiska styrning.




Din Specifika Profil:

Du förstår debet och kredit samt hur resultat- och balansräkningar hänger ihop.
Erfarenhet av semantisk modellering (t.ex. dbt eller LookML).




Gemensamt för alla roller

Oavsett vilket team du tillhör förväntar vi oss att du har en stabil grundplatta:

Gedigen erfarenhet av SQL, datamodellering samt verktyg som BigQuery och dbt.
Erfarenhet av strömmande data och meddelandeköer (t.ex. Kafka).
En prestigelös inställning till problemlösning där du prioriterar teamets framgång och kunskapsdelning.
Förmågan att läsa och förstå svenska.
Meriterande med kunskap i Python för avancerad data-automation.
Meriterande om du tidigare arbetat i en reglerad miljö såsom bank eller finans.




Redo att bli en av oss?

Vi går igenom ansökningar löpande och ser fram emot att höra mer om dig och din resa!




Ansökan: Klicka på "Ansök"-knappen och bifoga ditt CV eller din LinkedIn-profil.




Erbjudanden från rekryteringsföretag och annonssäljare undanbedes vänligen men bestämt.




Avanza är Sveriges ledande plattform för sparande och placeringar. Vi drivs av att förändra branschen genom att utmana de stora bankerna och försäkringsbolagen på den svenska sparmarknaden. Vi vill vara tjänsten som förenklar och förbättrar spararnas ekonomi, oavsett om man älskar aktier eller bara vill ha ett bra sparande. Vår vision är att skapa en bättre framtid för miljoner människor. Därför står våra kunders bästa alltid i fokus, precis som det borde vara. Affärsidén är enkel – mer till dig, mindre till banken. En idé som gett oss Sveriges nöjdaste sparare 16 år i rad.

Show more

  • En prestigelös inställning till problemlösning där du prioriterar teamets framgång och kunskapsdelning.
  • Förmågan att läsa och förstå svenska.
  • Meriterande med kunskap i Python för avancerad data-automation.
  • Meriterande om du tidigare arbetat i en reglerad miljö såsom bank eller finans.


Redo att bli en av oss?

Vi går igenom ansökningar löpande och ser fram emot att höra mer om dig och din resa!


Ansökan: Klicka på "Ansök"-knappen och bifoga ditt CV eller din LinkedIn-profil.


Erbjudanden från rekryteringsföretag och annonssäljare undanbedes vänligen men bestämt.


Avanza är Sveriges ledande plattform för sparande och placeringar. Vi drivs av att förändra branschen genom att utmana de stora bankerna och försäkringsbolagen på den svenska sparmarknaden. Vi vill vara tjänsten som förenklar och förbättrar spararnas ekonomi, oavsett om man älskar aktier eller bara vill ha ett bra sparande. Vår vision är att skapa en bättre framtid för miljoner människor. Därför står våra kunders bästa alltid i fokus, precis som det borde vara. Affärsidén är enkel – mer till dig, mindre till banken. En idé som gett oss Sveriges nöjdaste sparare 16 år i rad.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Kreditz is transforming how businesses assess credit and risk using real-time data and NextGen Credit Scoring solutions.

As one of Europe’s fastest-growing FinTech scaleups - we’ve earned a place on the Sifted 100: Nordics & Benelux list in both 2024 and 2025 and most recently, we were proud to receive a double win at the Credit Management & Compliance Awards 2025, being named both "Fintech Rocket of the Year" and "Breakthrough of the Year".

With strong growth across the Nordics and Europe, our clients range from leading banks and iGaming giants to fast-moving digital innovators.

About The Position

At Kreditz, data is not just a support function, it is the core of our product. We turn raw bank transactions into real-time insight that powers credit & risk decisions across Europe.

As we scale, we’re strengthening our data foundation and building a modern, reliable, and cost-efficient data platform. We’re now looking for a Data Engineer to join our Product team and take ownership of our Data Warehouse, pipelines, and reporting enablement across the business.

This is a hands-on role for someone who loves building scalable data infrastructure and wants to work close to both product and real business impact.

Your mission is to build and evolve a scalable, reliable, and high-performing data environment that supports both analytics and operational needs.

What Can Kreditz Offer


A once-in-a-lifetime growth journey, from startup to Global scaleup
A journey where everyone in the team plays a crucial role for our company’s success
Fun-loving, driven, and passionate colleagues who support each other’s success
A culture that celebrates progress, humility, and teamwork
We win together
24/7 access to gym, sauna and sports facilities at our office, plus additional wellness allowance
Competitive base salary


RESPONSIBILITES

Some Of The Main Responsibilities You Will Cover


Maintain & Enhance the DWH: Own the architecture of our Data Warehouse, ensuring it is scalable, cost-effective, and optimized for both analytical and operational needs.
Pipeline Automation: Design, build, and maintain robust ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest data from various sources into our AWS ecosystem. Work closely with our MLOPs Engineering team to assist the data pipelines for our AI / ML models
Stakeholder Enablement: Work closely with Product, Finance, and Commercial teams to automate internal reporting and create self-service data environments.
AWS Infrastructure: Leverage and manage AWS services (such as Glue, Redshift, Athena, Lambda, and S3) to ensure a high-performing data environment.
Data Quality & Governance: Implement monitoring and validation frameworks to ensure data integrity and lineage across all systems.
Collaborate: Partner with our DevOps and Backend teams to align infrastructure and ensure smooth data flow across the entire Kreditz platform.


WE ARE LOOKING FOR

We focus on practical engineering skill, strong foundations, and the ability to build systems that last.

In Addition To That We Believe You Have


BSc / MSc in a quantitative subject, i.e., Physics, Engineering, Mathematics, Computer Science or equivalent.
3+ years of experience in Data Engineering or a similar role focused on data infrastructure.
Expert-level SQL skills for complex transformations and performance tuning.
Proficiency in Python for building pipelines and automating tasks.
Strong hands-on experience with AWS (specifically services like Redshift/Snowflake, S3, Glue, and Lambda).
Solid understanding of data warehousing concepts (e.g., Star Schema, Kimball) and modern data stack tools.
Experience with orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow, Dagster) and CI/CD workflows (i.e., GitHub Actions).
Ability to translate complex technical data structures into clear insights for non-technical stakeholders.


Meritorious Skills


Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform).
Experience from FinTech, Open Banking, or working with highly regulated data.
Familiarity with BI tools (e.g., AWS QuickSight, PowerBI...etc)


WHY JOIN NOW?

At Kreditz, you’ll work with data that matters, powering real-time financial decisions across Europe. You’ll have real ownership, real impact, and the opportunity to shape the data foundation of a fast-growing FinTech scaleup.

APPOINTMENT OF ROLE

Q2/Q3 2026

SCOPE

Full-time, permanent employment (tillsvidareanställning)

SALARY

Competitive salary

REPORTING

You will be reporting to the company’s CPO

WORKPLACE

Danderyd, Stockholm

RECRUITMENT PROCESS

Ongoing selection, we review candidates continuously.

Last day for applications 31st March

CRIMINAL RECORDS EXTRACT

As part of our recruitment process we will ask you to present a clean criminal records extract

CONTACT

Lorenzo Puccio, VP Strategic Operations, Lorenzo@kreditz.com

For more information about the role feel free to reach out to our CPO, Spilios Tzouras, spilios.tzouras@kreditz.com

Intelligent credit- & risk decisions made easy with open banking & AI-technology

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Vi har nu ett nytt behov och söker därför en konsult och kollega med nedan kvalifikationer. Tjänsten är en del av vår konsultverksamhet vilket innebär att du blir anställd av oss och arbetar hos kund alt. interna projekt och åtaganden.

Din profil (Skall krav)


Minst 5 års arbetslivserfarenhet i rollen som Data Engineer, där konsult har erfarenhet av att utveckla organisationsövergripande data- och analysplattformar
Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av utveckling av dataflöden, datamodeller eller analyslösningar i Microsoft Fabric och/eller Microsoft Azure Synapse.
Minst 3 års erfarenhet av in- och utgående dataintegrationer inom data- och analysplattformar i molnmiljö.
Minst 5 års erfarenhet av ETL/ELT, varav minst 2 år i Microsofts molnmiljö.
Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av utveckling i Apache Spark Notebooks.
Minst 3 års arbetslivserfarenhet av dimensionsmodellering.
Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av utveckling, konfiguration och förvaltning av CI/CD-pipelines i moderna utvecklingsmiljöer.
Minst t3)års dokumenterad erfarenhet av aktivt mentorskap och tekniskt stöd till mer juniora utvecklare, exempelvis genom kodgranskning, kompetensöverföring, utbildning eller coachning i team


Meriterande


Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av semantisk modellering i Power BI eller Azure Analysis Services.
Minst 1 tidigare uppdrag med utveckling i Data Lakehouse arkitektur.
Minst 2 års arbetslivserfarenhet av att skapa finansiella modeller i en data- och analyslösning.


Sista ansökningdatum: 26-06-2026

Vi erbjuder dig följande, Valet är ditt!

🚀 Spännande uppdrag hos våra många kunder. Vi fokuserar på långsiktiga uppdrag, vilket ger dig möjlighet att skapa värde för kunden samtidigt som du utvecklas själv.

🌟 En trygg månadslön – tillsammans bestämmer vi en grundlön baserad på dina uppdrag.

💼 Tjänstepension och stora möjligheter till löneväxling.

🌴 Semester - Önskar du mer än 30 dagar semester, kanske 60 dagar?

🏥 Privat sjukvårdsförsäkring, olycksfallsförsäkring och livförsäkring för ökad trygghet.

📚 Kompetensutveckling – du får själv sätta din budget och välja vilka utbildningar du vill delta i.

🕑 Livskvalitet - Har du kanske svårt att få ihop pusslet i vardagen och önskar arbeta lite mindre och ändå kunna tjäna lika mycket om inte mer?


Vi hanterar ansökningar löpande, med hänsyn till höga arbetstrycket är det extra viktigt med en kort motivering för tjänsten som förklarar hur man möter kraven.

Title: Forskningsassistent
Description:
Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap. Med över 200 anställda och 4 500 studenter är Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap (DSV) en stark och dynamisk forsknings- och utbildningsmiljö. Ämnet data- och systemvetenskap rör sig mellan teknik, humaniora, samhällsvetenskap och beteendevetenskap, med stor relevans för hur vi ska leva i dag och i framtiden. Vår forskning handlar om hur IT ska utformas för att vara till nytta för människor, organisationer och hela samhällen.



Mer information om oss finns på: Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap.



Projektbeskrivning



Forskningsprojektet ”Sektorkoppling och verksamhetsforskning för miljömässig hållbarhet (SCORES): Effekter på klimatutsläpp, energipriser och ekonomisk nytta som en följd av Nordens energiomställning” kommer att ta itu med energiomställningen i Sverige innebär ett avsteg från fossila bränslen samt en ökad elektrifiering av industrin, där kopplingen mellan elproduktion och konsumtion (sektorkoppling) blir av större betydelse när icke-reglerbar förnybar energi (VRE) som vind- och solkraft får en större proportion. Då VRE är intermittent och platsspecifik krävs ett mer flexibelt energisystem, med reglerbar produktion som komplement till exempel i form av vattenkraft. Här kan flexibla producenter ha större förmåga för att påverka elpriser, vilket kräver mekanismer för att mildra välfärdsförluster. På samma sätt kommer överföringsplaneringen att behöva anpassas för att dämpa producenternas marknadsstyrka. I projektet ska spelteoretiska modeller tillämpas för att identifiera hur producenternas marknadsmakt påverkas av sektorskoppling och ta fram metoder att förutse ekonomiska snedvridningar på elmarknaden. Dessa insikter kommer att stödja policyutveckling för en välfärdshöjande övergång till ett hållbart energisystem.



Arbetsuppgifter



En tvärvetenskaplig ansats som omfattar ekonomiska, tekniska och samhällsvetenskapliga perspektiv utgör ramen för analys. En forskningsassistent i projektet kommer att undersöka incitament för marknadsmakt inom energilagring. Exempel på forskningsuppgifter är:

Uppdatera och konsolidera en databas över de nordiska värme- och kraftsektorernas attribut.
Använda Nash-Cournot-modeller för att undersöka strategiskt beteende inom energilagring.
Utveckla både öppna och slutna modeller som syftar till analytiska lösningar med kompletterande numeriska exempel.

Vi välkomnar sökande från olika metodologiska bakgrunder. 



Kvalifikationer



En forskningsassistent anställs i huvudsak för forskning och ska ha avlagt examen på masternivå eller har en utländsk examen som bedöms motsvara masternivå.



Vi söker en kandidat med följande kvalifikationer:

En meritlista som visar utmärkt modelleringsarbete relaterat till energisystem eller miljöpolitik publicerat i vetenskapliga tidskrifter.
Metodologisk verktygslåda relaterad till tillämpad matematik, miljöekonomi, spelteori eller operationsanalys.
Färdigheter i analytiska lösningsmetoder för spelteoretiska problem och beräkningsmässig implementering av optimeringsmodeller i GAMS eller liknande plattformar.
Domänkännedom om kraftsektorn och energipolitik.
Vilja att arbeta kollegialt som del av ett tvärvetenskapligt team och bevis på tidigare samarbete med forskare från olika discipliner.
Utmärkt engelska i tal och skrift (svenska är inte ett krav).

Vi letar efter en kandidat som kan arbeta inom ett mycket tvärvetenskapligt team och agendasättande forskningsprojekt. Du är ansvarsfull, lyhörd och en bra lagspelare. Du är proaktiv, tar ansvar för dina uppgifter och har ett strukturerat arbetssätt. Du är van att prioritera och kan hålla en överblick över bredare mål samtidigt som du levererar på konkreta uppgifter.



Om anställningen



Anställningen avser heltid och är tidsbegränsad till fyra månader. Stockholms universitet tillämpar individuell lönesättning. Tillträde enligt överenskommelse.



Vi erbjuder



Hos oss får du dynamiken i samspelet mellan högre utbildning och forskning som gör Stockholms universitet till en spännande och kreativ miljö. Du arbetar i en internationell miljö och får förmånliga villkor. 



Stockholms universitet värnar om att vara en arbetsplats som är fri från diskriminering och ger lika rättigheter och möjligheter för alla.



Kontakt



Ytterligare information lämnas av professor Afzal S. Siddiqui, tfn 08-16 13 85, asiddiq@dsv.su.se.



Ansökan



Du söker anställningen via Stockholms universitets rekryteringssystem. Du som sökande ansvarar för att ansökan är komplett och att den är universitetet tillhanda senast sista ansökningsdag.



Anvisningar för sökande finns på webbsidan: att söka en anställning.



Stockholms universitet bidrar till det hållbara demokratiska samhällets utveckling genom kunskap, upplysning och sanningssökande.

HTML:
Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap. Med över 200 anställda och 4 500 studenter är Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap (DSV) en stark och dynamisk forsknings- och utbildningsmiljö. Ämnet data- och systemvetenskap rör sig mellan teknik, humaniora, samhällsvetenskap och beteendevetenskap, med stor relevans för hur vi ska leva i dag och i framtiden. Vår forskning handlar om hur IT ska utformas för att vara till nytta för människor, organisationer och hela samhällen.

Mer information om oss finns på: Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap.

Projektbeskrivning

Forskningsprojektet ”Sektorkoppling och verksamhetsforskning för miljömässig hållbarhet (SCORES): Effekter på klimatutsläpp, energipriser och ekonomisk nytta som en följd av Nordens energiomställning” kommer att ta itu med energiomställningen i Sverige innebär ett avsteg från fossila bränslen samt en ökad elektrifiering av industrin, där kopplingen mellan elproduktion och konsumtion (sektorkoppling) blir av större betydelse när icke-reglerbar förnybar energi (VRE) som vind- och solkraft får en större proportion. Då VRE är intermittent och platsspecifik krävs ett mer flexibelt energisystem, med reglerbar produktion som komplement till exempel i form av vattenkraft. Här kan flexibla producenter ha större förmåga för att påverka elpriser, vilket kräver mekanismer för att mildra välfärdsförluster. På samma sätt kommer överföringsplaneringen att behöva anpassas för att dämpa producenternas marknadsstyrka. I projektet ska spelteoretiska modeller tillämpas för att identifiera hur producenternas marknadsmakt påverkas av sektorskoppling och ta fram metoder att förutse ekonomiska snedvridningar på elmarknaden. Dessa insikter kommer att stödja policyutveckling för en välfärdshöjande övergång till ett hållbart energisystem.

Arbetsuppgifter

En tvärvetenskaplig ansats som omfattar ekonomiska, tekniska och samhällsvetenskapliga perspektiv utgör ramen för analys. En forskningsassistent i projektet kommer att undersöka incitament för marknadsmakt inom energilagring. Exempel på forskningsuppgifter är:

  • Uppdatera och konsolidera en databas över de nordiska värme- och kraftsektorernas attribut.
  • Använda Nash-Cournot-modeller för att undersöka strategiskt beteende inom energilagring.
  • Utveckla både öppna och slutna modeller som syftar till analytiska lösningar med kompletterande numeriska exempel.

Vi välkomnar sökande från olika metodologiska bakgrunder. 

Kvalifikationer

En forskningsassistent anställs i huvudsak för forskning och ska ha avlagt examen på masternivå eller har en utländsk examen som bedöms motsvara masternivå.

Vi söker en kandidat med följande kvalifikationer:

  • En meritlista som visar utmärkt modelleringsarbete relaterat till energisystem eller miljöpolitik publicerat i vetenskapliga tidskrifter.
  • Metodologisk verktygslåda relaterad till tillämpad matematik, miljöekonomi, spelteori eller operationsanalys.
  • Färdigheter i analytiska lösningsmetoder för spelteoretiska problem och beräkningsmässig implementering av optimeringsmodeller i GAMS eller liknande plattformar.
  • Domänkännedom om kraftsektorn och energipolitik.
  • Vilja att arbeta kollegialt som del av ett tvärvetenskapligt team och bevis på tidigare samarbete med forskare från olika discipliner.
  • Utmärkt engelska i tal och skrift (svenska är inte ett krav).

Vi letar efter en kandidat som kan arbeta inom ett mycket tvärvetenskapligt team och agendasättande forskningsprojekt. Du är ansvarsfull, lyhörd och en bra lagspelare. Du är proaktiv, tar ansvar för dina uppgifter och har ett strukturerat arbetssätt. Du är van att prioritera och kan hålla en överblick över bredare mål samtidigt som du levererar på konkreta uppgifter.

Om anställningen

Anställningen avser heltid och är tidsbegränsad till fyra månader. Stockholms universitet tillämpar individuell lönesättning. Tillträde enligt överenskommelse.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Job Description

Do you want to take part in developing and implementing products for our Clients in Sweden? These systems support critical business needs for our Clients and their customers and you will be performing a key role in bringing digital solutions to our Clients. Then we will have the right challenge for you

DXC Technology has been driving the digitalization of Sweden for more than 50 years and we are seeking top-candidates, who thrive to work with technologies and solutions that will impact on a national scale, and who take pride in delivering state-of-the-art services that benefit citizens, financial institutions and the public sector.

Essential Job Functions

In this role you will be covering the full life cycle of product realization, including but not limited to the following capabilities:


Product Management
Stakeholder Management
Product Concept Identification
Product Concept Development
Product Development
Dependency Management


Scope of work will be determined by the client's products planning process and will align with strategic objectives and department roadmap.

Product Development for both data products and data platform will align with the client's requirements and built with considerations to best practice architecture and engineering.

Basic Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in a relevant field or equivalent combination of education and experience
3+ years of relevant work experience
Proven experience in data engineering
Must be able to manage the following technologies:
Snowflake.
Python.
SQL.
Cloud development and data environments (Azure).
Continuous deployment.
CI/CD
Data Warehouse experience.
Data Analytical Skills.
Data Modelling.
DBT (Data Build Tool) & Airflow in collaboration
Data visualization using stream-lit
Infrastructure cost management optimization for azure and snowflake
Fluent in English and Swedish


Other Qualifications


Advanced degree in a relevant field a plus
Relevant certifications, such as Data Engineering on Google Cloud Platform or IBM Certified Data Engineer a plus


What We Offer

You’ll benefit from ongoing training and development opportunities, access to certifications, and a supportive team environment. Strong collaboration in the entire European team drives our joint success. We offer a comprehensive benefits package including pension plans, life and health insurance, as well as flexible benefits tailored to your needs.

If you are a results-driven professional excited about leveraging your experience, we invite you to pursue this opportunity with us at DXC Technology.

At DXC Technology, we believe strong connections and community are key to our success. Our work model prioritizes in-person collaboration while offering flexibility to support wellbeing, productivity, individual work styles, and life circumstances. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive.

Recruitment fraud is a scheme in which fictitious job opportunities are offered to job seekers typically through online services, such as false websites, or through unsolicited emails claiming to be from the company. These emails may request recipients to provide personal information or to make payments as part of their illegitimate recruiting process. DXC does not make offers of employment via social media networks and DXC never asks for any money or payments from applicants at any point in the recruitment process, nor ask a job seeker to purchase IT or other equipment on our behalf. More information on employment scams is available here.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Mentimeter is an engagement tool with a clear goal in mind. To turn presentations into conversations. Through real-time interactivity and clear visualizations, we get people to participate, engage and become more productive. Transforming all those passive meetings, airless classrooms and drawn out trainings into valuable and memorable moments.

We truly believe that you achieve the best results by doing things together. And that successful leaders need to adopt a curious and collaborative mindset in order to get there. So with you at Mentimeter, you’ll be a big part of the ambition to help over 1 billion people listen, learn and work better together.

Modern data platforms are built on events - well-defined, trustworthy signals that describe how users interact with products and systems over time. As a Data Engineer you will help build and improve the data platform that powers analytics, growth, personalization and decision-making across the company.

This role is part of the Data Platform team, where we design and maintain the core data infrastructure that serves Product, Marketing, Growth, and Analytics. You will focus on the systems and infrastructure that supports transforming batch and streaming data into high-quality datasets that downstream systems - from warehouses to activation tools - can rely on.

We are currently in the early stages of building out a new event-driven architecture, and this role will be part of shaping it. That means owning parts of the streaming infrastructure, contributing to foundational decisions about how events are structured and evolved, and having real influence over how data flows across the company for years to come.

This is a hybrid/onsite role located in Stockholm, our guideline is to spend 3 days a week at our office.

What You’ll Do


Design and operate data infrastructure (streaming and batch) and the systems that move data reliably at scale.
Contribute to how events are defined, structured, and evolved across the platform.
Ensure data quality, consistency, and observability across event pipelines.
Ensure GDPR and PII compliance across data flows at scale.
Contribute to data and architecture best practices that may span across the whole organisation.



Who You Are


A few years of experience as a Data Engineer or data-heavy backend engineer.
Solid programming and data skills (e.g. SQL, Python, and/or JVM-based languages).
Experience building and maintaining data pipelines in a modern cloud data stack.
Experience managing infrastructure as code (e.g. Terraform or similar).
A good understanding of data quality, observability, and reliability.
Clear written communication skills in English and comfort working cross-functionally.
Experience with streaming systems or near real-time data infrastructure (e.g. Kafka, Flink, or similar).



📍 Location:

This is a hybrid/on-site role at Mentimeter HQ in Stockholm, where we expect you to be in the office at least 3 days/week.

Please note that we do not offer relocation sponsorship for the position.

Not Required

You don’t have to know Swedish (daily work is carried out in English and the Mentimeter team currently boasts over 50 nationalities!)

What Mentimeter Can Offer

At Mentimeter we can offer a diverse and inclusive work environment supported by smart and driven colleagues. We believe in continuous professional development for all of our colleagues and therefore offer access to a leadership program (including external personal coach) and relevant education to ensure that we continue to be state-of-the-art when it comes to innovating and building Mentimeter. Your place will be in a growing company with lots of career opportunities, working on a beloved product used by more than 300 million people. It’s not all about work though, we also offer a very healthy view on work-life balance.

All of this comes attached with a competitive compensation and benefits package, including pension contributions. Learn more about our benefits by visiting our Benefits & Perks page

AI and Hiring at Mentimeter

At Mentimeter, we believe AI helps us work smarter - but it never replaces the human assessment, curiosity, and personal connection that define our culture and our hiring. We use AI as a sparring partner: to bounce ideas, bring new perspectives, support structure, and make our work more efficient. But the meaning, decisions, and interactions always come from people.


AI does not screen or decide on candidates.
There is no automated filtering, ranking, or decision-making in our recruitment process. Every application is reviewed by a person.
Hiring teams may use AI to support their work - for example, to structure notes, prepare interview questions, or organize their thinking.



AI strengthens our work, but it does not define it. At Mentimeter, we’re not building an AI-driven hiring process - we’re building a people-first culture, where technology helps us listen, learn, and grow together.

Culture at Mentimeter

At Mentimeter we believe in giving everyone a voice - regardless of who you are. So we build a platform that does just that. Our platform is not only our product but also our organization. A platform where people feel safe, where differences are embraced, a place where you can have fun. We strongly encourage applicants who are people of color, LGBTQ+, women, people with disabilities, and/or formerly incarcerated people, and a college degree is not strictly required. In order to give everyone a voice, we need to be as diverse as our users.

Learn more about our culture by visiting our Culture page.

Review our Privacy Policy for more information.
