Title: Data Analyst
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
KAYAK/momondo, part of Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG), is a leading travel search engine. With billions of queries across our platforms, we help people find their perfect flight, stay, rental car and vacation package. We’re also transforming business travel with a new corporate travel solution, KAYAK for Business.

As an employee of KAYAK, you will be part of a travel company that operates a portfolio of global metasearch brands including momondo, Cheapflights and HotelsCombined, among others. From start-up to industry leader, innovation is in our DNA and every employee has an opportunity to make their mark. Our focus is on building the best travel search engine to make it easier for everyone to experience the world.

KAYAK’s Organic Growth team, within Marketing SEO, uses travel search data to create timely, data-driven stories for media and public relations. We’re looking for a Data Analyst who can turn raw search signals into clear, reusable datasets, automated pipelines, and easy-to-use dashboards that support global PR and editorial campaigns.

This role will be required to work from our Copenhagen office at least 3 days per week.

In This Role, You Will


Receive and scope data requests from PR and local media, then deliver clean, press-ready datasets.
Work with PR and communications partners to shape data angles and check feasibility.
Build and maintain automated SQL/Python pipelines and templates for recurring requests.
Create and maintain self‑service dashboards and lightweight tools (e.g., Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau) so teams can quickly explore trends.
Validate data quality and collaborate with Data Engineering to improve underlying models and delivery.
Document methods and make outputs easy for non-technical audiences to use.


Please Apply If You Have


A Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Statistics, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field.
2-4 years of experience in a data analyst, business analyst, marketing analyst, or similar role.
Practical experience working with SQL and extracting/manipulating large datasets.
Hands-on experience in Python (or similar) for data processing and automation.
Familiarity with visualization tools (Superset, Datawrapper, Tableau, or similar).
Strong analytical thinking and the ability to translate complex data into clear, media-ready insights.
Comfortable explaining technical decisions to non-technical partners and delivering work on tight timelines.


Nice-to-have


Experience building reusable pipelines or automation for recurring reports.
Experience working with marketing, search, or travel data.
Experience building lightweight self-serve dashboards.


Benefits And Perks


Work from (almost) anywhere for up to 20 days per year
Focus on mental health and well-being:
Company-paid therapy sessions through SpringHealth
Company-paid subscription to HeadSpace
Company-wide week off a year - the whole team fully recharges (and returns without a pile-up of work!)
No meeting Fridays
Paid parental leave
6 weeks paid vacation + a day off for your birthday
Paid volunteer time
Focus on your career growth:
Development Dollars
Leadership development
Access to thousands of on-demand e-learnings
Travel Discounts
Employee Resource Groups
Pension plan contributions
Private health & life insurance
Lunch provided five days per week & breakfast once a week
Professional espresso machine
Awesome office in the city centre of Copenhagen (Rådhuspladsen)
Monthly social events, yoga, Thursday bars

Inclusion

At KAYAK, we want everyone to have the space to grow, share ideas and do great work. That’s why we’re focused on hiring the best talent from all walks of life and experiences, supporting them well and making sure no one feels like they have to fit a mold to belong here.

If you need accommodations during the application or interview process, or on the job, we’re here to support you. Please reach out to your recruiter to request any accommodations.

Title: Junior BI Analyst
Description:
Are you our new Junior BI Analyst with a passion for data, business support, and developing Insight?

At DESMI, we are strengthening our data and analytics capabilities to support better decision-making across the organization. We are looking for a curious and analytical Junior BI Analyst who thrives on transforming data into meaningful insights and supporting the business with high-quality analysis. In this role, you will play a key part in running our BI support function, ensuring that stakeholders across DESMI have access to reliable data, insightful reporting, and clear answers to their business questions.

DESMI is a dynamic and innovative company where collaboration, continuous learning, and professional development are at the core of our culture. In this role, you will gain hands-on experience with modern BI tools, work closely with stakeholders across functions, and contribute to strengthening our data-driven culture.

A glimpse into the role

As our Junior BI Analyst, you will be the responsible for delivering business intelligence support and analysis. You will work closely with colleagues across departments, helping them understand data, answer key business questions, and translate requirements into impactful Power BI solutions.

You will be part of a dedicated Finance & BI environment and collaborate with both technical and business stakeholders, contributing to the continued development of our DESMI Insights platform.

Your Primary Responsibilities Will Include


Running the BI support function: Act as the first point of contact for BI-related questions, supporting users and ensuring they get value from our data and reports
Developing Power BI reports: Design, build, and maintain dashboards and reports that provide clear and actionable insights
Delivering business analysis: Conduct thorough and structured analyses to support decision-making across the organization
Translating business needs into data solutions: Work closely with stakeholders to understand their requirements and turn them into scalable BI solutions
Ensuring data quality: Validate and reconcile data to ensure consistency, reliability, and trust in reporting
Supporting continuous improvement: Contribute to developing and optimizing our BI platform, data models, and reporting standards Your next opportunity starts here


We are looking for a structured and curious analyst who enjoys diving into data and turning complexity into clarity. You are motivated by understanding the business behind the numbers and delivering insights that make a real impact.

We also imagine that you:


Hold a master’s degree in economics, business administration (e.g. Cand.merc), with a strong understanding of business strategy or performance management.
Have a solid understanding of how data supports business decision-making, including financial and operational metrics
Have experience with or a strong interest in Power BI both analysis and report development
Are analytical and detail-oriented, with the ability to perform thorough and well-documented analyses
Are proactive and service-minded, enjoying close collaboration with stakeholders across the organization
Communicate complex data insights in a clear and understandable way
Have a structured approach to problem-solving and a strong sense of ownership


Whether you are at the beginning of your career or have gained some initial experience, this role offers a unique opportunity to develop your skills in a global organization and become a key player in driving data-driven decision-making at DESMI.

The perks of being at DESMI


Flexible working hours – including possibilities for remote work, but on-site attendance is encouraged
Global career prospects – work with colleagues across Europe, Asia, and the U.S.
Skills development and career growth – Training, job rotation, and internal mobility
Pension – attractive employer contributions
Private healthcare and life insurance
On-site canteen – light breakfast, lunch buffet, and snacks
Company sports & social activities – from badminton and bowling to art clubs and summer parties


What We’re About

But we’re more than just pumps. At DESMI, we believe teamwork and innovation drive success.

When You Join DESMI, You Can Expect


A collaborative and supportive team environment.
Opportunities for continuous learning and professional development.
A chance to contribute your ideas and make a real impact.
A company that values sustainability and innovation.


We are a flat organization with an open-door policy, where decisions are made together, and ideas are always welcome. Here, doers thrive – if you see an opportunity for improvement, you have the freedom to act on it.

As innovators in reliable and sustainable flow solutions, we serve a wide range of industries from Aquaculture and Defense to Green Technologies – the list goes on. Sustainability is highly appreciated while developing high-performance and long-lasting technologies that help industries adapt to evolving markets while reducing environmental impact.

Ready to apply?

If you're a dedicated and motivated individual seeking a challenging and rewarding career where you can contribute to a more sustainable future, we encourage you to apply!

To learn more about this opportunity or to submit your application, please contact Lead BI Manager Mathias Dichmann-Schack (mdsc@desmi.com). We review applications on an ongoing basis and encourage you to apply as soon as possible, as the position will be filled once the right candidate is found.

  • Company sports & social activities – from badminton and bowling to art clubs and summer parties

What We’re About

But we’re more than just pumps. At DESMI, we believe teamwork and innovation drive success.

When You Join DESMI, You Can Expect

  • A collaborative and supportive team environment.
  • Opportunities for continuous learning and professional development.
  • A chance to contribute your ideas and make a real impact.
  • A company that values sustainability and innovation.

We are a flat organization with an open-door policy, where decisions are made together, and ideas are always welcome. Here, doers thrive – if you see an opportunity for improvement, you have the freedom to act on it.

As innovators in reliable and sustainable flow solutions, we serve a wide range of industries from Aquaculture and Defense to Green Technologies – the list goes on. Sustainability is highly appreciated while developing high-performance and long-lasting technologies that help industries adapt to evolving markets while reducing environmental impact.

Ready to apply?

If you're a dedicated and motivated individual seeking a challenging and rewarding career where you can contribute to a more sustainable future, we encourage you to apply!

To learn more about this opportunity or to submit your application, please contact Lead BI Manager Mathias Dichmann-Schack (mdsc@desmi.com). We review applications on an ongoing basis and encourage you to apply as soon as possible, as the position will be filled once the right candidate is found.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Kolding, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Every day, we get opportunities to make a positive impact – on our colleagues, partners, customers and society. Together, we’re pioneering the solutions of the future and unlocking the full potential of precious resources. Trusted to act on initiative, we challenge conventional thinking to develop world-leading technologies that inspire progress in vital areas, including energy, food, water and shipping.

As we push forward, the innovative, open spirit that fuels our 140-year-old start-up culture and rapid growth also drives our personal growth. So, as we shape a more resourceful, less wasteful world, we build our careers too.

About The Job

As a Data Analyst, you’ll provide data analytics and support for business and process data, working with large datasets (including product master data in PLM and ERP systems). You’ll be part of a cross-functional R&D team (60+ colleagues), collaborating closely with manufacturing, distribution, and product management teams.

Responsibilities Include


Data analysis and exploration
Collaborate with cross functional teams to understand the data
Acquire, preprocess, and clean data from various sources to ensure quality and reliability
Analyze business and process data to identify trends and patterns impacting operations
Apply statistical modeling and machine learning
Create informative dashboards and visualizations (primarily in Excel, Power BI).
Stay current with industry trends and best practices


What You Bring


Bachelor’s degree in information systems, Engineering, or similar, or equivalent experience.
Strong skills in Excel (required) and Power BI (nice to have).
Understanding of PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) and product master data management.
Experience in data governance and data quality management.
Language skills: English and Danish (preferred)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent organizational and cross-functional collaboration skills.
Ability to manage and drive changes in a global, multi-site manufacturing environment.
Effective communication and training abilities.


Who You Are


Systematic, detail-oriented, and collaborative. You thrive in diverse teams, balance independence with teamwork, and contribute positively to our culture.
You have a keen eye for detail, strong data discipline, and the ability to understand and manage complex data structures across multiple platforms.


We are conducting a continuous review of the applications received. We do not accept applications via email, they will be deleted, due to General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

We look forward to hearing from you soon!

We care about diversity, inclusion, and equity in our recruitment processes. We also believe behavioral traits can provide important insights into a candidate's fit to a role. To help us achieve this we apply Pymetrics assessments, and upon application you will be invited to play the assessment games.

Title: Lead Data Analyst - Marketing Analytics
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Trustpilot, we're on an incredible journey. We're a profitable, high-growth FTSE-250 company with a big vision: to become the universal symbol of trust. We run the world's largest open customer review platform, and while we've come a long way, there's still so much exciting work to do. Come join us at the heart of trust!

Data isn’t just a byproduct of our business; it’s the engine that drives our growth. We are looking for a Lead Marketing Analyst to join our Go-To-Market (GTM) Analytics team. This is a high-impact, individual contributor role designed for an accomplished professional who can bridge the gap between technical statistical modeling and strategic marketing execution.

Your mission is to help us answer the ultimate question: "How do we spend our next dollar to maximize impact?" You will shape marketing triangulation, combining Multi-Touch Attribution (MTA), Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM), and rigorous incrementality testing to provide a 360-degree view of our ROI.

What You’ll Be Doing:

Marketing Triangulation: Guide and own the methodology for measuring marketing effectiveness. You will move beyond surface-level metrics to develop a sophisticated "source of truth" using MMM, MTA, and lift studies.
Advanced Data Analysis: Design and execute complex analyses, including cohort analysis, customer segmentation, and predictive modeling to identify growth levers.
Collaborative Experimentation: Partner with Marketing Leads to design, implement, and analyze A/B and multivariate tests, fostering a culture of "test and learn."
Capability Development: Support and scale our analytics infrastructure. This includes developing solutions in R/Python and integrating them into our data stack.
Stakeholder Influence: Provide actionable insights to help stakeholders make data-informed decisions and drive data literacy around marketing optimization.
Actionable Reporting: Create intuitive dashboards in Looker that don't just show data, but provide actionable insights for stakeholders ranging from Channel Managers to the CMO.
Funnel Strategy: Guide the analytical strategy for the end-to-end GTM funnel. Analyze the sales funnel to increase lead generation, accelerate conversion rates, and drive revenue expansion by identifying bottlenecks and opportunities for improvements.
Data Engineering Collaboration: Work closely with our data engineering team using dbt to ensure our marketing data pipelines are robust, efficient, and reliable.

Who You Are:

Highly Skilled Technician: You are fluent in SQL and R or Python and have a deep-rooted understanding of regression models, clustering, and statistical significance with an ability to apply them to marketing problems.
Strategic Marketing Thinker: You have significant experience solving marketing problems using techniques such as MTA, MMM, and Incrementality testing. You don't just build models; you understand the "why" behind the spend.
Trusted Advisor: You possess strong collaboration skills, knowing how to challenge assumptions and guide leadership toward data-informed decisions.
Clear Communicator: You can explain complex statistical concepts to a non-technical audience and present a compelling case for budget optimization to executive leadership.
Data Storytelling: You have proven experience with Looker (LookML) or similar BI tools, with a focus on making data accessible and easy to understand.
Quality Focused: You have a commitment to data quality and experience working within modern data stacks (e.g., BigQuery, dbt, Airflow).

What's in it for you?


A range of flexible working options to dedicate time to what matters to you
Competitive compensation package + bonus
25 days holiday per year, with an extra 5 days holiday allocated after your 1 year anniversary (prorated) 
Two (paid) volunteering days a year to spend your time giving back to the causes that matter to you and your community
Rich learning and development opportunities are supported through the Trustpilot Academy and Blinkist
Comprehensive health package, pension, and full access to Headspace, a popular mindfulness app to promote positive mental health
Paid parental leave
Central office with  a laid-back vibe and constant buzz of different languages being spoken everywhere you go It’s complete with a coffee bar, canteen, and table tennis and has a wide variety of refreshments available - you can opt-in for breakfast and lunch at an affordable price
Regular opportunities to connect and get to know your fellow Trusties, including company-wide celebrations and events, ERG activities, and team socials
Opportunity to join the Trustpilot Social Club for a small monthly fee and attend regular social and leisure activities like go-karting, cooking classes, and escape rooms 
Employee discounts to restaurants and shops

Still not sure?

We want to be a part of creating a more diverse, equitable, and inclusive world of work for all. We’re excited to hear about your experiences along with how you will contribute to our working culture. Even if you don’t feel you meet all the requirements, we'd still really like to hear from you!

 

About us

Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial — we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.

Today, we have more than 350 million reviews and 60 million monthly active users across the globe, with 149 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1000 employees and we’re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in  Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York. 

We’re driven by connection. It’s at the heart of what we do. Our culture keeps things fresh –– it’s built on the relationships we create. We talk, we laugh, we collaborate and we respect each other. We work across borders and cultures to be the universal symbol of trust in an ever-changing world. With vibrant office locations worldwide and over 50 nationalities, we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace with diverse perspectives and ideas. 

Our purpose to help people and businesses help each other is a tall order, but we keep it real. We’re a great bunch of humans, doing awesome stuff, without fuss or pretense. A successful Trustpilot future is driven by you –– we give you the autonomy to shape a career you can be proud of. If you’re ready to grow, let’s go.

Join us at the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive and where different viewpoints and experiences are valued and respected. Trustpilot will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, religious beliefs, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, neurodiversity, disability, age, parental or veteran status. Together, we are the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is a global company and our data practices are designed to ensure that your personally identifiable information is appropriately protected. Please note that your personal information will be transferred, accessed, and stored globally as necessary for the uses and disclosures stated in our Privacy Policy.

If you have a disability and would like to discuss any adjustments you might need either in submitting your application, or to the recruitment process more generally, please let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition Team (talent.acquisition@trustpilot.com) quoting the role you wish to apply for.

Title: Digital Data Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
– Digital Services & Customer Experience

Vil du skabe målbare forbedringer i YouSees digitale kundeoplevelse?

Hos YouSee leder vi efter en ny Digital Data Analyst. Opgaven bliver at tage ansvar for vores tekniske trackingfundament og bruge data målrettet til at forbedre selvbetjening, kunderejser og forretnings-KPI’er.

Du bliver en del af Online Service & Content Management i afdelingen Digital Services & CX. Her kommer du til at arbejde tæt sammen med vores øvrige teams i Digital Services & CX, Martech og Customer Data.

Om rollen

I rollen som Digital Data Analyst er dit primære ansvar at sikre, at vi har et solidt, konsistent og pålideligt datagrundlag på tværs af vores digitale kanaler. Og ikke mindste at sikre, at denne data omsættes til konkrete anbefalinger og indsigter, der skaber dokumenterbare forbedringer.

Det er ikke en klassisk rapporteringsrolle. Du forventes at påtage dig ansvaret for at opdage muligheder, prioritere indsatser og løfte performance i samarbejde med forretning og udvikling.

Dine primære ansvarsområder

Teknisk tracking & datakvalitet:

Etablere, vedligeholde og videreudvikle tracking på tværs af vores digitale kundeservice kanaler som web og apps

Sikre høj datakvalitet og konsistente målinger på tværs af teams og platforme.

Samarbejde med udviklingsteams om tracking, events og datalag.

Dokumentere trackingstandarder og sikre fælles forståelse.

Analyse & indsigt:

Analysere brugeradfærd, kunderejser og performance i digitale flows.

Identificere friktion, drop-offs og forbedringspotentiale i selvbetjening.

Udarbejde dashboards i vores analytics tool og lave analyser, der er lette at handle på.

Assistere med ledelsesrapportering, opsætning og analyse af relevante KPIer, bl.a. til vores ”Monthly Business Review” og PMO.

Performance & forbedringsinitiativer:

Sætte datadrevne projekter i værk, der løfter målepunkter som selvbetjeningsgrad, reducerer opkaldsbelastning og øger digitale resultater.

Understøtte tests og eksperimenter (fx A/B-tests) og dokumentere effekt.

Prioritere indsatser i tæt samarbejde med Digital Self Service og CX & Journey Management.

Om dig

Vi forestiller os, at du:

Har solid teknisk forståelse for digital tracking og customer journey analytics (ex. fra Adobe eller GA4, data layers og tag management systemer).

Kan omsætte data til konkrete, handlingsorienterede anbefalinger og trives i krydsfeltet mellem data, forretning og kundeoplevelse.

Er god til at tænke struktureret og har erfaring med at håndtere komplekse brugerflows.

Tager initiativ og har let ved at arbejde sammen med forskellige teams og professionelle kompetencer.

Har lyst til at være en ’data evangelist’, som supporterer alt fra ideation til rollout og træning.

Har kendskab til web personalisering og hvordan man kan bruge data til at skabe personalisering og relevans.

Det er et krav, at du kan kommunikere tydeligt og professionelt mundtligt og skriftligt på dansk og engelsk.

Det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring med større digitale platforme, selvbetjeningsløsninger eller kunderejseoptimering. Men din tilgang og dit mindset er vigtigere end dit CV.

Det tilbyder vi

En nøglerolle med stor indflydelse på YouSees digitale kundeoplevelse.

Et engageret fagligt miljø med fokus på data, CX og digital performance.

Tæt samarbejde med både forretning, indhold, CX og udvikling.

Gode muligheder for faglig udvikling og specialisering.

Fleksible arbejdsforhold og en hverdag præget af tillid og ansvar.

Derudover tilbyder vi en række personalegoder som mobil + abonnement, bredbånd samt rabatter via TDC Brands.

Praktisk information

Stillingen er placeret i YouSee og organisatorisk forankret i Online Service & Content Management under Digital Services & CX. Arbejdssted er Sletvej 30, 8310 Tranbjerg J. eller Teglholmsgade 1, København

Har du spørgsmål til rollen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Peter Vadmand Sejersen, Digital Director.

Vi læser ansøgninger løbende og ser frem til at modtage din.

Ansøgning: Det er ikke nødvendigt at vedhæfte en motiveret an

Ansøgning: Det er ikke nødvendigt at vedhæfte en motiveret ansøgning, men du bedes venligst inkludere en kort introduktion i dit CV om, hvorfor du søger denne stilling. For at sikre en fair og inkluderende rekrutteringsproces beder vi dig samtidig undlade at tilføje et foto til dit CV

Ansøgningsfrist: 26/06/2026

Kontaktperson: Peter Vadmand Sejersen, Digital Director på petse@yousee.nuuway.dk

Arbejdssted: Teglholmsgade 1, København & Sletvej 30, Århus

TDC Brands (Tidl. Nuuday) – Sikkert ind i fremtiden

TDC Brands består af en række stærke brands, der tilsammen leverer innovative digitale services inden for TV, bredbånd, netværk og telefoni til private kunder og virksomheder. YouSee, Telmore, Hiper og eesy servicerer danskere på tværs af alder, køn og bopæl på privatmarkedet. På erhvervsmarkedet servicerer TDC Brands mindre såvel som store erhvervsvirksomheder med TDC Erhverv og Relatel. TDC Brands beskæftiger i dag omkring 3.000 medarbejdere og har ca. 6 mio. privat- og erhvervskundeforhold. Få mere information om TDC Brands på tdcbrands.dk.

Læs mere om, hvordan vi behandler dine personoplysninger her: https://www.tdcbrands.dk/karriere

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Are you ready to be Royal Greenland's next Business Intelligence Analyst?

Would you like to assist in collating industry data and strategic reporting?

If so, joining the Business Development & Intelligence team at Royal Greenland could be the perfect next step for you.

Role Overview

As a Business Intelligence Analyst, you will support the development of dashboards, reports, and analytical tools that enable data-driven decision-making across the business.

Working closely with Marketing, Commercial, and Category teams, you will help transform data into clear, actionable insights that support business performance and customer outcomes.

The role is office-based in Svenstrup, Denmark.

Key Responsibilities


Support the creation and maintenance of dashboards and reports using Power BI and SAP Analytics Cloud.
Analyse data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities across markets and channels.
Conduct market and competitive research to support strategic initiatives.
Contribute to clear, insight-led reports and presentations for stakeholders.
Apply strong data visualisation best practices to communicate insights effectively.
Support the Global Marketing team with ad-hoc analysis and strategic projects.


Key Skills & Capabilities

Technical & Analytical


Advanced Excel skills; strong PowerPoint capability.
Experience with Power BI and Tableau (dashboarding, data modelling); SAP Analytics Cloud is a plus.
Exposure to Python, SQL, or other analytical tools is advantageous.
Strong analytical mindset and attention to data quality.


Reporting & Communication


Ability to translate complex data into clear, structured insights.
Strong data visualisation and business storytelling skills.
Confident written and verbal communication in English (additional languages a plus).


About You


Team player, curious, proactive, and detail-oriented.
Comfortable working independently and managing multiple priorities.
Collaborative and eager to learn in a global environment.
Strong written and spoken English skills; additional languages are a plus


Your Qualifications

You likely hold a master’s degree in business economics or data Analytics and enjoy working systematically and analytically in an informal setting. The role is suitable for graduates as well as experienced analyst with 1-5 years of experience.

We look forward to receiving your application

If you have the motivation and the skills, send us your application and your CV as soon as possible. We call for interviews on an ongoing basis and hire as soon as we have the right applicant. If you want to know more about the position, you are welcome to contact: Sune Andersen, Marketing Director at suan@royalgreenland.com.

Application Deadline: Tuesday, June 30, 2026

Apply for this job

Title: Udviklingsorienteret dataspecialist til Personelkommandoen i Ballerup
Description:
Udviklingsorienteret dataspecialist til Personelkommandoen i Ballerup

Vil du være med til at sikre opbygningen af Det Danske Forsvar ved at løse komplekse opgaver inden for HR-data? Vil du bidrage til at levere skarpe og målrettede modeller, ledelsesinformation og analyser?

Så er du måske vores kommende kollega i Udviklings- og Planlægningssektionen.

Om os

Personelkommandoen er en myndighed under Forsvarskommandoen. Personelkommandoen har mange spændende og vigtige opgaver, og vi spiller en central rolle i at støtte opbygningen af Forsvaret på personelområdet og understøtte HR-strategiske beslutninger i Forsvarets øverste ledelse.

Udviklings- og Planlægningssektionen er en del af Center for Koordination og Udvikling. Sektionen er opdelt i to teams; HR-data teamet og Udviklingsteamet. Vi er i alt 16 medarbejdere, både militære og civile.

I sektionen arbejder vi tæt sammen, støtter hinanden og har masser af daglig sparring.

Du vil indgå i HR-data teamet og referere til sektionschefen. Du vil få syv tætte kollegaer, som sammen laver dataanalyser og gør HR-relateret data mere tilgængelig for hele Forsvaret.

Vi lægger stor vægt på en uformel tone, et stærkt samarbejde på tværs af sektionen og plads til den enkeltes udvikling. Du vil få nogle dygtige og erfarne kollegaer at sparre med i din dagligdag, ligesom vi lægger vægt på at skabe gode betingelser for din faglige og personlige udvikling.

Om stillingen

Det vigtigste er, at du nyder at arbejde med tal og data, især i Excel, som er vores primære arbejdsredskab. Du skal være nysgerrig på data og på, hvordan man får mest muligt ud af de data, som er til rådighed. Du skal kunne udforme kreative løsninger til at danne overblik over HR-data i Forsvaret. Kort sagt er det vigtigt, at du kan undre dig og er god til at spotte sammenhænge eller uoverensstemmelser i data om medarbejdere og deres løn- og ansættelsesvilkår.

Som dataspecialist vil du arbejde med at omsætte data til konkrete indsigter, som kan anvendes af ledelsen og andre beslutningstagere. Derfor lægger vi også vægt på, at du kan omsætte dataindsigterne til et sprog, der kan forstås af kollegaer og omverdenen. Dine præcise arbejdsopgaver aftales på baggrund af dine kompetencer og interesser.

Dine Primære Arbejdsopgaver Vil Være


At udarbejde HR-analyser på baggrund af data om Forsvarets ansatte
At udvikle ledelsesinformation herunder datamodeller
At udarbejde dashboards og automatiserede rapporteringsløsninger
At besvare henvendelser fra interne og eksterne samarbejdspartnere, herunder Forsvarsministeriet, Forsvarsstaben og offentlige myndigheder
At bidrage til udarbejdelse af beslutningsoplæg, briefinger og mødemateriale til Personelkommandoens ledelse
At være med til at implementere en mere myndighedsnær og lokal rådgivning baseret på HR-data


Stillingen giver dig mulighed for at arbejde i et stærkt fagligt miljø med komplekse HR-data og være med til at udvikle nye dataløsninger og analysemuligheder, der kan gøre Forsvaret endnu mere datadrevet.

Vi har gode muligheder for hjemmearbejde op til to dage om ugen.

Om dig

Stillingen er for dig, der enten har erfaring med dataarbejde i en tidligere stilling eller er nyuddannet med den rette profil.

Vi søger en dataspecialist med stærke analytiske evner og god forståelse for data, som kan arbejde med komplekse datasæt og tilhørende analyser. Vi ønsker en kollega, der både kan arbejde selvstændigt og bidrage til et stærkt teamfællesskab.

Vi Ser, At


Du har en relevant professionsbacheloruddannelse
Du har erfaring med databehandling, analyse og modellering – gerne fra en lignende stilling eller fra studiet
Du har stærke kompetencer inden for Excel, og evt. erfaringer fra Business Intelligence-værktøjer såsom Power BI eller Tableau
Du har erfaring med at omsætte komplekse data til brugervenlig information og som en del af dette kunne strukturere og validere data
Du har gode analytiske evner og kan identificere trends, sammenhænge og afvigelser i store datamængder
Du har en udviklingsorienteret og serviceminded tilgang og tænker altid over, hvordan et produkt bliver bedst for brugeren
Du kan formidle dataindsigter klart og præcist – både skriftligt og mundtligt – til forskelligartede målgrupper


Det vil være en fordel, hvis du også har:


Erfaring med SAP og forståelse for HR-data i en større organisation
Erfaring med at formidle analyseresultater i PowerPoint
Kendskab til data governance, GDPR og sikker håndtering af persondata
Erfaring med at udvikle dashboards og automatiserede rapporteringsløsninger
Kendskab til og interesse for Forsvaret og dets organisering


Ansættelsesvilkår

Ansættelsen sker i henhold til den til enhver tid gældende Fællesoverenskomst mellem Finansministeriet og Offentligt Ansattes Organisationer – Det Statslige Område (OAO-S-fællesoverenskomsten) og organisationsaftale for kontorfunktionærer, laboranter og it-medarbejdere.

Afhængigt af dine kvalifikationer og kompetencer, er der mulighed for forhandling af tillæg. Der er til stillingen knyttet en arbejdsgiverfinansieret pensionsordning.

Arbejdsstedet er i Ballerup.

Som ansat under Forsvarsministeriets område er du omfattet af nogle særlige vilkår. Du skal bl.a. kunne sikkerhedsgodkendes og opretholde sikkerhedsgodkendelsen under hele ansættelsesforholdet.

Du har som ansat pligt til at forrette tjeneste uden for landets grænser, herunder ved enheder i internationale operationer med henblik på deltagelse i konfliktforebyggende, fredsbevarende, fredsskabende, humanitære og andre lignende operationer.

Kontakt og ansøgning

Hvis du vil vide mere om stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte sektionschef Fenja Søndergaard Møller på telefon 2341 7431. Hvis du vil vide mere om opgaverne i HR-data teamet, er du velkommen til at kontakte Johanne Sofie Roelsgaard Bang på telefon 3266 5509.

Har du spørgsmål til løn- og ansættelsesvilkår er du velkommen til at kontakte Personelkommandoen, HR-konsulent Morgan Mathilde Nancke på telefon 3266 5492.

Ansøgningsfristen er den 18. maj 2026. Vi forventer at afholde samtalerne i uge 23 og 24. Stillingen er til besættelse 1. august.

Du søger stillingen via linket og vi beder dig vedhæfte ansøgning, CV og kopi af uddannelsesbevis. Vær opmærksom på at bilag skal tilføjes i PDF-format.

I Forsvarsministeriets organisation tror vi på, at mangfoldighed optimerer opgaveløsningen. Derfor opfordrer vi alle interesserede uanset baggrund til at søge stillingen.

Om Personelkommandoen

Vi rekrutterer og fastholder kompetente ansatte til mere end 40 uddannelser og 25.000 stillinger for Danmarks sikkerhed, og vi yder en helt særlig indsats over for vores veteraner og deres familier. Vores verden drejer sig om den operative kerneopgave, og vi understøtter alle lige fra den værnepligtige til forsvarsministeren.

Vi har et stærkt tværfagligt HR-miljø med fokus på udvikling. Når den operative virkelighed ændrer sig, gør vi det samme. Det kræver mere end almindelig HR.

Vi har hovedsæde i Ballerup med decentrale partnerelementer og rekrutteringscentre rundt om i hele landet.

Title: Fraud Detection Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Trustpilot, we're on an incredible journey. We're a profitable, high-growth FTSE-250 company with a big vision: to become the universal symbol of trust. We run the world's largest open customer review platform, and while we've come a long way, there's still so much exciting work to do. Come join us at the heart of trust!

We’re looking for a curious and analytical Fraud Detection Analyst to join our global Fraud & Investigations team. You’ll analyse data, spot patterns, and write detection rules that help stop fraud in its tracks — playing a vital role in protecting consumers, businesses, and the integrity of our platform.

You’ll work on complex, often ambiguous challenges in the ever-evolving world of online trust. If you love solving problems, diving into data, and making a real difference - this is your chance.

You’ll be joining a collaborative, inquisitive team that values transparency, fairness, and a good sense of humour. As part of the wider Trust & Transparency team, we’re driving change across the business—and working to make Trustpilot the universal symbol of trust online.

What you’ll be doing:

Identify fraud trends and develop a deep understanding of fraudulent behavior on our platform through the use of SQL, Google BigQuery, and Google Looker.
Formulate rules based on your analysis to mitigate fraudulent behavior across our platform.
Carry out investigations of escalated cases related to potential misuse of our platform and assist with media, legal, and customer inquiries.
Partner with our data science experts to advance our technological and procedural approaches to identifying fraudulent activity.
Work closely with our engineering teams to enhance internal tools and databases that enable us to combat fraud and scale effectively.
Act as a key resource for other departments within Trustpilot by providing analysis of customer and reviewer behavior on the platform.
Present and communicate the results of analyses and investigations to a variety of stakeholders across the department and wider company.
Work closely with and report to our Lead Fraud & Investigation Analyst.

Who you are:

Experience in data analytics or a similar analytical role—this could be through formal education (e.g., a degree in Science, Mathematics, Computer Science) or equivalent practical experience.
Comfortable working with and interpreting large datasets.
A knack for answering difficult questions using data and the ability to translate complex findings into clear, actionable reports.
Proficiency in writing queries and preparing reports using SQL or a similar language.
Motivated by self-learning and the desire to grow your skills when faced with new challenges.
Prior knowledge of formulating rules to combat fraudulent behavior is a plus, but not a requirement.
The ability to manage multiple priorities effectively with a proactive, forward-thinking mindset

What’s in it for you:


A range of flexible working options to dedicate time to what matters to you
Competitive compensation package + bonus
25 days holiday per year, with an extra 5 days holiday allocated after your 1 year anniversary (prorated) 
Two (paid) volunteering days a year to spend your time giving back to the causes that matter to you and your community
Rich learning and development opportunities are supported through the Trustpilot Academy and Blinkist
Comprehensive health package, pension, and full access to Headspace, a popular mindfulness app to promote positive mental health
Paid parental leave
Central office with  a laid-back vibe and constant buzz of different languages being spoken everywhere you go It’s complete with a coffee bar, canteen, and table tennis and has a wide variety of refreshments available - you can opt-in for breakfast and lunch at an affordable price
Regular opportunities to connect and get to know your fellow Trusties, including company-wide celebrations and events, ERG activities, and team socials
Opportunity to join the Trustpilot Social Club for a small monthly fee and attend regular social and leisure activities like go-karting, cooking classes, and escape rooms 
Employee discounts to restaurants and shops 

Still not sure?

We want to be a part of creating a more diverse, equitable, and inclusive world of work for all. We’re excited to hear about your experiences as well as how you will contribute to our working culture. So, even if you don’t feel you don't meet all the requirements, we'd still really like to hear from you!

About us

Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial — we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.

Today, we have more than 350 million reviews and 60 million monthly active users across the globe, with 149 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1000 employees and we’re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in  Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York. 

We’re driven by connection. It’s at the heart of what we do. Our culture keeps things fresh –– it’s built on the relationships we create. We talk, we laugh, we collaborate and we respect each other. We work across borders and cultures to be the universal symbol of trust in an ever-changing world. With vibrant office locations worldwide and over 50 nationalities, we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace with diverse perspectives and ideas. 

Our purpose to help people and businesses help each other is a tall order, but we keep it real. We’re a great bunch of humans, doing awesome stuff, without fuss or pretense. A successful Trustpilot future is driven by you –– we give you the autonomy to shape a career you can be proud of. If you’re ready to grow, let’s go.

Join us at the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive and where different viewpoints and experiences are valued and respected. Trustpilot will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, religious beliefs, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, neurodiversity, disability, age, parental or veteran status. Together, we are the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is a global company and our data practices are designed to ensure that your personally identifiable information is appropriately protected. Please note that your personal information will be transferred, accessed, and stored globally as necessary for the uses and disclosures stated in our Privacy Policy.

If you have a disability and would like to discuss any adjustments you might need either in submitting your application, or to the recruitment process more generally, please let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition Team (talent.acquisition@trustpilot.com) quoting the role you wish to apply for.

HTML:
At Trustpilot, we're on an incredible journey. We're a profitable, high-growth FTSE-250 company with a big vision: to become the universal symbol of trust. We run the world's largest open customer review platform, and while we've come a long way, there's still so much exciting work to do. Come join us at the heart of trust!

We’re looking for a curious and analytical Fraud Detection Analyst to join our global Fraud & Investigations team. You’ll analyse data, spot patterns, and write detection rules that help stop fraud in its tracks — playing a vital role in protecting consumers, businesses, and the integrity of our platform.

You’ll work on complex, often ambiguous challenges in the ever-evolving world of online trust. If you love solving problems, diving into data, and making a real difference - this is your chance.

You’ll be joining a collaborative, inquisitive team that values transparency, fairness, and a good sense of humour. As part of the wider Trust & Transparency team, we’re driving change across the business—and working to make Trustpilot the universal symbol of trust online.

What you’ll be doing:

  • Identify fraud trends and develop a deep understanding of fraudulent behavior on our platform through the use of SQL, Google BigQuery, and Google Looker.
  • Formulate rules based on your analysis to mitigate fraudulent behavior across our platform.
  • Carry out investigations of escalated cases related to potential misuse of our platform and assist with media, legal, and customer inquiries.
  • Partner with our data science experts to advance our technological and procedural approaches to identifying fraudulent activity.
  • Work closely with our engineering teams to enhance internal tools and databases that enable us to combat fraud and scale effectively.
  • Act as a key resource for other departments within Trustpilot by providing analysis of customer and reviewer behavior on the platform.
  • Present and communicate the results of analyses and investigations to a variety of stakeholders across the department and wider company.
  • Work closely with and report to our Lead Fraud & Investigation Analyst.

Who you are:

  • Experience in data analytics or a similar analytical role—this could be through formal education (e.g., a degree in Science, Mathematics, Computer Science) or equivalent practical experience.
  • Comfortable working with and interpreting large datasets.
  • A knack for answering difficult questions using data and the ability to translate complex findings into clear, actionable reports.
  • Proficiency in writing queries and preparing reports using SQL or a similar language.
  • Motivated by self-learning and the desire to grow your skills when faced with new challenges.
  • Prior knowledge of formulating rules to combat fraudulent behavior is a plus, but not a requirement.
  • The ability to manage multiple priorities effectively with a proactive, forward-thinking mindset

What’s in it for you:

  • A range of flexible working options to dedicate time to what matters to you
  • Competitive compensation package + bonus
  • 25 days holiday per year, with an extra 5 days holiday allocated after your 1 year anniversary (prorated) 
  • Two (paid) volunteering days a year to spend your time giving back to the causes that matter to you and your community
  • Rich learning and development opportunities are supported through the Trustpilot Academy and Blinkist
  • Comprehensive health package, pension, and full access to Headspace, a popular mindfulness app to promote positive mental health
  • Paid parental leave
  • Central office with  a laid-back vibe and constant buzz of different languages being spoken everywhere you go It’s complete with a coffee bar, canteen, and table tennis and has a wide variety of refreshments available - you can opt-in for breakfast and lunch at an affordable price
  • Regular opportunities to connect and get to know your fellow Trusties, including company-wide celebrations and events, ERG activities, and team socials
  • Opportunity to join the Trustpilot Social Club for a small monthly fee and attend regular social and leisure activities like go-karting, cooking classes, and escape rooms 
  • Employee discounts to restaurants and shops 

Still not sure?

We want to be a part of creating a more diverse, equitable, and inclusive world of work for all. We’re excited to hear about your experiences as well as how you will contribute to our working culture. So, even if you don’t feel you don't meet all the requirements, we'd still really like to hear from you!

About us

Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial — we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.

Today, we have more than 350 million reviews and 60 million monthly active users across the globe, with 149 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1000 employees and we’re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in  Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York. 

We’re driven by connection. It’s at the heart of what we do. Our culture keeps things fresh –– it’s built on the relationships we create. We talk, we laugh, we collaborate and we respect each other. We work across borders and cultures to be the universal symbol of trust in an ever-changing world. With vibrant office locations worldwide and over 50 nationalities, we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace with diverse perspectives and ideas. 

Our purpose to help people and businesses help each other is a tall order, but we keep it real. We’re a great bunch of humans, doing awesome stuff, without fuss or pretense. A successful Trustpilot future is driven by you –– we give you the autonomy to shape a career you can be proud of. If you’re ready to grow, let’s go.

Join us at the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive and where different viewpoints and experiences are valued and respected. Trustpilot will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, religious beliefs, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, neurodiversity, disability, age, parental or veteran status. Together, we are the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is a global company and our data practices are designed to ensure that your personally identifiable information is appropriately protected. Please note that your personal information will be transferred, accessed, and stored globally as necessary for the uses and disclosures stated in our Privacy Policy.

If you have a disability and would like to discuss any adjustments you might need either in submitting your application, or to the recruitment process more generally, please let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition Team (talent.acquisition@trustpilot.com) quoting the role you wish to apply for.

Title: Performance Analytics Analyst (Enterprise)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join a global Centre of Excellence shaping how data supports performance and decisions across a major sustainable energy company. As a Performance Analytics Analyst, you’ll turn complex business needs into clear insights, working at the intersection of finance, technology, and strategy. Be part of a collaborative, international team building a modern, “one version of the truth” analytics landscape that improves innovation and decision-making.

Finance > Group Financial Performance > Reporting & Analytics CoE

The Reporting & Analytics (R&A) Centre of Excellence is a global function in Finance responsible for enabling data-driven performance management and decision-making across Vestas. The CoE brings together business-facing analytics, governance and definitions, analytics engineering, and operations into a single, integrated operating model. Its purpose is to create a scalable, standardised, and high-quality analytics environment that delivers “one version of the truth” across Vestas. The R&A CoE works closely with Finance, Commercial, Operations, and Digital to translate business needs into analytics products, while ensuring consistency in data definitions, efficient delivery, and modern engineering practices. By combining subject matter knowledge, technical capability, and operational excellence, the CoE plays a critical role in improving performance transparency, accelerating decision-making, and reducing manual reporting across the enterprise.

Responsibilities

This role supports enterprise performance reporting and analytics, delivering data, insights, and reporting to stakeholders. It sits within the Performance & Decision Products pillar, contributing to core reporting and analytics capabilities.


Support enterprise performance reporting, including ExM deliverables
Identify and source relevant data across multiple systems
Translate business requirements into structured analytical requirements
Act as interface between business stakeholders and engineering teams
Specify requirements for data modelling and reporting builds
Ensure outputs meet business needs and usability standards and support alignment on definitions with Governance team
Continuously improve reporting outputs and processes


Qualifications


2–5 years in analytics, finance, or business analysis
Experience with performance reporting
Ability to translate business needs into technical requirements
Experience working with stakeholders at different levels
Familiarity with BI tools and data concepts


Competencies


Solid business understanding
Structured thinking and requirement articulation
Stakeholder communication
Ability to bridge business and technical teams
Resourceful in converting business objectives into clear analytical designs


What We Offer


Opportunity to work on enterprise-wide performance analytics and ExM reporting
Exposure to a global, data-driven organisation with diverse stakeholders
Strong learning and development curve within analytics and business partnering
Collaborative and international working environment
Modern analytics tools and platforms (e.g. Snowflake, Power BI)
Clear development pathway within the Reporting & Analytics CoE


Additional Information

Location: DK, Copenhagen

Expected travel up to 20 days annually.

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis, however, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to the advertised closing date. Please be advised to apply on or before 4th July 2026.

Our commitment to a fair hiring.

At Vestas, we evaluate all candidates solely based on their professional experience, education, and relevant skills. To support a fair recruitment process, we kindly ask that you remove any photos, dates of birth or graduation, gender pronouns, marital status, or other personal information not relevant to the role before submitting your CV/resume. Your CV/resume should focus on your professional and educational background, along with the necessary contact details (email and phone number). We train our hiring teams in inclusive evaluation and regularly review process outcomes to ensure fairness.

DEIB Statement

At Vestas, we recognize the value of diversity, equity, and inclusion in driving innovation and success. We strongly encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply, particularly those who may hesitate due to their identity or feel they do not meet every criterion. As our CEO states, "Expertise and talent come in many forms, and a diverse workforce enhances our ability to think differently and solve the complex challenges of our industry". Your unique perspective is what will help us powering the solution for a sustainable, green energy future.

BEWARE – RECRUITMENT FRAUD

It has come to our attention that there are a number of fraudulent emails from people pretending to work for Vestas. Read more via this link, https://www.vestas.com/en/careers/our-recruitment-process

About Vestas

Vestas is the energy industry’s global partner on sustainable energy solutions. We are specialised in designing, manufacturing, installing, and servicing wind turbines, both onshore and offshore.

Across the globe, we have installed more wind power than anyone else. We consider ourselves pioneers within the industry, as we continuously aim to design new solutions and technologies to create a more sustainable future for all of us. With more than 185 GW of wind power installed worldwide and 40+ years of experience in wind energy, we have an unmatched track record demonstrating our expertise within the field.

With 30,000 employees globally, we are a diverse team united by a common goal: to power the solution – today, tomorrow, and far into the future.

Vestas promotes a diverse workforce which embraces all social identities and is free of any discrimination. We commit to create and sustain an environment that acknowledges and harvests different experiences, skills, and perspectives. We also aim to give everyone equal access to opportunity.

To learn more about our company and life at Vestas, we invite you to visit our website at www.vestas.com and follow us on our social media channels. We also encourage you to join our Talent Universe to receive notifications on new and relevant postings.

Title: Er du vores nye Business Intelligence ressource med udviklingskompetencer i Forsvarsstaben?
Description:
Vil du arbejde med data, der skaber reel forretningsværdi?

I forbindelse med en omorganisering, hvor Business Intelligence området skal bruge flere ressourcer, søger vi en BI-analytiker, der kan være bindeled mellem data, økonomi og brugere – og som motiveres af at omsætte behov til løsninger, der gør en forskel i den daglige drift og beslutningsstøtte.

Du bliver en central del af et område i udvikling, hvor fokus er på at styrke beslutningsgrundlag, forbedre processer og sikre, at ledelse og organisation har adgang til valide og anvendelige informationer.

Om os

Vi sidder i et mindre element med ansvaret for generel udvikling og koordinering, på tværs af vores søjler i Forsvarskommandoen. Vores primære opgave er at tænke i ensartede løsninger, kompetenceudvikling og bedre værktøjer.

Om stillingen

Dine Primære Arbejdsopgaver


Udvikle og vedligeholde rapporter og ledelsesværktøjer
Sikre at brugere får værdi ud af data gennem sparring og support
Bidrage til bedre datagrundlag og mere effektive arbejdsgange
Understøtte økonomiske analyser og ressourceopfølgning
Deltage i udvikling og forbedring af eksisterende løsninger
Sikre kvalitet, dokumentation og ensartethed i data og leverancer
Oversætte forretningsbehov til konkrete og brugbare løsninger


Om dig

Du har en akademisk uddannelsesbaggrund og det vil være velset, hvis du har erfaring med analyse, rapportering, eller Business Intelligence.

Vi forestiller os, at du har en stærk forretningsforståelse – gerne med indsigt i økonomi, budgettering eller controlling, samt erfaring med at arbejde tæt sammen med brugere og interessenter.

Du trives i en rolle med både drift, udvikling og dialog med forretningen og formår at omsætte behov til løsninger, der skaber værdi i praksis.

Vi forventer at du arbejder selvstændigt, men er samtidig en holdspiller.

Det Er En Fordel, Hvis Du


Har erfaring fra større- eller komplekse organisationer
Har arbejdet med automatisering eller effektivisering af arbejdsgange
Har kendskab til datadrevne beslutningsprocesser i en økonomisk kontekst
Har erfaring med forandrings- eller udviklingsprojekter


Vi tilbyder

En central rolle i et vigtigt generationsskifte med stor indflydelse i en hverdag tæt på forretningen, med stor variation i arbejdsopgaverne.

Gode muligheder for faglig og personlig udvikling i et stærkt kollegialt miljø, med fokus på samarbejde og kvalitet.

Du får et meningsfuldt arbejde i en samfundsbærende organisation og der er mulighed for hjemmearbejde.

Ansættelsesvilkår

Er du akademiker ansættes du i henhold til den til enhver tid gældende overenskomst mellem Finansministeriet og en række akademikerorganisationer for akademikere i staten (Akademikeroverenskomsten).

Hvis du har en kontoruddannelse eller en anden relevant uddannelse ansættes du i henhold til den til enhver tid gældende Fællesoverenskomst mellem Finansministeriet og Offentligt Ansattes Organisationer – Det Statslige Område (OAO-S-fællesoverenskomsten) og organisationsaftale for kontorfunktionærer, laboranter og it-medarbejdere.

Afhængigt af dine kvalifikationer og kompetencer, er der mulighed for forhandling af tillæg. Der er til stillingen knyttet en arbejdsgiverfinansieret pensionsordning.

Arbejdsstedet er i Karup.

Som ansat under Forsvarsministeriets område er du omfattet af nogle særlige vilkår. Du skal bl.a. kunne sikkerhedsgodkendes og opretholde sikkerhedsgodkendelsen under hele ansættelsesforholdet.

Du har som ansat pligt til at forrette tjeneste uden for landets grænser, herunder ved enheder i internationale operationer med henblik på deltagelse i konfliktforebyggende, fredsbevarende, fredsskabende, humanitære og andre lignende operationer.

Kontakt og ansøgning

Hvis du vil høre mere om stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Pia Jacobsen på telefon 2242 3423.

Har du spørgsmål til løn- og ansættelsesvilkår er du velkommen til at kontakte Personelkommandoen, HR-konsulent Mia Backhausen på telefon 3266 5574.

Ansøgningsfristen er den 2. juli 2026 og samtaler forventes afholdt i forlængelse heraf. Stillingen er til besættelse snarest muligt.

Du søger stillingen via linket og vi beder dig vedhæfte ansøgning, CV og kopi af uddannelsesbevis. Vær opmærksom på, at bilag skal tilføjes i PDF-format.

I Forsvarsministeriets organisation tror vi på, at mangfoldighed optimerer opgaveløsningen. Derfor opfordrer vi alle interesserede uanset baggrund til at søge stillingen.

Om Forsvarskommandoen

Forsvarskommandoen er Danmarks og Rigsfællesskabets øverste militære myndighed.

Forsvarskommandoen løser opgaverne under mottoet ”Værd at kæmpe for”, og vores primære ansvar er at udvikle, producere og indsætte de militære kapaciteter og enheder i nationale og internationale opgaver.

Forsvarskommandoen ledes af forsvarschefen, der samtidig er forsvarsministerens øverste militærfaglige rådgiver. Forsvarschefen har tillige ansvaret for den operative ledelse af de danske militære styrker. Forsvarskommandoen består af Forsvarsstaben og de fem kommandoer; Hærkommandoen, Søværnskommandoen, Flyverkommandoen, Specialoperationskommandoen og Arktisk Kommando.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Tech-first. Ego-free. User-obsessed. Over én million danskere bruger Skatteguiden til at forstå og optimere deres skat. Vi automatiserer skatteovervågning, finder fradrag, som folk ikke vidste de havde, og giver brugerne indsigt til bedre økonomiske beslutninger — direkte forbundet til SKAT. Vi er ~50 mennesker i København, med en 4,7-stjernet rating og én mission: gør finansiel indsigt tilgængelig for alle, ikke kun dem der har råd til en revisor.







Rollen:




Vi har brug for en analytiker, der kan gå i dybden med den skattedata, der driver vores produkter. Du bliver den i datateamet, der forstår skattedata bedst — og en central sparringspartner for de produktteams, der bygger vores skatteberegnings- og fradragsprodukter. Du behøver ikke kende dansk skattelovgivning fra dag ét. Du skal være skarp nok på data til at lære det hurtigt.







Hvad du kommer til at arbejde med




Modellere og dokumentere skattedata i dbt på Snowflake
Udvikle og validere prædiktive modeller — dokumenteret klar til engineering handover
Designe og køre simuleringer med live data og konstruerede scenarier
Vedligeholde skattedatamodeller i takt med regulatoriske ændringer
Bidrage til observability og datakvalitet med et datadrevet perspektiv







Hvad du bringer




Stærkt SQL-fundament og erfaring med kompleks datamodellering
Erfaring med vores data stack: Snowflake, dbt, Lightdash, Evidence eller tilsvarende tooling.
Solid erfaring med prædiktiv modellering og modelvalidering
Analytisk skarp med øje for edge cases og korrekthed
Trives i et tværfunktionelt produktteam — ikke en ren engineering-rolle
Erfaring med Lightdash. Python.
Godt at have kendskab til dansk skattelovgivning, finans eller revision.
Taler flydende dansk







Hvad vi tilbyder




En teknisk dyb rolle i et komplekst og meningsfuldt domæne
Muligheden for at blive organisationens foretrukne ekspert på skattedata
Et stærkt data team med høje faglige standarder og moderne tech stack
Office first i København — fem min fra Rådhuspladsen, frokost hos Claus Meyer, aftensmad hvis dagen er lang




Praktisk Start: Hurtigst muligt

Rapporterer til: Head of Data & Operations Planning

Løn: Kompetitiv og erfaringsbaseret







Our Lean Hiring process




Screening call med Christian Payne, 30 min (online)
45 minutters møde med hiring manager og product manager (onsite)
1 times case session med hiring manager og teammedlem, efterfulgt af kort snak med Nikolai (CEO), 30 min (onsite)

Title: Analytics Manager, Product & Marketing, LEGO® Education
Place: Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Join LEGO Education as a Web Analyst, where you will dive into web data, drive insights, and collaborate with global teams to enhance sales and product experiences. If you have a talent for analytics and love turning data into action, join us to make a real impact!

Please note This role is an individual contributor role at Manager Specialist skyline and we do not offer relocation.


Core Responsibilities





Be an expert on LEGO Education web data; owning and continuously improving the analytics setup end-to-end in collaboration with Product Experience and Digital Technology
Own and maintain the web analytics data pipeline from Matomo (web tracking tool) all the way to gold-layer tables in Databricks, including tracking and metric definition, data QA, and silver/gold view modeling.
Define and govern the Matomo event taxonomy in collaboration with Marketing and PX, ensuring clean, consistent data that downstream reporting can trust.
Map out customer journeys in collaboration with Global Marketing and Product Experience, and pinpoint drop-offs so that we can prioritize enhancements that impacts sales and performance
Provide Product Experience and Global Marketing with proactive insights and intelligence on new trends in marketing – related to new channels, trends, competition etc., ensuring the team is up to date with what moves in the industry. For Product Experience, the analysis is homing in around usage of our product suite and lessons provided.
Enable reporting on in-market results for all media types. This includes analyzing e-com and lead generation results from owned, earned, paid media and collaborating with the business on making recommendations on what may or may not be working and why
Work in a matrix organization; succeeding in acting as a trusted and close sparring partner for the several team members within the Global Marketing and Product Experience team while being anchored in the Global Insights & Analytics team.




Play your part in our team succeeding

This role helps create clear overview on performance of LEGO Education across marketing and product, by helping to pull the global perspective together across different platforms. It will support the business by adding valuable insights to the table and looking for ways to drive demand across both eCommerce, field sales and partner driven sales as well as. fuel Product Experience with new insights on how to further enhance their offering. This role will, together with the full I&A (Insights & Analytics) team, find ways to sharpen insights to the organization through our partnering style setup.

Do you have what it takes?

The ideal candidate brings a relevant educational background combined with solid experience in Analyst roles having translated data into actionable insights as well as demonstrated strong business partnership with senior leaders. In addition, we expect that you have experience with/in





Writing production-grade SQL and Python code for analytical data modeling, including multi-layer transformations (silver/gold or equivalent medallion pattern)
Hands-on web analytics instrumentation, ideally Matomo or comparable, covering tag setup, event tracking, and data validation.
Working with PowerBI or similar data visualization tools
Developing and understanding marketing and product analytics metrics
Managing Senior stakeholders and an ability to navigate tight time pressures




As a person you take initiative, recognizes and acts on opportunities as well as bring am analytical/problem solving mindset. You work and take decisions independently, are a team player and used to working collaboratively across distributed teams. Finally, you show strong communication and presentation skills.

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

Title: Junior Business Analyst
Description:
Are you passionate about data and eager to kick-start your career in e-commerce? Do you want to turn analysis into decisions that influence product availability, sales performance and business growth across international markets?

We are looking for a Junior E-commerce Business Analyst to join VERO MODA's E-commerce team and help strengthen the analytical foundation behind our online business.

THE TEAM

You will join an ambitious E-commerce department focused on growing and developing VERO MODA's online business across markets and channels.

As part of our Business Analytics setup, you will work closely with E-commerce Business Analysts, Buyers and key stakeholders across the organisation. Together, we use data and insights to improve assortment decisions, optimise stock availability and support commercial growth.

We are a team that values curiosity, collaboration and continuous improvement. We challenge assumptions, share knowledge and use data to drive better decisions every day.

THE ROLE

In this role, you will help transform data into actionable insights that support buying decisions, forecasting and commercial performance. You will gain broad exposure to the operational and commercial side of an international e-commerce business while developing your analytical toolbox alongside experienced colleagues. Working closely with different functions across the team, you will support day-to-day operations while taking ownership of your own analytical tasks and projects.

Your key tasks will include:


Developing and refining reporting tools to ensure clear insights
Participating in exciting projects to drive our e-commerce business forward
Evaluating sales trends across channels, countries, and product categories
Supporting the development and optimization of buying tools
Translating sales and stock data into actionable recommendations that support business growth
Assisting with planning and analyzing seasonal buying strategies
Building and improving forecasting tools to better predict trends


Your Benefits


A steep learning curve and close sparring with experienced analysts and buyers
Exposure to key commercial decisions within a growing international e-commerce business
The opportunity to develop strong analytical and business understanding early in your career
A high degree of responsibility and ownership from day one
A collaborative and informal work environment where learning and development are prioritised


YOUR PROFILE

We imagine you’re at the start of your career, excited to learn and grow in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. You enjoy working with data and making sense of numbers, and you are motivated by seeing your insights turn into real results. You are detail-oriented, thrive on collaboration, and enjoy advising your colleagues on how to improve our business.

We imagine that you:


Hold a relevant degree in Business Economics, Supply Chain Management or a related field
Have strong analytical skills and enjoy working with data and numbers
Are curious and eager to learn how data supports commercial decision-making
Work in a structured manner and pay attention to detail
Have experience working with Excel and preferably Power BI. Knowledge of VBA, SQL and/or Python is an advantage
Communicate confidently in English, both written and spoken


WE LOOK FORWARD TO HEARING FROM YOU

If you have any questions about the position, feel free to contact International E-commerce Manager, Mette Vanger Rask Borch at +45 25 51 39 93. Otherwise, please send us your CV as soon as possible no later than 28 June 2026. We review applications on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to remove the advertisement when we find the right candidate.

Workplace: Aarhus C, Denmark

About Vero Moda

Fashion is something we do together – a collective sense of togetherness and belonging.

VERO MODA is BESTSELLER's first fashion label and one of the most recognisable high-street European fashion brands - appealing to all generations, with an authentic and honest expression of style. Our consumer is defined – not by age – but by lifestyle. They are a group of individuals with their own preferences and identity.

We are driven by a collective mindset; that our knowledge, passion and craftsmanship must serve to provide better fashion and create a sense of confidence and free feel. Our clothes infuse a mentality of belonging - and more importantly, a sense of empowerment. Indications of the world, we want to create.

And the heritage from 1987 still runs through every design stitch. But now, join us on our journey to the now & next.

WE ARE VERO MODA – Now & Next.

VERO MODA is part of the worldwide fashion company BESTSELLER. Visit www.veromoda.com or www.bestseller.com.
