Title: Global People Analytics Specialist
Place: Hillerød, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
As the Senior Reporting & Analytics Process Owner, you will own the end-to-end HR reporting and analytics capability for our People & Culture function. You’ll set the standards and governance that ensure a single, trusted source of workforce truth—designing scalable reporting, data models, integrations, and dashboards across Workday, Tableau, and related systems. Your work will fuel strategic decisions for leaders across HR, Finance, and the business.

What you’ll do


Define and govern HR reporting standards and KPI frameworks across People & Culture—ensuring consistent metrics, calculations, and data lineage across Workday and Tableau.
Establish and steward reporting/data governance, role-based security, and access controls aligned with global privacy requirements (e.g., GDPR/CCPA).
Build, optimize, and maintain advanced Workday reports and dashboards (Matrix, Composite, BIRT), calculated fields, schedules, prompts, and secure distribution.
Create and maintain a scalable self-service reporting ecosystem, including a data dictionary, documentation, and development guidelines—co-owned with process partners.
Design and deliver Tableau dashboards and analytics solutions for executives and business stakeholders; model HR datasets (incl. Tableau Prep) and apply best practices for usability and data storytelling.
Translate business questions into actionable analytics and insights on trends, forecasts, segmentation, and workforce dynamics—tracking usage and adoption.
Operate the intake and prioritization process for reporting requests; set clear SLAs, communicate timelines, and manage a backlog of enhancements aligned with quarterly planning.
Enable stakeholders through training and data literacy initiatives; empower HR analysts and business users to self-serve responsibly.
Coordinate and execute the Employee Satisfaction survey program in partnership with P&C leaders.
Partner with HRIS and IT to manage integrations and pipelines between Workday and downstream systems; coordinate defect remediation, release testing, and incident response with solid runbooks.


Impact you’ll have


Elevate decision quality through standardized, timely, and trusted insights.
Reduce operational risk via stronger controls, security, and compliance.
Accelerate planning cycles and executive reporting with scalable, well-governed data products.


Minimum Qualifications


Education
Master’s degree in Business Administration, Data Science, Human Resources, IT, Process Optimization, or a related field.
Equivalent experience may be considered in lieu of a degree for highly qualified candidates.
LEAN SIX Sigma.
Experience
8+ years of progressive experience in HR reporting and analytics, including data governance and stakeholder engagement.
3+ years hands-on Workday reporting experience (Report Writer, Calculated Fields, Security Domains; BIRT and Prism preferred).
2+ years building Tableau and Workday dashboards and data models (Tableau Desktop; Tableau Prep preferred).
2+ years with HR data integrations (Workday EIB/Studio/Core Connectors, REST/SOAP APIs; ETL tools such as Informatica or Boomi a plus).
Experience with privacy and compliance (e.g., GDPR, CCPA), data security, and controls.
Preferred certifications: Workday Pro Reporting; Tableau Desktop Specialist/Certified.
Thorough understanding of HR data models and downstream system impacts.
Proven ability to build strong customer relationships and deliver customer-centric solutions with a global mindset.
Ability to partner across multiple business areas (IT, HR, and country organizations).
Solid and in-depth insights into HR data platforms and tools.

What you’ll bring


Mastery of Workday reporting and security concepts; confidence in advanced report types and performance optimization.
Strong Tableau skills, with a passion for clean design, clear narratives, and practical insights.
Data governance mindset—metadata, quality checks, audit readiness, and privacy-by-design.
Business fluency across the HR lifecycle (Recruiting, Core HCM, Compensation/Benefits, Learning, Talent) and workforce planning.
Clear, audience-appropriate communication and stakeholder management skills; experience training and enabling non-technical users.
Curiosity, ownership, and a continuous-improvement approach to processes and technology.


How we work


Global, cross-functional collaboration with HR COEs, Finance, IT/Data, Legal/Privacy, and regional stakeholders.
A culture that values quality, security, and compliance—without sacrificing speed or usability.
Hybrid flexibility and modern tools to do your best work.


This is a global position that will support all our FUJIFILM Biotechnologies sites. This position can be based at any of our locations around the globe. Benefits and compensation will be governed by the location that you are based from and considered your home site.

As part of any recruitment process, FUJIFILM Biotechnologies collects and processes personal data relating to job applicants. The organisation is committed to being transparent about how it collects and uses that data and to meeting its data protection obligations and may share this as part of the global recruitment process with hiring managers in Europe and the United States.

To all agencies: Please, no phone calls or emails to any employee of FUJIFILM about this requisition. All resumes submitted by search firms/employment agencies to any employee at FUJIFILM via-email, the internet or in any form and/or method will be deemed the sole property of FUJIFILM, unless such search firms/employment agencies were engaged by FUJIFILM for this requisition and a valid agreement with FUJIFILM is in place. In the event a candidate who was submitted outside of the FUJIFILM agency engagement process is hired, no fee or payment of any kind will be paid.

Title: Commercial Analyst
Description:
Are you motivated by data, product performance and commercial decision-making? Do you want to work close to the commercial heart of a growing E-Commerce business, turning sales data, stock insights and product performance into clear commercial actions?

Then you might be the Commercial Analyst we are looking for. This is a newly created role in VILA E-Commerce, where you will develop at the intersection of Business Analysis and Buying. As part of our growing online business, you will work across our own site and Partner Program, including Zalando, About You and ASOS. You will also support NOISY MAY as part of our shared E-Commerce portfolio, gaining exposure to two strong fashion brands and multiple digital sales channels.

TOGETHER, WE ARE THE BEST

You will be part of a dynamic and business-driven E-Commerce department consisting of 14 employees today, who are specialists in each of their areas of responsibility. We take pride in our tasks, both individually and as a team. On top of this, we have a central E-Commerce team within BESTSELLER that we collaborate with on a weekly basis. 

A COMMERCIAL ROLE WITH A STRONG ANALYTICAL FOUNDATION

You will work at the intersection of Business Analysis and Buying across our own site and Partner Program, collaborating closely with our Business Analyst and Senior E-commerce Buyer. Through reporting, KPI follow-up, commercial analysis and NOOS insights, you will help turn data into concrete commercial actions and support product planning and buying decisions. As you build experience, you will take on greater ownership of product performance, stock depth, sell-through and assortment planning across styles and colours, becoming an increasingly important sparring partner in the buying process.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with numbers and performance, but who also wants to move closer to products, buying and commercial decision-making

Your key responsibilities


Follow up on sales, stock, availability and sell-through performance
Use product performance and NOOS-related insights to support commercial recommendations
Identify product opportunities, missed sales and replenishment potential
Support product-level planning across styles, colours, width and depth
Follow up on performance across own site and Partner Program


What We Offer


At VILA, you will gain broad exposure across Business Analysis, Buying and product planning, giving you a unique opportunity to develop your commercial skillset in E-Commerce.
As you grow in the role, you will take on increasing responsibility and play a bigger part in product planning and buying decisions.
Flexible working hours and the opportunity to work remotely are part of our culture.


YOUR PROFILE

You are structured, curious and motivated by understanding what drives sales. You enjoy working with numbers and details, but you are also interested in products, fashion and consumer behaviour. You may come from either an analytical or commercial background - what matters most is that you are curious, structured and motivated by turning data into better product decisions.

We imagine that you:


Have experience from E-Commerce, buying, merchandising or a similar commercial role, where you have worked with products, performance, analysis or planning. 
Are strong in Excel and confident working with numbers
Are curious about product performance and what drives E-Commerce sales
Have an interest in buying, product planning and commercial performance
Enjoy working closely with different stakeholders
Have a digital mindset and use data to drive decisions
Are fluent in English, both written and spoken
Experience with Power BI, NOOS, Partner Program, marketplace performance or buying support is an advantage, but not a requirement.


What Success Looks Like

Success in this role means that our commercial decisions become more data-driven, our product follow-up becomes sharper, and our buying preparation is supported by clear, actionable insights. You will help us understand what is working, where we see potential, and how product performance should influence future decisions on stock, width, depth, replenishment and commercial priorities.

WE ARE LOOKING FORWARD TO HEARING FROM YOU

For questions, please contact Rasmus Bech at +4530852934. Please send your application and CV as soon as possible, but no later than July 14, 2026. Applications are processed on an ongoing basis, and we reserve the right to remove the ad when we find the right candidate. We accept both Danish and English applications.

About Vila

The design philosophy of VILA is based on a feminine approach to fashion, uniting femininity and trend insights. VILA’s signature style is discovered in the mix of prints, delicate cuts and fine details. VILA was established in 1994 as part of BESTSELLER, a 100% family- owned company founded in 1975.Today, VILA is sold through 100+ chain stores all over Europe and 2000 multi-brand stores. VILA also covers vila.com and shop-in-shop. For more information, please visit www.vila.com or www.bestseller.com.

We take pride in our tasks, both individually and as a team. On top of this, we have a central E-Commerce team within BESTSELLER that we collaborate with on a weekly basis. 

A COMMERCIAL ROLE WITH A STRONG ANALYTICAL FOUNDATION

You will work at the intersection of Business Analysis and Buying across our own site and Partner Program, collaborating closely with our Business Analyst and Senior E-commerce Buyer. Through reporting, KPI follow-up, commercial analysis and NOOS insights, you will help turn data into concrete commercial actions and support product planning and buying decisions. As you build experience, you will take on greater ownership of product performance, stock depth, sell-through and assortment planning across styles and colours, becoming an increasingly important sparring partner in the buying process.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with numbers and performance, but who also wants to move closer to products, buying and commercial decision-making

Your key responsibilities

  • Follow up on sales, stock, availability and sell-through performance
  • Use product performance and NOOS-related insights to support commercial recommendations
  • Identify product opportunities, missed sales and replenishment potential
  • Support product-level planning across styles, colours, width and depth
  • Follow up on performance across own site and Partner Program

What We Offer

  • At VILA, you will gain broad exposure across Business Analysis, Buying and product planning, giving you a unique opportunity to develop your commercial skillset in E-Commerce.
  • As you grow in the role, you will take on increasing responsibility and play a bigger part in product planning and buying decisions.
  • Flexible working hours and the opportunity to work remotely are part of our culture.

YOUR PROFILE

You are structured, curious and motivated by understanding what drives sales. You enjoy working with numbers and details, but you are also interested in products, fashion and consumer behaviour. You may come from either an analytical or commercial background - what matters most is that you are curious, structured and motivated by turning data into better product decisions.

We imagine that you:

  • Have experience from E-Commerce, buying, merchandising or a similar commercial role, where you have worked with products, performance, analysis or planning. 
  • Are strong in Excel and confident working with numbers
  • Are curious about product performance and what drives E-Commerce sales
  • Have an interest in buying, product planning and commercial performance
  • Enjoy working closely with different stakeholders
  • Have a digital mindset and use data to drive decisions
  • Are fluent in English, both written and spoken
  • Experience with Power BI, NOOS, Partner Program, marketplace performance or buying support is an advantage, but not a requirement.

What Success Looks Like

Success in this role means that our commercial decisions become more data-driven, our product follow-up becomes sharper, and our buying preparation is supported by clear, actionable insights. You will help us understand what is working, where we see potential, and how product performance should influence future decisions on stock, width, depth, replenishment and commercial priorities.

WE ARE LOOKING FORWARD TO HEARING FROM YOU

For questions, please contact Rasmus Bech at +4530852934. Please send your application and CV as soon as possible, but no later than July 14, 2026. Applications are processed on an ongoing basis, and we reserve the right to remove the ad when we find the right candidate. We accept both Danish and English applications.

About Vila

The design philosophy of VILA is based on a feminine approach to fashion, uniting femininity and trend insights. VILA’s signature style is discovered in the mix of prints, delicate cuts and fine details. VILA was established in 1994 as part of BESTSELLER, a 100% family- owned company founded in 1975.Today, VILA is sold through 100+ chain stores all over Europe and 2000 multi-brand stores. VILA also covers vila.com and shop-in-shop. For more information, please visit www.vila.com or www.bestseller.com.

Title: FP&A Analyst
Description:
Nobia - a leading European kitchen specialist listed on Nasdaq Stockholm - is looking for a Business Analyst / Business Controller to join our Finance function in Aarhus. This is not a traditional controlling role. We are building the finance function of the future: one where data, AI and sharp business insight come together to drive real value across our Nordic brands.

You will work closely with the Finance leadership and across the wider business to elevate our performance management agenda, automate how we work with data, and bring AI-powered solutions into the heart of finance. You will be part of a function in transformation, with significant room to shape both the way we work and your own development path.

What You’ll Do

Your work will sit at the intersection of business partnering, analytics and AI-driven automation. Concretely, you will:


Build and drive our monthly performance management cycle - from data to insight to decision - in close collaboration with business and finance leaders.
Lead deep-dive analyses on margin, customer and product profitability, and cost drivers, translating complex data into clear, actionable recommendations.
Identify and implement AI pilot projects within finance processes - from automating reporting flows to building intelligent analytical workflows.
Act as a trusted business partner, asking the right questions and challenging assumptions with a calm, data-driven perspective.
Contribute to building the analytical foundation and tooling that our finance function will rely on for years to come.


Who You Are

We are looking for a sharp analytical profile, with strong ambitions and the right mindset. Most importantly, you are genuinely curious - constantly looking for ways to do things better and create greater value. You want to be part of shaping the future finance function, where we drive impact for the business by keeping a cool, clear overview of what really matters.

We expect that you bring:


2 - 4 years of relevant experience from a controlling, FP&A, business analyst or consulting role - ideally in a multi-brand, multi-country or retail/consumer goods context.
A strong analytical toolkit: solid command of Python and/or SQL for data analysis and automation.
Hands-on experience with modern AI tools (Copilot, Claude or similar) - and an active interest in how AI changes the way finance works.
A relevant academic background (e.g. cand.merc., cand.oecon., cand.polit., or similar).
Strong business acumen - you see numbers as a means to better decisions, not an end in themselves.


The mindset we’re looking for

More than any specific skill, we care about how you think. You are curious by default, and you ask “how could we do this better?” before you ask “how have we always done it?” You see AI and automation not as buzzwords, but as practical levers to free up time for the analyses that actually move the business. You are comfortable challenging stakeholders constructively, and you keep a cool head when complexity rises.

What we offer


A pivotal role in building the next chapter of finance at Nobia Denmark.
Direct exposure to senior business and finance leadership, with real influence on the agenda.
A workplace based in Tilst with strong flexibility around how and where you work.
Competitive compensation and the opportunity to grow alongside a finance function in transformation.
A culture where curiosity, ownership and continuous improvement are genuinely valued.


Curious for more?

Get a glimpse of what life is like at Nobia right here!

We look forward to hearing from you!

If you have any questions about the position, feel free to reach out to our CFO Nobia Denmark, Rasmus Sylvester Nørgreen on rsl@nobia.dk.

A cover letter is not necessary, but please include a short motivation in your CV. We process applications on an ongoing basis, so we encourage you to send your CV as soon as possible.

Start date: As soon as possible — we are ready when you are.

Title: Data Scientist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Hver dag sikrer Safe & Secure Operations, at lufthavnen fungerer sikkert og effektivt fra start til slut. Vi har ansvaret for den operative drift på tværs af lufthavnen fra flyoperationer og bagage til infrastruktur, compliance og støttefunktioner.

Du bliver en del af et område med tæt kobling til driften, hvor vi arbejder døgnet rundt for at få tusindvis af rejsende og fly sikkert og rettidigt gennem lufthavnen og skabe en stabil og velfungerende hverdag for passagerer, flyselskaber og samarbejdspartnere.

Om at være Data Scientist

Som Data Scientist får du en central rolle i arbejdet med vores prognosemodeller og ressourcebehov. Modellerne er centrale styringsværktøjer for driften og danner grundlag for planlægning, kapacitet og prioriteringer. Dine analyser omsættes hurtigt til beslutninger med direkte betydning for både den daglige drift og den langsigtede udvikling.

Du arbejder tæt sammen med to kolleger om løbende at udvikle og forbedre modellerne samt styrke beslutningsgrundlaget. Samtidig er vi i gang med at flytte vores setup til DataBricks, hvor du får en vigtig rolle i den videre udvikling.

Du bliver desuden en del af CPH’s fælles datacommunity på tværs af organisationen. Communitiet drives af Digital, som forankrer vores fælles data og platformstandarder. Her får du adgang til sparring, videndeling og et tæt samarbejde med centrale data- og platformsteams, så vi sikrer et fælles teknisk fundament og sammenhængende dataløsninger.

Vi ser frem til, at du bidrager med at:


Tage ejerskab for modeller og prognoser – fra udvikling til implementering
Omsætter komplekse problemstillinger til robuste, beslutningsunderstøttende løsninger
Analysere konsekvenser af ændringer i trafik, processer og produktivitet på kapacitets- og ressourcebehov
Kommunikerer klart og skaber tillid til datadrevne indsigter på tværs af interessenter
Udfordrer eksisterende metoder og bidrager til teamets faglige udvikling


Du har fornøjelsen af et tæt samarbejde med kolleger i Security, Operational Airport Services, Digital, Politiet og Ground Handler, og du er en vigtig sparringspartner i arbejdet med at skabe datadrevne løsninger, der gør en forskel i Nordens største lufthavn.

Om teamet

Teamet består af medarbejdere med kompetencer inden for forecasting, dataanalyse, ressourceplanlægning og udvikling af digitale løsninger. Sammen understøtter teamet den operationelle drift ved at levere beslutningsgrundlag, planlægningsværktøjer og digitale løsninger på tværs af operationen.

Om dig

Du trives i et miljø, hvor data gør en reel forskel. Du motiveres af at arbejde med komplekse datasæt samt at udvikle og vedligeholde prognosemodeller. Du skaber visuelle og letforståelige analyser, som understøtter konkrete beslutninger i driften og bidrager til bedre planlægning og prioritering. Du kommunikerer tydeligt og kan forklare komplekse sammenhænge på en måde, der giver mening for både tekniske og ikke tekniske kolleger.

For at komme i betragtning forventer vi, at du har:


Erfaring med udvikling af modeller til estimering ressourcebehov
Solid erfaring med Machine Learning og statistisk modellering
Stærke kompetencer i Python og SQL, kendskab til Databricks og Power BI er en fordel, men ikke en nødvendighed
Gode samarbejdsevner og lyst til at arbejde tæt med forretningen


Derudover ser vi gerne, at du:


Har en relevant uddannelsesbaggrund, fx matematik, økonomi, ingeniørfag eller lignende
Er nysgerrig, struktureret og trives i et miljø med højt tempo og kort vej til handling


Om os | Hvad tilbyder vi

Du bliver en del af en organisation med en helt særlig puls og et fælles mål om at levere den bedste rejseoplevelse hver dag.

Vi tilbyder et arbejdsmiljø med høj faglighed, stærkt samarbejde og gode muligheder for både personlig og faglig udvikling.

Ud over frokostordning, telefon og it-udstyr tilbyder vi blandt andet:


Gratis parkering og cykelservice
Fysioterapi og træningsfaciliteter
Sundhedsforsikring hvor børn er dækket gratis
Attraktiv barselordning uanset køn


Om ansøgningen | Stig ombord på dit nye job

Vi ser frem til at læse din ansøgning, så send den gerne snarest muligt og senest d. 28. juni 2026.

Vi modtager kun ansøgninger via vores rekrutteringssystem og ikke pr. mail.

Har du spørgsmål til jobbet inden da, kan du kontakte Ali Agha Javed, Senior Manager Analytics på ali.agha.javed@cph.dk.

Vi ønsker at besætte stillingen hurtigst muligt og afholder derfor samtaler løbende, samtidig med at vi tager hensyn til sommerferien og planlægger fleksibelt i forhold til både dine og vores planer.

Læs mere om hvordan det er at være medarbejder hos os på cph.dk/job.

Det er en forudsætning for ansættelse, at du kan blive politigodkendt, ligesom du skal kunne opretholde politigodkendelsen gennem hele din ansættelse.

Derudover er det en forudsætning for at arbejde i Københavns Lufthavne, at du begår dig komfortabelt på dansk- både skriftligt og mundtligt.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join Vestas and help turn data into powerful business insights. As part of our Reporting & Analytics team, you will build scalable data models and dashboards using modern cloud platforms, driving smarter decisions across a global organization.

Finance > Group Financial Performance > Reporting & Analytics CoE

The Reporting & Analytics (R&A) Centre of Excellence is a global function in Finance responsible for enabling data-driven performance management and decision-making across Vestas. The CoE brings together business-facing analytics, governance and definitions, analytics engineering, and operations into a single, integrated operating model. Its purpose is to create a scalable, standardised, and high-quality analytics environment that delivers “one version of the truth” across Vestas. The R&A CoE works closely with Finance, Commercial, Operations, and Digital to translate business needs into analytics products, while ensuring consistency in data definitions, efficient delivery, and modern engineering practices. By combining subject matter knowledge, technical capability, and operational excellence, the CoE plays a critical role in improving performance transparency, accelerating decision-making, and reducing manual reporting across the enterprise.

Responsibilities

In this role, you will contribute to the development of analytical solutions, including presentation layers with data visualizations, semantic layers, and core analytical data models that support business decision-making. As part of the Analytics Engineering pillar, you will play a key role in building and delivering scalable analytics products.


Design and develop analytical solutions, including dashboards, semantic layers, and data models.
Collaborate closely with Product Owners and Business Analysts to translate business requirements into effective analytics solutions. Work cross-functionally with domain experts, governance teams, and other stakeholders to ensure alignment and consistency.
Contribute to the design and implementation of analytics data architecture.
Develop and enhance the core layers of analytical solutions (data models, semantic and presentation layers).
Maintain, optimize, and continuously improve existing data models and analytics solutions.
Support the rollout of analytics solutions, including validation, testing, documentation, and training.
Drive performance optimization and efficient maintenance of analytics products.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s in Computer Science or a related field, with a preference for specialisation in data analytics or data modelling.
3–5+ years of relevant experience in designing and building analytical solutions
Interest in understanding business processes and domain concepts. Demonstrated ability to translate business requirements into effective analytical solutions.
Hands-on experience with cloud-based data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Microsoft Fabric)
Expertise in data modelling and proficiency in SQL
Experience with data visualization tools, particularly Power BI
Exposure to predictive modelling is considered an advantage


Competencies


Ability to approach problems logically, interpret data effectively, and derive meaningful insights
Proactive in identifying challenges and delivering practical, data-driven solutions
Eagerness to learn, adapt, and explore new tools, technologies, and business areas
High level of accuracy and precision in data handling and solution development
Team player with the ability to work effectively across cross-functional teams
Clear and structured communication, with the ability to explain complex concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Openness to feedback and a drive to continuously enhance skills, processes, and outcomes


What We Offer

You will gain exposure to a global working environment, collaborating with diverse teams across regions, while working on large-scale, cloud-based analytical platforms. The role offers good opportunities for continuous learning and professional development, supported by access to modern tools and technologies within a forward-looking analytics ecosystem.

Additional Information

Primary work location is Copenhagen, Denmark

We do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to the advertised closing date. Please be advised to apply on or before 4th July 2026.

Our commitment to a fair hiring.

At Vestas, we evaluate all candidates solely based on their professional experience, education, and relevant skills. To support a fair recruitment process, we kindly ask that you remove any photos, dates of birth or graduation, gender pronouns, marital status, or other personal information not relevant to the role before submitting your CV/resume. Your CV/resume should focus on your professional and educational background, along with the necessary contact details (email and phone number). We train our hiring teams in inclusive evaluation and regularly review process outcomes to ensure fairness.

DEIB Statement

At Vestas, we recognize the value of diversity, equity, and inclusion in driving innovation and success. We strongly encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply, particularly those who may hesitate due to their identity or feel they do not meet every criterion. As our CEO states, "Expertise and talent come in many forms, and a diverse workforce enhances our ability to think differently and solve the complex challenges of our industry". Your unique perspective is what will help us powering the solution for a sustainable, green energy future.

BEWARE – RECRUITMENT FRAUD

It has come to our attention that there are a number of fraudulent emails from people pretending to work for Vestas. Read more via this link, https://www.vestas.com/en/careers/our-recruitment-process

About Vestas

Vestas is the energy industry’s global partner on sustainable energy solutions. We are specialised in designing, manufacturing, installing, and servicing wind turbines, both onshore and offshore.

Across the globe, we have installed more wind power than anyone else. We consider ourselves pioneers within the industry, as we continuously aim to design new solutions and technologies to create a more sustainable future for all of us. With more than 185 GW of wind power installed worldwide and 40+ years of experience in wind energy, we have an unmatched track record demonstrating our expertise within the field.

With 30,000 employees globally, we are a diverse team united by a common goal: to power the solution – today, tomorrow, and far into the future.

Vestas promotes a diverse workforce which embraces all social identities and is free of any discrimination. We commit to create and sustain an environment that acknowledges and harvests different experiences, skills, and perspectives. We also aim to give everyone equal access to opportunity.

To learn more about our company and life at Vestas, we invite you to visit our website at www.vestas.com and follow us on our social media channels. We also encourage you to join our Talent Universe to receive notifications on new and relevant postings.

  • Support the rollout of analytics solutions, including validation, testing, documentation, and training.
  • Drive performance optimization and efficient maintenance of analytics products.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s in Computer Science or a related field, with a preference for specialisation in data analytics or data modelling.
  • 3–5+ years of relevant experience in designing and building analytical solutions
  • Interest in understanding business processes and domain concepts. Demonstrated ability to translate business requirements into effective analytical solutions.
  • Hands-on experience with cloud-based data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Microsoft Fabric)
  • Expertise in data modelling and proficiency in SQL
  • Experience with data visualization tools, particularly Power BI
  • Exposure to predictive modelling is considered an advantage

Competencies

  • Ability to approach problems logically, interpret data effectively, and derive meaningful insights
  • Proactive in identifying challenges and delivering practical, data-driven solutions
  • Eagerness to learn, adapt, and explore new tools, technologies, and business areas
  • High level of accuracy and precision in data handling and solution development
  • Team player with the ability to work effectively across cross-functional teams
  • Clear and structured communication, with the ability to explain complex concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
  • Openness to feedback and a drive to continuously enhance skills, processes, and outcomes

What We Offer

You will gain exposure to a global working environment, collaborating with diverse teams across regions, while working on large-scale, cloud-based analytical platforms. The role offers good opportunities for continuous learning and professional development, supported by access to modern tools and technologies within a forward-looking analytics ecosystem.

Additional Information

Primary work location is Copenhagen, Denmark

We do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to the advertised closing date. Please be advised to apply on or before 4th July 2026.

Our commitment to a fair hiring.

At Vestas, we evaluate all candidates solely based on their professional experience, education, and relevant skills. To support a fair recruitment process, we kindly ask that you remove any photos, dates of birth or graduation, gender pronouns, marital status, or other personal information not relevant to the role before submitting your CV/resume. Your CV/resume should focus on your professional and educational background, along with the necessary contact details (email and phone number). We train our hiring teams in inclusive evaluation and regularly review process outcomes to ensure fairness.

DEIB Statement

At Vestas, we recognize the value of diversity, equity, and inclusion in driving innovation and success. We strongly encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply, particularly those who may hesitate due to their identity or feel they do not meet every criterion. As our CEO states, "Expertise and talent come in many forms, and a diverse workforce enhances our ability to think differently and solve the complex challenges of our industry". Your unique perspective is what will help us powering the solution for a sustainable, green energy future.

BEWARE – RECRUITMENT FRAUD

It has come to our attention that there are a number of fraudulent emails from people pretending to work for Vestas. Read more via this link, https://www.vestas.com/en/careers/our-recruitment-process

About Vestas

Vestas is the energy industry’s global partner on sustainable energy solutions. We are specialised in designing, manufacturing, installing, and servicing wind turbines, both onshore and offshore.

Across the globe, we have installed more wind power than anyone else. We consider ourselves pioneers within the industry, as we continuously aim to design new solutions and technologies to create a more sustainable future for all of us. With more than 185 GW of wind power installed worldwide and 40+ years of experience in wind energy, we have an unmatched track record demonstrating our expertise within the field.

With 30,000 employees globally, we are a diverse team united by a common goal: to power the solution – today, tomorrow, and far into the future.

Vestas promotes a diverse workforce which embraces all social identities and is free of any discrimination. We commit to create and sustain an environment that acknowledges and harvests different experiences, skills, and perspectives. We also aim to give everyone equal access to opportunity.

To learn more about our company and life at Vestas, we invite you to visit our website at www.vestas.com and follow us on our social media channels. We also encourage you to join our Talent Universe to receive notifications on new and relevant postings.

Title: Senior Logistics Analyst
Place: Odense, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join Teradyne Robotics and shape the future of automation

Are you ready to make a real impact on the world of robotics? At Teradyne Robotics, a division of Teradyne, we are revolutionizing industries through cutting-edge collaborative and autonomous mobile robotic technologies. As part of our team, you’ll work with global leaders Universal Robots (UR) and Mobile Industrial Robots (MiR), driving innovation that enhances manufacturing, logistics and workplaces across the world.

Your role

When joining Teradyne Robotics as a Senior Logistics Analyst you will become a part of our Global Supply Chain organization and play a critical role in optimizing our worldwide inbound and outbound logistics operations. You will partner closely with the Enterprise Logistics team to ensure cost‑effective, reliable, and scalable logistics performance across our global network.

While the primary focus is logistics excellence, you will also support adjacent areas such as indirect spend analysis, supplier performance insights, and broader supply chain optimization initiatives. The ideal candidate brings not only deep logistics expertise but also a forward‑looking mindset, leveraging AI‑driven analytics tools to enhance decision‑making, forecasting, and operational efficiency.

Key Responsibilities


Lead analysis and optimization of global inbound and outbound logistics, ensuring cost efficiency, service reliability, and alignment with business needs.
Evaluate and manage shipping lanes, carrier performance, and transportation modes to drive continuous improvement.
Develop and implement tariff management strategies, including duty optimization, trade compliance considerations, and risk mitigation.
Partner with the Enterprise Logistics team to build scalable processes, dashboards, and KPIs that support global logistics visibility.
Use AI‑enabled analytics tools to enhance forecasting, scenario modeling, anomaly detection, and cost‑to‑serve analysis.
Identify opportunities to automate routine logistics workflows using AI‑supported solutions to improve speed and accuracy.
Conduct cost modeling, scenario analysis, and forecasting to support strategic decision‑making.
Collaborate with cross‑functional teams—including Procurement, Manufacturing, Planning, and Finance—to support broader supply chain initiatives.
Provide analytical support for indirect spend categories, identifying opportunities for cost reduction and process improvement.
Monitor global trade trends, regulatory changes, and market conditions to proactively adjust logistics strategies.
Drive root‑cause analysis and corrective actions for logistics disruptions, delays, or cost variances.
Support supplier negotiations with data‑driven insights and performance metrics.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Logistics, Business, Engineering, or related field.
7+ years of experience in global logistics, transportation management, or supply chain analytics, ideally in a manufacturing or technology environment.
Strong knowledge of international shipping practices, freight forwarding, Incoterms, customs processes, and tariff structures.
Demonstrated experience optimizing shipping lanes, carrier networks, and logistics cost structures.
Proficiency with analytics tools (Excel, Power BI, Tableau, Rapid Response, or similar) and ERP systems (Oracle, AX, or similar).
Experience using or implementing AI‑powered analytics, automation tools, or machine‑learning‑based forecasting within supply chain or logistics environments.
Strong understanding of trade compliance, duty mitigation strategies, and global regulatory environments.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to influence cross‑functional stakeholders.
Proven ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic, global environment.
Experience supporting or analyzing indirect spend categories is a plus.


What We’re Looking For

A strategic thinker who can dive deep into data, challenge assumptions, and bring forward solutions that improve cost, speed, and reliability across our logistics network. Someone who embraces modern digital tools—including AI—to elevate the quality and speed of decision‑making. A senior‑level contributor who is comfortable operating with autonomy, partnering across global teams, and shaping the future of logistics at Teradyne Robotics.

Why Teradyne Robotics?


Work with cutting-edge robotic technology
Join a fast-paced, innovative, global environment
Make a real impact on quality and supply chain performance.


Ready to Make an Impact?

Join us and help build ahigh-performing, quality-driven supply chain that delivers excellence from concept to customer.

With over 1,200 employees worldwide, Teradyne Robotics provides a global, fast-paced and highly innovative working environment. If you are passionate about working with cutting-edge robotics, solving complex challenges and collaborating with motivated colleagues across the world, this is the place for you.

We offer a dynamic environment that fosters creativity, growth, and career advancement. If you’re passionate about cutting-edge technology, solving complex challenges, and building the future alongside talented, forward-thinking colleagues—this is the place for you. Let’s change the way the world works together.

Title: Business Analyst med et stort kommercielt mindset søges til Q8 Danmark
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Ansøg senest: 1. juli 2026
Hybrid



Jobbeskrivelse

Vil du være med til at udvikle fremtidens B2B-salg i Q8 – og hjælpe vores kunder med at lykkes i den grønne omstilling? Så søg stillingen som Business Analyst & Sales Excellence Coordinator hos Q8 Danmark i dag. Det kan være dit livs måske vigtigste job. Seriøst!

Hos Q8 Danmark har vi en klar ambition: Vi vil være en stærk partner for vores B2B-kunder og hjælpe dem med løsninger, der både understøtter deres daglige drift og deres rejse mod en mere bæredygtig fremtid.

Derfor søger vi en Business Analyst & Sales Excellence Coordinator, der kan bidrage til at styrke vores salgsorganisation gennem data, struktur, digitale værktøjer og kommerciel indsigt. Du bliver en del af et team, hvor vi arbejder tæt på kunderne, markedet og salgsledelsen – og hvor målet er at omsætte indsigt til handling, bedre kundeoplevelser og stærkere resultater.

Rollen

Du bliver en del af B2B Sales og får en central rolle i at understøtte og udvikle salgsorganisationen. I hverdagen vil du arbejde tæt sammen med både salgsledelsen og sælgerne, hvor du bidrager med analyser, struktur og koordinering, der hjælper organisationen med at træffe bedre beslutninger og arbejde mere effektivt.

Rollen er en blanding af analyse og forretningsforståelse, hvor du skal kunne omsætte data og indsigter til konkrete tiltag i salget. Samtidig vil du fungere som bindeled mellem forskellige afdelinger og være med til at sikre fremdrift på opgaver og projekter.

Du vil få mulighed for gradvist at tage mere ansvar og drive egne initiativer i takt med, at du vokser i rollen. Derfor er det vigtigt, at du er nysgerrig, proaktiv og motiveret for at lære – og at du trives i en rolle, hvor du både arbejder med detaljer i data og samarbejde på tværs af organisationen.

Dine primære opgaver


Drive og udvikle Sales Excellence initiativer
Bidrage til udarbejdelse af strategier for salgsinitiativer og sikre, at de omsættes til konkrete aktiviteter
Udarbejde analyser og beslutningsgrundlag (Power BI, CRM, segmentering)
Sikre ledelsesrapportering og opfølgning
Bidrage til budgettering, opfølgning og faktabaserede vurderinger af salgsresultater og forretningspotentiale
Optimere salgsprocesser og værktøjer
Understøtte segmentering og kundeindsigter
Drive projekter på tværs af organisationen
Bidrage til øget lønsomhed i forretningen



Dine kvalifikationer

Hvem er du?


Du har en stærk kommerciel forståelse
Du er analytisk og struktureret
Du har gennemslagskraft og kan skabe fremdrift – også uden formelt ledelsesansvar
Du er handlekraftig og klar til at lære og udvikle dig
Du er selvkørende og tager initiativ
Du kan samarbejde på tværs og skabe relationer i organisationen
Du kommunikerer klart og tydeligt



Dine kompetencer


Erfaring med analyse (Power BI, Excel eller lignende)
Interesse for CRM og datadrevne beslutninger
Evne til at strukturere og visualisere data
God forståelse for sammenhængen mellem data, kunder, salg og lønsomhed



Vi tilbyder

Vi tilbyder en central rolle med gode udviklingsmuligheder og tæt samarbejde med salgsledelsen. Du får mulighed for at præge Sales Excellence arbejdet og blive en del af et dynamisk miljø med høj udvikling og et stærkt kollegaskab.

Vi ønsker, at alle har en sund balance mellem arbejde og fritid. Derfor giver vi mulighed for at arbejde fleksibelt ud fra, hvordan det passer ind i dit liv.

Lyder det interessant?

Send os din ansøgning og CV senest den 01.07.2026.

Vi afholder løbende samtaler, og vi tillader os derfor at tage jobopslaget ned, når vi har fundet et godt match.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, kan du kontakte Gert Thomasen, Head of Sales på gert.thomasen@q8.dk

Vi glæder os til at modtage din ansøgning og at høre mere om, hvorfor det lige netop er dig, der er den bedste til jobbet.

Vi gør opmærksom på, at vi kun tager imod ansøgninger via ansøgningslinket.

Rekrutteringsprocessen

I denne rekrutteringsproces har du forskellige muligheder for at ansøge hos os.

Du kan både sende os dit CV og din motiverede ansøgning, eller du kan sende os en video, hvor du her fortæller om dig selv, hvad du har lavet førhen og hvorfor du er den rette til jobbet.

Uanset hvad du vælger, ser vi frem til at høre fra dig.

Det kan være dit livs måske vigtigste ansøgning - seriøst.

Title: Business Intelligence Manager Specialist
Place: Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

As a Business Intelligence Manager Specialist, you will lead the delivery of high-impact data insights supporting Supplier Services team within the Business Service Organization (BSO). You will own the end-to-end BI lifecycle - from scoping and design to delivery and governance - while acting as a trusted partner to senior stakeholders. At the same time, you will support your team with elevating the standard of BI practice.

This role combines strategic thinking with deep technical expertise. You will drive reporting standardization, lead data-driven initiatives, and shape how the organization uses data to make better informed decisions faster.

This role is based in Billund. No relocation support is offered.


Core Responsibilities

Insight & analytics leadership





Lead the design and delivery of complex analyses across large, multi-source datasets, translating findings into clear, actionable business recommendations
Own the end-to-end reporting lifecycle creation, maintenance, documentation, improvement, and decommissioning of BI reports and dashboards
Identify business trends proactively and drive insight-led recommendations for process improvement and strategic growth
Define and facilitate prioritization of reporting requirements




Power BI & technical excellence





Architect and maintain advanced interactive dashboards and reports with full Power BI proficiency, including complex semantic models and DAX querying - this is a core requirement of the role
Champion data visualization best practices and enforce consistent standards across the team's BI output
Drive reporting standardization and ensure alignment on definitions, metrics, and governance across the organization




Stakeholder partnership





Act as the primary BI partner for senior stakeholders facilitate requirements gathering, manage expectations, and deliver outputs that directly inform leadership decisions
Present findings and recommendations to leadership in a clear, compelling, and audience-appropriate manner
Assist in the design and implementation of data-driven strategies that support business growth objectives




Governance, enablement & continuous improvement





Lead training and ongoing support for business users in BI reporting, data governance, and self-service analytics
Serve as a go-to expert for BI best practices, tools, and emerging technologies -continuously raising the bar within the team




AI-powered efficiency





Champion the adoption of AI tools (including LLM-based co-pilots, generative AI assistants, etc.) across the BI team’s workflows, from report and model development and DAX authoring to documentation, QA, and data storytelling
Identify and implement AI-driven efficiencies in recurring BI processes, including automated documentation generation, insight narration, anomaly detection, and report testing, reducing time-to-insight without compromising quality or governance
Define and role-model responsible AI usage within the BI function, establishing guardrails, sharing best practices with the team, and ensuring AI-assisted outputs meet the organization’s data accuracy and governance standards
Contribute to a culture of data literacy across BSO, enabling stakeholders to engage confidently with data




Play your part in our team succeeding

You will join the Service Experience team within BSO - a collaborative environment that spans Business Account Management, Service Management, Business Process Management and Insights Hub. At Manager Specialist level, you are expected to take ownership, operate with a high degree of independence, and bring others along – supporting colleagues and elevating the standard of BI practice across the team.

Do you have what it takes?





Bachelor's degree in Business Intelligence, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
5 - 7 years of experience in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, or a closely related discipline
Expert-level Power BI proficiency, including advanced DAX modelling - non-negotiable for this role
Demonstrated ability to lead BI projects end-to-end, from requirements definition through to delivery and adoption
Strong command of data visualization principles and the ability to translate complex data into executive-ready narratives
Experience partnering with and influencing senior stakeholders, including leadership-level audiences
Excellent communication skills - able to bridge the gap between technical teams and business decision-makers
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and structured thinking skills, with comfort navigating ambiguity




And even better if you also bring;





Experience with the Databricks platform or equivalent cloud data environments
Exposure to BI governance frameworks, data cataloguing, or data product thinking
Proven track record of driving standardization or center-of-excellence-type initiatives within a BI function
Practical experience using AI tools (e.g. Microsoft Copilot, GitHub Copilot, or similar) to accelerate BI delivery - including report development, documentation, insight generation, etc.




Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

Power BI & technical excellence



  • Architect and maintain advanced interactive dashboards and reports with full Power BI proficiency, including complex semantic models and DAX querying - this is a core requirement of the role
  • Champion data visualization best practices and enforce consistent standards across the team's BI output
  • Drive reporting standardization and ensure alignment on definitions, metrics, and governance across the organization



Stakeholder partnership



  • Act as the primary BI partner for senior stakeholders facilitate requirements gathering, manage expectations, and deliver outputs that directly inform leadership decisions
  • Present findings and recommendations to leadership in a clear, compelling, and audience-appropriate manner
  • Assist in the design and implementation of data-driven strategies that support business growth objectives



Governance, enablement & continuous improvement



  • Lead training and ongoing support for business users in BI reporting, data governance, and self-service analytics
  • Serve as a go-to expert for BI best practices, tools, and emerging technologies -continuously raising the bar within the team



AI-powered efficiency



  • Champion the adoption of AI tools (including LLM-based co-pilots, generative AI assistants, etc.) across the BI team’s workflows, from report and model development and DAX authoring to documentation, QA, and data storytelling
  • Identify and implement AI-driven efficiencies in recurring BI processes, including automated documentation generation, insight narration, anomaly detection, and report testing, reducing time-to-insight without compromising quality or governance
  • Define and role-model responsible AI usage within the BI function, establishing guardrails, sharing best practices with the team, and ensuring AI-assisted outputs meet the organization’s data accuracy and governance standards
  • Contribute to a culture of data literacy across BSO, enabling stakeholders to engage confidently with data



Play your part in our team succeeding

You will join the Service Experience team within BSO - a collaborative environment that spans Business Account Management, Service Management, Business Process Management and Insights Hub. At Manager Specialist level, you are expected to take ownership, operate with a high degree of independence, and bring others along – supporting colleagues and elevating the standard of BI practice across the team.

Do you have what it takes?



  • Bachelor's degree in Business Intelligence, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
  • 5 - 7 years of experience in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, or a closely related discipline
  • Expert-level Power BI proficiency, including advanced DAX modelling - non-negotiable for this role
  • Demonstrated ability to lead BI projects end-to-end, from requirements definition through to delivery and adoption
  • Strong command of data visualization principles and the ability to translate complex data into executive-ready narratives
  • Experience partnering with and influencing senior stakeholders, including leadership-level audiences
  • Excellent communication skills - able to bridge the gap between technical teams and business decision-makers
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and structured thinking skills, with comfort navigating ambiguity



And even better if you also bring;



  • Experience with the Databricks platform or equivalent cloud data environments
  • Exposure to BI governance frameworks, data cataloguing, or data product thinking
  • Proven track record of driving standardization or center-of-excellence-type initiatives within a BI function
  • Practical experience using AI tools (e.g. Microsoft Copilot, GitHub Copilot, or similar) to accelerate BI delivery - including report development, documentation, insight generation, etc.



Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

Title: Analytic Engineer
Place: Høje-Taastrup Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
We are looking for an Analytics Engineer to join our IT organisation and take ownership of our data and reporting landscape—helping drive smarter, data-driven decision-making across the business. This is a broad, hands-on role with significant responsibility, where you will work closely with stakeholders across the organisation and translate business needs into impactful data solutions. The role reports directly to the Head of IT.




Your Role

You will act as the bridge between business and technology—combining strong technical skills with the ability to understand and translate business requirements into actionable solutions.




Your key responsibilities will include:




Gathering and clarifying requirements from internal stakeholders and translating them into concrete data and reporting solutions
Acting as a technical resource who can turn business needs into practical, scalable solutions
Maintaining and further developing existing Power BI reports and applications
Designing and building new reports, dashboards, and data visualisations
Taking ownership of key data domains, including master data, ensuring data quality and consistency across systems
Supporting and driving data integration projects, ensuring data flows effectively between platforms, including ERP systems
Working with and understanding our existing data platform in Microsoft Fabric, including data pipelines for ingestion from source systems and PySpark notebooks for data transformation (bronze → gold layers)
Exploring opportunities to leverage data and AI to improve business processes and insights




Your Profile

We are open to both junior and mid-level candidates—the right mindset and capabilities matter more than years of experience. We are looking for a technical profile with strong business understanding—someone who can bridge the gap between data and decision-making.




You:

Have experience with Microsoft Power BI (report development, data modelling, DAX)
Are comfortable working with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Can translate business requirements into structured, technical solutions
Work in a structured and independent manner




It is a plus if you have experience with:

Microsoft Fabric, Azure, or similar platforms
PySpark or data transformation workflows
Data integration and working with ERP systems
AI tools or data-driven automation




What You’ll Get

A role with high ownership and real impact
Direct exposure to decision-makers and close collaboration with the business
The opportunity to shape and develop our data, reporting, and AI capabilities
A flexible role where you can grow with the responsibility




Why Join Us?

This role sits within our IT organisation, reporting directly to the Head of IT, and plays a key role in shaping how IT supports and enables the business through data. At TITAN, we are a family-owned company, which means there is a very short distance from idea to action. Within IT, this translates into real influence—you won’t be stuck in layers of governance or long decision cycles. Instead, you will work closely with both IT and business stakeholders to quickly turn ideas into solutions. As part of IT, you will take ownership of our data and reporting landscape, acting as a key link between technology and the business. You will not only build solutions, but also help define how data, reporting, and AI are used across the organisation.




About TITAN

We safeguard what matters most to you. Because for us, every container is a box of opportunities. Founded in Copenhagen in 1987, TITAN has grown into the world’s largest family-owned containerised storage solutions company, operating in more than 90 countries. With a fleet of over 50,000 containers and a presence across Europe, the Americas, and Asia, we provide flexible Storage-as-a-Service solutions across a wide range of industries. Despite our global scale, we have maintained the entrepreneurial spirit and culture that have defined us since day one. With the support of Kirk Kapital as co-owner, we continue to invest in technology, digitalisation, and sustainability to drive the next phase of our growth. If you are looking for a role where you can own a critical area, work closely with the business, and see your impact quickly, TITAN offers exactly that.

Title: Analytiker med flair for data og formidling til tværgående analyser indenfor klima, mobilitet og byudvikling i København
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Søg jobbet

Område

Politik og administration

Ansøgningsfrist

10.08.2026

Arbejdstid

Fuldtid

Jobtype

Fastansættelse

Søg jobbet

Vil du være med til at skabe viden, der præger udviklingen af København?

I Analyseenheden arbejder vi med analyser og evalueringer af komplekse problemstillinger inden for blandt andet klima, mobilitet, byudvikling, miljø og adfærd.

Som analytiker får du mulighed for at arbejde med hele analyseprocessen – fra afklaring af problemstillingen og valg af metode til dataanalyse, formidling og dialog med fagområderne. Opgaverne spænder fra mindre undersøgelser til større tværgående analyser og evalueringer, som bidrager til udviklingen og driften af København. Eksempler på analyser er evaluering af biodiversitetspuljen, vurdering af parkeringsordninger og analyse af socioøkonomiske karakteristika i almene bofællesskaber.

Om dig

Stillingen er målrettet en profil med erfaring med analyse af forskellige datakilder og med at omsætte data til anvendelig viden. Det kan fx være fagdata, registerdata, surveydata eller andre datakilder. Vi forestiller os, at du har mindst to års erfaring med analysearbejde.

Vi vil rigtigt gerne se, at du har


en relevant videregående uddannelse – fx sociologi, statskundskab, økonomi eller geografi
erfaring med dataanalyse gerne i Python – erfaring fra R eller andre statistikprogrammer og lyst til at lære nyt er også fint
stærke skriftlige formidlingsevner – du kan formidle resultater klart og præcist
evne til at omsætte komplekse problemstillinger til analysespørgsmål og udvikle analysedesigns, der giver et solidt grundlag for beslutninger.
interesse for at arbejde i en politisk styret organisation med høj samfundsmæssig relevans
evne til at samarbejde på tværs af fagområder


Det er et plus, men ikke et krav, at du har


erfaring med Danmarks Statistiks registre, herunder myndighedsordning/forskerservice
kendskab til Power BI eller lignende visualiseringsværktøjer


Kvalitative metoder indgår også i en del af vores analyser, og det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring med at arbejde med kvalitative metoder og kan bringe begge metoder i spil, når problemstillingen kræver det

Om Analyseenheden

Du bliver en del af et stærkt tværfagligt miljø med engagerede kollegaer med forskellige analytiske værktøjer i bagagen – overvejende samfundsvidenskabelige. I teamet arbejder vi oftest to eller flere sammen om de enkelte analyser. Vi er optaget af, hvordan AI og store sprogmodeller kan understøtte analysearbejdet, og vi afprøver løbende nye muligheder for at anvende teknologierne i praksis.

Analyseenheden har 19 medarbejdere og består af tre teams: Analyseteamet, Copenhagen Solutions Lab og Visionssekretariatet. Stillingen er i vores analyseteam som har ni medarbejdere.

Vi tilbyder


op til to hjemmearbejdsdage om ugen og flekstid
stor faglig frihed og indflydelse på egne opgaver
et tværfagligt miljø med engagerede kolleger og et godt socialt fællesskab
mulighed for at arbejde med en stor variation af analyser og evalueringer forvaltningens område
analyseopgaver med politisk relevans og synlig effekt på Københavns udvikling


Ansættelsesvilkår

Løn- og ansættelsesvilkår efter gældende overenskomst og inden for rammerne af lokal løndannelse (fuldmægtig, special- eller chefkonsulent afhængig af din erfaring).

Ansættelsesstedet er Njalsgade 13, 2300 København S

Arbejdstiden udgør 37 timer ugentligt.

Vi ønsker stillingen besat den 1. oktober 2026.

Mere information

Hvis du har spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte enhedschef Ole Vissing på 22 70 65 13 eller analytiker Stine Thougaard på 29 29 54 59.

Søg via linket senest mandag den 10. august 2026

Ansættelsessamtalerne vil blive afholdt i uge 34 og her vil du blive bedt om at vise relevante eksempler på analyser, du har medvirket til. Vi er både nysgerrige på at høre om proces og resultatpræsentation.

Om Klima-, Miljø- og Teknikforvaltningen

Klima-, Miljø- og Teknikforvaltningen varetager myndighedsopgaver og servicerer borgere og erhvervsliv blandt andet inden for miljøområdet, lokalplanlægning, byfornyelse, byggesagsbehandling og trafikområdet. Desuden er det Klima-, Miljø- og Teknikforvaltningen, der står for renholdelse, vedligeholdelse og udvikling af byens veje og parker samt foretager parkeringskontrol. Vi beskæftiger ca. 2.700 medarbejdere og administrerer et bruttobudget på knap 4,8 mia. kr.

Title: Lead Product Data Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Tech-first. Ego-free. User-obsessed. Over one million Danes use Skatteguiden to understand and optimise their taxes. We automate tax tracking, surface deductions people didn't know they had, and give users the insights they need to make better financial decisions — all directly connected to the Danish tax authority. We're an independent company of around 50 people based in Copenhagen, with a 4.7-star rating and a simple mission: make financial insight accessible to everyone, not just those who can afford an accountant.







The role




We need someone who owns product analytics — not just executes it. You'll set the direction for how Skatteguiden uses data to understand its users and make product decisions, and you'll be the person who raises the bar for what good analytics looks like here.




You'll work closely with product managers across our product teams and have real influence on what gets built and why. This isn't a support function. It's a lead role with ownership.







What you'll be working on




Own the product analytics function — define the strategy, set the standards, and drive adoption across product teams
Define and maintain core product metrics and KPIs across the organisation
Design and run experiments — from hypothesis to conclusion
Analyse user behaviour and funnels to identify growth opportunities and friction points
Build scalable dashboards and reporting in close collaboration with the data team
Translate findings into concrete recommendations for product leadership and senior stakeholders







What you bring




Strong SQL skills and experience with complex data modelling
Experience with Snowflake, dbt, Lightdash, Evidence or comparable tooling.
Solid product analytics experience — you've worked closely with product teams and understand what drives a digital product
Strong experimentation design and statistical analysis skills
A track record of owning a product analytics function, not just contributing to one
Ability to communicate complex findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Self-directed and structured — you can take an ambiguous question and turn it into a sharp analysis







Nice to have




Experience with Snowflake or a comparable cloud data warehouse
Experience with Lightdash, Tableau, or comparable BI tools
Python for data processing and analysis







What we offer







Ownership of the product analytics function at one of Denmark's fastest-growing fintech companies
Direct influence on product direction and a seat at the table where decisions are made
A strong data team with high professional standards and a modern tech stack
The opportunity to build something — not inherit a finished function
Office first in Copenhagen — five minutes from Rådhuspladsen, lunch at Claus Meyer, dinner covered if the day runs long







Practical




Start: As soon as possible

Reports to: Head of Data & Operations Planning

Salary: Competitive and based on experience







Our Lean Recruitment Process




Screening call with Christian Payne, 30 min (online)
45-minute onsite meeting with hiring manager and product manager
1-hour onsite case session with hiring manager and team member, followed by a short conversation with Nikolai (CEO), 30 min

Title: Stærk analysekonsulent til analyser og resultatopfølgning i Center for Beskæftigelse og Erhverv
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Søg jobbet

Område

Politik og administration

Ansøgningsfrist

21.06.2026

Arbejdstid

Fuldtid

Jobtype

Fastansættelse

Søg jobbet

Er du stærk til tal, og formår du at sætte dem ind i en strategisk kontekst? Er du god til at formidle klart? Og har du lyst til at være med, når data og analyser bliver en central del af den strategiske styring og udvikling? Så er du måske den, vi leder efter.

Vi søger en samfundsvidenskabelig profil, der kan kombinere dataforståelse med stærke formidlingsevner og kan tænke strategisk. Du leverer ikke bare data, men formår at udarbejde analyser, der understøtter udvikling, resultatskabelse og kvalificerer beslutninger. Du er hurtig til at skabe overblik og har øje for relevante data og effektmål. Du har forståelse for de strategiske linjer og formår at bringe disse ting i spil i din opgaveløsning.

Jobcenter København, Center for Beskæftigelse og Erhverv hjælper syge og ledige københavnere tæt på arbejdsmarkedet hurtigst muligt i job eller uddannelse og bidrager til at skaffe virksomhederne den arbejdskraft, de efterspørger.

Samtidig skal vi kunne agere hurtigt og fleksibelt, og vi hjælper hinanden – og vi forventer, at du bidrager til dette. Du skal være samarbejdsorienteret og kunne samarbejde med forskellige niveauer i organisationen.

Med beskæftigelsesreformen er det stærke fokus på resultatskabelse intensiveret. Der er brug for en klar prioritering af det, der virker. udarbejde analyser og opgørelser af vores resultater og indsatser
udarbejde og understøtte resultatopfølgning i centeret
udarbejde analyser og oplæg til ledelsen

udarbejde ad hoc-analyser og data om vores drift og resultater
udarbejde måltal og opfølgningskoncepter for vores indsatser

har grundlæggende forståelse og interesse for at arbejde med data og analyser – kendskab til jobindsats.dk er en fordel, men ikke et krav

Stillingen er på 37 timer om ugen med tiltrædelse den 1. august 2026 eller snarest derefter.

Løn- og ansættelsesvilkår er i henhold til relevant gældende overenskomst og inden for rammerne af lokal løndannelse.

Title: HR Analytics & Compensation Specialist
Place: Brøndby Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Brænder du for data, HR-teknologi og at omsætte tal til klare beslutningsgrundlag? Har du erfaring med dashboards, HR-masterdata og analyse – og lyst til at styrke Bravidas HR-setup i Danmark? Så er du måske vores nye HR Tech & Analytics Specialist i Brøndby.

I rollen får du ansvaret for at skabe et solidt datagrundlag for både HR’s drift og strategi. Du arbejder selvstændigt med analyser, dashboards og HR-nøgletal og bliver den, der skaber overblik, finder mønstre og leverer de indsigter, der understøtter beslutninger i HR og forretningen. Herudover arbejder du med at understøtte implementeringen af løngennemsigtighedsdirektivet, herunder pay bands, lønpolitik og andre centrale opgaver inden for lønstruktur, benchmarking og analyser af intern lønlighed.

Du vil bringe din viden om HR‑processer, og gerne også Compensation & Benefits, i spil, når du understøtter løbende forbedringer og nye initiativer. Stillingen er forankret i HR Operations Services, hvor du arbejder tæt med kollegaer på tværs af hele organisationen.

I rollen får du ansvar for at:


Sikre og vedligeholde HR-data i hele medarbejderrejsen – herunder datakvalitet, korrekt masterdata og overholdelse af HR-processer
Udvikle dashboards og datarapporter, der omsætter komplekse data til overskuelige og anvendelige visualiseringer
Udarbejde og vedligeholde HR-nøgletal, der giver et aktuelt og klart billede af HR’s drift.
Levere strategiske analyser og KPI-indsigter, der understøtter HR’s udvikling og ledelsens beslutninger.
Udvikle og implementere digitale initiativer, der effektiviserer HR-processer gennem automatisering, dataindsigt og muligheder med AI.
Videreudvikle og drifte HR-systemer, fungere som superbruger og bindeled til løn og leverandører samt sikre GDPR-overholdelse
Støtte det nordiske HR-tech projekt ved at konfigurere lokale løsninger og bidrage til implementeringsprocessen.


Om dig:

Vi forestiller os, at du har:


Erfaring med HR‑systemer, datahåndtering og anvendelse af digitale værktøjer.
Arbejde sikkert, struktureret og ansvarligt med data – herunder forståelse for GDPR og korrekt databehandling.
Har en relevant baggrund fra en tilsvarende rolle inden for HR, dataanalyse, IT eller lignende.
Er en proaktiv teamplayer, med god forretningsforståelse
Har en struktureret og kvalitetsbevidst tilgang med sans for detaljer.
Er analytisk stærk, og kan omsætte komplekse data til enkel og forståelig formidling.
Har erfaring med Compensation & Benefits, herunder løn, fordele og overenskomster, hvilket er en fordel.
Har kendskab til mobility-relaterede processer, som fx international mobilitet eller udstationering.
Du taler og skriver flydende dansk


Hvad kan vi tilbyde dig?

Du bliver en del af et ambitiøst HR-team i en spændende udvikling hos en af Nordens bedst etablerede leverandør af tekniske installations- og serviceløsninger. Vi er i en proces med at opbygge et HR-team, der kan levere den bedste support og service til vores mange afdelinger. Det betyder at du vil få stor indflydelse på vores fremtidige setup og succes.

Du får en bred kontaktflade, der spænder over mange forskellige afdelinger og samarbejdspartnere på tværs af landet såvel som Norden. Du vil opnå både personlig og faglig udvikling gennem de mange varierende opgaver. Samtidig tilbyder vi en arbejdsplads med gode arbejdsvilkår, løn efter kvalifikationer og god pensionsordning med sundhedsforsikring.

Hvis du allerede nu kan se dig selv i ovenstående, opfordre vi til at du uploader dit CV og en ansøgning på Bravidas hjemmeside snarest muligt, da vi vurderer ansøgninger og indkalder til samtaler løbende. Har du nogle spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Head of HR Operations Stine Villum på stine.villum@bravida.dk.

Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig.
