Title: Data Insights Specialist
Description:
Vil du arbejde i en rolle, hvor du både skal forstå teknikken og kunne omsætte den til løsninger, der skaber værdi for kunden?




Din rolle

Rollen er til dig, der ikke nødvendigvis skal bygge alt selv, men som har nok praktisk erfaring til at forstå muligheder, begrænsninger og de tekniske valg, der skal træffes. Du skal kunne være en troværdig sparringspartner for både kunder og kolleger og være med til at sætte retning for, hvordan data bruges bedst.




Du kommer til at arbejde med moderne dataløsninger og være med til at forme løsninger med afsæt i især Google Cloud, Azure og Microsoft Fabric. Rollen kombinerer teknisk forståelse, løsningsdesign og kundekontakt. Det betyder, at du både skal kunne bidrage til konkrete løsninger og samtidig have blik for det større billede, kundens behov, datagrundlag, arkitektur og retning.




Du bliver en vigtig sparringspartner for kunderne og en del af den dialog, hvor behov omsættes til praktiske og teknisk holdbare løsninger. Samtidig kommer du til at arbejde tæt sammen med kolleger, der bygger og videreudvikler de tekniske løsninger i praksis.




Du kommer blandt andet til at:

bidrage til design og videreudvikling af dataplatforme, dataflows og cloud-baseret datainfrastruktur
arbejde med løsninger inden for SQL, Python og data-pipelines
omsætte forretningsbehov til tekniske løsninger i samarbejde med kunder og kolleger
deltage i kundedialog og rådgive om datagrundlag, muligheder og næste skridt
være med til at sætte retning for kunders arbejde med data

Din profil

Vi leder efter dig, der:

har et par års erfaring fra en rolle som fx Data Consultant, Analytics Engineer, BI Consultant, Data Engineer eller lignende
har praktisk erfaring med SQL og Python og forstår, hvordan moderne dataløsninger bygges
har forståelse for cloud, datainfrastruktur og moderne dataplatforme
forstår principperne bag datamodellering og data warehouse-løsninger
trives i dialogen med kunder og kan gøre tekniske problemstillinger forståelige
har lyst til at arbejde i spændet mellem teknik, rådgivning og forretning




Det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring med Google Cloud, Azure, MS Fabric, Git eller data inden for marketing, web eller e-commerce.


Hvad du får

Hos Web2Media får du en rolle med både teknisk tyngde, kundekontakt og indflydelse. Du bliver en del af et fagligt stærkt miljø, hvor du er med til at forme løsninger, skabe retning og omsætte data til reel værdi for kunderne.




Vi vægter fællesskabet højt og gør en særlig indsats for at arrangere aktiviteter, der har fokus på trivsel, sundhed og arbejdsglæde. Herudover kan du se frem til:

Fleksible arbejdstider og mulighed for hjemmearbejdsdage
Fri fitness og rabatordninger
Frokostordning, god kaffe og lækker frugt
En fredagsbar samt årlige arrangementer
Sundhedsforsikring og pensionsordning
5 feriefridage efter 9 måneders anciennitet

Om Web2Media

Vi er et rådgivningshus med hovedkontor i hjertet af Aarhus og tæller pt. ca., 80 ildsjæle. Kulturen er åben og ligefrem, og vi vil altid hinanden, vores kunder og leverandører det bedste. Vi sætter vores ambitioner højt, men er samtidigt nede på jorden som mennesker og vægter ordentlighed, integritet og kvalitet.

Læs mere om vores virksomhedskultur her.

 

 

Praktisk

Lyder ovenstående som dig, så send din ansøgning og dit CV til os. Du giver med din ansøgning tilladelse til, at dine data evt. deles med ekstern samarbejdspartner inden for rekruttering. Data behandles i henhold til GDPR.

Arbejdssted: Aarhus C

Opstart: Snarest muligt

Ansøgning: Samtaler afholdes løbende

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Jonas Nørgaard Nielsen på mail: jnn@Web2Media.dk.

Title: ESG Controller
Description:
Are you a data-driven controller looking to apply your skills within sustainability and ESG? Food Folk, the company behind McDonald’s in the Nordics, is seeking a structured and analytical ESG Controller to strengthen their ESG reporting and performance management.




Drive ESG-Reporting

At Food Folk, sustainability is an integral part of the business agenda, and as ESG Controller, you will play a key role in building and managing the data foundation that supports their sustainability strategy, targets, and reporting. You will work closely with the Impact team and stakeholders across the organisation to ensure high-quality ESG data and enable informed decision-making.




This is a unique opportunity to take part in shaping and maturing ESG data processes in a dynamic, international organisation, while remaining closely connected to a broader finance environment.




Your Key Responsibilities

Establish and develop ESG data infrastructure to support reporting, disclosures, and performance tracking
Ensure collection, validation, consolidation, and maintenance of ESG data across systems
Support the development of new data flows, master data structures, and basic integrations
Analyse ESG data and apply standardized methodologies to support strategic planning and target setting
Prepare insights, dashboards, and reports for internal stakeholders
Facilitate ESG data collection across the organisation and relevant parts of the value chain, including support for CSRD compliance
Drive continuous improvement by optimising and automating ESG data processes
Collaborate with internal teams and external stakeholders to ensure accurate, transparent, and relevant ESG reporting
Support the broader finance function with ad hoc analytical and controlling tasks




What Makes This Role Exciting?

This role sits at the intersection of sustainability, data, and business performance, making it ideal for a controller profile motivated by working with ESG-related data. You will play a key role in transforming complex information into meaningful insights and concrete actions that support Food Folk’s sustainability agenda.




You will enter an area that continues to evolve, requiring you to create structure, ensure data quality, and build scalable processes. The role offers broad exposure across the organization and involves close collaboration with both internal and external stakeholders.




What We’re Looking For

Solid experience working with financial and/or non-financial data, ideally from a controlling or finance-oriented role
A strong data and performance mindset, with the ability to create structure in complex and evolving areas
Experience with data infrastructure, governance, data quality, and data transformation
Strong analytical skills and ability to work with complex datasets and translate them into actionable insights
Experience with Power BI or similar tools
A structured, detail-oriented, and proactive approach, with the ability to drive tasks independently
Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to collaborate across functions and levels
Ability to communicate complex topics in a clear and pragmatic way




Experience with ESG, sustainability reporting, CO₂ accounting, CSRD, SAP, or Product Lifecycle Management systems is considered an advantage, but not a requirement.




A relevant degree in finance, economics, business, or similar is preferred.




Why Join Food Folk?

Food Folk is the licensee for McDonald’s in the Nordics, operating more than 500 restaurants and serving over 600,000 guests every day. With strong growth ambitions and a clear sustainability agenda, Food Folk is committed to using its scale to drive positive impact across markets.




You will join an ambitious and collaborative organization with offices across the Nordics, working closely with colleagues from different functions and markets.




Interested?

The recruitment process is carried out in collaboration with the consulting company Compass Human Resources Group. If you have any questions regarding this position, please do not hesitate to contact Senior Research Consultant, Cathrine Tams at cat@compasshrg.com. As we review CVs on an ongoing basis, we encourage you to apply as soon as possible. All enquiries are handled with full confidentiality.




Deadline for applications: As soon as possible

Workplace: Food Folk HQ at Frederiksberg, Copenhagen

Reference: 2607.128

Company: Food Folk Danmark ApS

Title: Business Data Analyst
Place: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
Description:
We are super proud to be partnering with a leading tech consultancy who, are on the hunt for a Data Operations Business Analyst to join a team delivering a large-scale data platform that's used by data scientists, engineers, and business stakeholders alike.




If you're someone who loves getting hands-on with data, investigating issues, asking questions, and turning findings into clear actions, this could be a great fit.




What you'll be doing

✨ Investigating production data to identify issues, anomalies, and trends

✨ Using SQL and PySpark to explore data, validate assumptions, and uncover root causes ✨ Supporting data scientists and engineers with queries, investigations, and data related challenges

✨ Gathering requirements through stakeholder engagement and workshops

✨ Translating business needs into epics, stories, and deliverable outcomes

✨ Helping shape priorities while balancing user needs, security, and governance requirements

✨ Supporting and improving data governance across the platform




What we're looking for

✔ Experience working as a Business Analyst in a data focused environment

✔ Strong SQL skills and confidence working directly with production data

✔ Exposure to PySpark or large scale data platforms

✔ Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills

✔ Experience working within regulated or highly governed environments

✔ Someone who enjoys problem solving, digging into detail, and making sense of complex data challenges




Why join?




You'll be joining a collaborative team where data is at the heart of decision-making. You'll have the opportunity to work closely with technical specialists, influence platform improvements, and play a key role in helping people get the most value from their data.




💜 At G&T, we're passionate about building diverse teams and inclusive workplaces. If you're excited by the role but don't tick every box, we'd still love to hear from you.




Apply now or get in touch for a confidential chat✨

Title: Insights Analyst
Description:
Permanent
Up to £ 50,000 + Pension + PMI + ShareSave + 6.6 weeks holiday + Hybrid Working ( 1 day per week in the office)
Southampton, Store Support Office




We believe anyone can improve their home to make life better. From our Southampton Store Support office (SSO) we equip our stores, our people, and our whole business with everything it takes to help our millions of customers create a home they’ll love. Join us as a Data Insights Analyst and you’ll be a big part of this.

Behind every project, purchase and customer interaction ther is a wealth of data that helps us understand what our customers need, what inspires them, and how we can create even better experiences.

We’re looking for an Insights Analyst to join our growing Analytics & Data Science team at an exciting time. Data is playing an increasingly important role across our business, shaping decisions across customer, loyalty, supply chain & logistics. If you’re naturally curious, passionate about solving business problems, and love turning complex data into meaningful insight and stories, we’d love to hear from you.




What's the job?

Use data to uncover insights that drive better business decisions and improve customer outcomes
Translate complex datasets into clear, compelling stories and actionable recommendations
Partner with stakeholders across Customer, Digital, Supply Chain, Logistics and Commercial teams
Tackle a wide range of business problems, from customer behaviour and performance optimisation to operational efficiency
Deliver deep-dive analysis and identify opportunities for growth and improvement
Build strong relationships with stakeholders, influencing and supporting decision-making at all levels
Support experimentation and test-and-learn approaches, bringing creativity and new ideas to how we solve problems
Work collaboratively with analysts and data scientists to deliver impactful analytical solutions
Contribute to a culture of continuous improvement, innovation and data-driven thinking




What we need:

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience working with complex datasets
SQL skills, with experience in Python and/or R (desirable)
Experience working in cloud-based data environments (e.g. Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery)
The ability to translate data into clear insight and compelling storytelling
A passion for understanding customers and putting them at the heart of decision-making
Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build relationships across different areas of the business
Curiosity, creativity and a willingness to challenge the status quo
A proactive mindset — not afraid to take risks, test ideas and try new approaches
Attention to detail and a commitment to data quality




What's in it for me?

As well as a competitive salary, our benefits package includes:

Award-winning pension scheme
Bonus
ShareSave options
6.6 weeks holiday
Payroll giving
Employee Assistance Programme
Shopping discounts
Colleague wellbeing benefits
…and much more.




Equality and Inclusion

We welcome applications from all candidates; however, please note that we are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role, and applicants must have the right to work in the UK.




We want to ensure that all employees, future employees, and applicants to all Kingfisher companies are treated equally regardless of age, gender, marital or civil partnership status, colour, ethnic or national origin, culture, religious belief, philosophical belief, political opinion, disability, gender identity, gender expression, or sexual orientation.

Find out more about us, including the benefits we offer and our community, sustainability, and inclusion projects at bandqcareers.com.

  • Up to £ 50,000 + Pension + PMI + ShareSave + 6.6 weeks holiday + Hybrid Working ( 1 day per week in the office)
  • Southampton, Store Support Office


We believe anyone can improve their home to make life better. From our Southampton Store Support office (SSO) we equip our stores, our people, and our whole business with everything it takes to help our millions of customers create a home they’ll love. Join us as a Data Insights Analyst and you’ll be a big part of this.

Behind every project, purchase and customer interaction ther is a wealth of data that helps us understand what our customers need, what inspires them, and how we can create even better experiences.

We’re looking for an Insights Analyst to join our growing Analytics & Data Science team at an exciting time. Data is playing an increasingly important role across our business, shaping decisions across customer, loyalty, supply chain & logistics. If you’re naturally curious, passionate about solving business problems, and love turning complex data into meaningful insight and stories, we’d love to hear from you.


What's the job?

  • Use data to uncover insights that drive better business decisions and improve customer outcomes
  • Translate complex datasets into clear, compelling stories and actionable recommendations
  • Partner with stakeholders across Customer, Digital, Supply Chain, Logistics and Commercial teams
  • Tackle a wide range of business problems, from customer behaviour and performance optimisation to operational efficiency
  • Deliver deep-dive analysis and identify opportunities for growth and improvement
  • Build strong relationships with stakeholders, influencing and supporting decision-making at all levels
  • Support experimentation and test-and-learn approaches, bringing creativity and new ideas to how we solve problems
  • Work collaboratively with analysts and data scientists to deliver impactful analytical solutions
  • Contribute to a culture of continuous improvement, innovation and data-driven thinking


What we need:

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with experience working with complex datasets
  • SQL skills, with experience in Python and/or R (desirable)
  • Experience working in cloud-based data environments (e.g. Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery)
  • The ability to translate data into clear insight and compelling storytelling
  • A passion for understanding customers and putting them at the heart of decision-making
  • Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to build relationships across different areas of the business
  • Curiosity, creativity and a willingness to challenge the status quo
  • A proactive mindset — not afraid to take risks, test ideas and try new approaches
  • Attention to detail and a commitment to data quality


What's in it for me?

As well as a competitive salary, our benefits package includes:

  • Award-winning pension scheme
  • Bonus
  • ShareSave options
  • 6.6 weeks holiday
  • Payroll giving
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Shopping discounts
  • Colleague wellbeing benefits
  • …and much more.


Equality and Inclusion

We welcome applications from all candidates; however, please note that we are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role, and applicants must have the right to work in the UK.


We want to ensure that all employees, future employees, and applicants to all Kingfisher companies are treated equally regardless of age, gender, marital or civil partnership status, colour, ethnic or national origin, culture, religious belief, philosophical belief, political opinion, disability, gender identity, gender expression, or sexual orientation.

Find out more about us, including the benefits we offer and our community, sustainability, and inclusion projects at bandqcareers.com.

  • ssorecruitment@b-and-q.co.uk
  • recruitment@b-and-q.co.uk

Title: Business Analyst
Place: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Location: Newcastle (5 days onsite or willingness to relocate)




About the Opportunity

If you have 2+ years of experience as a Business Analyst and are looking to take the next step in your career, this could be a great opportunity for you. As a Business Analyst, you’ll join a collaborative team where you’ll continue to develop your expertise through meaningful project work, ongoing mentoring, and structured support from an experienced manager.




This is a fantastic opportunity for someone analytical and curious who is looking to deepen their experience in business analysis within a supportive, growth-focused environment. We’re committed to helping you further build your skills so you can progress into more senior roles across analysis, product, or delivery




Key Responsibilities

Design and implement scalable, efficient data architectures aligned with business and application requirements
Define data models, schemas, storage solutions, and integration frameworks
Collaborate with engineering, product, and analytics teams to ensure data integrity, availability, and accessibility
Translate business requirements into logical and physical data models
Develop and maintain comprehensive documentation for data structures, pipelines, and processes
Identify, troubleshoot, and resolve data-related performance and quality issues
Support and guide team members on best practices in data architecture and engineering
Participate in architectural discussions and contribute to strategic decision-making




Required Skills & Experience

2–3 years of experience in data architecture, data engineering, or a related field
Strong proficiency in Python for data processing and automation
Advanced knowledge of SQL for data querying and transformation
Solid understanding of data modeling concepts (e.g., relational, dimensional models)
Experience with data storage technologies (e.g., data warehouses, lakes, cloud platforms)
Familiarity with data integration tools and ETL/ELT processes
Understanding of AI & Data Processes and data-driven system design




Preferred Skills

Exposure to AI & Data Strategy and modern data platforms
Knowledge of data governance, security, and access frameworks
Experience working in cloud environments (e.g., Azure, AWS, or GCP)
Familiarity with big data tools (e.g., Spark, Databricks)




Key Competencies

Problem-solving mindset with strong analytical skills
Ability to work collaboratively across multiple teams
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
Proactive approach to identifying and improving data processes




What You’ll Bring

A passion for building robust and scalable data systems
Ability to balance technical depth with business needs
A collaborative attitude and willingness to contribute to team success




Why Join Us?

Dedicated support: Ongoing mentoring and guidance from experienced professionals
Upskilling opportunities: Training and development to further strengthen your skillset
Career growth: Work on impactful projects and progress into more senior roles
Collaborative culture: A team environment that values knowledge sharing and continuous improvement
Competitive salary and benefits package




Apply Today!

If you’re ready to take the next step in your career and grow with the support of nology and a dedicated team, submit your application now. We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Your new company




You will be joining a well-established and growing organisation that places technology and data at the centre of its operational strategy. The business has a strong reputation for innovation and continuous improvement, offering a collaborative environment where systems and process optimisation are actively encouraged.




Your new role




We are currently recruiting for a Data Analyst to support the IT Manager and wider business prepare for a large data migration project and to support the wider business to better utilise customer data to enable and support strategic business objectives. As the Data Analyst, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the stability, performance and ongoing development of key business systems.




You will work closely with stakeholders across the business to deliver meaningful data insights. This will involve developing reports and dashboards using tools such as SQL, Excel, Power BI and Tableau, while also supporting data quality and governance standards.

The business is currently in the very early stages of migrating to Salesforce. This role will play a critical part in that project. Longer term, you will take ownership of driving process automation to improve operational efficiency and support business users to make data more accessible and meaningful. You will design and implement workflows, streamline manual processes and take ownership of SharePoint administration, including site structure and permissions. You will also play an active role in troubleshooting and maintaining workflow solutions.




What you'll need to succeed




To be successful in this role, you will need advanced Excel skills (Power Query, pivot tables and complex formulas), strong SQL skills and great communication - bridging the gap between IT/ Data and the wider business. Experience with Power BI and Tableau is advantageous but not essential. You will have a proven ability to analyse data and produce clear, insightful reporting.




What you'll get in return




Salary up to £50,000, 1 day a week working from (possibly 2 days from home), health cash back plan, Life Insurance (2x salary), free food and catered lunches on-site, free on-site parking with electrical charge points. Full benefits package to be confirmed shortly.

In return, you will have the opportunity to work across a broad technology landscape in a role that offers real variety and impact. You will be part of a supportive and forward-thinking team, with opportunities to develop your technical skills and contribute to meaningful business improvements.




What you need to do now... If you are interested in this role, click 'apply now' to forward an up-to-date copy of your CV.

Title: Analytics Engineer (Mid level)
Description:
Department: Engineering

Location: Edinburgh

Compensation: £37,500 - £48,000 / year



Description

Our Analytics Team
The analytics engineering team’s mission is to empower and enable the business to make rigorous and impactful use of data. You can read more about our work on our engineering blog. After successfully hiring recently, we're now looking for an analytics engineer to come in at mid level.

The role
Analytics engineers develop and build on our data platform to ensure we collect and make available the data the business needs, while maintaining accuracy and security. They engage with our colleagues to ensure they have the knowledge and skills to work with data effectively, provide support with questions, and carry out detailed analysis applying specialist knowledge when required.

We believe in a learning culture and encourage self-development. Our regular company hack days and wiggle weeks provide a great opportunity for analysts to pursue their own ideas and learn new technologies.


Day to day your role will involve
Ownership of data pipelines, data warehouse and BI tooling
Sourcing, modelling and making available data required to underpin the company strategy and day to day activities
Maintaining the accuracy and security of our data sources
Managing stakeholder relationships and priorities in your area of focus
Promoting pragmatic and effective use of data to drive decision-making
Training stakeholders across the organisation to improve their use of data
Supporting stakeholders by carrying out analysis requiring specialist knowledge
Reducing ambiguity or reconciling disagreement between teams
Encouraging a culture of experimentation and scientific rigour in the organisation
Developing and mentoring other members of the team



You 🙌

Our data platform is AWS based and we use modern tooling based around Dagster, dbt, dlt, Redshift and Looker. Prior experience with these tools is beneficial. However if you have experience with equivalent tools we’d still love to hear from you. Recruitment is a great chance for us to bring alternate perspectives into the team!

Ideally you'll have the following skills and experience:

Proficiency with a modern data platform stack
You have designed and implemented pipelines to process structured and unstructured data to enable stakeholders to solve business problems
You can demonstrate proficiency with modern data platform languages and tools such as Python, S3, Redshift, Lambda, Dagster, dbt and dlt
Business intelligence
You are proficient in SQL for data modelling and transformation
You have developed data models to enable stakeholders to self-serve business questions by using BI software such as Looker
Supporting Decision making
You have created appropriately designed reports and visualisations to support non-technical audiences
You have experience designing, running and analysing the results of A/B tests together with business stakeholders
Stakeholder management
You have a demonstrable ability to manage your own work backlog in collaboration with leadership and business stakeholders in a commercial setting
You are comfortable working with the team to answer questions from stakeholders
Please also make sure you're familiar with the hiring process for this role so you can ensure your application contains all the information we need.

Our work life 👩🏽‍💻

We believe in a strong work-life balance, we don’t work long hours (35 hour working week), and we have a fabulous Edinburgh HQ expertly designed for collaboration including high-quality A/V equipment to provide a great experience for distributed teams.

At FreeAgent, we support each other and operate a no-blame culture. Our mindset is: work hard, be nice to people, and the rest will sort itself out!

Work pattern for this role

This is a permanent, full time role (Monday to Friday, 9am to 5pm)

This role will be based in our Edinburgh office, however there will be an opportunity to adopt hybrid working, under an agreed pattern of 2 days from the office and 3 days at home each week.

One last thing, FreeAgent is moving house! 🏡
Next year we’ll be moving to our swanky new pad to the west of Edinburgh, with superb employee facilities and close to multiple transport links. We’ll share more details with you as you go through the hiring process.

Perks 🎁

Mid level annual starting salary of £37,500 - £48,000 per annum depending on experience, with room to progress to £55,500 within the band.

Our starting salaries typically leave some opportunity for growth within the band as salaries are linked to performance expectations to nurture our culture of fairness, learning and development.

We pride ourselves on creating a fantastic place to work, with benefits and perks including (but not limited to!) the following:
33 days holiday (and an extra day for each year you're with us)
Flexible/hybrid working
Private health care
Life assurance
5% matched pension contributions
Summer 4 day weeks (giving you 8 additional days off!)
Buy-as-you-earn share schemes
Sabbatical leave (unpaid)
Cycle to work scheme
Wellness webinars
Focused learning time and learning budgets
Volunteering day
Find out more about our benefits package here!

Tempted to apply? Read about the hiring process for this role so you know what to expect.

FreeAgent is an equal opportunities employer, and we invest in our people and culture to ensure we have a happy, diverse and high-performing team. We strongly encourage applications from people with varied backgrounds and perspectives. All applicants are considered for employment without attention to ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, family or parental status.

We hope the detail in our job advertisements is informative but not limiting. Research shows, and we’ve observed, that underrepresented groups are less likely to apply for a role without meeting all of the criteria listed. If this feels relatable we’d encourage you to apply, even if you think there may be areas for development.

Title: Open-Source Data Analyst
Description:
About The Job

Job summary

Discover what it's like to work in a compliance role that makes an impact. Could you help us shape a stronger, fairer future? Your next career move starts here.

These exciting posts are within the Open Source Data Analytics team, part of Risk Insight & Analytics within the Risk and Intelligence Service.

The Open Source Data Analytics team uses a bespoke cloud based private network and a variety of tools that enables the team to access all areas of the web, identify relevant data and analyse it.

Our Data Analysts use techniques from a range of analytical disciplines to combine, understand and visualise web derived data within a business context. We provide robust Open Source data analysis in a suite of products tailored to business needs and that can be used to inform business decisions and policy. Our analysis enhances the way the department understands, identifies and tackles risk to revenue.

Our ability to safely and legally search the internet to capture, analyse and exploit Open Source data from all areas of the web places us in a unique position to offer a different perspective and insight on issues that impact on tax compliant behaviour now and in the future.

Job Description

RIS collects, integrates and analyses one of the largest and most diverse range of data and intelligence in the world. This enables HMRC to understand and manage risks associated with non-compliance with the UK tax system.

The Team Has Two Core Functions

Horizon Scanning: Identification of new and emerging trends in web technologies, e-commerce and digital developments that impact on the department's strategic objectives.

Data Insight: Complex data collection, interpretation and analysis of compliance populations and/or specific elements of serious non-compliant behaviour to inform tactical and operational decision making. Analysis of social media platforms, their use and growth, the sentiment and language used across them and the compliance behaviour exhibited in reaction to changes in HMRC's approach to compliance and/or legislative change.

We work with our stakeholders and customers to understand their requirements, develop and deliver bespoke solutions and suggest new ideas to support them.

The team employs analytical skills to design and deliver products that inform a diverse customer base within HMRC.

Person specification

You Will

Have experience conducting research to explore complex questions, assess available data sources, and evaluate their suitability for analysis.

Be confident in learning new tools to collect and analyse open source data, including unstructured and large datasets from public, government, and commercial sources.

Be experienced in using analytical tools (for example, Excel, Power BI, R, Python or similar) to clean, manipulate, and interpret data, drawing out meaningful insights.

Be comfortable managing stakeholder relationships: understanding their needs, providing updates, and navigating competing priorities.

Be able to work individually and as part of an agile team to progress projects at pace and deliver actionable insights.

Essential Criteria

To be considered for this role, your application must demonstrate all of the following :


Experience in technical analytical roles including data analysis or intelligence work.
Communicating analytical findings clearly through written reports, visualisations, and storytelling techniques tailored to non-technical audiences.


Behaviours

We'll assess you against these behaviours during the selection process:


Making Effective Decisions


Technical skills

We'll assess you against these technical skills during the selection process:


Technical Assessment - Data Analysis


Benefits

Alongside your salary of £37,682, HM Revenue and Customs contributes £10,916 towards you being a member of the Civil Service Defined Benefit Pension scheme. Find out what benefits a Civil Service Pension provides (opens in a new window).

HMRC operates both Flexible and Hybrid Working policies, allowing you to balance your work and personal commitments. We welcome applications from those who need to work a more flexible arrangement and will agree to requests where possible, considering our operational and customer service needs.

We offer a generous leave allowance, starting at 25 days and increasing by a day for every year of qualifying service up to a maximum of 30 days.


Pension - We make contributions to our colleagues' Alpha pension equal to at least 28.97% of their salary.
Family friendly policies.
Personal support.
Coaching and development.


To find out more about HMRC benefits and find out what it's really like to work for HMRC hear from our insiders or visit Thinking of joining the Civil Service .

Things you need to know

Artificial intelligence

Artificial intelligence can be a useful tool to support your application, however, all examples and statements provided must be truthful, factually accurate and taken directly from your own experience. Where plagiarism has been identified (presenting the ideas and experiences of others, or generated by artificial intelligence, as your own) applications may be withdrawn and internal candidates may be subject to disciplinary action. Please see our candidate guidance (opens in a new window) for more information on appropriate and inappropriate use.

Selection process details

This vacancy is using Success Profiles (opens in a new window) , and will assess your Behaviours, Strengths, Ability, Experience and Technical skills.

How To Apply

Stage 1 - Online tests

After submission of the first stage of your application, you will be invited to complete two online tests,the Civil Service Numerical Test and the Civil Service Verbal Test. You may wish to have paper, pens and calculator ready for completing them. You will receive instructions on how to complete the tests when you are invited. If you successfully pass the test, you will be invited to complete the final stage of the application.

Please complete the online tests as soon as possible (within 24-48 hours is recommended), the closing date for the tests is 23:55pm on 25 June 2026.

Stage 2 - Full Application

As part of the application process, you will be asked to provide the following:


A name-blind CV including details of your Job History, up to your last 5 roles, giving a brief summary of what you delivered and any key achievements in each role (Maximum of 100 words for each role).
A 750-word personal statement. Your Personal Statement should be used to describe how your skills and experience would be suitable for the advertised role, making reference to the Essential Criteria and Person Specification outlined in the advert.
A 250-word statement demonstrating how you meet the Behaviour Making Effective Decisions.


Further details around what this will entail are listed on the application form.

Sift

In the event of a large number of applications being received, an initial sift may be held on the Behaviour statement Making Effective Decisions.

At full sift your Behaviour statement, CV and Personal Statement will be assessed, with the successful candidates being invited to interview.

We may also raise the score required at any stage of the process if we receive a high number of applications.

Interview

Stage 1 - Virtual Interview

During the panel interview, your experience will be assessed and you will be asked strength-based questions.

This Is An Example Of a Strengths-based Question

"It is often said that the customer's needs should come first. To what extent do you agree or disagree with this statement?"

There is no expectation or requirement for you to prepare for the strengths-based questions in advance of the interview, though you may find it helpful to spend some time reflecting on what you enjoy doing and what you do well.

Interviews will take place via video link and last approximately 60 minutes.

Applicants who are successful at Stage 1 of the interview process will progress to a face-to-face interview to access their technical abilities.

About The Job

Job summary

Discover what it's like to work in a compliance role that makes an impact. Could you help us shape a stronger, fairer future? Your next career move starts here.

These exciting posts are within the Open Source Data Analytics team, part of Risk Insight & Analytics within the Risk and Intelligence Service.

The Open Source Data Analytics team uses a bespoke cloud based private network and a variety of tools that enables the team to access all areas of the web, identify relevant data and analyse it.

Our Data Analysts use techniques from a range of analytical disciplines to combine, understand and visualise web derived data within a business context. We provide robust Open Source data analysis in a suite of products tailored to business needs and that can be used to inform business decisions and policy. Our analysis enhances the way the department understands, identifies and tackles risk to revenue.

Our ability to safely and legally search the internet to capture, analyse and exploit Open Source data from all areas of the web places us in a unique position to offer a different perspective and insight on issues that impact on tax compliant behaviour now and in the future.

Job Description

RIS collects, integrates and analyses one of the largest and most diverse range of data and intelligence in the world. This enables HMRC to understand and manage risks associated with non-compliance with the UK tax system.

The Team Has Two Core Functions

Horizon Scanning: Identification of new and emerging trends in web technologies, e-commerce and digital developments that impact on the department's strategic objectives.

Data Insight: Complex data collection, interpretation and analysis of compliance populations and/or specific elements of serious non-compliant behaviour to inform tactical and operational decision making. Analysis of social media platforms, their use and growth, the sentiment and language used across them and the compliance behaviour exhibited in reaction to changes in HMRC's approach to compliance and/or legislative change.

We work with our stakeholders and customers to understand their requirements, develop and deliver bespoke solutions and suggest new ideas to support them.

The team employs analytical skills to design and deliver products that inform a diverse customer base within HMRC.

Person specification

You Will

Have experience conducting research to explore complex questions, assess available data sources, and evaluate their suitability for analysis.

Be confident in learning new tools to collect and analyse open source data, including unstructured and large datasets from public, government, and commercial sources.

Be experienced in using analytical tools (for example, Excel, Power BI, R, Python or similar) to clean, manipulate, and interpret data, drawing out meaningful insights.

Be comfortable managing stakeholder relationships: understanding their needs, providing updates, and navigating competing priorities.

Be able to work individually and as part of an agile team to progress projects at pace and deliver actionable insights.

Essential Criteria

To be considered for this role, your application must demonstrate all of the following :

  • Experience in technical analytical roles including data analysis or intelligence work.
  • Communicating analytical findings clearly through written reports, visualisations, and storytelling techniques tailored to non-technical audiences.

Behaviours

We'll assess you against these behaviours during the selection process:

  • Making Effective Decisions

Technical skills

We'll assess you against these technical skills during the selection process:

  • Technical Assessment - Data Analysis

Benefits

Alongside your salary of £37,682, HM Revenue and Customs contributes £10,916 towards you being a member of the Civil Service Defined Benefit Pension scheme. Find out what benefits a Civil Service Pension provides (opens in a new window).

HMRC operates both Flexible and Hybrid Working policies, allowing you to balance your work and personal commitments. We welcome applications from those who need to work a more flexible arrangement and will agree to requests where possible, considering our operational and customer service needs.

We offer a generous leave allowance, starting at 25 days and increasing by a day for every year of qualifying service up to a maximum of 30 days.

  • Pension - We make contributions to our colleagues' Alpha pension equal to at least 28.97% of their salary.
  • Family friendly policies.
  • Personal support.
  • Coaching and development.

To find out more about HMRC benefits and find out what it's really like to work for HMRC hear from our insiders or visit Thinking of joining the Civil Service .

Things you need to know

Artificial intelligence

Artificial intelligence can be a useful tool to support your application, however, all examples and statements provided must be truthful, factually accurate and taken directly from your own experience. Where plagiarism has been identified (presenting the ideas and experiences of others, or generated by artificial intelligence, as your own) applications may be withdrawn and internal candidates may be subject to disciplinary action. Please see our candidate guidance (opens in a new window) for more information on appropriate and inappropriate use.

Selection process details

This vacancy is using Success Profiles (opens in a new window) , and will assess your Behaviours, Strengths, Ability, Experience and Technical skills.

How To Apply

 Stage 1 - Online tests

After submission of the first stage of your application, you will be invited to complete two online tests,the Civil Service Numerical Test and the Civil Service Verbal Test. You may wish to have paper, pens and calculator ready for completing them. You will receive instructions on how to complete the tests when you are invited. If you successfully pass the test, you will be invited to complete the final stage of the application.

Please complete the online tests as soon as possible (within 24-48 hours is recommended), the closing date for the tests is 23:55pm on 25 June 2026.

Stage 2 - Full Application

As part of the application process, you will be asked to provide the following:

  • A name-blind CV including details of your Job History, up to your last 5 roles, giving a brief summary of what you delivered and any key achievements in each role (Maximum of 100 words for each role).
  • A 750-word personal statement. Your Personal Statement should be used to describe how your skills and experience would be suitable for the advertised role, making reference to the Essential Criteria and Person Specification outlined in the advert.
  • A 250-word statement demonstrating how you meet the Behaviour Making Effective Decisions.

Further details around what this will entail are listed on the application form.

Sift

In the event of a large number of applications being received, an initial sift may be held on the Behaviour statement Making Effective Decisions.

At full sift your Behaviour statement, CV and Personal Statement will be assessed, with the successful candidates being invited to interview.

We may also raise the score required at any stage of the process if we receive a high number of applications.

During the panel interview, your experience will be assessed and you will be asked strength-based questions.

This Is An Example Of a Strengths-based Question

"It is often said that the customer's needs should come first. To what extent do you agree or disagree with this statement?"

There is no expectation or requirement for you to prepare for the strengths-based questions in advance of the interview, though you may find it helpful to spend some time reflecting on what you enjoy doing and what you do well.

Title: Customer Data Insights Analyst
Description:
Overview

Permanent

Up to £ 50,000 + Pension + BUPA + ShareSave + 6.6 weeks holiday 

Southampton/ Paddington Office ( few days per month )

We believe anyone can improve their home to make life better. From our Southampton Store Support office (SSO) we equip our stores, our people, and our whole business with everything it takes to help our millions of customers create a home they’ll love. Join us as a Customer Data Analyst and you’ll be a big part of this.

Working within the Analytics and Data Science team, you’ll turn data into powerful insights that make a real impact. You’ll collaborate with a wide range of stakeholders to deepen our understanding of customers, helping to shape future strategy, enhance the customer experience, drive engagement and revenue, and improve business efficiency across B&Q and TradePoint.

What's the job?


Help deliver the information provision for “The health of the customer”, including looking at contributions to growth, cohort analysis, churn rates.
Working within a matrix structure with other analysts, delivering a wide range of analytical products and projects, focusing on customer behaviour.
Deliver PIRs related to the B&Q Loyalty schemes (Club & TradePoint) and CRM activities: identifying opportunities and making informed recommendations.
Create forecasting and measure campaign performance across Marketing channels; true incrementality, ROI and contribution to owned exclusive brand mix.
Support on deep-dives and incremental analysis into performance to generate actionable insights.
Be an active member of the wider data community across the group, engaging with the group data team and other banners across the group.
Bring external thinking to B&Q by maintaining an awareness of analytics usage in retail and other sectors and by keeping abreast of competitor technology and developments.
Support the growth of new channels, Services, Digital, MarketPlace and Retail Media with key analysis and information.
Alongside the Analytics Manager, help build relationships with key stakeholders from across the business and translate their ambitions and goals into analytical challenges.


What We Need


Strong SQL skills, with experience in Python and/or R
Experience working in Agile, matrix environments, collaborating across teams and adapting to change
Hands-on experience with cloud data platforms (e.g. Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery) to analyse large, complex datasets
Proven experience in marketing campaign design and analysis, using customer data to optimise journeys, retention, and loyalty
Solid grounding in statistical analysis (e.g. hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, p-values)
High attention to detail, with a focus on data accuracy, validation, and quality
Strong understanding of data governance and data quality best practices
Interest in customer behavioural analysis and omnichannel personalisation
Experience using Jira and Confluence for workload management and documentation


What's in it for me?

As well as a competitive salary, our benefits package includes:


Award-winning pension scheme
Bonus
ShareSave options
6.6 weeks holiday
Payroll giving
Employee Assistance Programme
Shopping discounts
Colleague wellbeing benefits …and much more.


We welcome applications from all candidates; however, please note that we are unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role, and applicants must have the right to work in the UK.

Equality and Inclusion

We want to ensure that all employees, future employees, and applicants to all Kingfisher companies are treated equally regardless of age, gender, marital or civil partnership status, colour, ethnic or national origin, culture, religious belief, philosophical belief, political opinion, disability, gender identity, gender expression, or sexual orientation.

Find out more about us, including the benefits we offer and our community, sustainability, and inclusion projects at bandqcareers.com.

If you require any additional support or adjustments to help you make an application, please contact us at:


ssorecruitment@b-and-q.co.uk
recruitment@b-and-q.co.uk

  • Bring external thinking to B&Q by maintaining an awareness of analytics usage in retail and other sectors and by keeping abreast of competitor technology and developments.
  • Support the growth of new channels, Services, Digital, MarketPlace and Retail Media with key analysis and information.
  • Alongside the Analytics Manager, help build relationships with key stakeholders from across the business and translate their ambitions and goals into analytical challenges.

What We Need

  • Strong SQL skills, with experience in Python and/or R
  • Experience working in Agile, matrix environments, collaborating across teams and adapting to change
  • Hands-on experience with cloud data platforms (e.g. Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery) to analyse large, complex datasets
  • Proven experience in marketing campaign design and analysis, using customer data to optimise journeys, retention, and loyalty
  • Solid grounding in statistical analysis (e.g. hypothesis testing, confidence intervals, p-values)
  • High attention to detail, with a focus on data accuracy, validation, and quality
  • Strong understanding of data governance and data quality best practices
  • Interest in customer behavioural analysis and omnichannel personalisation
  • Experience using Jira and Confluence for workload management and documentation

What's in it for me?

As well as a competitive salary, our benefits package includes:

  • Award-winning pension scheme
  • Bonus
  • ShareSave options
  • 6.6 weeks holiday
  • Payroll giving
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Shopping discounts
  • Colleague wellbeing benefits …and much more.

Title: Data Visualisation and Automation Analyst
Description:
Permanent
Up to £50,000 + Pension + PMI + ShareSave + 6.6 weeks holiday + Hybrid Working (1 day per week in the office)
Southampton, Store Support Office




We believe anyone can improve their home to make life better. From our Southampton Store Support office (SSO) we equip our stores, our people, and our whole business with everything it takes to help our millions of customers create a home they’ll love. Join us as a Data Visualisation and Automation Analyst and you’ll be a big part of this.

Working within the Data Visualisation and Automation Team, the Data Visualisation & Automation Analyst will work to produce high quality self-serve dashboards as well as our foundational data enablement products. They will interact with a varied group of stakeholders to drive up the understanding of customers across the organisation, helping to shape our future strategies, improve customer experience, deepen engagement, drive revenue, and improve business efficiencies across both B&Q and TradePoint.




What's the job?

Develop our suite of self-serve dashboards, helping the business to get closer to our customers, informing business strategy and decision making.
Develop our customer data ETL pipelines and data foundation for B&Q, working closely alongside and in collaboration with Group IT when applicable.
Curate and put in place the controls for our customer data lake, democratising our data in a controlled manner, ensuring our analysts and data scientists have access to reliable and consistent data for analysis.
Provide input to initiatives to help define the right data flow architecture that deliver tangible results quickly.
Be an active member of the wider data community across the group, engaging with the group data team and other banners across the group.
Support the growth of new channels, Services, Digital, MarketPlace and Retail Media with key data sets and self-serve reporting.
Build relationships with key stakeholders from across the business and translate their ambitions and goals into analytical challenges.
Bring external thinking to B&Q by maintaining an awareness of analytics usage in retail and other sectors and by keeping abreast of competitor technology and developments.




What we need:

You have experience in creating and delivering actionable self-serve solutions, using modern BI tools such: PowerBI, Tableau, Looker etc.
You’re proficient in SQL and have some experience using other languages such as Python or R.
You’ve had experience using cloud based analytical platforms such as Databricks, Snowflake, Google BigQuery and the like.
You’ve built and managed data workflows before using tools like Airflow and Databricks.
You are fully aware of the importance of Data Governance and good Data Quality and what best practice looks like.
You’ve used Jira/Confluence before for managing workloads and documentation.
You’re curious: about how things work and getting to the bottom of business problems and data nuances.
You understand and maximise the value of analysis and self-serve data products, and act with a commercial lens, prioritising the actions and usefulness of outputs.
You’re a skilled storyteller who can bring that value to others – you know numbers don’t always speak for themselves and have excellent visuals to support.




What's in it for me?

As well as a competitive salary, our benefits package includes:

Award-winning pension scheme
Bonus
ShareSave options
6.6 weeks holiday
Payroll giving
Employee Assistance Programme
Shopping discounts
Colleague wellbeing benefits
…and much more.




Equality and Inclusion

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At Frasers Group we’re rethinking retail. Through digital innovation and unique store experiences, we’re serving our consumers with the world’s best sports, premium and luxury brands globally. As a leader in the industry, we’re elevating the retail experience for our consumers through our collection of established brands, including Sports Direct, FLANNELS, USC, Frasers, and GAME.



Why join us?



Our vision – we are building the worlds most admired and compelling brand ecosystem



Our purpose – we are elevating the lives of the many with access to the world’s best brands and experiences



At Frasers Group, we fear less and do more. Our people are forward thinkers who are driven to operate outside of their comfort zone to change the future of retail, embracing challenges along the way. The potential to elevate your career is massive, the experience unrivalled. To be able to make the most of it you need to live and breathe our principles:



Think without limits – Think, think fast, think fearlessly
Own it and back yourself - Own the basics, own your role and own the results
Be relevant - Relevant to our people, our partners and the planet

Are you ready to join the Fearless?





Job Description



This is a Data Analyst role where you will be working as part of the analytics team within the Estates function, focusing on developing, analysing & creating tools/models/reports used by the business. You must have an analytical mindset, strong communication skills & be able to adapt to fast paced, ever changing business environments.



Role Description:



Serve as the key analytical point of contact for one of the fastest-growing areas of the business, leading forecasting and delivering analysis to support continued growth.
Liaise with c-suite and senior leadership, fulfilling data and report requests and developing strategies.
Create and implement AI and automation projects, primarily in Airtable.
Leverage VBA to automate manual processes.
Assist in the maintenance and development of Power BI reports.
Produce ad-hoc reports and perform detailed analysis of property data using Excel.
Write and optimise database queries.
Identify trends and provide strategic recommendations.
Collaborate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders across the business.


Qualifications



Essential Skills:



Advanced Excel skills, including VBA and Power Query.
Experience in developing Power BI reports, including writing and optimising DAX measures.
Proficient in SQL, and experienced working with relational databases.
Understanding of financial concepts such as EBITDA, or an interest in developing financial knowledge is advantageous.
Familiarity with Airtable is advantageous.
Experience with programming languages such as Python or R is advantageous.
Background in commercial property, utilities or retail analytics is advantageous.
Able to hold your own in meetings and confident talking to senior stakeholders
Able to manage and prioritise workload.
Strong problem-solving ability & a commercial mindset
Able to communicate with a wide range of stakeholders with various levels of technical understanding.
Proactive, independent, self-starter who is ready to jump in and get things done


Additional Information



Along with your benefits package we also offer a wide range of perks for our colleagues:



Frasers Champion- Our employees are at the heart of our business and we ensure individuals are recognised every single month for their hard work. Frasers Champion is a peer nominated scheme where 8 winners will receive double their pay for a month where they have lived the Frasers Group values.



Retail Reconnect – In order to build the planets most admired and compelling brand ecosystem, all employees must understand our business, product and customers. Each financial year, head office employees will gain insights by spending one to two days in one of our stores or the warehouse. The goal is to learn how the work you do impacts our teams on the frontline, and to bring ideas back to the office which will improve how we work.



Fearless 1200 – Fearless 1200 is our way of recognising our growth. It’s bold, ambitious, and designed to reward colleagues across the business for living our values and delivering impact. Fearless 1200 links how we perform to how we're rewarded – check out the video link to find out more - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O0qmvJofMew



Employee Welfare



Frasers Fit – Our Everlast Gyms Team are on a mission to make our workforce the best, and fittest on the planet! We run free gym classes for employees as well as discounted memberships to our clubs. Frasers Fit is our wellbeing programme which aims to support and improve colleagues Physical, Financial & Mental wellbeing. The app is accessible for every employee and includes training, nutrition and lifestyle advice- all completely free.



Retail Trust – We know that its not just about physical health, mental wellness is equally important which is why all of our employees get free access and support from the Retail Trust charity. This includes a 24 hour wellbeing helpline, wellness hub, counselling and financial/legal support.



What’s next?



Our Recruitment Team will be reviewing applications and all candidates will receive a response, whether you are successful or unsuccessful. Shortlisted applicants may be asked to confirm a few key details before being booked in for a first stage interview with the Recruiter - this will be behaviourally focussed and centred around how you align with our Culture and Values. If successful we anticipate one or two further interview stages with the Hiring Manager/wider team which will be more technically focussed and could include a presentation/task so we can see your skills in action.

Title: Open-Source Data Analyst
Description:
About The Job

Job summary

Discover what it's like to work in a compliance role that makes an impact. Could you help us shape a stronger, fairer future? Your next career move starts here.

These exciting posts are within the Open Source Data Analytics team, part of Risk Insight & Analytics within the Risk and Intelligence Service.

The Open Source Data Analytics team uses a bespoke cloud based private network and a variety of tools that enables the team to access all areas of the web, identify relevant data and analyse it.

Our Data Analysts use techniques from a range of analytical disciplines to combine, understand and visualise web derived data within a business context. We provide robust Open Source data analysis in a suite of products tailored to business needs and that can be used to inform business decisions and policy. Our analysis enhances the way the department understands, identifies and tackles risk to revenue.

Our ability to safely and legally search the internet to capture, analyse and exploit Open Source data from all areas of the web places us in a unique position to offer a different perspective and insight on issues that impact on tax compliant behaviour now and in the future.

Job Description

RIS collects, integrates and analyses one of the largest and most diverse range of data and intelligence in the world. This enables HMRC to understand and manage risks associated with non-compliance with the UK tax system.

The Team Has Two Core Functions

Horizon Scanning: Identification of new and emerging trends in web technologies, e-commerce and digital developments that impact on the department's strategic objectives.

Data Insight: Complex data collection, interpretation and analysis of compliance populations and/or specific elements of serious non-compliant behaviour to inform tactical and operational decision making. Analysis of social media platforms, their use and growth, the sentiment and language used across them and the compliance behaviour exhibited in reaction to changes in HMRC's approach to compliance and/or legislative change.

We work with our stakeholders and customers to understand their requirements, develop and deliver bespoke solutions and suggest new ideas to support them.

The team employs analytical skills to design and deliver products that inform a diverse customer base within HMRC.

Person specification

You Will

Have experience conducting research to explore complex questions, assess available data sources, and evaluate their suitability for analysis.

Be confident in learning new tools to collect and analyse open source data, including unstructured and large datasets from public, government, and commercial sources.

Be experienced in using analytical tools (for example, Excel, Power BI, R, Python or similar) to clean, manipulate, and interpret data, drawing out meaningful insights.

Be comfortable managing stakeholder relationships: understanding their needs, providing updates, and navigating competing priorities.

Be able to work individually and as part of an agile team to progress projects at pace and deliver actionable insights.

Essential Criteria

To be considered for this role, your application must demonstrate all of the following :


Experience in technical analytical roles including data analysis or intelligence work.
Communicating analytical findings clearly through written reports, visualisations, and storytelling techniques tailored to non-technical audiences.


Behaviours

We'll assess you against these behaviours during the selection process:


Making Effective Decisions


Technical skills

We'll assess you against these technical skills during the selection process:


Technical Assessment - Data Analysis


Benefits

Alongside your salary of £37,682, HM Revenue and Customs contributes £10,916 towards you being a member of the Civil Service Defined Benefit Pension scheme. Find out what benefits a Civil Service Pension provides (opens in a new window).

HMRC operates both Flexible and Hybrid Working policies, allowing you to balance your work and personal commitments. We welcome applications from those who need to work a more flexible arrangement and will agree to requests where possible, considering our operational and customer service needs.

We offer a generous leave allowance, starting at 25 days and increasing by a day for every year of qualifying service up to a maximum of 30 days.


Pension - We make contributions to our colleagues' Alpha pension equal to at least 28.97% of their salary.
Family friendly policies.
Personal support.
Coaching and development.


To find out more about HMRC benefits and find out what it's really like to work for HMRC hear from our insiders or visit Thinking of joining the Civil Service .

Things you need to know

Artificial intelligence

Artificial intelligence can be a useful tool to support your application, however, all examples and statements provided must be truthful, factually accurate and taken directly from your own experience. Where plagiarism has been identified (presenting the ideas and experiences of others, or generated by artificial intelligence, as your own) applications may be withdrawn and internal candidates may be subject to disciplinary action. Please see our candidate guidance (opens in a new window) for more information on appropriate and inappropriate use.

Selection process details

This vacancy is using Success Profiles (opens in a new window) , and will assess your Behaviours, Strengths, Ability, Experience and Technical skills.

How To Apply

Stage 1 - Online tests

After submission of the first stage of your application, you will be invited to complete two online tests,the Civil Service Numerical Test and the Civil Service Verbal Test. You may wish to have paper, pens and calculator ready for completing them. You will receive instructions on how to complete the tests when you are invited. If you successfully pass the test, you will be invited to complete the final stage of the application.

Please complete the online tests as soon as possible (within 24-48 hours is recommended), the closing date for the tests is 23:55pm on 25 June 2026.

Stage 2 - Full Application

As part of the application process, you will be asked to provide the following:


A name-blind CV including details of your Job History, up to your last 5 roles, giving a brief summary of what you delivered and any key achievements in each role (Maximum of 100 words for each role).
A 750-word personal statement. Your Personal Statement should be used to describe how your skills and experience would be suitable for the advertised role, making reference to the Essential Criteria and Person Specification outlined in the advert.
A 250-word statement demonstrating how you meet the Behaviour Making Effective Decisions.


Further details around what this will entail are listed on the application form.

Sift

In the event of a large number of applications being received, an initial sift may be held on the Behaviour statement Making Effective Decisions.

At full sift your Behaviour statement, CV and Personal Statement will be assessed, with the successful candidates being invited to interview.

We may also raise the score required at any stage of the process if we receive a high number of applications.

Interview

Stage 1 - Virtual Interview

During the panel interview, your experience will be assessed and you will be asked strength-based questions.

This Is An Example Of a Strengths-based Question

"It is often said that the customer's needs should come first. To what extent do you agree or disagree with this statement?"

There is no expectation or requirement for you to prepare for the strengths-based questions in advance of the interview, though you may find it helpful to spend some time reflecting on what you enjoy doing and what you do well.

Interviews will take place via video link and last approximately 60 minutes.

Applicants who are successful at Stage 1 of the interview process will progress to a face-to-face interview to access their technical abilities.

Stage 2 - Face to Face Interview - Technical Assessment

You will be invited to a 2-hour and 20-minute face-to-face technical assessment.

You will be asked to locate open-source data online, undertake an analytical exercise using the data gathered, and present findings to the panel.

These Assessments Will Take Place In Either


Nottingham Regional Centre - 1 Unity Square, NG2 1AW, or,
Liverpool Regional Centre - India Buildings, 31 Water Street, Liverpool, L2 0RD


Sift and interview dates to be confirmed.

Online Tests - Banked Scores

When an applicant has a banked score they are not asked to retake the same test, providing it is:


set at the same job level, and
within the same system.


Scores will be banked for six months, or until a new score benchmark is applied, whichever is sooner. This means that if a candidate applies for another vacancy at this grade they may not have to retake the test within that time, and their previous score will be used for the new vacancy.

Eligibility

Please take extra care to tick the correct boxes in the eligibility sections of your application form. Mistakes sometimes happen but if you contact us later than two working days (Monday-Friday) before the vacancy closes, we may not be able to reopen your application for you . If you do make a mistake with your eligibility form, or have withdrawn yourself in error and need your application reinstated whilst the campaign is still live, please contact us via: unitybusinessservicesrecruitmentresults@hmrc.gov.uk - Use the subject line to insert appropriate wording for example - 'Please re-open my application - [insert vacancy ref] & vacancy closing date [insert date]'

To check that you are eligible to apply for this role, please review the eligibility information before submitting your application .

External applicants will join on the pay band minimum.

The HMRC app can help you with your application

The HMRC app can provide you with your past 5 years' employment history, making the process of filling in your application quicker and easier.

If successful in your application, you will need your National Insurance number for the onboarding process.

Download the HMRC app now and save your National Insurance number to your digital phone wallet.

How to download the HMRC app and sign up for an account

Download the free HMRC app from the App Store or Google Play store.

If you have an HMRC online account already, sign straight in using your ID and password. If not, you can prove your identity by answering some questions or providing your photo ID.

You'll then be able to access the app quickly and easily by signing in using a 6-digit PIN, your fingerprint, or facial recognition.

You can find guidance for technical issues on GOV.UK: Technical support with HMRC online services .

Reserve List

A reserve list may be held for up to 12 months from which further appointments may be made for the same or similar roles - if this applies to you, we'll let you know via your Civil Service Jobs account.

Criminal Record Check

Applications received from candidates with a criminal record are considered fairly in accordance with the DBS Code of Practice and the Recruitment of ex-offenders Policy.

Merit List

After interview, a single merit list will be created, and you will only be considered for posts in locations you have expressed a preference for. Appointments will be made in strict merit order in line with the set number of roles in each location.

Hybrid working at HMRC

HMRC is an office-based organisation, and colleagues are expected to spend 60% of their working time in the office. Our offices provide opportunity for interaction, collaboration which aids learning and development and a sense of community. Where the role allows it, and where the home environment is suitable, colleagues can work from home for up to 2 days a week, averaged over a calendar month (or a proportionate amount of time for colleagues who work less than full time).

Reasonable Adjustments

About The Job

Job summary

Discover what it's like to work in a compliance role that makes an impact. Could you help us shape a stronger, fairer future? Your next career move starts here.

These exciting posts are within the Open Source Data Analytics team, part of Risk Insight & Analytics within the Risk and Intelligence Service.

The Open Source Data Analytics team uses a bespoke cloud based private network and a variety of tools that enables the team to access all areas of the web, identify relevant data and analyse it.

Our Data Analysts use techniques from a range of analytical disciplines to combine, understand and visualise web derived data within a business context. We provide robust Open Source data analysis in a suite of products tailored to business needs and that can be used to inform business decisions and policy. Our analysis enhances the way the department understands, identifies and tackles risk to revenue.

Our ability to safely and legally search the internet to capture, analyse and exploit Open Source data from all areas of the web places us in a unique position to offer a different perspective and insight on issues that impact on tax compliant behaviour now and in the future.

Job Description

RIS collects, integrates and analyses one of the largest and most diverse range of data and intelligence in the world. This enables HMRC to understand and manage risks associated with non-compliance with the UK tax system.

The Team Has Two Core Functions

Horizon Scanning: Identification of new and emerging trends in web technologies, e-commerce and digital developments that impact on the department's strategic objectives.

Data Insight: Complex data collection, interpretation and analysis of compliance populations and/or specific elements of serious non-compliant behaviour to inform tactical and operational decision making. Analysis of social media platforms, their use and growth, the sentiment and language used across them and the compliance behaviour exhibited in reaction to changes in HMRC's approach to compliance and/or legislative change.

We work with our stakeholders and customers to understand their requirements, develop and deliver bespoke solutions and suggest new ideas to support them.

The team employs analytical skills to design and deliver products that inform a diverse customer base within HMRC.

Person specification

You Will

Have experience conducting research to explore complex questions, assess available data sources, and evaluate their suitability for analysis.

Be confident in learning new tools to collect and analyse open source data, including unstructured and large datasets from public, government, and commercial sources.

Be experienced in using analytical tools (for example, Excel, Power BI, R, Python or similar) to clean, manipulate, and interpret data, drawing out meaningful insights.

Be comfortable managing stakeholder relationships: understanding their needs, providing updates, and navigating competing priorities.

Be able to work individually and as part of an agile team to progress projects at pace and deliver actionable insights.

Essential Criteria

To be considered for this role, your application must demonstrate all of the following :

  • Experience in technical analytical roles including data analysis or intelligence work.
  • Communicating analytical findings clearly through written reports, visualisations, and storytelling techniques tailored to non-technical audiences.

Behaviours

We'll assess you against these behaviours during the selection process:

  • Making Effective Decisions

Technical skills

We'll assess you against these technical skills during the selection process:

  • Technical Assessment - Data Analysis

Benefits

Alongside your salary of £37,682, HM Revenue and Customs contributes £10,916 towards you being a member of the Civil Service Defined Benefit Pension scheme. Find out what benefits a Civil Service Pension provides (opens in a new window).

HMRC operates both Flexible and Hybrid Working policies, allowing you to balance your work and personal commitments. We welcome applications from those who need to work a more flexible arrangement and will agree to requests where possible, considering our operational and customer service needs.

We offer a generous leave allowance, starting at 25 days and increasing by a day for every year of qualifying service up to a maximum of 30 days.

  • Pension - We make contributions to our colleagues' Alpha pension equal to at least 28.97% of their salary.
  • Family friendly policies.
  • Personal support.
  • Coaching and development.

To find out more about HMRC benefits and find out what it's really like to work for HMRC hear from our insiders or visit Thinking of joining the Civil Service .

Artificial intelligence

Artificial intelligence can be a useful tool to support your application, however, all examples and statements provided must be truthful, factually accurate and taken directly from your own experience. Where plagiarism has been identified (presenting the ideas and experiences of others, or generated by artificial intelligence, as your own) applications may be withdrawn and internal candidates may be subject to disciplinary action. Please see our candidate guidance (opens in a new window) for more information on appropriate and inappropriate use.

 Stage 1 - Online tests

After submission of the first stage of your application, you will be invited to complete two online tests,the Civil Service Numerical Test and the Civil Service Verbal Test. You may wish to have paper, pens and calculator ready for completing them. You will receive instructions on how to complete the tests when you are invited. If you successfully pass the test, you will be invited to complete the final stage of the application.

Please complete the online tests as soon as possible (within 24-48 hours is recommended), the closing date for the tests is 23:55pm on 25 June 2026.
